Based on these processed inputs, a compact neural architecture, termed twinkle little star net (TLSNet), is developed to explicitly exploit multiplicative and high-order feature interactions through a structured three-stage design consisting of feature supply, feature resifting, and feature recombination. This architecture enhances nonlinear representational capacity while maintaining parameter efficiency and stable optimization behavior. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves centimeter-level localization accuracy, attaining a mean absolute error (MAE) of 3.92 cm, outperforming several representative learning-based VLP models under the same experimental settings. TLSNet also effectively suppresses large-error outliers, with 95% of positioning errors remaining below 9.47 cm across receiver heights up to 1.2 m.

title: IEEE Standard for Electric Traction System for Short Primary Maglev Vehicles
authors: N/A
abstract: The system structure, usage conditions, basic functions, performance requirements, and RAMS requirements of the electric traction system for short primary maglev vehicles are described in this standard. Additionally, the test rules and methods are provided. It is intended to guide and standardize the design, inspection, evaluation, and acceptance processes of the electric traction system for short primary maglev vehicles.

title: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
authors: N/A

title: Satellite Coverage Probability with Multi-RIS Deployments under Correlated LoS Conditions
authors: Ahmed Al-Amri, Akram Al-Hourani, Saman Atapattu, Bassel Al Homssi
abstract: Urban environments pose a fundamental challenge to millimeter-wave (mmWave) satellite communications (SatCom) by introducing spatially correlated line-of-sight (LoS) blockage, also known as shadowing, which degrades reliability, even with the assistance of reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS). We develop a tractable analytical framework to evaluate the success probability of SatCom systems aided by multi-RIS deployments while considering the crucial factor of correlated LoS conditions. First, we derive an analytical closed-form expression for the exact per-realization success probability. To address the intractability of averaging over minimum-distance constrained (repulsive) RIS deployments, we propose a geometry-averaged formulation by approximating the Matérn hard-core (MHC) process with an inhomogeneous PPP (IPPP) of a known conditional intensity. We then propose a scalable annular-averaging method that replaces the radial integral with ringwise averages, enabling efficient large-scale analysis. Monte Carlo simulations confirm the model’s validity and reveal the impact of blockage intensity, correlation decay, and RIS–user separation, thus providing practical guidelines for RIS deployment to assist future non-terrestrial networks.

title: Multi-Expert Genetic Programming based Ensemble for Long-Tailed Image Classification
authors: Zhuoya Chen, Qinglan Fan, Ruwang Jiao, Bing Xue, He Huang, Yifan Dai, Mengjie Zhang
abstract: Long-tailed image classification faces challenges of data imbalance and poor feature representation for tail classes, leading to biased predictions favoring head classes. While most existing methods rely on deep neural networks (DNNs), they typically require large amounts of training data and lack interpretability. Genetic Programming (GP) has shown promise in few-shot learning but has seldom been investigated in long-tailed image classification, primarily due to its limited ability to handle class imbalance and its tendency for fitness functions to be biased toward head classes. To fill this gap, this paper proposes a multi-expert GP method for long-tailed image classification.We develop three objective functions, each serving as an expert: 1) a longtailed expert focusing on head-class performance; 2) a balanced-class expert that promotes equal class representation; and 3) an inverse long-tailed expert emphasizing tail classes. This triexpert framework enables GP to jointly optimize complementary objectives and learn robust feature representations for both head and tail classes. To further improve classification performance, the evolved GP individuals from the final population are used to train base learners, and their outputs are integrated via a voting-based ensemble model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art GP and DNN approaches without pretraining across seven long-tailed image classification datasets.

title: Adaptive Modeling for Air Quality: A Continual Learning Framework for PM Estimation in Vietnam_supp1-3668935.pdf
authors: Thanh Thi Nhat Nguyen
abstract: Fine particulate matter (PM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</sub>) poses severe health risks, particularly in rapidly developing and heavily polluted regions such as Vietnam, where sparse monitoring networks and evolving environmental conditions challenge accurate air quality estimation. To bridge this data gap, large-scale pollution maps are typically generated using machine learning or statistical models. However, the widely adopted "one-off" training paradigm, where a single static model is trained on all available historical data, struggles with data drift, is computationally inefficient to update, and generalizes poorly to yearly updated data and station variation. In this study, we investigate continual learning (CL) as an adaptive framework to overcome these limitations. We use multisource data, including ground measurements, meteorological data, land use, emissions, and satellite products, to implement geoiLSTM-based knowledge-transfer models. We benchmark against two representative one-off baselines that bracket common practice, an all-years geoiLSTM and a current-year geoiLSTM, under two evaluation regimes: (i) 10-fold cross-validation (CV) to assess temporal accuracy, and (ii) spatial cross-validation (SCV) (leave-location-out) to test generalization to unseen areas. The results show that our continual learning approach significantly outperforms traditional models, especially in generalizing to unseen areas. Under 10-fold CV, geoiLSTM_MOF achieves comparable accuracy to all-years training (R ≈ 0.88, RMSE ≈ 10.10 μg/m³) while requiring only incremental updates. Under the more challenging SCV setting, geoiLSTM_MOF substantially outperforms baselines, achieving a 26–70% improvement in correlation (R = 0.65), 14–20% lower RMSE (17.02 μg/m³), and 41–64% reduced bias. These findings highlight continual learning as a promising approach for robust PM2.5 estimation in data-scarce, non-stationary environments, enabling timely and accurate air quality assessments for public health and policy.

title: Can IEEE Members Be Voted off the Island? [From The Editor’s Desk]
authors: Dave B. Durocher

title: Graph-Transformer for Spatiotemporal Soil Moisture Forecasting Using Multimodal Remote Sensing Data
authors: Mahdi Saki, Daniel Franklin, Mehran Abolhasan, Justin Lipman, Negin Shariati
abstract: Soil moisture is a key variable in the hydrological cycle, essential for drought monitoring, water resource management, and agriculture decision-making. However, accurate forecasting of soil moisture remains a challenge due to the nonlinear nature of soil water dynamics and the complex influencing factors. While deep learning models such as Long Short-Term Memory and Transformers have advanced temporal modelling, they struggle with spatial dependencies across diverse geographic locations, as their architecture primarily focuses on temporal dynamics. To address this limitation, we propose a novel hybrid framework that integrates graph neural networks with state-of-the-art time series models. We introduce a Graph-Transformer architecture for improved soil moisture forecasting by jointly modelling spatiotemporal dependencies. Our model forecasts soil moisture at two depths including 0–7 cm, referred to as surface soil moisture (SSM), and 7–28 cm, representing the root-zone soil moisture (RZSM). Evaluation using four years of multimodal data from 19 sites across Australia shows that the proposed Graph-Transformer model outperforms the standard Transformer, achieving improvements of up to 10% in the coefficient of determination (R2) and 8% in Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) for SSM, and up to 11% and 9% in R2 and RMSE, respectively, for RZSM. These findings highlight that integrating graph architecture with temporal models can potentially enhance soil moisture forecasting across diverse geographic regions, benefiting effective drought monitoring and agricultural planning.

title: Modeling and Prediction of Conducted Electromagnetic Interference in Phase-Shifted Full-Bridge Converters
authors: Lihong Xie, Sifan Li, Ying Li, Yiting Huang, Xinbo Ruan
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The phase-shifted full-bridge (PSFB) converter, featuring simple topology and soft-switching, has been extensively employed in medium-to-high power conversions. As an isolated topology with multiple power switches, the PSFB converter is characterized by multiple noise sources and multiple noise propagation routes, leading to complex and harsh electromagnetic interference (EMI) challenges. In this article, the equivalent common-mode (CM) and differential-mode (DM) noise models for the PSFB converter are built. Then, the CM and DM spectral properties are presented, and the worst cases of the CM and DM noises over various input voltage and load situations are obtained. The analysis indicates that the CM noise is concentrated at odd harmonics of the switching frequency, and its worst spectrum occurs at the fundamental frequency with high input voltage and light load conditions; while the DM noise is concentrated at even harmonics of the switching frequency, and its worst spectrum occurs at the second harmonic frequency with high input voltage and full load conditions. A prototype is fabricated in the laboratory, and tested data are provided to confirm the effectiveness of theoretical analysis.

title: Improvement of deep learning-based reconstruction for MRI using phase-scrambling Fourier transform imaging
authors: Kazuki Yamato, Satoshi Ito
abstract: Deep learning-based reconstruction for MRI has attracted attention as an effective method for reducing reconstruction time and improving reconstructed image quality. In an application of compressed sensing MRI (CS-MRI), the reconstructed image quality can be improved by using phase-scrambling Fourier transform (PSFT) imaging, which applies a quadratic phase modulation to the subject. In this study, we propose a deep learning-based image reconstruction method using PSFT signal to further improve image quality. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, image reconstruction experiments were conducted for both real-valued and complex-valued images. In these experiments, image reconstruction experiments using various sampling pattern were executed. For complex-valued images, a two-step reconstruction strategy incorporating phase correction was introduced, since reconstruction from PSFT signals exhibits high performance when a real-valued constraint can be imposed. The experimental results showed that the peak signal-to-noise ratios (PSNR) of the reconstructed images using PSFT imaging were higher than those using conventional Fourier transform imaging for both real and complex-valued images. For complex-valued images, the quasi-equal space sampling patterns achieved superior PSNRs compared to the equal space patterns. In addition, the reconstructed images obtained from complex-valued data achieved higher PSNR than state-of-the-art methods such as ReconFormer. Therefore, a visual comparison of the real- and complex-valued images reconstructed using the proposed method revealed improvements in both sharpness and the preservation of fine detail structure. These findings confirm that PSFT-based CS-MRI reconstruction enhances image quality, particularly when combined with deep learning techniques.

title: Achieving Distributive Justice in Federated Learning via Uncertainty Quantification_supp1-3668474.pdf
authors: Alycia Noel Carey

title: Electric Vehicles Energy Consumption Prediction Based on Seasonal Transfer Learning Model
authors: Hongxing Chen, Jinjun Tang, Benxiao Lou, Lipeng Hu
abstract: Accurate energy consumption (EC) prediction is essential for alleviating drive range anxiety in electric vehicles (EVs). However, differences in the training data due to seasonal fluctuations can easily lead to poor performance of the EC prediction model, while collecting fully labelled data across all seasons remains costly and labour-intensive. To address this problem, this paper proposes a seasonal transfer learning-based (TL-based) model to achieve accurate EC estimation across seasons. Specifically, the impact of seasonal variations on EC is elucidated using detailed numerical and theoretical methods, aiming to quantify differences in EC across seasons and to reveal the influence mechanisms based on the electrochemistry, battery thermal management, and air conditioning system aspects. Then, the route-based, battery-based, drive-motor-based, and environmental features are extracted to ensure the comprehensiveness of the model input and build the EC estimator. Subsequently, a seasonal TL-based bidirectional gated recurrent unit neural network with attention (BIGRUNN Attention) is established to map the relationship between the extracted features and EC. Lastly, validation on real-world operational data from different latitudes and seasons in China shows that the proposed model reduces the mean absolute error, root mean square error, and symmetric mean absolute percentage error by approximately 22%, 22%, and 20%, respectively, outperforming baseline models (i.e., BIGRUNN Attention without any TL strategy).

title: Geometric Model Predictive Path Integral for Agile UAV Control with Online Collision Avoidance_supp1-3668528.mp4
authors: Pavel Pochobradsky

title: Task Placement Optimization of Redundant Robots Using Reliability Maps under Locked Joint Failures_supp1-3668506.mp4
authors: Biyun Xie
abstract: The failure-tolerant workspace of a robot is defined as the reachable workspace both before and after an arbitrary joint is locked at an arbitrary angle during a motion. Once the task is located within the failure-tolerant workspace, task completion can be guaranteed. However, a failure-tolerant workspace is typically only a small region within the robot workspace, and existing methods are limited to computing it in the positional workspace. This work introduces reliability maps, which provide information on the reliability of different regions across the entire workspace. A method is developed to compute reliability maps in both positional and orientational workspace accurately and efficiently. The computed reliability map can be applied to optimize end-effector task placement to maximize the task completion ratio. Examples of reliability maps for a planar 3R robot, a spatial 4R robot, and a spatial 7R robot are computed using the proposed method, along with uniform and random sampling for comparison. Simulation and physical experiments are conducted to further validate the proposed task placement optimization method. Results indicate that using the reliability map to optimize task placement can significantly improve the task completion ratio after experiencing arbitrary locked joint failures.

title: $\pi$-BA: Probabilistic Neural Bundle Adjustment with Iterative Cycle Optimization for Driving Scene Reconstruction
authors: Yunxuan Mao, Dongkun Zhang, Lilu Liu, Yue Wang, Rong Xiong
abstract: Urban scene reconstruction under noisy camera poses remains a critical challenge for autonomous driving. While recent neural dense Bundle Adjustment (BA) methods have shown promising results in specific settings, their performance often degrades in real-world urban scenarios due to noisy correspondences and imbalanced optimization between camera poses and scene parameters, which leads the scene representation to overfit to erroneous geometric constraints, causing the system to converge to suboptimal local minima. We propose <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi$</tex-math></inline-formula>-BA, a neural BA framework designed to improve robustness in large-scale outdoor reconstruction. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi$</tex-math></inline-formula>-BA introduces an uncertainty-aware probabilistic model that adaptively down-weights unreliable correspondences, enabling more stable joint optimization of camera poses and scene geometry. To decouple pose estimation from geometry artifacts, we employ a cyclic optimization strategy that periodically reinitializes the radiance field while preserving refined camera poses. Extensive experiments on diverse real-world datasets demonstrate that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi$</tex-math></inline-formula>-BA consistently outperforms existing neural BA methods in terms of pose and reconstruction accuracy under noisy initialization. These results highlight the effectiveness of integrating probabilistic modeling with iterative refinement for scalable and robust neural reconstruction in complex outdoor environments.

title: Hierarchical Trajectory Planning of Floating-Base Multi-Link Robot for Maneuvering in Confined Environments_supp1-3669051.mp4
authors: Moju Zhao
abstract: Floating-base multi-link robots can change their shape during flight, making them well-suited for applications in confined environments such as autonomous inspection and search and rescue. However, trajectory planning for such systems remains an open challenge because the problem lies in a high-dimensional, constraint-rich space where collision avoidance must be addressed together with kinematic limits and dynamic feasibility. This work introduces a hierarchical trajectory planning framework that integrates global guidance with configuration-aware local optimization. First, we exploit the dual nature of these robots—the root link as a rigid body for guidance and the articulated joints for flexibility—to generate global anchor states that decompose the planning problem into tractable segments. Second, we design a local trajectory planner that optimizes each segment in parallel with differentiable objectives and constraints, systematically enforcing kinematic feasibility and maintaining dynamic feasibility by avoiding control singularities. Third, we implement a complete system that directly processes point-cloud data, eliminating the need for handcrafted obstacle models. Extensive simulations and real-world experiments confirm that this framework enables an articulated aerial robot to exploit its morphology for maneuvering that rigid robots cannot achieve. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first planning framework for floating-base multi-link robots that has been demonstrated on a real robot to generate continuous, collision-free, and dynamically feasible trajectories directly from raw point-cloud inputs, without relying on handcrafted obstacle models.

title: Sub-6$\,\text{GHz}$ SAR Imaging With Frequency Hopping Toward JCAS Applications in Automotive Scenarios
authors: Max Basler, Alexander Grathwohl, Timo Grebner, Christian Waldschmidt
abstract: As highly assisted and autonomous driving systems evolve, the need for detailed environmental information becomes crucial for accurate trajectory planning and safety assurance. To achieve this, sub-6<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,\text{GHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula> vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication signals could be leveraged for imaging purposes, merging communication and environmental sensing. However, the low center frequencies and limited bandwidths in this joint communication and sensing (JCAS) approach pose challenges for imaging performance. This article presents a method for using bandwidths across multiple communication bands, leveraging the synthetic aperture radar (SAR) principle to combine data of various measurement points and achieve high-resolution environmental images. To accomplish this, a channel sounding measurement is performed using a 1<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,\text{GHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula> to 4<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,\text{GHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula> linear frequency modulated (LFM) sensor system. The frequency ramp is then restricted to various subband constellations for each measurement point in the SAR processing. By randomly selecting subbands within the full ramp, the performance of random frequency hopping between measurement points is quantitatively assessed. The experimental SAR results validate the theoretical predictions and anticipated imaging characteristics. Images obtained with an effective bandwidth of 100<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,\text{MHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula> remain comparable to those acquired using the full bandwidth. To assess its real-world applicability in an automotive scenario, the evaluations are applied to various environments, with a particular focus on a parking lot. Despite frequency hopping being restricted to defined communication bands and an effective bandwidth of approximately 100<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,\text{MHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, the resulting SAR image clearly shows targets such as cars, a bicycle, a wooden fence, bushes, and a person. This demonstrates the feasibility of sub-6<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\,\text{GHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula> SAR imaging under communication constraints within a JCAS context.

title: Lightweight and Personalized Single-Eye Emotion Recognition via CNN-SNN Spatiotemporal Learning and Memory-Inferred Event Features
authors: Qianhui Liu, Jiqing Zhang, Yang Wang, Malu Zhang, Xin Yang, Gang Pan, Haizhou Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Emotion recognition is essential for improving user experience and interaction quality in human-centered applications. While recent studies have leveraged both event and traditional cameras to enhance eye-based emotion recognition, their practical deployment is hindered by the scarcity of event cameras and the complexity of dual-modality frameworks. Personalization, which is critical for handling individual differences in emotional expression, is also affected by these factors, resulting in reduced performance and adaptation efficiency. To address these challenges, we propose a lightweight and personalized single-eye emotion recognition network, called LPSEER. LPSEER introduces a novel hybrid neural architecture that integrates a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a spiking neural network (SNN) to capture spatiotemporal features from video frames and events, respectively. Additionally, we design a memorybased event feature inference (MEFI) module that recalls event features from video frames, eliminating the reliance on event cameras during inference and personalization while retaining the discriminative advantages of event-based representations. Experimental results demonstrate that LPSEER achieves state-of- the-art recognition accuracy while maintaining the smallest model size and lowest computational cost. Further experiments confirm the strong generalization capabilities and the ability to achieve faster, more accurate personalization. These advantages collectively enable lightweight, accurate, and efficient emotion recognition for real-world human-centered applications.

title: Comprehensive Review on Vehicle Verification and Authentication Using Blockchain Technology
authors: Surajit Dutta, Dilip Kumar Barman
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The shortcomings of centralized authentication have led to a move toward decentralized, tamper-resistant solutions as vehicle networks develop. An organized review of blockchain and cryptographic techniques for vehicle authentication and verification is presented in this study. It divides existing approaches into identity management models (decentralized IDs, PKI-less systems), cryptographic techniques (ECC, zero-knowledge proofs, group signatures), consensus mechanisms (PBFT, PoW, DPoS), and hybrid blockchain-IoT frameworks. The analysis examines trade-offs between security, latency, and scalability while presenting a novel taxonomy that matches focused solutions with risks unique to VANETs, like message forgery and Sybil attacks. The increasing use of privacy-preserving authentication techniques and the possibility of post-quantum secure blockchain systems are highlighted. Important insights for boosting resilience and confidence in next vehicle systems are provided by this work.

title: An Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Predicting Traffic Accident Risk and Identifying Critical Factors Using CNN–BiLSTM–Attention and SHAP
authors: S.V.N Sreenivasu, Thirupatamma Kallam, Velangini Rani Biraka, Mythri Yenumala, Neelima G, Malathi Latha T
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Accurate estimation of accident risk and the identification of critical contributing factors are critical for improving traffic safety and road fatality reduction. This paper introduces a new deep learning architecture that combines Convolutional Neural Networks(CNN),Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory(BiLSTM),and Attention to capture both spatial and temporal trends in traffic accident data. To provide interpretability and credibility for decision-making, the model is augmented further with SHAP(Shapley Additive explanations) to identify the most significant features responsible for accident severity. The model is tested on two real datasets: the US Accident Dataset (March 2023) and the UK STATS19 dataset, following rigorous preprocessing, feature normalization, and class balancing using SMOTE Tomek and RandomOverSampler methods. The suggested CNN–BiLSTM–Attention model presents high predictive accuracy, MAE of 0.137 (US) and 0.179 (UK), far exceeding conventional models such as Logistic Regression. Through SHAP analysis, the framework reveals transparent insights regarding the key factors determining accident severity, and the framework is both precise and interpretable. This paper presents an efficient and scalable solution for real time traffic risk prediction and traffic authority decision support.

title: Gait-Triggered Neuromuscular Electrical Stimulation with Unloader Knee Braces: A Feasibility Study
authors: Mohadeseh Jafarian, Reza Khosrozadeh Sarijalou, Amin Komeili, Kourosh Barati, Navid Arjmand
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Objective. Knee osteoarthritis (OA) commonly involves the medial compartment of the joint. Valgus unloader braces can reduce joint loading in this region but may also suppress quadriceps activation. Neuromuscular electrical stimulation (NMES) can strengthen quadriceps when voluntary activation is limited. We evaluated the feasibility of a gait-triggered NMES system used in combination with a valgus unloader brace. Methods. A portable module was developed incorporating a commercial NMES stimulator, a microcontroller, and an inertial measurement unit (IMU). Gait events (mid-swing, initial contact, and toe-off) were detected in real time to trigger quadriceps stimulation. The system was tested in eight adults with medial knee <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{O A}$</tex> and five healthy controls, each completing a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex>-minute supervised walking session. Results. The device operated continuously throughout all sessions. OA patients walked with a significantly slower cadence than controls (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.1 \pm 0.4$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.0 \pm 0.4$</tex> steps <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\cdot \mathrm{s}^{-1}, p=0.008$</tex>). Cycle detection accuracy remained high in both groups (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91-93 \%$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}= 0.72$</tex>). No serious adverse events occurred. Conclusion. This study introduced and evaluated a novel brace-compatible, gait-triggered NMES system for knee OA. The prototype reliably synchronized stimulation with walking, was safe and represented a reproducible model for future clinical translation. Further refinements and multi-week clinical trials are warranted.

title: A Design of T/R Module Based on SIP Technology
authors: Dongxin Yin, Hongjiang Wu, Peng Chen, Shuxun He, Huixiang Li, Pan Deng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: This paper proposes and designs a 64-channel X-band Transmit/Receive (T/R) module based on a multi-System-in-Package (SIP) architecture. Inter-module signal transmission is achieved through Ball Grid Array (BGA) solder ball bonding technology. The core functional unit of the module is a four-channel transceiver module, which integrates 6-bit phase shifter, 6-bit attenuator, power amplifier chip, and limiting low-noise amplifier chip. All other functional modules adopt bidirectional amplifiers to implement a shared transmit-receive path. And realize a multifunctional module whose size is 15mm×15mm, On this premise, the developed T/R module achieves outstanding performance parameters: the output power per channel exceeds 33 dBm, the receive gain is greater than 25 dB, the standing wave ratio (SWR) of each channel is less than 1.6, and the gain flatness is within 1 dB. Moreover, the module realizes a high-reliability design by virtue of a tile-type vertical interconnection structure, which effectively enhances the structural stability and signal integrity of the system.

title: Analysis of Adolescent Interests for Vocational Guidance with a Data-Driven Decision Support System
authors: Manisha Koranga, Arslonbek Nurjonov, Gulkhan Dadenova, Adilbek Dauletov, Rakhimjon Rajapboyevich Rakhimov, Yashin Khudoyberganov
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Following their higher education, several students globally struggle to choose a suitable professional route. Adolescents may lack the ability to understand the processes needed to choose a professional route that is right for them. As they progress through this phase, adolescents frequently have questions and concerns regarding their career goals. Additionally, they can doubt their capacity to succeed in their preferred area. Machine learning (ML), a key element of artificial intelligence (Al), is gaining popularity for its ability to assist with individualized vocational guidance by assessing educational history, personal interests, and employment data. Investigation in this area is still uneven and systematically varied despite increased interest. This work used a decision support system (DSS) and ML algorithms to examine adolescent interest in vocational guidance. A comprehensive DSS-based assessment score framework is created employing big data evaluation. Multiple metrics were selected to assess students' literacy and generate DSS and variable matrices. In this study, classification evaluation is used to forecast and evaluate the vocational guidance employing AdaBoost, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and logistic regression (LR). Based on the evaluation results, 58% of students were classified as strong or extremely strong, showing that the vast majority possess the necessary vocational guidance for employment. Students prefer to rate their vocational knowledge higher, with values around 4.1, whereas corporate evaluations average 3.7 points.

title: Overpressure Effect on Type L IV 36L Tank as a Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle (FCEVs) Using HyRam+
authors: Achmad Nurfanani, Handrea Bernando Tambunan, Wigas Digwijaya, Budi Setiyana, Muchammad Muchammad, Dhami Johar Damiri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This study investigates the overpressure effects on Type L IV 36L tanks used in Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles (FCEVs), utilizing HyRam% software for analysis. As the global energy landscape shifts toward sustainable alternatives, hydrogen fuel cell vehicles have emerged as a promising solution for reducing emissions in the transportation sector. However, the inherent properties of hydrogen, including its high diffusion rate and flammability, present significant safety challenges that require careful analysis and mitigation strategies. The research methodology employed a systematic approach to analyze overpressure effects under various operating conditions. The study examined three different pressure levels (420 bar, 500 bar, and 600 bar), four leak sizes ($0.0095 \mathrm{~mm}, 0.095 \mathrm{~mm}, 0.475 \mathrm{~mm}$, and 0.95 mm), and four spray angles ($5^{\circ}, 10^{\circ}, 15^{\circ}$, and 20°) to evaluate their impact on system safety comprehensively. The investigation utilised numerical overpressure models to calculate critical parameters, including mass flow rate, flammable mass, overpressure, and impulse values. Results demonstrated a clear correlation between system parameters and safety outcomes. The analysis revealed that increases in pressure, leak size, and spray angle led to proportional increases in mass flow rate, flammable mass, overpressure, and impulse values. The most significant effects were observed at 600 bar pressure with a leak size of 0.95 mm and a spray angle of 20°, resulting in a mass flow rate of $2.419 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{s}$, flammable mass of $2.057 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~kg}$, overpressure of 14.318 kPa, and impulse of $3.4831 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{kPa}$-s. This research provides valuable insights for the design and operation of hydrogen refuelling stations and FCEVs, highlighting the critical relationship between system parameters and potential safety risks. The findings contribute to the development of more effective safety protocols and risk mitigation strategies in hydrogen fuel infrastructure, supporting the broader adoption of hydrogen as a clean energy carrier in transportation applications.

title: Transformer-based Volleyball AR/VR Intelligent Training System and Tactical Decision Optimization
authors: Liying Luo, Menghui Shi, Yunju Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Volleyball training requires sophisticated tactical coordination and real-time decision-making that traditional coach-led instruction struggles to effectively convey. Existing sports analysis systems rely on passive video review lacking immersive feedback mechanisms essential for motor skill development. This paper proposes a Transformer-based volleyball AR/VR intelligent training system integrating spatiotemporal pattern recognition with tactical decision optimization. We develop a dual spatial-temporal Transformer architecture that separately models player interactions within frames and movement dynamics across sequences, capturing complex multi-player coordination patterns. The system employs reinforcement learning through Proximal Policy Optimization to generate personalized tactical recommendations adapted to individual skill levels. An immersive AR/VR interface deployed on Meta Quest Pro delivers multi-modal feedback including visual trajectory overlays, auditory rhythm cues, and haptic timing corrections for kinesthetic learning. Experiments on 4,320 rally sequences demonstrate 88.4% tactical classification accuracy, outperforming CNN (76.8%), BiLSTM (79.5%), and GCN (82.1%) baselines by substantial margins. Human training studies with 45 participants show significant skill acquisition acceleration compared to conventional instruction. Hyperparameter analysis identifies optimal configurations at 6 Transformer layers and 12 attention heads. This work advances AI-enhanced sports training by combining deep learning-based tactical understanding with immersive technology for practical athletic applications.

title: Artificial intelligence Comparative Analysis Job Role Predicting Resume Evaluation Human Resource Management
authors: P. Asifa Tazeem, S. Usha, Madhuri Sailaja Jampana, M. Indrapriya, P. Saradha, Muthuvel Kumari E
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The Artificial intelligence-based enhancement of job matching effectiveness, through an innovative algorithm for employment information systems in the labor market, is still essential for optimising the job matching component and its effectiveness. In light of this, a novel employment data information system algorithm is still a popular method for process optimisation that makes use of machine learning models and sophisticated data analytics. The application of such an algorithm is centred on a number of elements, such as dynamic adaptability, continuous learning, and an exact matching process. Beyond just using keyword matching, the algorithm mainly uses natural language processing (NLP) to comprehend resumes and job descriptions. Semantics and context are extensively taken into account in the process to guarantee that precise matches are found. The system matches a candidate to various roles based on an assessment of their experiences, credentials, and abilities. As a result, this supports the effectiveness of the system in dynamically adjusting and ensuring that responsive measures are provided to the shifting dynamics within the labor market. Through a parameter that analyses real-time labor market data, algorithms are designed to continuously provide regular updates. Such adaptability's nature offers employers and job seekers timely and current recommendations. Therefore, the purpose of this article is to investigate how to maximise the efficiency of job matching by utilising information system algorithms in a novel way. Findings show that information system algorithms are essential to improving predictive accuracy over time, which raises the job matching process's accuracy and dependability. Because human judgment is subjective and the number of job applications is increasing, it is still difficult to evaluate resumes effectively and precisely in modern human resource (HR) management.

title: Adaptive Load Shedding for Tambora Grid Using Differential Evolution Algorithm
authors: I Putu Rastaman Putra, Sarjiya, Yusuf Susilo Wijoyo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Tiny power systems such as the Tambora grid are particularly vulnerable to frequency instability caused by sudden generator outages. To address this, an adaptive load shedding is developed to determine the optimal load shedding strategy in real-time. The total power imbalance is first estimated using a swing equation-based model, which incorporates system inertia, nominal frequency, and the Rate of Change of Frequency (ROCOF). A scaling coefficient is then applied to the calculated power loss, representing the proportion of power that needs to be shed. This coefficient is optimized using the Differential Evolution (DE) algorithm, where each candidate solution is simulated in DIgSILENT PowerFactory through Python automation to ensure the resulting steady-state frequency exceeds $\mathbf{4 9 . 6 ~ H z}$. To strengthen the system’s voltage performance, the Fast Voltage Stability Index (FVSI) is computed for all load buses under pre-disturbance conditions. These values are normalized and used to prioritize buses according to their vulnerability to voltage collapse. Once the total shedding amount is determined, it is distributed proportionally, giving higher load curtailment to buses with higher FVSI values. This ensures that the response not only addresses frequency drop but also mitigates voltage instability in weak areas of the grid. The integrated approach enhances both frequency and voltage resilience, making the scheme suitable for implementation as a Special Protection Scheme (SPS) in specific contingency to prevent system blackout.

title: Hivanta-Animal Voice Recognition Using Machine Learning
authors: Ashish Virendra Chandak, Harshita Mali, Himanshi Bandwal, Vedant Somankar, Praful R. Pardhi, Pratik K Agrawal
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The field of bioacoustics has witnessed significant advancements through machine learning, enabling automated species identification. This paper presents Hivanta, a web-based animal voice recognition system that classifies animal sounds using a Convolutional Neural Network. The system processes audio inputs via Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients and delivers real-time predictions through a TensorFlow.js-powered interface. Evaluated on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5,000+$</tex> samples across five species, the model achieved 92.6% accuracy with a prediction latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1.5$</tex> seconds. This paper demonstrates the viability of lightweight, browser-based solutions for ecological research and education, addressing limitations of manual sound analysis.

title: A Fine-Grained U-Net Approach with Attention Mechanism for Segmentation of Martensite and Retained Austenite
authors: Xiaofeng Gai, Fei Wang, Sen Liu, Liang Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: As a critical component, the performance and service life of gear shafts are of paramount importance. Carbonitriding enhances their properties, while metallographic images serve as a fundamental basis for inspection. Traditional manual identification of metallographic images suffers from high costs and low efficiency. Deep learning has found extensive application in image recognition. This paper proposes an improved attention-based U-Net model. By employing shallower downsampling layers, attention-guided skip connections, and multi-scale feature fusion, it achieves precise identification of martensite/retained austenite.

title: Escaping Local Optima: A Quad-Directional Hill Climbing Algorithm for Rugged Three Dimensional Landscapes
authors: Pranamya Prasanna Belvai, Benazeer Ali, Anjali Bansal, Jimcymol James, Rajesh Mahadeva, Krishnaraj Chadaga
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Escaping local optima remains a central challenge in navigating rugged, multi-modal optimization landscapes. Traditional methods such as Steepest Ascent Hill Climbing (SAHC) often suffer from premature convergence due to their limited one-directional search capability. In this study, a novel QuadDirectional Hill Climbing (QDHC) algorithm is proposed to enhance the local search strategy by evaluating pseudo-gradients in four cardinal directions (North, East, West, South). This multidirectional approach enables the algorithm to make more informed decisions about ascent in complex terrains. The QDHC algorithm is evaluated on a suite of synthetic 3D benchmark functions with varying degrees of ruggedness. In highly rugged terrain, QDHC achieves an improvement of 890% in the final value of the function over SAHC, while on smoother terrains it outperforms SAHC by 96%. These results demonstrate that QDHC significantly improves convergence and robustness in non-convex, high-dimensional search spaces where traditional hill climbing often fails.

title: Lightweight AI Framework for Fault Detection in Photovoltaic Cells Using Electroluminescence Imaging
authors: Pragya Shrivastava, Shailesh Pancham Khapre
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This research proposes a lightweight artificial intelligence framework for electroluminescence (EL) image-based fault detection in photovoltaic (PV) cells. Our approach uses Random Forest classifiers to provide a hardware-friendly and efficient alternative to conventional deep learning methods that demand significant CPU resources. The dataset was preprocessed using OpenCV to flatten and resize the images, and the labels were cleaned using Pandas. The model's performance was comprehensively evaluated using five- fold cross-validation, and the average accuracy, precision, recall, and F 1 score were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.5 \%, 80.9 {\%}$</tex>, and 75.3%, respectively. These results demonstrate that lightweight AI approaches can achieve robust classification performance in resourceconstrained environments, providing a practical baseline for industrial PV quality inspection.

title: On the Generation of Phononic Frequency Combs Using Defect Modes of Phononic Crystals
authors: Suhas Suresh Bharadwaj, Murtaza Rangwala, Adarsh Ganesan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for generating phononic frequency combs (PFCs) using defect-localized modes in a two-dimensional hexagonal phononic crystal. Localized vibration modes from a singular point defect produce evenly spaced spectral lines corresponding to PFCs. Numerical modelling reveals robust energy transfer under a single-tone drive, generating spectral sidebands. These results demonstrate defect engineering in phononic crystals as a tunable platform for PFC generation with significant applications in high-resolution sensing, timing, and quantum-acoustic technologies.

title: Bayesian Monocular Depth Refinement via Neural Radiance Fields
authors: Arun Muthukkumar
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Monocular depth estimation has applications in many fields, such as autonomous navigation and extended reality, making it an essential computer vision task. However, current methods often produce smooth depth maps that lack the fine geometric detail needed for accurate scene understanding. We propose MDENeRF, an iterative framework that refines monocular depth estimates using depth information from Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs). MDENeRF consists of three components: (1) an initial monocular estimate for global structure, (2) a NeRF trained on perturbed viewpoints, with per-pixel uncertainty, and (3) Bayesian fusion of the noisy monocular and NeRF depths. We derive NeRF uncertainty from the volume rendering process to iteratively inject high-frequency fine details. Meanwhile, our monocular prior maintains global structure. We demonstrate improvements on key metrics and experiments using indoor scenes from the SUN RGB-D dataset.

title: Graph neural networks for recommendation systems: A survey and future directions
authors: R. Keerthika, Abduvali Sottarov, Anant Deogaonkar, Chetna Vaid Kwatra, Rakhimjon Rajapboyevich Rakhimov, Abhinav Singhal
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are now a powerful means of learning about data in a graph format, with special support of recommendation systems, where the interactions between users and items naturally form complex graphs. This survey provides a close-up view of the application of GNNs in recommendation systems. By highlighting their contribution to representation learning through the acquisition of context, user-item relations, and higher-order relations. In a broad sense, we categorize existing GNN-based recommendation models into four primary categories that are knowledge-aware recommendation, session-based recommendation, user-item bipartite graph methods, and social recommendation. The significant aspects of design, like graph building, aggregation methods, and scalability concerns, are discussed in detail. We also analyze the GNNs' performances in various areas and benchmarks, which are more advantageous than traditional deep learning and collaborative filtering methods. GNN-based recommenders have drawbacks in terms of explainability, dynamic modelling, and scalability, notwithstanding their potential. We list potential research avenues to solve issues, such as effective large-scale training, including temporal and heterogeneous data, and making recommendations that are interpretable. For scholars and practitioners looking to comprehend and progress the state-of-the-art in GNN-powered recommendation systems, this survey attempts to act as a fundamental resource.

title: HierAD: Hierarchical Audio Description Generation via Multi-Stage Visual-Linguistic Reasoning
authors: Yudong Wang, Xiaoyu Wu, Xiankang Qin, Yifei Li, Senyu Tang, Yijun He
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Generating coherent audio descriptions (AD) for movies requires understanding both fine-grained visual details and long-range narrative context. Existing methods process segments independently, producing fragmented descriptions lacking character consistency. We propose HIERAD, a training-free, three-stage hierarchical pipeline: Stage 1 uses a VLM with sliding-window context to generate dense visual descriptions; Stage 2 constructs global story context through character graph building, semantic scene aggregation, and event chain extraction; Stage 3 refines descriptions by integrating visual details with character relationships and narrative structure. Experiments on MAD-EVAL demonstrate state-of-the-art semantic understanding, achieving BertScore of 54.3 (+15.1 over prior best) and SPICE of 8.9, outperforming both training-free and fine-tuned methods.

title: Instantaneous Deployment Algorithm for Communication Relay UAV Based on Improved PSO
authors: Jiawei Zhou, Chenqi Xu, Lei Yin
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have emerged as a promising solution for communication relay deployment (CRD) due to their three-dimensional mobility, dynamic resource allocation, and rapid deployment capabilities. However, optimizing UAV relay positioning in dynamic environments remains a critical challenge. This paper proposes a novel wireless communication system integrating ground robot swarms (GRS), UAVs, and ground base stations (GBS) to enable efficient airground information flow via decode-and-forward (DF) relaying. This paper derives an analytical outage probability expression to establish the optimization objective and develop a gradient-based particle swarm optimization (GPSO) algorithm for relay UAV deployment. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the GPSO algorithm significantly outperforms traditional particle swarm optimization (PSO) methods in terms of trajectory planning and bit error rate (BER) reduction. Specifically, in scenarios with 10 and 15 GRS units, the GPSO algorithm achieves a 97.96% and 99.23% reduction in BER, respectively, compared to 95.52% and 99.22% for PSO. Furthermore, the GPSO algorithm exhibits faster convergence and superior adaptability in large-scale complex scenarios, validating its effectiveness for communication relay deployment based on unmanned aerial vehicle (CRD-UAV) applications.

title: Recent Advances in Dynamic Threshold-Aided Energy Detection for Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: M.Ramamohan Reddy, Shaik Jagadeesh Babu, Pradyumna Kumar Mohapatra, Vundavalli Ravindra, Ravi Narayan Panda, Saroja Kumar Rout
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Efficient and reliable spectrum sensing remains a cornerstone of cognitive radio networks (CRNs), enabling opportunistic access to underutilized frequency bands. Traditional energy detection techniques rely on fixed thresholds, which are often suboptimal in dynamic wireless environments due to noise uncertainty and fluctuating signal conditions. This review paper explores recent advancements in dynamic threshold-based spectrum sensing, comparing their performance to conventional static methods. A comprehensive analysis is presented, supported by MATLAB R2023a simulations evaluating key performance metrics such as probability of detection (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{P}_{\text{D}}$</tex>), probability of false alarm (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{P}_{\text{F A}}$</tex>), missed detection (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P_{M}$</tex>), and total error rate (TER) under varying signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. Matched filtering techniques, including averaging and rational impulse response filters, are assessed in conjunction with adaptive threshold estimation. Results highlight the superiority of dynamic thresholding regarding improved detection exactness and robustness across extended SNR levels. The paper concludes with open research challenges and potential directions, including machine learning-based adaptive sensing and real-time implementation for energy-efficient CRNs.

title: Optimizing End-to-End Delay, Energy Consumption, and Task Success in Satellite-Based Edge Computing for Smart Farming in Isolated Environments
authors: Messaoud Babaghayou, Fatima Zahra Zaoui
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: Smart farming is increasingly dependent on Internet of Things (IoT) devices, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), and automation to improve productivity and sustainability. However, in isolated rural regions, limited terrestrial infrastructure leads to high latency, unstable connectivity, and strict energy constraints, which hinder the adoption of traditional cloud-based solutions. To address these challenges, we propose a satellite-enabled edge computing framework that adapts task offloading to the specific requirements of agricultural applications. The proposed IsoLink algorithm prioritizes responsiveness through proximity-aware scheduling and dynamic weighting of delay and execution costs across a multi-tier satellite hierarchy (mist, edge, and cloud). We evaluate the framework in SatEdgeSim with smart farming workloads and compare it against baseline strategies such as Round Robin, Trade Off, and Weighted_Greedy. Results show that IsoLink significantly reduces end-to-end delay, lowers energy consumption, and improves task success rates, with only a slight increase in CPU utilization. These findings demonstrate the potential of satellite edge computing to bridge connectivity gaps in rural agriculture and contribute to more resilient and sustainable farming systems.

title: Designing a Multi-Tier Data Governance Framework for SAP Applications on AWS Hybrid Cloud Infrastructures
authors: Ramesh Julakanti, Sumanth Bontha, Kiran Kumar Reddy, Tejas Vepala, Sharath Reddy Gade, Narendra Balaji Medikonda
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Abstract: Data governance is also essential to guarantee data quality, compliance, and security in enterprise systems, notably in SAP application over hybrid cloud systems. This paper introduces a Multi-Tier Data Governance Framework (MT-dGF) which utilizes the application of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) to automate the data classification, policy violations, and forecast governance risks. The framework has three levels, which include Data Ingestion & Classification, Policy Enforcement, and Predictive Governance and Auditing, that allows the growth of governance as scalable, adaptive, and proactive governance. The empirical testing based on SAP S/4HANA logs, SAP BW/4HANA metadata and AWS Cloud Trail data shows that the MT-dGF is 95.8 percent accurate in compliance, the system is less prone to latency than the traditional rule-based methods and is efficient in detecting anomalies in structured, unstructured and sequential data. The findings substantiate the assumption that the combination of ML and DL models in a tiered architecture improves the efficiency of governance, reliability, and prediction. The future directions will be incorporation of explainable AI, federated learning, and energy efficient model that will further enhance hybrid cloud governance frameworks.

title: Emotion Recognition for Student Learning Enhancement Using Multi-Layer Feature Fusion and AI Techniques
authors: Ankita Awasthi, Munish Sabharwal, Sariyev Shokhrukh, Vanya Arun, R. Sateesh
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: FER remains a focal point for research interests within computer vision due to its capability to enhance security measures for both human-computer interactions and healthcare system applications. Our work presents a method that combines features from various layers based on InceptionV3 CNN model guidance. The abbreviation for the Multi-Layer Feature-Fusion based Classification model is MLFFC. The MLFFC is unique as compared to other traditional models because it extracts features from different layers of network and in particular from Inception Module C. The novel feature-fusion inter-layer technique for the proposed model is also constructed in which the model fuses multiple network depths to learn face features from a much wider spectrum. Two benchmark datasets namely CK+ and FER2013 serve as testing grounds for evaluating the MLFFC model. The experimental results demonstrate that the MLFFC model reached the greatest possible accuracy ratings of 99.69% on CK+ and 70.29% on FER2013 which surpasses the performance of current methods. Face expression recognition models achieve enhanced stability and reliability for real-world applications by using combined features extracted from multiple network layers.

title: Hyperparameter Optimization for Improved Mine Detection Using Machine Learning: A Methodical Analysis of Classification Techniques
authors: Parag Ekbote, Suraj Maharana, Priyanshi Mulwani, Swati Sharma, Dr. Santosh Kumar, Mily Lal
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The potent performance of ML models is essential for obtaining intelligent insights from the complexity of modern data. To enhance the performance, we explore the usage of hyperparameter optimization across models. In this study, we analyze several hyperparameter optimization strategies that can enhance the performance of the models. The findings are reproducible and reliable, thereby significantly advancing the academic applications by providing a scalable foundation for model building.

title: Empowering Educators Through Large Language Models: A Systematic Review
authors: Zarrin Jalgaonkar, Harsha Gaikwad, Manjushree Laddha
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This article critically explores the fresh approaches of Large Language Models (LLMs) in supporting educators, with a specific focus on their integration in routine instructional tasks, including lesson planning, question creation, automated feedback delivery, and teacher development. Even though LLMs have garnered a lot of consideration in student-centered research (nearly 80% of recent studies), only about 20% explicitly focus on teacher-oriented implementations. To address this imbalance, the present research foregrounds the practical and utility-based roles of LLMs in enhancing educators' professional development and routine pedagogical tasks, rather than limiting the analysis to theoretical potentials. The overarching research questions in the review discuss emerging trends and challenges of LLMs for educators and the gaps in the current research. This review synthesises current literature and identifies critical areas where LLMs can serve as effective, context-aware pedagogical tools for teacher empowerment.

title: A Novel PV Array Reconfiguration Technique to Reduce Mismatch and Power Losses Under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Sumit Kataria, Afzal Sikander
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: The partial shading conditions in solar PV systems affect its performance which can be addressed by reconfiguration of the connections of PV array. Therefore, motivated by various reconfiguration methods available in the literature, in this study a novel and optimal technique is proposed for the placement of photovoltaic (PV) panels within a photovoltaic array to mitigate the effects of partial shading. The proposed approach employs a modified SuDoKu puzzle-based reconfiguration technique, implemented using Python on the conventional Tied Cross-Tied (TCT) PV array. This method effectively disperses shadows across the PV array, thereby reducing their adverse impact. A comprehensive performance evaluation is conducted in MATLAB/Simulink under three distinct shading scenarios: Bottom-Left Corner (BLC), Bottom-Right Corner (BRC), and Top-Left Corner (TLC). The proposed SuDoKu-Optimised Array Methodology (SOAM) is compared with conventional TCT, standard Sudoku (SDK), and SKYSCRAPER configurations using key performance metrics, including percentage power enhancement, fill factor, mismatch losses, and percentage power loss. Under BLC shading, the SOAM configuration achieves a maximum power output of 731.94 W with only 9.78 % power loss, compared to 16.89 % for TCT and 10.91 % for both SDK and SKYSCRAPER. In the BRC case, SOAM produces 729.09 W with a 10.13 % loss, outperforming TCT (16.89 %), SDK (10.84 %), and SKYSCRAPER <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10.90 \%)$</tex>. For the TLC scenario, SOAM delivers 731.88 W with the same low loss of 9.78 %, again surpassing all compared configurations. These results confirm that the proposed SOAM approach significantly reduces mismatch and power losses while improving fill factor and overall energy yield across multiple shading conditions. Consequently, SOAM presents an efficient, effective, and sustainable reconfiguration strategy for enhancing the performance and economic feasibility of PV arrays under partial shading.

title: A Slot-Loaded Rectangular Microstrip Patch Antenna with Multi-Shape Slots for 47 GHz V-Band Single-Band Applications
authors: Ekata Singh, Garima Harihar, Ishika Talukdar, Vaishali Kikan, Ashwni Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: A rectangular microstrip patch antenna, that has been updated is proposed in this work. It has been substantially, set up to function at V-band frequencies (47 GHz) in order to target the 5G millimeter-band wireless networks. Square, circular, rectangular, and triangular slots are among the various geometric customizations, included in this suggested antenna design. These modifications improves, the far-field performance and enhance impedance characteristics. Fabrication process is then carried out, using a low-loss RT/Duroid 5880 dielectric substrate, with a thickness of 0.254 mm (about 0.01 in), which is a low loss tangent, and high stability at higher frequencies. HFSS software, is then used for the design and simulation process of the patch antenna. The developed antenna performs, exceptionally well in terms of impedance at 47 GHz, achieving a reflection coefficient of-33.9 dB. The antenna’s viability is supported by a thorough analysis of S-parameters, VSWR, impedance plots, and radiation parameters. It is beneficial for next-generation wireless applications, secure and meet the high-capacity military communications standards, small-form-factor 5G devices, and high-data-rate wireless systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Arithmetic Circuits for Low Power and High Speed Applications
authors: Malla Reddy Potlacheruvu, Kavitha Neerukanti, Sanjay Kotlapuram, Sangeeta Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The arithmetic Circuits, 4-Bit Multiplier implemented by CMOS ,PTL ,and Transmission Gate (TG) logic styles has been compared using 45nm technology at a supply voltage of 1.0V. The comparison is done based on important design parameters: transistor count, delay, power, and power-delay product (PDP). Out of the three implementations, the PTL-based multiplier offers the best overall performance. On a count of transistors, the PTL design utilizes 198 transistors, which is roughly 29.3% less than the CMOS design (280T).The multiplier based on TG employs 245 transistors, registering a 12.5% reduction in area from CMOS.This decrease in transistor count directly contributes to smaller silicon area and potentially lower fabrication cost.Regarding delay, the PTL design achieves the lowest propagation delay of 210.7ps, offering a 6.5% improvement over the CMOS design’s delay of 225.3ps. The TG logic design performs slightly better than CMOS as well, with a delay of 218.6ps, which is a 3.0% improvement.For power consumption, the PTL multiplier consumes 0.178 μW, which is approximately 27.0% lower than the 0.244μW consumed by the CMOS-based multiplier.The TG implementation draws 0.195μW,which is 20.1% below that of CMOS counterpart. Comparing power-delay product (PDP)—one of the most important indicators of energy efficiency—the PTL design once again takes the lead with a PDP of 0.374 fJ,which is a 52.6% decrease from the CMOS PDP value of 0.789fJ. The TG logic similarly demonstrates marked improvement,with a PDP value of 0.426fJ,corresponding to a 46.0% decrease relative to CMOS.In short, with 45nm technology at 1.0V, the PTL-based 4-bitmultiplier exhibits the most effective performance in area, velocity, power, and energy .While PTL logic is favorable for low-power and high-speed design, but it could suffer from decreased voltage swing and signal degradation, which have to be carefully considered during circuit implementation.

title: LLMs for Pharmacovigilance
authors: Mukund Savaliya, Swati Jain, Kavita Tewani, Zunnun Narmawala
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Pharmacovigilance (PV) plays a crucial role in safeguarding public health by identifying and mitigating potential risks associated with pharmaceutical products. In recent years, the emergence of machine learning techniques, particularly Large Language Models (LLMs), has sparked a revolution in various domains, including healthcare. In this report, we explore the intersection of LLMs and PV, delving into the potential applications, methodologies, challenges, and future directions of employing these models in this crucial healthcare domain, ultimately contributing to the improved safety and well-being of patients worldwide. Specifically, we develop a machine learning model designed to extract knowledge about drugs, focusing on their reactions, seriousness, and effect of drug in a particular gender. The model achieved remarkable results, achieving a 99% training accuracy, 98% validation accuracy, and 97% testing accuracy and a F1 score of 98, outperforming the comparator models (GPT 3.5 and GPT 4) by nearly 89% and showcasing its effectiveness in improving Adverse Drug Reactions (ADRs) detection in clinical practice. Furthermore, the model's capability to differentiate between gender-specific effects could be valuable for personalized medicine, guiding healthcare professionals in making more informed treatment decisions.

title: Stable and Delay Efficient Routing Objective Function for Mobile IoT Networks
authors: Soumya Nandan Mishra, Deepak Parashar, Dibya Nandan Mishra, Kanhaiya Sharma
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Routing Protocol for Low Power and Lossy Networks (RPL) is the default routing standard used in many Internet of Things (IoT) deployments. It is specifically tailored for environments where devices operate with strict limitations on memory, computation capability, energy, and communication rate. RPL typically relies on two standardized objective functions—OF0 and MRHOF—to determine efficient routing paths. However, these mechanisms struggle in scenarios involving node mobility. Ensuring stable and efficient routes becomes a major challenge when the network includes mobile IoT devices. Hence, in this paper, a Stable and Delay Efficient Objective Function (SDOF) is proposed to optimise stability and efficiency by assuming node density, node mobility, and expected data delay (EDD) for routing decisions. SDOF improves the average end-to-end delay of the network by 57% compared to OF0 and the stability of the network by 30% compared to MRHOF. In addition, SDOF shows improved performance with respect to packet delivery, throughput, and control packet overhead compared to the existing benchmark schemes.

title: Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting Using Optimally Tuned Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System
authors: Arif Bourezami, Mohammed Amroune, Khaled Belhouchet, Ismail Ghadbane, Assam Zorig
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: Wind energy is well known for its high volatility, primarily due to its strong dependence on climatic conditions. To achieve optimal management of smart grids, it is essential to develop accurate forecasting tools capable of predicting the future production of wind power plants. The existing literature presents a wide range of forecasting methods, which can generally be categorized into four main groups: statistical methods, physical methods, machine learning approaches, and hybrid models. This study aims to investigate a novel hybrid forecasting technique that integrates An Adaptive ANFIS (Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System) optimized using the Manta Ray Foraging Optimization (MRFO) algorithm is developed to improve the precision and robustness of wind power forecasting. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated using real-world wind power data collected from the Sotavento Galicia wind farm in Spain. The forecasting performance is further benchmarked against several conventional approaches, including DT (Decision Tree) and RF (Random Forest) models, traditional Support Vector Regression (SVR) and hybrid SVR-Gray Wolf Optimization (SVR-GWO) model.

title: Analysis of Blood Report Images Using General-Purpose Vision-Language Models
authors: Nadia Bakhsheshi, Hamid Beigy
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: The reliable analysis of blood reports is important for health knowledge, but individuals often struggle with interpretation, leading to anxiety and overlooked issues. We look for the potential of general-purpose Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to address this challenge by automatically analyzing blood report images. We conduct a comparative evaluation of three VLMs-Qwen-VL-Max, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Llama 4 Maverick-determining their performance on a dataset of 100 diverse blood report images. Each model was prompted with clinically relevant questions adapted to each blood report. The answers were then processed using SentenceBERT to compare and evaluate how closely the models responded. The findings suggest that general-purpose VLMs are a practical and promising technology for developing patient-facing tools for preliminary blood report analysis. Their ability to provide clear interpretations directly from images can improve health literacy and reduce the limitations to understanding complex medical information. This work establishes a foundation for the future development of reliable and accessible AI-assisted healthcare applications. While results are encouraging, they should be interpreted cautiously given the limited dataset size. The similarity between the answers is also very high. The similarities are calculated pairwise and across all responses. This suggests that VLMs have a shared understanding of how to process and reason about structured clinical data presented in images, specifically blood report images.

title: Enhancing Grid Resilience and Sustainability: A Solar-Powered Electric Vehicle Charging Station with G2V and V2G Capabilities
authors: Mohammed Yassine Dennai, Hamza Tedjini
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This paper presents a solar-powered electric vehicle (EV) charging station equipped with grid-to-vehicle (G2V) and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) functionalities. The system features a solar panel array paired with a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) controller, buck-boost converters for charging EVs and energy storage, and an AC load. All components are linked to a 480V DC bus, facilitating smooth transitions between grid charging and solar energy use. The system can either deliver surplus power during peak demand or store energy in batteries when sunlight is insufficient. The bidirectional buck-boost converter allows EVs to actively engage with the energy grid. The paper outlines three operational modes based on the photovoltaic (PV) system's output and the state of charge of both the EV and the energy storage unit (ESU). In Mode-1, the PV system charges the battery electric vehicle (BEV) and ESU, while also supplying energy to the grid and powering the AC load. Mode-2 occurs when the PV system is inactive, allowing EVs to charge the ESU and provide grid power. In Mode-3, if the ESU's output is insufficient, EVs draw power from the utility grid and ESU. The paper concludes by emphasizing the system's potential to enhance grid resilience and promote sustainability.

title: Electoral Integrity through Technology: A Recurrent Neural Network Analysis of Public Response
authors: Yozika Arvio, Jakfar Ahmad, Rahmad Evan Purnawan, Dine Tiara Kusuma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The global pursuit of electoral integrity is increasingly reliant on digital technologies to enhance transparency and public trust. This study proposes a framework for evaluating public sentiment toward election technology by analyzing user reviews of vote-counting applications-a critical, yet understudied, feedback channel. Using a large-scale dataset of 17,710 user reviews from a national vote-counting application during a general election period, we implement a robust Natural Language Processing (NLP) pipeline. The text preprocessing involves cleansing, slang normalization, tokenization, stopword removal, and stemming. Sentiment labels are assigned using a lexicon-based approach (InSet Lexicon), resulting in a distribution of $\mathbf{1 0, 6 5 1}$ negative, 3,129 positive, and 1,710 neutral reviews. A Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) model is employed for classification, achieving an high accuracy of $89 \%$ and demonstrating strong performance across sentiment classes: negative (F1-score: 0.93), positive (F1-score: 0.82), and neutral (F1-score: 0.69). The results confirm the efficacy of RNNs in processing large-scale, noisy, and linguistically diverse user feedback from civic technology applications. Beyond the immediate case study, this research provides a transferable methodological blueprint for election management bodies worldwide to leverage AI-driven sentiment analysis. This approach enables real-time monitoring of public perception, offering actionable insights to improve algorithmic accountability, user experience, and ultimately, strengthen trust in digital electoral ecosystems.

title: Exponential Sliding Mode Controller to Track the Human Upper Limb During Topspin Forehand in Table Tennis
authors: Erfan Sedaghat, Seyyed Arash Haghpanah
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Understanding and improving the biomechanics of sports movements is essential to both injury prevention and performance optimization. This study presents a controloriented simulation of the human upper limb during the topspin forehand motion in table tennis. A 4-degree-of-freedom skeletal model of the right arm—comprising the shoulder and elbow joints—is developed using the Denavit-Hartenberg convention. To track experimentally obtained reference trajectories, an exponential sliding mode controller (ESMC) is proposed. The controller ensures finite-time convergence of tracking errors. Simulation results demonstrate high tracking accuracy and proper control torque behavior, indicating the potential of the proposed method in biomechanics and intelligent rehabilitation systems. This framework can be extended to upper-limb exoskeleton control, motion analysis for injury prevention, and intelligent training tools for athletes.

title: Towards Accurate Multimodal Defformable Image Registration via Image Translation and Weak Supervision
authors: Maryam Nasr, Mohammadreza Yazdchi, Mohsen Safdari
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: This study proposes a weakly supervised learning strategy for training deep learning models on multimodal registration tasks, using loss functions based on monomodal similarity metrics. Since monomodal metrics are generally more reliable and robust than their multimodal counterparts, this strategy stabilizes the training process and tends to produce more plausible outcomes. The approach is implemented using synthetic image generation models that convert images from one modality to another to reduce intensity inconsistencies across domains. We apply this method to MRI-to-CT deformable registration, which is a critical component of the radiotherapy planning process.
