The results demonstrate significant improvement in image alignment while preserving their realistic appearance. In addition, we compare this learning strategy with an unsupervised approach trained using multimodal similarity metrics. The comparison indicates a more stable training process. Quantitative results show notable improvements in both SSIM (from 0.8429 to 0.8510) and the mean Dice score (from 0.7750 to 0.7985).

title: DietGlance : Dietary Monitoring and Personalized Analysis at a Glance with Knowledge-Empowered AI Assistant
authors: Zhihan JIANG, Running Zhao, Lin Lin, Yue Yu, Handi Chen, Xinchen Zhang, Xuhai Xu, Yifang Wang, Xiaojuan Ma, Edith C.H. Ngai

title: A Novel 3-D Cell Array Architecture for Capacitorless DRAM
authors: Yung-Yueh Chiu, Wen-Chien Liu, Te-Chang Tseng, Riichiro Shirota
abstract: A novel 3-D capacitor-less dynamic random access memory (DRAM) array is proposed as a high-density and high-performance memory solution. The device incorporates a double-gate (DG) and stacked-channel architecture to realize a 3-D one-transistor DRAM (1T-DRAM) array. The control gate (CG) executes read and write operations, while the storage-gate (SG) manages the storage or removal of holes within the storage region. Insulating blocks (IBs) isolate the n<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> source/drain (S/D) regions from the p-type storage regions, thereby enabling high retention characteristics with minimal performance degradation. To ensure robust simulations, the retention characteristics were calibrated against experimental data from conventional silicon-on-insulator (SOI) 1T-DRAM, as reported in Ohsawa et al. (2002). By optimizing the bias voltage scheme and doping concentration, a retention time (RT) of approximately 2.9 s and a sensing margin (SM) of approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7.3~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>A/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m were achieved at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$85~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C.

title: Exceptional Thickness Scalability of MFM Stack: Outstanding Performance in 3-nm TiN/3-nm HZO/3-nm TiN Capacitors
authors: Xiaolin Wang, Yang Feng, Yaoyu He, Yuye Kang, Jixuan Wu, Wei Shi, Zhilun Zhang, Zijie Zheng, Leming Jiao, Zuopu Zhou, Dong Zhang, Chen Sun, Jiezhi Chen, Xiao Gong
abstract: Advancing ultrahigh-density 3-D ferroelectric random-access memory (FeRAM) integration, we demonstrate excellent thickness scalability in fully ALD-deposited metal–ferroelectric–metal (MFM) stacks consisting of 3-nm TiN/3-nm HZO/3-nm TiN. These ultrathin devices exhibit robust ferroelectricity (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}} \gt 22~{\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{{2}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> together with a record-low coercive voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{V}_{\text {c}} \lt 0.65$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V). This breakthrough is enabled by a polycrystalline-to-amorphous transition in the TiN electrodes that occurs as they are thinned. This thickness-driven amorphization reduces the lattice mismatch with the ferroelectric o-phase, thereby promoting o-phase formation in HZO and enabling simultaneous downscaling of both HZO and TiN layers. Beyond reducing the overall stack thickness, thinner TiN electrodes also enhance reliability by providing smoother interfaces and suppressing oxygen scavenging, which together minimize leakage and increase breakdown strength. As a result, our ultrathin capacitors set several benchmarks for 3-nm ferroelectric layers, achieving an ultralow leakage current (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\text {leakage}} \lt 10^{-{3}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> A/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at 3 MV/cm), an ultrahigh breakdown field (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${E}_{\text {BD}} \gt 9.5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> MV/cm), and durable endurance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\gt 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cycles) without ferroelectric failure (final <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}} \gt 18~{\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and no electrical breakdown). Our work offers critical insights and unlocks new possibilities for pushing the boundaries of high-performance, ultradense, and low-power 3-D ferroelectric memories.

title: How do Top Managers in Small-and-Medium-Sized Enterprises Form Opportunity Beliefs for Digital Transformation Strategy? An Attention-based View
authors: Yueqi Wang, Chenguang Hu, Yang Cheng
abstract: The engineering management (EM) literature has widely accepted that digital technologies provide new opportunities for small-and-medium-sized enterprises (SMEs)' growth. Identifying and acting upon opportunities require top managers (TMs) to allocate and sustain their attention toward monitoring and interpreting signals from the external environment. However, present studies largely view TMs as invisible or perfectly rational, thus lacking sufficient investigation of their attention-based practices in SMEs' digital transformation (DT). Drawing on the attention-based view (ABV), we examine how TMs in SMEs utilize attention engagement efforts to form opportunity beliefs for DT strategies. Specifically, we adopt a case study within a typical SME in China, which has transformed from a traditional manufacturer to a digital platform enterprise over the period of 2009–2025. The analyses are based on the fundamental logic of “noticed environmental changes―attention engagement efforts―opportunity belief” in ABV. With rigorous case study tactics for validity, reliability, and generalizability, we develop a framework with different aggregates of TMs' attention engagement efforts in forming four opportunity beliefs successively for DT strategies (i.e., market development, market penetration, product development, and platform development). Overall, by investigating the role of TMs' attention engagement efforts in DT, we contribute to the micro foundation of DT within the EM literature in the context of SMEs.

title: Lightweight Federated Learning Based on SNNs and Gradient Quantization Clipping
authors: Jianfeng Shi, Zhongping Tan
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving distributed learning but faces significant communication bottle-necks, especially for resource-constrained edge devices. Existing approaches often incur additional computational cost or require auxiliary data. To address these problems, we propose FedSNN-QC, a lightweight FL framework leveraging Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) for their efficiency and robustness to Non-IID and noisy data. The framework incorporates a gradient quantization scheme exploiting SNN sparsity to minimize transmission and joint gradient clipping techniques to enhance robustness and convergence. Experimental results demonstrate that the FedSNN-QC maintains competitive accuracy while substantially reducing both communication and computational energy costs compared to conventional methods.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Routing for Zero-Trust Low-Altitude Intelligent Networks
authors: Ziye Jia, Sijie He, Ligang Yuan, Fuhui Zhou, Qihui Wu, Zhu Han, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Due to the scalability and portability, low-altitude intelligent networks (LAINs) are essential in various fields such as surveillance and disaster rescue. However, in LAINs, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are characterized by the distributed topology and high mobility, thus vulnerable to security threats, which may degrade routing performances for data transmissions. Hence, how to ensure the routing stability and security of LAINs is challenging. In this paper, we focus on the routing with multiple UAV clusters in LAINs. To minimize the damage caused by potential threats, we present the zero-trust architecture with the software-defined perimeter and blockchain techniques to manage the identify and mobility of UAVs. Besides, we formulate the routing problem to optimize the end-to-end (E2E) delay and transmission success ratio (TSR) simultaneously, which is an integer nonlinear programming problem and intractable to solve. Therefore, we reformulate the problem into a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process. We design the multi-agent double deep Q-network-based routing algorithms to solve the problem, empowered by the soft-hierarchical experience replay buffer and prioritized experience replay mechanisms. Finally, extensive simulations are conducted and the numerical results demonstrate that the proposed framework reduces the average E2E delay by 59% and improves the TSR by 29% on average compared to benchmarks, while simultaneously enabling faster and more robust identification of low-trust UAVs.

title: GNN-Based Secrecy Rate Optimization in Multi-Satellite Collaborative Systems
authors: Xuyang Zhang, Zhen Liu, Zizheng Hua, Xuanhe Yang, Shuai Wang, Gaofeng Pan, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Next-generation satellite systems require efficient collaboration in terms of wide area coverage and signal augmentation, enabling intelligent allocation of available wireless resources to ensure the security of information. Meanwhile, machine learning (ML) is widely considered well-suited to massive, real-time data scenarios in satellite communication networks, and graph neural network (GNN) is a specific branch for processing the irregular data within such networks. In this paper, we propose physical layer security for a multi-satellite collaborative (MSC) system involving LEO satellites, users, and eavesdroppers. Specifically, the GNN-based security communication of the MSC (G-MSC-SC) architecture is designed to maximize the secrecy rate. Since heterogeneous and isomorphic methods can effectively solve multi-type node mapping and complex communication problems, the G-MSC-SC architecture is divided into two steps: A heterogeneous graph pruning attention coefficient network (HGPAN) and an isomorphic graph eavesdropper as an auxiliary node network (IGEAN). In the HGPAN architecture, different types of device nodes are embedded in the same dimensional space, addressing the challenge of matching LEO satellites to users. The IGEAN architecture maps user channel state information (CSI) to beamforming (BF) vectors through attention aggregation and an improved loss function. Moreover, the corresponding conventional optimization algorithms are designed as test and comparison baselines. Simulation results show that 1) the G-MSC-SC architecture outperforms neural networks and heuristic algorithms in terms of accuracy and efficiency; 2) as the numbers of users and virtual eavesdroppers increase, the directional alignment between the BF vectors and the LEO satellite-user channels shows an improvement; and 3) with imperfect CSI, the G-MSC-SC architecture still achieves an excellent balance between user secrecy rate and communication rate.

title: A Secure Full-Duplex Wireless Circulator enabled by Non-Reciprocal Beyond-Diagonal RIS
authors: Ziang Liu, Bruno Clerckx
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Beyond-diagonal reconfigurable intelligent surface (BD-RIS) has arisen as a promising technology for enhancing wireless communication systems by enabling flexible and intelligent wave manipulation. This is achieved through the interconnections among the ports of the impedance network, enabling wave reconfiguration when they flow through the surface. Thus, the output wave at one port depends on waves impinging on neighboring ports, allowing non-local control of both phase and magnitude. Non-reciprocal (NR)-BD-RIS further enhances this capability by breaking circuit reciprocity and, consequently, channel reciprocity. In contrast to conventional reciprocal (R)-BD-RIS and diagonal (D)-RIS that are constrained by circuit and channel reciprocity such that they only allow bidirectional communications, i.e., UE1 ⇄ UE2, NR-BD-RIS can additionally enable uni-directional communications, that is, UE1 → UE2 → UE3, hence effectively enabling a wireless circulator. Specifically, this paper introduces a novel application of NR-BD-RIS in full-duplex (FD) wireless circulators, where multiple FD devices communicate via an NR-BD-RIS. This system is particularly beneficial for secure transmission, as it enforces one-way communication among FD devices, suppresses signal from all other users (UE), and thus prevents eavesdropping. In addition, a physics-compliant system model is considered by incorporating structural scattering, also known as specular reflection. By accounting for this effect, the advantages of NR-BD-RIS are further validated. Specifically, we formulate an sum-rate maximization problem and propose an iterative optimization algorithm that employs block coordinate descent (BCD) and penalty dual decomposition (PDD) methods. Numerical evaluations illustrate that NR-BD-RIS outperforms conventional R-BD-RIS and D-RIS in terms of sum-rate and secrecy rate.

title: Collaborative Docking with Spatiotemporal Constraints
authors: Zikang Su, Zhuolin Xing, Chenyu Li, Lin Xu, Honglun Wang, Changhui Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Aiming to enhance the docking efficiency and safety of aerial recovery (AR), this paper proposes a bilateral collaborative docking strategy with spatiotemporal constraints. To improve efficiency, the strategy simultaneously controls the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) and the drogue to move toward the desired docking position. To ensure safety, the bilateral recovery systems are strictly constrained within a safe docking space and are required to complete docking within a prescribed time. An appointed-time prescribed performance control (ATPPC) is employed to describe these spatiotemporal requirements. Unknown system dynamics estimator (USDE) observers are established for each subsystem to reconstruct the unknown nonlinear dynamics. Based on the USDE–ATPPC framework, a collaborative docking controller is constructed to ensure efficient and safe docking without violating the constraints. Simulation results show that the propose strategy improves docking efficiency by about 43% while ensuring safety and accuracy.

title: Prediction-Powered Communication with Distortion Guarantees_supp1-3668947.pdf
authors: Matteo Zecchin
abstract: The development of 6G wireless systems is taking place alongside the development of increasingly intelligent wireless devices and network nodes. The changing technological landscape is motivating a rethinking of classical Shannon information theory that emphasizes semantic and task-oriented paradigms. In this paper, we study a prediction-powered communication setting, in which devices, equipped with artificial intelligence (AI)-based predictors, communicate under zero-delay constraints with strict distortion guarantees. Two classes of distortion measures are considered: (i) outage-based metrics, suitable for tasks tolerating occasional packet losses, such as real-time control or monitoring; and (ii) bounded distortion metrics, relevant to semantic-rich tasks like text or video transmission. We propose two zero-delay compression algorithms leveraging online conformal prediction to provide per-sequence guarantees on the distortion of reconstructed sequences over error-free and packet-erasure channels with feedback. For erasure channels, we introduce a doubly-adaptive conformal update to compensate for channel-induced errors and derive sufficient conditions on erasure statistics to ensure distortion constraints. Experiments on semantic text compression validate the approach, showing significant bit rate reductions while strictly meeting distortion guarantees compared to state-of-the-art prediction-powered compression methods.

title: Toward 6G: Spectrum Management in Low-Altitude Aerial Information Networks
authors: Rui Ding, Fuhui Zhou, Marco Giordani, Qihui Wu, Michele Zorzi
abstract: The low-altitude economy (LAE) is an emerging economic paradigm that has attracted widespread attention from both academia and industry. Moreover, with the development of the six-generation (6G) mobile networks, LAE is creating diverse low-altitude application scenarios and fostering emerging industries. However, the take-off of the LAE has witnessed a proliferation of low-altitude wireless devices, creating unprecedented spectrum demands and posing significant challenges in terms of low-altitude network security. Efficient spectrum management in the low-altitude aerial information network is a powerful technique to improve spectrum utilization, and offers new opportunities for low-altitude communications. Therefore, in this paper, we first present promising low-altitude operating frequency bands. Then, we elaborate on the unique low-altitude communication characteristics, including the high mobility and compact structure of low-altitude vehicles. Moreover, we present key enabling techniques for low-altitude spectrum management, including spectrum monitoring, spectrum allocation, interference management, spectrum sharing, and integrated sensing and communication. Finally, key challenges and open issues are outlined to facilitate the applications of spectrum management in the low-altitude aerial information network.

title: Joint Secrecy and Covertness Analysis of RSMA-Assisted AAV Communications With an Internal Eavesdropper and External Wardens
authors: Gaofeng Pan, Yanxin Wu, Zizheng Hua, Shuai Wang, Rui Zhang, Changhao Du, Hongjiang Lei
abstract: This paper investigates the internal secrecy and external covertness of a mixed-trust autonomous aerial vehicle (AAV) communication system assisted by rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA). In this setting, a semi-trusted user with partial decoding capability poses an internal eavesdropping threat, while multiple distributed wardens attempt to detect the transmission from the AAV to the semi-trusted user, creating an external covertness challenge. To characterize these security aspects, a unified analytical framework is developed. First, the internal eavesdropping capability of the semi-trusted user is quantified by deriving a closed-form expression for its eavesdropping success probability. Based on the outcome of the eavesdropping attempt, tractable expressions for the secrecy outage probability of the legitimate user are obtained. Furthermore, the external covertness performance is analyzed by deriving closed-form false alarm probability, missed detection probability, and detection error probability (DEP) for an individual warden, together with the optimal detection threshold and the corresponding minimum DEP. The cooperative global detection performance with multiple wardens is further characterized under conservative fusion rules. Extensive Monte Carlo simulations validate the analytical results and, through a joint evaluation of secrecy, reliability, and covertness metrics, illustrate the feasible operating regions enabled by RSMA power allocation in comparison with a NOMA baseline. The results provide a comprehensive theoretical basis for the design of secure and covert AAV communication strategies in mixed-trust environments.

title: Digital Twins and Edge AI for Small/Off-Grid Water Systems – A Review
authors: Anwur Alenezi, Yousef Alabaiadly
abstract: Here, we review compiled field evidence on Digital Twins and Edge AI for small off-grid water systems. We examined 28 studies from 2018-2025, including 19 peer-reviewed papers and 9 gray literature sources. All included works reported experimental pilots, edge deployments, or testbeds implemented in decentralized environments. Three reference architectures were identified: edge-only DTs for ultra-off-grid settings, federated DT networks for community-scale coordination, and hybrid edge-cloud DTs for intermittent connectivity. Common hardware included Raspberry Pi Zero, sub-$15 sensors, and ESP32, typically operating within 10-30 Wh/ day energy budgets powered by solar systems. Results consistently indicated that simplified models outperform complex alternatives. Reduced-order physics models form the foundation, and lightweight ML correctors make them adaptable. Deployed models remain static and are updated seasonally using Bayesian recalibration. Popular uses include atmospheric water harvesting, containerized wastewater treatment, leak detection, brackish water filtration, and small-scale irrigation. Empirical results across pilots were consistent: energy or water consumption dropped 18-40%, downtime or repair time fell by 27-55%, and component life grew by over four months. This review proposes a standard comparison tool for future studies, which identifies key nontechnical problems and presents a practical guide for field trials. We outline a research pathway for self-healing models, including discussions on shared standards, integration of social and technical aspects, and long-term testbed development. Researchers, policymakers, and NGOs may find clear, actionable steps for improving access to reliable and affordable water services in resource-limited areas.

title: Thin-Film Characterization Via Referenced Spectroscopic Ellipsometry Enpowered by Weak Measurement
authors: Tian Tong, Fenglin Xian, Zhaolou Cao
abstract: Quantitative characterization of thin films is critically important across diverse fields. In this study, a novel technique called referenced spectroscopic ellipsometry enpowered by weak measurement (RSEWM) is introduced for high-precision detection of minute variations of thin film parameters, which leverages the amplified sensitivity of weak measurement to polarization shift of reflected light. Numerical simulation and experimental validation confirm that the spectra obtained by RSEWM exhibit exceptional sensitivity to deviations in thin film parameters from a reference standard. By solving the inverse problem through iterative spectrum fitting, thin film parameters can be retrieved with sub-nanometer resolution. Given its high efficiency, we anticipate RSEWM will emerge as a powerful tool for nanoscale metrology, with broad applications spanning semiconductor manufacturing to materials science.

title: MSET: Multimodal Semantic-Enhanced Real-World Beam Prediction via Temporal Modeling with Visual Foundation Models
authors: Feixiang Liu, Xiaohui Li, Wenhui Gao, Jiaqing Xiong, Guanchong Niu, Chung Shue Chen
abstract: While machine learning (ML) has been explored for beam prediction, many methods remain constrained by single-modality inputs, shallow temporal modeling, and limited robustness to interference and domain shift. We introduce Multimodal Semantic-Enhanced Real-World Beam Prediction via Temporal Modeling with Visual Foundation Models (MSET), a multimodal framework that couples visual semantics with positional priors in a causal, lightweight design. A visual foundation model (VFM), instantiated as a Swin-Transformer, learns rich spatial and semantic cues from RGB images and Segment Anything Model (SAM)-derived region masks, and a lightweight ResNet-18 student distills this knowledge for efficient inference. Short-horizon dynamics are captured by a causal Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) with an adaptive receptive field, whose volatility-driven depth gate expands context under motion spikes and contracts it in calm periods. On top of semantic-aware frame embeddings, a Temporally Aware Cross-Attention (TACA) aligns original and semantic-enhanced tokens, while a Mambaconditioned GPS prior, implemented via a selective state-space model (SSM), issues a location-conditioned single query with positional bias to attend the fused tokens. We further extend inference to dense deployments with multiple proximate candidates and address target selection under ambiguity. Experiments on DeepSense 6G dataset show consistent Top-k improvements across single/multi-target and day/night scenarios, indicating reduced sweep reliance and strong generalization under realistic V2I dynamics.

title: Adaptive Learning Enhanced Cross-Entropy Algorithm for RIS-Aided Communications: Blind Beamforming Design and Experimental Validation
authors: Kai Bin, Yonggang Zhu, Yifu Sun, Kang An, Zhi Lin, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Widely adopted reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) beamforming schemes generally rely on estimating accurate channel state information (CSI) exploiting convex optimization algorithms, leading to high estimation overhead, computational complexity, estimation errors, and poor compatibility with existing protocols. Fortunately, blind beamforming schemes eliminate the need of CSI estimation and convex optimization. However, conventional blind beamforming schemes (e.g., cross-entropy (CE) scheme) search for a satisfactory solution in extremely large RIS configuration space, which may suffer from slow convergence and falling into the local optimum. To address these issues, we propose an adaptive learning-enhanced cross-entropy (ALECE) blind beamforming algorithm. Specifically, ALECE adaptively determines the number of elite samples for updating the probability transition matrix, thereby refining the search direction, reducing the solution space, and accelerating convergence. Besides, to avoid local optimum and loss of promising solutions, we incorporate a learning factor conditional and sample mean (CSM) to facilitate exploration and preserve high-quality samples. Furthermore, we provide convergence proof and formal complexity analysis. Finally, both simulation and experiment results demonstrate that, compared with the conventional CE method, the proposed ALECE algorithm can effectively trade off convergence speed while incurring only a marginal performance loss through an appropriate choice of learning factor.

title: UNITE-NBV: Uncertainty-driven and Information-enhanced Gain Estimation for Next Best View_supp1-3668579.mp4
authors: Sicong Li
abstract: Next Best View (NBV) algorithms are a critical area of research in 3D reconstruction. They aim to efficiently reconstruct 3D scenes by maximizing information gain from the next optimal viewpoint. However, current NBV methods often neglect the importance of high-quality candidate view sampling, leading to inconsistent quality of the candidate viewpoint set. Moreover, these methods frequently encounter difficulties in extracting effective information for accurate information gain estimation, especially when reconstructing complex objects or large-scale scenes. To address these challenges, we propose UNITE-NBV. Our method achieves accurate next best view selection across various scenes by effectively uniting the proposed high-quality candidate view sampling strategy and information gain estimation network. Specifically, we introduce Spherical Uncertainty Sampling (SUS), a novel candidate viewpoint sampling method. This method calculates the scene's uncertainty field and maps it into a spherical sampling space. Within this space, the uncertainty distribution guides the sampling of high-quality candidate viewpoints. Additionally, we propose a Multi-Expert Information Gain Network (MEIGN) that performs disentangled encoding and expert encoder sequence processing on features extracted from the reconstructed scene. These processed features are then dynamically fused using sparse gating and Multi-Head Self-Attention, enabling accurate information gain estimation for candidate views. Extensive experimental results on both the small-object dataset ShapeNet and various large-scale 3D scene datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and superior performance of our proposed method. The code will be released.

title: Compensation-guided Reverse Time Migration Jointly Accounting for Relaxation and Ohmic Losses in Ground Penetrating Radar Imaging
authors: Honghua Wang, Zhiyang Hou, Minling Wang, Yi Lei, Zihan Gao, Bin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Reverse time migration (RTM) is widely used for ground penetrating radar (GPR) data processing, typically formulated assuming non-dispersive wave propagation with frequency-independent feature. However, real subsurface media exhibit dispersion and attenuation, causing GPR waves to suffer significant amplitude, phase, and travel time alterations compared to non-dispersive cases. Conventional RTM neglects frequency-dependent attenuation and velocity dispersion, degrading imaging accuracy and resolution. To provide a feasible solution for achieving high-precision migration imaging of GPR data from high dispersive and attenuating geological structures, as well as from weak deep GPR signals, we address this limitation by proposing a novel GPR RTM algorithm incorporating joint compensation for relaxation and Ohmic losses, based on the time reversal invariance principle. This method employs finite element forward modeling and reverse-time extrapolation on triangular meshes, achieving loss compensation by reversing loss terms in the time-reversed equation to restore temporal symmetry. A zero-time imaging condition is adopted, and a time-varying Butterworth filter suppresses amplified noise for stable extrapolation. Validated through three representative Debye-type dispersive synthetic datasets and in-situ experimental GPR data from wet sand detection, our proposed approach mitigates energy attenuation when compared to conventional RTM and individual loss compensation methods. The results demonstrate superior imaging resolution and enhanced reflectivity amplitudes, while accurately recovering energy losses caused by both Ohmic and relaxation mechanisms.

title: Demo: RHOAR: Live On-Device Demonstration of Mobile AR Occlusion with Depth Switching
authors: Daisuke Goto, JeongGil Ko, Tadashi Okoshi
publish: Proceedings of the 27th International Workshop on Mobile Computing Systems and Applications
abstract: None

title: Enhancing Aerodynamic Prediction of Airfoils Using Advanced Machine Learning Models
authors: Samuel Maju, Aby Thomas, Sangeeth Siva R, Keerthi Krishnan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study on the application of advanced deep learning architectures—Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Capsule Networks (CapsNet)—for predicting the aerodynamic performance of airfoils, quantified through the lift-to-drag ratio (LDR). A dataset comprising 6,283 airfoil geometries generated from high-fidelity computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations was used to train and validate the models. The MLP achieved a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 0.926, outperforming CapsNet, which recorded an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.700. These results highlight MLP's capability to accurately capture nonlinear aerodynamic behavior while offering a computationally efficient alternative to CFD. The comparative analysis provides insight into the suitability of different neural architectures for aerodynamic modeling. Future work will focus on hybrid and physics-informed neural approaches to improve generalization and flow-field prediction accuracy.

title: Scaling and Securing Enterprise Healthcare Networks with Blockchain
authors: Constantine Hinds, Macchelssy Dinganga, Pradeeban Kathiravelu
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Software Defined Systems (SDS)
abstract: Electronic Health Records (EHRs) facilitate research in diverse areas of healthcare informatics. However, this digital shift also introduces significant security challenges. At the enterprise level, for example, in the USA, many EHRs and the ecosystems in which they operate are still only barely compliant with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). However, centralized systems are vulnerable to data breaches through web application flaws such as Structured Query Language (SQL) injection, phishing, and malware. Blockchain frameworks have had a significant impact on building decentralized database systems, overcoming the security and scalability limitations inherent to centralized systems. Research and enterprise use blockchain to scale and secure the networks. The networks range from computer networks to radio networks to healthcare networks. This paper explores the literature surrounding EHRs and their security vulnerabilities and investigates the potential for a blockchain-based solution for the identified challenges. It thus presents the challenges in these diverse domains and how blockchain addresses the scalability and security challenges.

title: A discrete-time deep learning framework for temporal heterogeneous networks forecasting
authors: Manuel Dileo, Matteo Zignani, Sabrina Gaito
publish: 2025 IEEE 12th International Conference on Data Science and Advanced Analytics (DSAA)
abstract: Temporal Heterogeneous Networks (THNs) are evolving networks that characterize many real-world applications such as citation and events networks, recommender systems, and knowledge graphs. Forecasting THNs involves predicting future connections within a network that evolves over time and comprises diverse types of nodes and interactions with varying temporal dynamics. Although some Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) models have been successfully applied to forecast THNs, there is a lack of a general overview of how the message-passing computation could be extended to treat THNs. Moreover, most of the current solutions exhibit pitfalls in their training and evaluation strategies. Hence, in this work, we propose a graph deep learning framework for THN forecasting. Our framework decomposes the computation of a GNN layer into multiple components and introduces two different schemes to update embedding representations for THNs. This design allows the classification of existing solutions into special instances of our framework and highlights their potential limitations. We also extend the set of benchmarks for THNs by introducing two novel high-resolution temporal heterogeneous graph datasets derived from an emerging Web3 platform and a well-established e-commerce website. Overall, we conducted the first massive evaluation of THNs solutions over four temporal heterogeneous network datasets on two different future link prediction tasks using a fair newly introduced evaluation setting that considers the evolving nature of the data. Based on the limitations of existing solutions, we develop a new model that combines working techniques from previous models and leverages a new embedding update scheme. Experiments show the prediction power of our model compared to current solutions for link prediction in temporal graphs. Moreover, the experimental evaluation highlights the strengths and weaknesses of the different solutions and shows the effectiveness of our framework design.

title:
Design and Optimization Performance of FMSs Modeled with S
                    <sup>4</sup>
                    PR Petri Nets
authors: Mowafak Hassan Abdul-Hussin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The design and optimization of flexible manufacturing systems (FMSs) using Petri nets (PNs) aim to maximize task utilization and make effectively utilize available resources. One notable aspect of the S4PR-net is its high distribution efficiency for adaptation-intricate interactive FMSs. In the PN model, a reconfigurable FMS is optimized and implemented through control transitions(CTs). An S4PR can be utilized to model these FMS. More specifically, this framework enables PNs to access full reachability graphs (RGs) to ensure that all firing sequences have been investigated. Furthermore, the PN model optimally controls the high utilization of the resources system that results from the maximally permissive behavior. To make the final model of the FMS live, PNs must be developed to accurately model the FMS, where each process needs arbitrary resource types and essentially no deadlocks. Three experimental results have been presented, demonstrating the significantly higher efficiency of PNs with computer simulation models for dynamic control.

title: Behavioral Spillovers in FinTech-Enabled Investing: Evidence from UPI, BNPL, and Micro-Investing Platforms
authors: Christina N, Arindam Banerjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: This study looks into how using FinTech platforms a lot might spill over into other behaviors, especially with stuff like UPI for payments, BNPL where you buy now and pay later, and those micro-investing apps. You know, these things make everything easier, more reachable, and open to everyone. But the way they're built, with their designs and all, could be messing with how people think about money and risks in the long run. It pulls from ideas in behavioral finance, and uses data that's out there already, like financial reports anyone can see, breakdowns of user experiences, and what people say in reviews. Basically, it checks out how the app layouts, the game-like features, and those little nudges connect to things like sticking to investment plans, handling risks, and deciding over time. They used tools like mapping trends, correlation setups, and digging into sentiments with Python and Tableau. What comes up is patterns where biases like focusing too much on the now, treating money in separate buckets, or thinking you're more in control than you are, get boosted by how these apps are made. That lines up with people jumping into short-term moves and not being steady with investments. The study doesn't say it's all cause and effect. It just points out these links in how folks act with digital money, and gives some thoughts for designers of FinTech, people investing, and the regulators watching over it all.

title: Benchmarking Class Activation Map Methods for Explainable Brain Hemorrhage Classification on Hemorica Dataset
authors: Zahra Rafati, Mohammad Hoseyni, Javad Khoramdel, Amirhossein Nikoofard
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has become an essential component of medical imaging research, aiming to increase transparency and clinical trust in deep learning models. This study investigates brain hemorrhage diagnosis with a focus on explainability through Class Activation Mapping (CAM) techniques. A pipeline was developed to extract pixel-level segmentation and detection annotations from classification models using nine state-of-the-art CAM algorithms, applied across multiple network stages, and quantitatively evaluated on the Hemorica dataset, which uniquely provides both slice-level labels and high-quality segmentation masks. Metrics including Dice, IoU, and pixel-wise overlap were employed to benchmark CAM variants. Results show that the strongest localization performance occurred at stage five of EfficientNetV2-S, with HiResCAM yielding the highest bounding-box alignment and AblationCAM achieving the best pixel-level Dice <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 0.57)$</tex> and IoU <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 0.40)$</tex>, representing strong accuracy given that models were trained solely for classification without segmentation supervision. To the best of current knowledge, this is among the first works to quantitatively compare CAM methods for brain hemorrhage detection, establishing a reproducible benchmark and underscoring the potential of XAI-driven pipelines for clinically meaningful AI-assisted diagnosis.

title: The Effect of Mass Fraction on the Thermal Cycle Efficiency of the PGE Kamojang IV Geothermal Power Plant Before and After Overhaul
authors: Prayudi Suparmin, Patricia Gloria Gani, Nofirman Firdaus
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This study analyzes the impact of the mass fraction on thermal cycle efficiency and turbine efficiency at PGE Kamojang Unit IV Geothermal Power Plant before and after an overhaul. The plant is running at 59 MW of power. Operational data cover the period of June–September 2024 (before overhaul) and November 2024–February 2025 (after overhaul). Data analysis employs Microsoft Excel and the Water and Steam Properties Table, with validation using Engineering Equation Solver (EES). The average thermal cycle efficiency increases from $\mathbf{2 1 . 6 6 \%}$ before the overhaul to 21.92% after the overhaul. Turbine efficiency improves by $2.02 \%$ on average compared to the pre-overhaul condition. The improvement results from repair and cleaning of major components, including turbines and scrubbers, which enhance steam quality and increase mass fraction. A consistent correlation appears, where higher mass fractions produce higher thermal cycle efficiency. Routine overhaul restores and optimizes plant performance, particularly under low mass fraction conditions. The analysis shows greater heat balance deviations after the overhaul, which are associated with changes in production well performance and operational adjustments. These findings confirm that routine maintenance plays an essential role in sustaining the efficiency and reliability of geothermal power plants.

title: 5-Watt Ka-band Power Amplifiers in a 120-nm GaN MMIC Process
authors: Jack Molles, Zoya Popović
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: Two narrowband 5-watt power amplifiers (PAs) designed for deep-space communications at 32 GHz are presented. The MMIC PAs are implemented in a 120-nm GaN-on-SiC process. One of the PA designs uses standard reactive power combining and four <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6\times 100\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> devices and is fabricated as a benchmark. The 3-stage PA demonstrates an output power of 5 W with a saturated gain of 20 dB and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PAE }=24.5\%$</tex> • The measured results agree well with simulated output power and gain, but are shifted by 800 MHz in frequency compared to the simulations. The measured PAE is reduced by 6 pp, implying an increase in dc current draw. The second PA design uses a new nonlinear device model that helps explain the frequency shift and includes thermal effects. This design shows a saturated gain of 23dB and a PAE of 32% while reaching 5W of output power. Additionally, both versions of the nonlinear model are compared through simulated large-signal performance with the same full-wave EM simulations of the passive circuit elements.

title: Smart Diagnosis of Blood Cell Cancer (ALL) Using CNN-Based Transfer Learning Techniques
authors: Somya Pokharna, Shivang Agarwal, Vedhit Suresh Katekhaye, Kavipriya G
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is a life-threatening blood cancer that mostly alters the white blood cells, impairing the immune system's ability to fight infections. Early and accurate detection of ALL is critical for prompt treatment and improving patient outcomes. However, traditional diagnostic methods mainly depends on manual examination of blood cells, which is time-consuming, prone to error, and dependent on specialized expertise. To address these challenges, this study probes the applications of deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) techniques for automating and enhancing the detection of ALL from blood cell images. Using a convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture with pretrained weights, this project leverages transfer learning to efficiently classify blood cells as cancerous or non-cancerous. The methodology involves systematic data preprocessing, augmentation, and splitting into training, validation, and test sets to guarantee strong model performance. Data augmentation techniques such as blurring, noise addition, and flipping are applied to improve the diversity of training samples, increasing the model's ability to generalize across different datasets. The results demonstrate the efficacy of using ML models for ALL detection, achieving high classification accuracy and offering significant improvements over traditional methods in terms of speed and scalability. The model's performance is evaluated using confusion matrices and visual plots to identify areas for optimization. The study also highlights the potential for integrating these tools into real-world clinical workflows, particularly in resource-constrained settings.

title: XF-MSIS: XOR-Based Fault-Tolerant Multi-Secret Image Sharing with Enhanced Recovery
authors: Yogendra Singh Gound, Purushothama B R
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: In today's digital era, where sensitive image data is increasingly vulnerable to breaches, secure multi-image sharing has become a critical challenge. This paper presents an advanced secret sharing scheme that addresses both security and practical reconstruction needs for grayscale images. Building upon cryptographic transformations and image processing principles, the proposed method ensures robust protection against unauthorised access while enabling reliable recovery even with incomplete shares. The approach demonstrates superior performance compared to conventional techniques, achieving perfect secrecy without compromising computational efficiency. Experimental results validate the scheme's effectiveness in maintaining visual quality under realistic deployment scenarios, particularly for medical imaging and secure cloud storage applications. This work helps close the gap between how secure a system is on paper and how easy it is to use in real situations, providing a major improvement for systems that need both privacy and the ability to handle errors when sharing images.

title: Lightweight AI Model for Smart Parking: Vehicle Classification with YOLOv8-Tiny Optimized by OpenVINO
authors: Darma Rusjdi, Agus Mulyanto, Atikah Rifdah Ansyari, Ocha Nia Martcya Situmorang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Traffic congestion at parking gates remains a common problem in Indonesia, especially in one-gate parking systems used in campuses and shopping malls. Vehicles of various types must pass through a single barrier, causing bottlenecks and inefficient use of parking space. An automated vehicle classification system is therefore needed to improve traffic flow and optimize resource allocation. This study proposes a lightweight AI-based vehicle classification system using the YOLOv8-tiny model optimized with Intel OpenVINO for real-time edge deployment. The model classifies three main vehicle types in Indonesia, namely cars, motorcycles, and bajaj, directly at the parking gate. The system achieved a final mAP@0.5 of 0.971 with low latency (30.9 ms) and high throughput (32.37 FPS) on NVIDIA Jetson Nano. These results demonstrate that lightweight object detection models combined with edge acceleration can provide fast, accurate, and efficient solutions for smart parking in resource-constrained environments.

title: Design of Passive IRS Meta-Atoms for High-Gain Beamforming and Capacity Enhancement
authors: Chameli Mitra, Sudipta Dey, Arijeet Ghosh, Iti Saha Misra
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: This paper analyzes the performance of two meta-atom designs for passive Intelligent Reflecting Surfaces (IRS) in wireless communications. Using MATLAB simulations with Rician fading and multi-link path loss models, we evaluate Designs I and II under varying network geometries,including varying AP-user distance, number of reflecting elements, IRS height, and user elevation. Results show Design II achieves higher spectral efficiency (16.7–19.9 bits/s/Hz) than Design I (15.4–18.5 bits/s/Hz), demonstrating that reactive element configuration significantly enhances IRS performance.The novelty lies in the proposed structure of the meta-atoms,which enables enhanced performance and adaptability to diverse geometric configurations. These findings highlight the importance of careful metaatom design for effective IRS implementation in next-generation networks.

title: A Comprehensive Study on Predicting Social Anxiety Using Classification Techniques
authors: Karpagam C, Ezhil Rathinavadivu K, Jeevika A
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Social Anxiety Disorder is recognized as a prevalent mental health condition that is characterized by an intense fear of social interactions. It is experienced by millions of individuals worldwide and no single cause or factor has been identified. Instead, it is understood as a multifactorial disorder resulting from the interaction of genetic predispositions, brain function, personal history and external stress. Early identification and intervention are regarded as crucial for effective management. However, traditional diagnostic methods are largely based on self-reporting and clinical assessment, which are often subjective and time-consuming. In this study, a comprehensive investigation has been conducted into the application of machine learning classification algorithms for predicting social anxiety. A labelled dataset comprising demographic information, lifestyle factors, mental/ physical health history and recent life events was employed. These variables were used to train and evaluate multiple classification models including K-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest and Naïve Bayes using the Orange tool. The models were assessed using performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score. Among the evaluated classifiers, Random Forest was found to yield the highest predictive accuracy.

title: Design and Analysis of Meander-Shaped Coplanar Waveguide Resonators with Adjacent Arms for Quantum Computing Applications
authors: Adhinarayan Ashok, Aryan Sharma, Adarsh Ganesan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: The paper presents the design and parametric analysis of meander-shaped coplanar waveguide (CPW) resonators for quantum-computing applications in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 - 8 ~ G H z}$</tex> band. Key geometric parameters were swept in a COMSOLbased simulation workflow to quantify their effects on effective electrical length, effective permittivity, resonance frequency, and coupling strength. Results identify predictable trends that enable resonance tuning while highlighting trade-offs between internal quality factor and fabrication complexity. The parameter maps and coupling guidelines are intended to inform device layout choices prior to fabrication.

title: A Smart Irrigation System Using IoT and Machine Learning for Real-Time Environmental Monitoring and Crop Recommendation
authors: Dheeraj Doma, Kota KedharNadh, M. Sai Krishna, M. Rishitha, Abhishek S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive smart irrigation system integrating Internet of Things (IoT) and machine learning (ML) to monitor environmental parameters—temperature, humidity, soil moisture, and Total Dissolved Solids (TDS)—and recommend optimal crops in real-time. The system employs an Arduino Mega as a sensor hub, an ESP32 for Wi-Fi connectivity, a Flask backend with a Random Forest Classifier for crop prediction, and a React frontend for visualization and control. A synthetic dataset, synthesized from multiple agricultural sources, was curated to train the model, enabling accurate predictions for crops such as Maize, Rice, Wheat, Tomato, and Potato. Implementation challenges, including port conflicts, ESP32 communication errors, and CORS restrictions, were systematically resolved. The system optimizes irrigation decisions and crop selection, offering a scalable, cost-effective solution for precision agriculture. Future enhancements include weather forecasting integration and advanced trend analysis.

title: The research on cardiovascular disease risk prediction based on multi-model comparison and explainable machine learning
authors: Likang Zhang, Baotao Zhang, Yunxiao Zou, Yue Sun, Xinyi Li, Kuixing Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease has become the leading cause of mortality worldwide. Early and accurate risk prediction is crucial for disease prevention. Current machine learning-based prediction models for cardiovascular diseases still face several limitations in practical applications, with notable issues including the lack of systematic performance comparisons between different models, insufficient generalization ability, and weak interpretability.Based on a large-scale cardiovascular disease dataset from the Kaggle platform, this study constructed a risk prediction model that combines high accuracy, strong generalization ability, and good interpretability. The research involved data cleaning, feature engineering, and other preprocessing steps, followed by the training of five classic machine learning models: KNN, SGD, random forest, LightGBM, and logistic regression. A dual evaluation framework combined with a multi-metric evaluation system was employed to comprehensively assess the performance and stability of each model. Additionally, an automated overfitting control mechanism was introduced to further enhance model robustness.Experimental results show that the LightGBM model outperformed others across all evaluation metrics. Through cross-validation, it also demonstrated the smallest performance fluctuations, indicating superior stability. Interpretability analysis using the SHAP tool revealed that body mass index (BMI), age, and blood pressure are key factors influencing cardiovascular disease risk. Moreover, these factors exhibit complex nonlinear relationships with disease risk. This study confirms that by integrating systematic model comparison, generalization ability regulation, and interpretability analysis, a more reliable cardiovascular disease risk prediction tool can be developed, providing strong support for clinical health management.

title: A Front-End Passive Step-Up Circuit for RF Energy Harvesting in Low-Power IoT Devices
authors: Yuxuan Fu, Jinzhe Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: With the rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT), conventional wired and battery-based powering approaches are increasingly constrained by cost, maintenance, and environmental impact. RF energy harvesting (RFEH) can enable long-term powering of IoT nodes by scavenging weak RF power from ambient sources or dedicated transmitters. However, at incident levels around −30 dBm, a conventional front end (antenna–matching–direct rectification) typically cannot provide sufficient voltage for rectifying devices and energy-harvesting ICs, limiting range and reliability.To overcome this limitation, we introduce a purely passive step-up module at the RFEH front end and design two fully passive 916 MHz ISM-band step-up structures: an LC series-resonant step-up and a compact transformer-based step-up. Without any active amplifiers or complex power-management circuitry, the module leverages passive resonance and electromagnetic coupling to amplify a −30 dBm RF input. Prototype measurements show voltage amplitudes of approximately 250 mV for the LC design and 50 mV for the transformer design, improving input conditions for downstream rectification, energy storage, and cold-start of commercial harvesting ICs.We develop equivalent circuit models, evaluate them in ADS, and fabricate PCB prototypes. A dedicated testbench performs a head-to-head comparison of the two structures under −30 dBm excitation, confirming the passive step-up effect and summarizing key design trade-offs. The results provide guidance for front-end implementation of RF energy-harvesting systems.

title: Encrypted Text Based Emotion Detection System Using Transformer Model
authors: Ashna Yoosaf, Salim A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: The Encrypted Text Based Emotion Detection System is designed to identify emotions in written text while ensuring the privacy of the user data. In today's world, where a large amount of our personal information is shared and stored online, protecting sensitive inputs especially language based data has become increasingly important. To maintain privacy, this system uses Fernet algorithm to secure the text. This ensures that the content remains protected throughout the entire process, from input to output. Emotion detection in text is an important aspect of natural language processing (NLP). It has a wide range of real world applications, such as understanding customer feedback, analyzing trends and sentiments in social media posts, and supporting tools for mental health monitoring. To handle this, the system tested different neural network models including CNNs, RNNs, and Transformer based approaches to catch patterns that reveal feelings like happiness, sadness, fear, or anger. After trying these models, Transformer based DistilBERT model performed best. It has strength to understand the meaning and context behind words, and it make more accurate emotion prediction even when working with encrypted text.

title: Smart Farming Through Multi-Modal Attention Networks: Climate and Yield Forecasting from IoT Sensors
authors: Rekha R Nair, Thrilok Kolla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Integration of emerging technologies, such as the Internet of Things in sensing and machine learning in analyzing remote-sensing photographs, is opening new avenues for precision agriculture in the management of field crop production in an especially timely and data-driven manner. Unfortunately, agricultural datasets are heterogeneous and high-dimensional, including static soil properties, dynamic meteorological sequences, and multi-resolution images. Hence, none of these methods has been developed so far to understand the hierarchical time-space dependencies of such data, apart from creating interpretable outputs for decision-makers. With this, we have proposed the development of a Hierarchical Attention Network for Climate and Yield Impact Prediction that is, in itself, a promising novel articulated end-to-end deep learning architecture for universality in realtime agriculture predictability in multi-modal aspects. It employs a multi-level attention mechanism to capture temporal evolutions in each modality and combines ensemble-inspired diversity for further robustness. The tendency is that attention becomes part of a prediction process, under the rationale of primary drivers of yield variability, thereby creating transparency for decision-making. Empirical validation through widely diverse agricultural datasets showed that the new model significantly enhanced predictor performance and interpretability vis-à-vis cutting-edge techniques in this field, and as such, best fit in operational deployment under those climates for accuracy improvements in precision agriculture resilience.

title: Voice-Driven Intelligent Home Automation for Enhancing the Lives of Paralyzed Patients
authors: A. Vasantharaj, C. Arockia Subhiksha, N.C. Carolin Mary, S. Bhalasingham
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This work presents an adaptive speech-driven automation platform for elderly people with limited mobility and those who are in need for simpler environmental control. Instead of using complicated switches or handheld remote controls, the system make use of spoken instructions to control household utilities. The user can seek help whenever they need it. In the event that equipment is accidentally left running, an automatic cut-off delay saves electricity, and a built-in light-sensing circuit dims or turns off light when there is sufficient daylight. To improve reliability, the solution makes use of a Raspberry Pi's autonomous mode, which allows continuous operation without internet access. Furthermore, a personalized voice-profiling engine captures and improves paralyzed individuals' speech patterns, improving recognition accuracy even when articulation is slurred or faint. Together, these elements form a user-centered, energy-efficient smart home ecosystem.

title: Gesture Recognition in Human-Computer Interaction Using K-Nearest Neighbors and Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Akanksha Gutthe, Sharon Suryawanshi, Shristi Sarkar, Arun Kumar S, Sejal Bagade, Shivani Shinde
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Gesture Recognition is an important area of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) because it allows for more intuitive and natural interaction without the need for traditional input devices (e.g. keyboard and mouse) . The intention of this paper is to examine gesture recognition through the use of two primary algorithms K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). KNN is a basic non-parametric classifier that is among the simplest to classify with; meanwhile CNN is a deep learning architecture that is well- known for its high accuracy with image-based tasks . This paper will discuss the preprocess of the data, performance metrics, dataset descriptions, and HCI applications. Experimental analysis demonstrates that KNN achieves an accuracy of 85.2% providing a reliable classical benchmark ,whereas CNN attains 97.5% accuracy, delivering a substantial improvement in both precision and real-time interaction. The results of the experiment show how KNN is able to provide baseline performance, with a minimal computational cost to classify, while CNN is shown to provide an accuracy score and is responsive to real-time gestures. KNN works well on gesture recognition tasks, however CNN is a much better class of algorithm for gesture recognition tasks in modern HCI systems.

title: Beyond the Horizon: Exploring Osteoarthritis Predictive Models
authors: Zareena Jamaluddin, V Amsaveni
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Osteoarthritis (OA) is a prevalent chronic rheumatic condition causing pain and disability worldwide, mostly affecting weight-bearing joints like the knees. The diagnosis relies on criteria such as osteophyte development, cartilage loss, and anatomical abnormalities, with primary and secondary classifications based on various factors. Knee OA, in particular, is a important health concern due to its impact on mobility and quality of life. Cartilage degradation plays a crucial role in the progression of OA, leading to stiffness, pain, and reduced joint function. Traditional radiographic techniques, such as X-rays, are generally used for OA diagnosis, with grading systems like the Kellgren-Lawrence scale helping classify disease severity. However, challenges persist in accurately identifying and grading OA, prompting the exploration of advanced technologies like machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL). In recent years, DL algorithms have shown promise in automatically diagnosing and tracking OA progression from various imaging modalities, offering personalized risk assessments and guiding treatment decisions. Similarly, ML approaches have demonstrated effectiveness in enhancing OA diagnosis accuracy by leveraging features extracted from knee X-ray images. Hybrid models combining DL, ML, and other techniques have emerged as well, further improving OA detection and classification. This review provides insights into recent advancements in AI-based approaches for knee OA diagnosis, highlighting their potential to revolutionize patient care and outcomes in OA management.

title: Development of a High-Speed LED-Based UWOC Terminal System Using Hardware Equalization and 16QAM-OFDM Modulation
authors: Shengjie Qi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: To address the engineering demands of underwater wireless optical communication (UWOC) systems for high data rates and strong robustness, this paper presents the design and implementation of a UWOC terminal system based on light-emitting diode (LED) array light source. This system employs an orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) modulation scheme based on 16-quadrature amplitude (16QAM) implemented on field-programmable gate array (FPGA), combined with channel coding techniques, to enhance spectral efficiency and improve resistance to interference. Additionally, a π-type post-equalization circuit was designed, expanding the system’s 3 dB bandwidth from 3 MHz to 20 MHz, effectively compensating for the high-frequency response (FR) limitations of the LED light source. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves stable 10 Mbps data transmission in a 3 m underwater channel with a bit error rate (BER) below the forward error correction (FEC) threshold. It also supports stable communication over a 3 m underwater + 6 m air cross-medium channel. When the receiver deviates from the main optical axis by up to 20 degrees, the system continues to exhibit excellent performance, demonstrating strong transmission robustness and promising application potential.

title: Proposed Amniotic Membrane/Alginate Dialdehyde Based Injectable Hydrogel as a Biofunctional Scaffold for Soft Tissue Engineering
authors: Yasaman Pahlavanzadeh, Yousef Mohammadi, Maryam Saadatmand
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: This study proposes a novel biofunctional hydrogel composed of decellularized human amniotic membrane (dHAM) and alginate dialdehyde (ADA) as a potential injectable scaffold for regenerative medicine. Human amniotic membranes (HAM) were decellularized, and histological evaluation using hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, Masson's trichrome (MT) staining, and Alcian blue (AB) staining confirmed the complete removal of cellular material while preserving extracellular matrix components such as collagen and glycosaminoglycans. ADA with a theoretical oxidation degree of 5 % was synthesized using sodium metaperiodate, and its oxidation degree and oxidation yield were quantified by ultraviolet visible spectrophotometry and iodometric titration, resulting in mean values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.58 \pm 0.1 \%$</tex> and 71.6 %, respectively. The composite hydrogel of dHAM and ADA was prepared via Schiff-base crosslinking between aldehyde groups of ADA and amino groups of solubilized dHAM, combined with ionic gelation mediated by calcium carbonate, calcium sulfate dihydrate, and glucono- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\delta}$</tex>-lactone. Rheological analysis confirmed the formation of a stable, solid-like three-dimensional network, with the storage modulus (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{G}^{\prime}=773 \text{Pa}$</tex>) exceeding the loss modulus <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{G}^{\prime \prime}=96-104 \text{Pa}\right)$</tex> across the frequency range, indicating strong elastic dominance. The hydrogel demonstrated shear-thinning behavior, with viscosity decreasing from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.75 \times 10^{5} \text{mPa} \cdot \mathrm{s}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.24 \times 10^{4} \text{mPa} \cdot \mathrm{s}$</tex> as frequency increased, ensuring smooth injectability. Thanks to its short gelation time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(13.5 \pm 2.12 \min)$</tex>, this formulation appears promising for in situ hydrogel formation applications. Injectability tests showed continuous and uniform extrusion through a 21-gauge needle without clogging, confirming the material's balanced viscosity and mechanical stability under shear stress. This hybrid hydrogel, introduced for the first time, retained the key biochemical and structural features of native extracellular matrix and demonstrated suitable gelation behavior, highlighting its potential as a promising candidate for tissue engineering applications.

title: Stacking Ensemble Learning for Reliable Brain Tumor Diagnosis Using MRI and Deep Transfer Models
authors: Shibani Tripathy, Murali Gopal Kakita, Chandrakanta Mahanty, S. Gopal Krishna Patro
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Accurate classification of brain tumors from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is critical for timely diagnosis and treatment planning. In this study, a stacking ensemble framework is proposed to enhance the performance of transfer learning models for multi-class brain tumor classification. The Figshare Brain Tumor dataset, containing 3,064 T1-weighted contrast-enhanced MRI images categorized into glioma, meningioma, and pituitary tumors, was utilized. Four pre-trained convolutional neural networks (VGG16, InceptionV3, Xception, and NASNetMobile) were fine-tuned to serve as base learners. Each model generated probability distributions across tumor classes, which were concatenated and used as input to a logistic regression meta-learner. Experimental results demonstrate that while individual models achieved accuracies ranging from 94 % (VGG16) to 97.8% (NASNetMobile), the stacking ensemble consistently outperformed them, reaching 98.8 % accuracy with precision and recall near 99% for all tumor types. The ensemble method effectively reduced misclassifications, stabilized training, and demonstrated superior generalization across classes. These findings highlight the potential of stacking ensembles in medical imaging applications, offering a reliable tool for clinical decision support systems in neuro-oncology.

title: Classifying Roundwood Stacks into Frozen and Non-Frozen Using Radio Transmission Signals
authors: Daniel Ranta, Daniel Rönnow, Kari Hyll, Patrik Ottosson, T. Sunil Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on Wireless Sensors and Sensor Networks (WiSNeT)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a nondestructive automated approach for classifying stacked roundwood into frozen and non-frozen phase state. More specifically, ultrawideband (UWB) radio transmission data was used to train a neural network. For this approach, data from 175 spruce logs stacks was acquired at different temperatures. Three features were extracted based on the radio transmission signals' zero crossings and amplitude information. These features were fed to a feed-forward neural network for binary classification purposes. A classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4. 9 \%}$</tex> was achieved. These preliminary results show that time-domain features acquired from UWB radio transmission data can be used for classification of roundwood in frozen and non-frozen phase state.

title: A Double Deep Q Network Approach to Control HVAC Systems
authors: Yaqub Moosa Mohamed, Syed Muhammed Fahd, Imthias Ahamed T P, Mohammed Yaseen S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: HVAC (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning) systems are the largest consumers of energy in buildings, with operational energy use comprising over 30% of global consumption, and HVAC systems alone accounting for 60% of energy use in public buildings. A key challenge in building energy efficiency is to reduce HVAC energy consumption while maintaining indoor thermal comfort. However, existing HVAC optimization models often overlook the complexities of humid climates, where both temperature and humidity significantly influence thermal comfort. This research focuses on developing a Double Deep Q Network (DDQN) model designed for humid climates, with a particular emphasis on Kerala's weather conditions. The objective of the model is to enhance thermal comfort in buildings while minimizing power consumption. The proposed DDQN model demonstrated effective control of indoor temperature, keeping it within the desired comfort range. Experimental results show that the DDQN model effectively maintains indoor temperature within the comfort range and outperforms a Model Predictive Control (MPC) approach over a 24-hour period. The DDQN achieved a lower cumulative temperature deviation of 4.0°C compared to 10.2°C for the MPC, demonstrating superior comfort regulation with reduced power usage. These findings highlight the significance of a climate-specific reinforcement learning approach in achieving energy-efficient and adaptive HVAC control for humid regions.

title: Integrating FEA and Machine Learning for Smart Tool Wear Detection
authors: Pankaj Prasad, Sanjana Kulkarni, Bhanavi Shetty, Madhusudhana H K, Satish G J, Anand R Lakkundi
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Tool wear is one of the most main factors affecting the effectiveness, accuracy, and economics of machining processes. This project presents a simulation-based methodology for predictive tool wear monitoring by combining Finite Element Analysis (FEA) with vibration signal analysis to evaluate the capability of key machining parameters, such as cutting speed, tool tip radius, and depth of cut, on the stress distribution, deformation, and vibrational characteristics of a single-point cutting tool. Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models of the tool workpiece were designed and analyzed through FEA to gain insight into the analytical stress and modal characteristics of the tool under varying cutting conditions. A key conclusion of the study was that the vibrational frequency response and the stress response are sensitive to the progression of tool wear. In addition, the Python and MATLAB regression modeling helped to validate the simulation results of the study and allowed for the predictions of the tool condition trends. The study results suggest that vibrational frequency and deformation patterns provide indications of early tool degradation and thus, provide a novel intelligent and non-intrusive method for tool condition monitoring. The study also provides a foundation for future integration with real-time sensor data and machine learning based adaptive monitoring systems.

title: Neurofeedback in Brain Functional Networks
authors: Kasra Momeni, Gholam-Ali Hossein-Zadeh
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: The unknown procedure of neurofeedback interaction on brain networks is a critical drawback of this method. In this study, a dynamic functional connectivity (dFC) framework, using spatially constrained independent component analysis, was used to capture transient changes of brain networks due to reality monitoring neurofeedback. As a result of applying the analysis on a fMRI dataset of healthy individuals, four recurring connectivity states involving the default mode (DMN), cognitive control (CC), and sensorimotor (SM) networks were identified. After training, participants showed a significant increase (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) in time spent in a DMNintegrated state (State 2/Cluster 2), occurring 37% of the time and marked by strong within-DMN coupling, reflecting enhanced internal processing. Conversely, dwell time decreased <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{p}<0.05)$</tex> in a CC-SM dominated state (State 1/Cluster 1), suggesting reduced reliance on externally driven control or sensorimotor interactions. Transition analyses supported these effects, with increased shifts toward the DMN-integrated state (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 5 \%}$</tex> to 4.5%) and fewer transitions to Cluster <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1}$</tex> (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 8 \%}$</tex> to 0.2%). Overall, this dFC framework effectively captured neurofeedback-induced reorganization, offering a promising tool for optimizing interventions. Its ability to detect subtle, time-varying network changes highlights its potential clinical utility for monitoring and personalizing treatments in some brain disorders.

title: Weekly Direction Forecasting of Hang Seng Index: An EMD-based Decomposition and XGBoost
authors: Tsung-Jui Chiang Lin, Yong-Shiuan Lee, Tzong-Hann Shieh, Yung-Hung Wang
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of stock market trends is crucial for investment decision-making, particularly for major Asian market indices such as the Hang Seng Index. This study proposes a hybrid forecasting framework that integrates signal decomposition methods with machine learning to predict the weekly directional movement of the HSI.
