abstract: Embodied Intelligence, as an emerging technology, transforms Internet of Things (IoT) systems into closed-loop intelligent ecosystems, leveraging sensors and edge devices to enable agents to sense, reason, and act in continuous interaction with the physical world. However, despite this potential, existing approaches often rely on implicit representations derived from raw image or language features, lacking explicit spatial reasoning and semantic interpretability, which hinders effective generalization in policy learning. In this paper, we propose a unified Path-guided Contrastive Learning framework (PaCL) that integrates structured path generation and contrastive learning with vision-language models for robot manipulation. PaCL leverages a pre-trained vision-language model to generate semantically path which indicates robot end-effector trajectory, rendered as additional image channels to augment the visual observations. A path-guided contrastive learning module aligns embeddings of observations, language, and paths with future actions, improving generalization. PaCL builds upon a low-level control policy to improve action prediction accuracy and robustness, while promoting better generalization. Experiments on 18 diverse RLBench tasks show that PaCL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an average performance gain of 3.7% over RVT-2.

title: Finite Element Analysis of Mechanical Stability in Hip Joint Implants: A Comparative Study of Ti-6Al4V and Ti-13Nb-13Zr Alloys
authors: Mohammad Amin Parsaei Tashi, Mohammad Haghpanahi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: In this study, stress and displacement analysis of a titanium hip joint implant was conducted using the Finite Element Method and the Abaqus software. The objective was to examine the effect of design and material composition on the mechanical performance of the implant. The implant design included a solid model created using SolidWorks software. For the simulation, two titanium alloys, TI_6AL_4V and TI_13NB_13ZR, were used, which are suitable for medical applications due to their high biocompatibility. After designing, meshing, and applying boundary conditions and loading similar to walking conditions, the process was completed. The results of relative displacement showed that both materials have displacement values within the permissible range (40 to 150 micrometers), which is beneficial for proper bone growth. In terms of stress distribution, the TI_13NB_13ZR alloy showed better performance compared to TI_6AL_4V. Ultimately, the design with the material TI_13NB_13ZR was introduced as the best option because it generates the least displacement and the lowest stress concentration. Unlike previous studies that overlooked the femoral implant head, this research incorporates its design and analysis, providing a more realistic simulation of implant-bone interaction.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Integrated Facial Recognition Model for Efficient Attendance Automation
authors: Inderjeet Singh Devra, Mukul Singh Jadoun, Arpana Sinhal, Anay Sinhal
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence-based face recognition provides a contactless and automated alternative to manual roll call, but practical deployments must balance accuracy, latency, and privacy on modest hardware. This paper presents a classroom attendance system that combines a lightweight Haar cascade detector with deep metric embeddings from a pretrained convolutional network, exposed through a Flask based web interface. During enrollment, multiple images per subject are captured under varying pose and illumination, preprocessed, and converted to 128 dimensional embeddings that are stored as compact templates. At run time, live video frames are processed in real time, matched using cosine similarity, and written to an institutional attendance database. The system is evaluated under moderate, bright, and dim lighting with variations in pose, expression, and partial occlusion. On the collected dataset the prototype achieves an overall recognition accuracy of 97.8 percent with a false acceptance rate of about 1.8 percent and alse rejection rate of about 2.3 percent at an average processing time of 180 ms per frame on a mid-range CPU. Remaining limitations and directions for future improvement are discussed.

title: Smart Weather Forecasting Using a Deep-Learning Approach - a Case Study with Cyclone Fani
authors: Satya Sundar Mallick, Monideepa Roy, Sujoy Datta
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The traditional forecasting models are very complex and have intrinsic biases caused by linearization and parameterization of the contrasting calculations that compromise the forecasting accuracy. Weather stations normally receive millions of observations from multiple sites every day which come from satellites, ships, planes, ground measurements and balloons etc. This data has been collected over the last decades combining hundreds of petabytes if we include climate modelling and simulation results. The huge volume of data and cost are the main concerns for traditional weather forecasting systems. Here we have used a machine learning approach for weather forecasting which can be used the post-processing of the output of the weather forecast model or can be used independently for predicting weather. In this research paper we have analyzed the Minimum/Maximum wind speed, Minimum/Maximum surface temperature, Surface pressure & relative humidity of cyclone Fani predicted in the WRF model with respect to LSTM machine learning model outcomes. We have trained the machine learning LSTM model with a twenty-year history <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1999-2019)$</tex> to research the comparison of outcomes with WRF models to ML models.

title: FISBA: A Frequency-domain Image Steganography-based Covert Backdoor Attack Method for Self-supervised Learning Models
authors: Zi Huang, Yanxin Wang, Yubang Dong, Yan Zhang, Zheng Zhao, Qi Zhao
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Existing backdoor attacks on self-supervised pretraining models struggle to balance trigger stealthiness and downstream transferability. To address this issue, we propose a frequency-domain image steganography-based backdoor attack method, termed FISBA. First, we design a frequency-domain trigger injection network based on discrete wavelet transform and invertible neural networks. Second, we train the injection network with multiple losses and perturbation layers to ensure visual fidelity and trigger robustness. Finally, the backdoor is implanted into the pre-trained encoder using feature-alignment and distribution losses. Experimental results demonstrate that FISBA achieves a favorable balance between stealthiness and downstream transferability.

title: XF-SISS: XOR-Based Fault Tolerant and Flexible Secret Image Sharing Scheme
authors: Devansh Garg, Purushothama B R
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Sensitive visual data can be protected by splitting it into encrypted fragments recoverable only through collaboration. Traditional (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{n}, \mathbf{n}$</tex>) threshold schemes require all shares for re-construction, making them impractical in lossy or unpredictable environments. We propose a flexible XOR-based Secret Image Sharing method using Boolean operations to enable incremental, part-wise recovery. This scheme overcomes all-or-nothing limitations while maintaining perfect secrecy. Experiments confirm its fault tolerance and suitability where participant availability is uncertain.

title: Hemostatic Clips: Stress Distribution and Hinge Geometry Optimization
authors: Parastoo Kamali, Hadi Taghizadeh
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Uncontrolled intraoperative bleeding remains a critical surgical challenge. Polyoxymethylene (POM) hemostatic clips are radiolucent and easy to handle, yet hingecentric stress concentrations can jeopardize their reliability, and quantitative design-level evidence remains limited. We assess how hinge geometry governs the mechanical response of POM clips using nonlinear finite-element analysis (FEA) in ABAQUS/CAE. Four optically reconstructed designs-a solid-hinge baseline, a commercial-type reference, a modified commercial variant with an altered hinge cavity, and a rounded-hinge prototype-were examined under forcecontrolled closure (2 N and 3 N) and an equal-displacement case (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$U=9 ~\text{mm}$</tex>). Stresses consistently localized at the hinge; under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2} \mathbf{N}$</tex>, peak von Mises values spanned <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 7 - 8 8 ~ M P a}$</tex> across designs. Under 3 N, the rounded-hinge prototype sustained a lower peak <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sim 95 \text{MPa})$</tex> than the commercial-type <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sim 105 \text{MPa})$</tex> and exhibited a more homogeneous displacement field. These findings identify hinge architecture as a dominant driver of stress distribution in polymer clips and provide quantitative targets for design optimization and subsequent experimental validation.

title: Intelligent Energy Management for Sustainable Microgrids: A Machine Learning Approach to Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Dhami Johar Damiri, Ibnu Hajar, Samsurizal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: One of the biggest challenges in integrating renewable energy sources with conventional generating systems in microgrids is maintaining the optimal supply-demand balance while reducing operating costs and environmental effect. This paper develops an intelligent energy management framework for sustainable microgrids using machine learning approaches to optimize the integration of renewable energy sources. The proposed system integrates a 15 kW waste-to-energy (WTE) gasification plant, a 14.5 kWp solar photovoltaic (PV) array, and a 2.4 kWh battery energy storage system (BESS) using advanced evolutionary algorithms. The optimization framework considers biomass feedstock cost of ${\$}$45/ton, carbon price of ${\$}$25 / ton CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, electricity tariff of ${\$}$0.12 / kWh, and 20-year system lifetime with 5% discount rate. Three optimization techniques were utilized and compared: Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II), Convex Optimization, and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The simulation results demonstrate that NSGA-II outperformed the baseline, lowering carbon emissions by $37.6 \%$ (4.90 vs. 7.86 tons CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (year), improving system reliability by $67.3 \%$, and lowering operating expenses by $32.3 \%$ (${\$}$12,352 /year vs. ${\$}$18,245 / year baseline). The intelligent energy management system provided practical solutions for waste management and energy access while supporting sustainable development goals by reducing grid dependency by $33.0 \%$ and the supply-demand imbalance standard deviation by 64.2%

title: Exploring the Latest Innovations in Cloud Computing: A Comprehensive Systematic Review of Current Trends
authors: Poonam Sharma, Dr. Meenakshi, Gulbir Singh, Prabhjot Kaur, Kiran Dhiman, Shikha Rani
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Cloud computing in the today era is one of the most demanding internet-based technology to provide services like Virtual machines, Database storage and servers, facilities, etc to user, business, and organization on demand. This is a fast-growing technology which allow us in enhancing access to computational resources else that would be unreachable Due to elevated Cost. Systematic review 1st Section: -In this section we brief cloud computing with definition given by various researchers.In the 2nd Section: - In this section we discussed some key concept related to cloud computing.In the Next Section: -Main objective of this review is to lay down Lights on the present Scenario of Cloud computing research. What we found was totally insane and that is difficulties have been gaining encouragement on this area from 2012 to 2025.Privacy preserving strategically information for Security on cloud. We evaluate 79 publications using a systematic review framework in this Study. Articles have been considered from 2012 to 2025.We Concluded that the Current Cloud-Computing based research are in heavy requirement of greater Security and privacy preserving strategically information for security on cloud.

title: Independent FOC Control of Dual Front Induction Motors Considering Differential Wheel Speeds in Electric Vehicles
authors: Mohammed Boukhari, Ismail Ghadbane, Riad Bouzidi, Abdelmalik Zorig, Khaled Belhouchet, Khaled Benzaoui
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This paper presents an independent field-oriented control (FOC) strategy for dual induction motors installed in the front wheels of an electric vehicle (EV). The proposed control structure enables each motor to be driven individually, allowing accurate torque and speed regulation under various driving conditions. Special attention is given to managing the differential wheel speeds that naturally occur during vehicle cornering, ensuring balanced traction and stable operation. The mathematical formulation of the reference speeds for the right and left motors is derived as a function of the steering angle, vehicle speed, and geometric parameters. The overall control system, including motor models and FOC algorithms, is implemented and evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in maintaining smooth dynamic performance, precise speed tracking, and efficient torque response for both motors, validating its suitability for front-wheel-drive electric vehicle applications.

title: From Detection to Decision: Adaptive Orchestration of Cyber Defense in IT/OT Converged IoT Networks
authors: Vangelis Malamas, Dimitris Koutras, Panagiotis G. Giannopoulos
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: The increasing convergence of information technology (IT), operational technology (OT), and Internet of Things (loT) devices in manufacturing systems introduces unprecedented cybersecurity challenges. Traditional protection mechanisms, designed for static enterprise environments, lack the adaptability required to counter multi-vector attacks that propagate across industrial and loT domains. This paper presents a modular, adaptive orchestration framework that integrates real-time monitoring, analytics, and automated response within a unified architecture. Implemented and validated in a Manufacturing Cyber-Physical System (CPS) deployed on the Ludus cyber-range platform, the framework leverages Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) principles to ensure reproducibility, scalability, and interoperability across IT and OT infrastructures. Benchmarking under four adversarial scenarios—credential theft, lateral movement, unauthorized Modbus operations, and data exfiltration—demonstrates measurable improvements, including a 16.7% increase in detection accuracy, 41.5% faster response, and 35% reduction in recovery time, with minimal resource overhead. The results confirm that adaptive, IaC-driven orchestration significantly enhances the responsiveness and resilience of IoT-enabled industrial networks. The proposed architecture provides a replicable foundation for future research on autonomous, self-healing cybersecurity mechanisms in critical manufacturing environments.

title: Detecting Emotions from Hindi Text Using Natural Language Processing
authors: Salabh Shashank, Neha Tyagi, Rajat Kumar Behera
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: With the ever-growing world of user-generated content in local languages, particularly on communication and social media platforms, the need for emotion analysis and interpretation in such content is on the rise. Hindi being one of the biggest global languages provides a complex and rich language structure and uniqueness that calls for emotion classification in a unique way. This paper outlines a method to categorize Hindi text into pre-established emotional classes through Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning methods. The preprocessing process includes language-dependent operations like handling Devanagari script, removing stopwords, normalization, and TF-IDF vectorization. Various machine learning models like Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, Extra Trees Classifier, Ridge Classifier, Random Forest Classifier, and Support Vector Classifier was trained and their results were compared using different performance metrics. Performance metrics measured in terms of the following parameters: F1-score, recall, accuracy, precision, false positives, false negatives, true positives, and true negatives. Among the classifiers, Extra Trees Classifier had the maximum accuracy (0.8497). The research proves the efficacy and applicability of machine learning algorithms in analyzing emotional expressions in Hindi language and lays down a basis for sentiment-aware applications in low-resource languages.

title: IoT-Based Automated Office System for Energy Efficiency and Environmental Control in a Modern Workspace Management
authors: Shweta Singh, Akanksha Singh
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The increasing popularity of the Internet of Things concept has made automated solutions in societies common, which increases comfort and control and saves energy as well. One possible use of this technology is in offices, where the work environment could be automated as per the energy feedback in a given space. As energy depletion is becoming a center of concern across the world, control of basic requirements such as lighting or ventilation is paramount for energy reduction. In all those examples, introducing a smart office system is a more suitable option as it always chooses the best options for maintaining indoor conditions in proportion to outside fluctuations. Such systems are capable of incorporating real-time collecting and processing data in such a manner that temperature control and occupant detection are automatic and activated only when there is a deviation from the comfort parameters. The resulting product allows easy movement of operations and has a simple interface for operations with a minimum number of manual controls, as well as the capability to control the environment from a distance. This article investigates the features of creating an automatic office system, which uses IoT technology for modifying the space of office utilities per the environmental conditions. The fan and lighting are efficiently used, based on the data gathered from the surroundings, and the system is also remotely controlled through a simple mobile application. The model proposed within the study is an illustration of the IoT capabilities that allow for the creation of greener offices that enhance energy usage, incur lower operational costs and increase the satisfaction of the users. Taking into account user interaction and remote control, as well as data control, this paper provides evidence about how energy-efficient automated working spaces are likely to transform the modern workplace for more sustainable development.

title: Behavioral Intelligence for Multi-Account Detection Using Enhanced CPBG-GNN
authors: Nikita Manohar Shelke, Vikas Kumar Singh, Arun Kumar S, Sadikshya Bhattarai, Arya Jagdale, Abhinav Pathak
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Fraud detection systems continually face huge problems because cybercriminals are using different digital platforms and applying very sophisticated methods to hide their identities. Old-time systems that are depending on static identification markers, such as IP addresses and device fingerprints, are enjoying limited efficacy against the so-called patient trouble actors who keep changing their digital signatures. This paper introduces a Behavioral Intelligence framework that utilizes human-computer interaction patterns including keystroke dynamics, gesture-based navigation, and behavioral metrics to spot multi-account fraud activities. The suggested method applies Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) within the framework of a Cross-Platform Behavioral Graph (CPBG), thus allowing thorough integration and connection of the behavioral patterns over diverse devices, networks, and platforms. The framework incorporates explainable AI (XAI) components to guarantee transparency of decisions. The results of tests prove an 18 improvement of detection accuracy and a 12 drop of false positive rates compared to traditional methods of detection based on device and IP. As reported, enhanced CPBG-GNN gives a 91.2 accuracy.

title: Advances in Low-Noise, High-Speed SiGe BiCMOS for LEO Satellite Communications and Optical Interconnects
authors: Pascal Chevalier
publish: 2026 IEEE 26th Topical Meeting on Silicon Monolithic Integrated Circuits in RF Systems (SiRF)
abstract: This paper goes into SiGe BiCMOS technology requirements to serve LEO SATCOM User Terminals and Optical Interconnects. Technology characteristics and circuit results of platforms currently in production for these applications are presented. It also discusses prospects of low-noise and high-speed BiCMOS for both applications and technology roadmap, including the heterogeneous integration panorama.

title: Transformer-Based Approach for Pragmatic Ambiguity Detection in Natural Language Processing
authors: Reena S. Satpute, Nura Muhammad Sani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Pragmatic ambiguity is a sentence or phrase that can mean different things, depending upon the situation. In machine understanding, in order to choose the correct meaning, it is often necessary to examine many possible contexts. Because of this, computers get confused with the text, and that leads to ambiguity errors. In this paper we are introducing a new method that involves modern machine learning to detect pragmatic ambiguity. We used a base logistic regression model and current transformer models like Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) to determine whether a text is ambiguous. The baseline of the logistic regression model achieved 75 percent accuracy, which indicates there is scope for improvement. In comparison, our system based on BERT achieved an accuracy of 88 percent, which is a significant improvement. These results are shown to be able to capture the subtle ambiguations of natural language much better with fine-tuned, contextual models. The work illustrates the progress that has occurred through using transformer-based models such as BERT to detect pragmatic ambiguity. The findings conclude that further tuning and adapting may go even higher in accuracy, hence making such systems more reliable ones in real-world natural language processing tasks.

title: Toward Secure and Energy-Efficient 6G SWIPT: A MAPPO Framework for Joint UAV-ARIS Optimization
authors: Xiao Cai, Chengzhong Kuang, Shifeng Ou, Xiaoxuan Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: The evolution of 6G intensifies physical layer security (PLS) challenges. Traditional fixed reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) face energy and adaptability constraints in dynamic scenarios. This paper investigates a secure downlink system where an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) carries an aerial RIS (ARIS) to assist 6G simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) over probabilistic line-of-sight air-ground channels. A system model with a Harvest-Transmit-Store (HTS) dual-domain (time-space) resource allocation structure is established. We define a secrecy net energy efficiency (SNEE) metric jointly accounting for UAV propulsion power, ARIS-related transmission power, and harvested energy. To solve the resulting high-dimensional non-convex optimization problem, a multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) algorithm is designed to jointly optimize the UAV trajectory, ARIS reflection phase shifts, and energy-harvesting time allocation. Simulation results show that the proposed method converges faster and increases the highest and final SNEE by 46.43% and 64.02%, respectively, compared to the proximal policy optimization (PPO) baseline, demonstrating significant performance gains.

title: Enhancing Classification Performance of Incremental Extreme Learning Machine Using Fox Optimization Algorithm
authors: Swagatika Mohapatra, Sayak Hatui, Partha Pratim Sarangi, Bhabani Shankar Prasad Mishra
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) is a fast single-hidden-layer network training method that progressively adds neurons one-by-one without iterative backpropagation. However, random node generation in Incremental-ELM (I-ELM) can slow convergence and require many neurons for complex medical diagnosis tasks involving nonlinear data. This paper proposes a novel two-stage training framework, called Fox-ERS-IELM to overcome this limitation. The model combines a global Fox metaheuristic algorithm of a compact base ELM with an Enhanced Random Search based incremental refinement using the Fox algorithm to add neurons. The two-stage design promotes compact networks and mitigates overfitting by growing the model only as needed. Results show Fox-ERS-IELM outperforms standard ERS-IELM and a Fox-optimized MLP baseline, delivering substantial accuracy gains and improved sensitivity-specificity balance. As a result, the Fox-ERS-IELM method demonstrates high reliability in medical data classification.

title: Integrated Detection And Recognition Of Low-altitude Uavs Using Airport Security Radar, Yolo, And Vit Network
authors: Weiying Lu, Wenying Wang, Jiaming Li, Wenjun Han
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Low-altitude unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) pose severe threats to airport operation safety due to their small radar cross-section (RCS) and low flight altitude, which make them difficult to detect via traditional surveillance systems. This paper proposes an integrated low-altitude UAV detection and recognition framework fusing airport security radar and deep learning. First, airport security radar collects echo signals of low-altitude targets, and Range-Doppler (RD) maps are generated through signal processing to convert spatial-temporal radar data into visual feature representations. Second, the YOLO (You Only Look Once) object detection algorithm is optimized with adaptive attention and improved loss functions for real-time localization of potential UAV targets, filtering out clutter such as birds and weather interference. Finally, the Vision Transformer (ViT) network is enhanced with sequential feature reasoning and gradient-efficient optimization to realize high-precision sequential recognition of detected targets, leveraging its global feature dependency capture capability to distinguish UAVs from other low-altitude objects. Experimental results show that the framework achieves a detection accuracy of 95.2% and a recognition accuracy of 92.8% for low-altitude UAVs, with a processing latency of less than 100ms, meeting the real-time requirements of airport security. This integrated solution provides a promising technical means for enhancing airport low-altitude security.

title: Analysis of the Admittance Properties of a Two-Terminal Network Composed of a Reactive Element and a Piezoelectric Vibrator
authors: Hongyan Sun, Hongquan Wang, Mingxiang Zhu, Tao Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: The piezoelectric vibrator can form a well-performing bandpass filter, but due to its capacitive admittance below the resonance frequency and above the anti-resonance frequency, it is difficult to achieve the functions of a high-pass filter and a low-pass filter using only the piezoelectric vibrator. To overcome this limitation, a reactive element must be introduced. The introduction of a reactive element allows the piezoelectric filter to cover a broader frequency range and achieve more precise frequency selection properties. In bandpass filter design, to optimize the performance of the piezoelectric vibrator and adjust its passband width, inductance and capacitance are usually introduced to provide better frequency response properties for the filter. Therefore, this article will focus on piezoelectric filters with a reactive element and explore how to optimize the bandpass properties of the piezoelectric vibrator by reasonably configuring the reactive element. Additionally, this article will study how the introduction of a reactive element can improve the electromagnetic interference (EMI) suppression effect of the piezoelectric vibrator to enhance its electromagnetic compatibility in wideband applications.

title: RT-DETR-Palm A Transformer Based Approach for Oil Palm Tree Detection Using UAV Imagery
authors: Kamarul Hawari Ghazali, Slamet Riyadi, Annisa Divayu Andriyani, Razali Muda, Adyanata Lubis, Suqing Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Accurate and efficient detection of oil palm trees is essential for plantation auditing, yield estimation, and sustainable management practices. This paper presents The Real-Time Detection Transformer (RT-DETR-Palm), a customized transformer-based object detection model designed for UAV imagery. The model’s optimization with a ResNet-50 backbone and RT-DETR framework eliminates the need for complex post-processing and anchor boxes, enabling real-time performance. Annotated datasets from plantations in Pahang, Malaysia, and Riau, Indonesia, are used to train and assess RT-DETR-Palm. The results show that it outperforms the conventional Faster R-CNN model in terms of overall accuracy, localization precision (IoU), and counting error, while achieving significantly faster inference speed. The model demonstrates strong generalization across varying plantation layouts and canopy densities, making it suitable for scalable deployment in Southeast Asian agricultural contexts. This work establishes the groundwork for future integration with yield forecasting and autonomous plantation management systems while also providing a workable and effective solution for UAV-based oil palm monitoring.

title: Biomedical Applications of Pectin Nanomaterials: Progress and Perspectives
authors: Maryam Rajabzadeh-Khosroshahi, Ali Baradar Khoshferat, Mehdi Salami-Kalajahi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Pectin polysaccharide is a natural biopolymer that has recently emerged as a sustainable platform for the design of functional nanomaterials in biomedical research. Its excellent biocompatibility, biodegradability, low toxicity, chemical versatility, low cost, and ease of availability make it well-suited for nanoscale delivery platforms designed to encapsulate and protect therapeutic agents until they reach the target tissues to be released sustained/controlled. Pectin-based nanomaterials are particularly effective in oral delivery because pectin retains its structure in acidic media of small intestine. On the other hand, pectin and pectin-coated nanomaterials have strong potential in liver cancer-targeted drug delivery through selective binding to hepatocyte cancer cell receptors. While pectin-coated nanocomposites are being explored as non-viral vectors for gene delivery, further clinical validation is needed. In addition, pectin has intrinsic biological properties, including anticancer, antidiabetic, and anti-cholesterol, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, that enhance therapeutic effects. Pectin nanomaterials have illustrated significant potential in wound healing by promoting cell proliferation, regulating immune responses, and enhancing tissue regeneration, especially when loaded with therapeutic/pharmaceutical agents. This review highlights recent advances, advantages, limitations, and future directions of pectin-based nanomaterials in biomedical applications, specifically in drug/gene delivery systems and wound healing and theragnostic applications.

title: A Machine Learning Based Analysis About Adoption of an OTT Educational app by Learners in India from Diverse Backgrounds
authors: Monika Sharma, Neha Mishra, K K Mishra, Rajnish Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: This paper analyses the Indian Over-the-Top (OTT) educational app market, using the Random Forest (RF) machine learning algorithm to deconstruct user sentiments and strategic orientation towards the country's objectives. Using responses from 536 users, three main research questions have been examined: (1) the relative performance of apps in the AI and UI dimensions; (2) the regional and demographic factors determining their perceived fit with the 'Viksit Bharat' and 'Digital India' programs; and (3) the effectiveness of the apps in enabling students to learn future-oriented skills. Among the key findings: Platforms such as Coursera.org and LinkedIn Learning top the list for AI/UI satisfaction, especially for those with more advanced degrees. Belief in an app’s fit with national goals is greatest with urban users and highly predicted by the app’s technological sophistication. Central to the analysis is the "Popularity vs. Preparedness" gap that highlights that the most-used platforms are not necessarily the ones most attuned to the skills demand for the 2025 workforce. The authors recommend joint collaborations in the form of a prescriptive led binding between multiple stakeholders towards developing future of work skill modules to be embedded into the mass market educational platforms to re-orient the EdTech sector to India's long term developmental plan.

title: Cancer-Associated Actin Mutations Enhance Cofilin Binding Affinity: Insights from Steered Molecular Dynamics Simulations
authors: Danial Sedighpour, Farzan Ghalichi, Iman Zoljanahi Oskui
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Cofilin plays a fundamental role in remodeling of the actin cytoskeleton by binding to actin filaments and facilitating their severing and depolymerization. Alterations in cofilin-actin interactions due to actin mutations have been implicated in various cancers, including skin cancer. This study investigates the binding affinity of cofilin to both wild-type (WT) and mutant actin (D288N, G168N, R63Q) using steered molecular dynamics (SMD) simulations. Structural models were constructed and subjected to constant velocity pulling to measure maximum rupture forces and interaction energies. Results demonstrate that all three cancer-associated mutations exhibit increased rupture forces and more negative total energy compared to WT, indicating stronger binding affinities. Notably, the R63Q mutation produced the highest rupture force and most favorable interaction energies, suggesting a significant stabilizing effect on the actin-cofilin complex. Results indicate that R63Q increases force-dependent mechanostability at the cofilin-actin interface. This can be leveraged to (i) provide a stabilized control for biochemical and single-filament severing assays and (ii) guide interface-targeted modulators that tune cofilin activity to control filament turnover. These findings provide critical insight into the molecular effects of oncogenic actin mutations and their potential role in cytoskeletal dysregulation during cancer progression.

title: Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Network for Photothermal Optical Coherence Tomography Signal Enhancement
authors: Amir Hossein Osooli, Mohammadhossein Salimi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Photothermal Optical Coherence Tomography is an emerging functional imaging modality that combines the high spatial resolution of OCT with molecular specificity through photothermal contrast. However, PT-OCT signals typically require extensive temporal averaging to achieve adequate signal-to-noise ratios, limiting imaging speed and realtime applications. This work presents a novel one-dimensional Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (1D-SRGAN) that reconstructs full-length PT-OCT temporal responses (176 data points) from truncated initial signal segments (22 data points), achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times$</tex> temporal super-resolution while preserving critical spectral characteristics at the modulation frequency. The proposed architecture employs a deep residual generator with skip connections and adversarial training, validated through comprehensive frequency-domain analysis. Statistical evaluation demonstrates significant improvement in signal reconstruction fidelity, with preserved modulation frequency characteristics and enhanced signal-to-noise ratios compared to conventional interpolation methods.

title: Stacking Ensemble Model for Predicting M Dwarf Spectroscopic Properties from Photometry
authors: Sukanya Meethong, Papangkorn Inkeaw, Sirinrat Sithajan, Jakramate Bootkrajang
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Effective temperature, surface gravity, and metallicity are important fundamental parameters for the study of stars. The established approach to determine these parameters is by analyzing data collected from time-consuming spectroscopy. Recently, photometry emerged as a cheaper alternative to spectroscopy to obtain stellar data, and though less accurate, it provides a cost-effective alternative for large-scale observations. This raises an important research question about how accurately and extensively photometric data can be used to predict the spectroscopic parameters across large stellar populations.In this paper, we present a stacking ensemble regression model for predicting the effective temperature, surface gravity, and metallicity of M dwarf stars from photometric data. Our method combines a non-linear global function approximator, as instantiated by a neural network model, and a local approximator, as instantiated by a tree-based gradient boosting model, to capture the regularities in the data. We evaluated our approach on a dataset constructed by cross-matching LAMOST DR8 spectroscopic parameters with multi-wavelength photometric data from the SDSS, 2MASS, and WISE surveys. The experimental results demonstrated the potential of the proposed stacking ensemble compared to the baselines.

title: Efficient Nonlinear Solid Mechanics Computation via Krylov Subspace Methods in a Finite Volume Framework
authors: Puthalapattu Sukanya
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This paper introduces a computationally efficient approach for solving nonlinear solid mechanics problems using the finite volume method. The method employs a Jacobian- free Newton-Krylov (JFNK) technique, which avoids the explicit formation and storage of the Jacobian matrix, thereby reducing memory requirements and computational cost. The implementation leverages Krylov subspace solvers, such as GMRES, in conjunction with a compact-stencil approximation of the Jacobian as a preconditioner. The performance of this JFNK approach is assessed and compared against a traditional segregated solution method across a range of benchmark problems, including linear elasticity, hyperelasticity, and elastoplasticity. Results demonstrate the method’s superior performance, particularly in terms of computational speed, for linear and nonlinear elastic cases. The implementation is integrated into an open-source computational mechanics library, providing a foundation for further research and application in solid mechanics simulations.

title: AI-Powered Fraud Detection in Financial Transactions: A Data-Driven Perspective
authors: Ravi Kumar, Pradeep Kumar Kushwaha, Ajay Rana, Km Meenakshi, Kirti Dilip Singh, Deepshikha Bhargava
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The fast growth of digital platforms used to conduct financial transactions, online purchases and delivery of services has brought up new vulnerabilities that have been used by fraudsters. The conventional rule-based detection schemes, as effective as they may be in identifying familiar patterns of fraud, are in most cases inefficient in keeping up with the ever-evolving character of the fraud. This paper introduces a detailed, information-based approach consisting of the combination of the use of supervised and unsupervised learning, anomaly detection approaches and domain-specific feature engineering to detect suspicious behaviors in the most productive way. Using historical data, behavioral patterns and information regarding relationships, the system identifies both suspected frauds that it has never encountered before, and frauds in which it has been aware of all along. The methodology facilitates adaptive learning, that allows constantly updating of the model from new attack strategies with no lost efficiency. Experimental analysis of big transaction data sets indicate significant improvement of the detection accuracy, false alarm reduction and scalability of the high volume financial transactions. This research highlights the need to have open an privacy aware and cross platform integration of Al in order to achieve the creation of resilient fraud detection systems.

title: AnaPose - Anatomy Aware Loss for Pose Estimation in the Operating Room
authors: Jasira P K, Jobin Jose
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Human pose estimation is a field with vast applications, and its use in surgical rooms holds significant potential. It can be leveraged to enhance human-robot interactions in surgical environments, improving precision and efficiency. Keypoint R-CNN serves as the backbone for pose estimation, leveraging its ability to detect and localize key points with high precision. By integrating anatomy-aware loss functions, the model improves the accuracy and consistency of pose predictions, making it particularly useful in medical and surgical applications. This approach ensures anatomically plausible estimations, enhancing the reliability of human pose tracking in complex environments like a surgical room. The anatomy-aware loss includes components such as symmetric limb loss, plausible bone lengths, and plausible joint angles, ensuring anatomically accurate predictions. The proposed model, AnaPose, with the anatomy-aware loss functions ensure that predictions comply with the anatomical constraints. By incorporating these loss functions, the proposed method can achieve improved consistency in terms of accuracy across diverse scenarios, addressing challenges such as occlusions, complex poses and anatomical plausibility. It demonstrates better predictions compared to other existing methods, making it a promising solution for pose estimation in surgical rooms.

title: Invsio Track: An integrated crime investigation tool
authors: Shreya Punani, Yash Kumar Gaur, Sahil Panwar, Sunil Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This work proposes a crime management and investigation system for different police departments and investigation agencies to work together and collaborate on different criminal cases. It is a digital platform for the computerized handling of different criminal cases and complaints. Currently, all the details and complaints of thieves and cases are stored manually in a large space. The data is not centralized, which is why much time is required to integrate and analyze to arrive at a conclusion. The process of investigation is quite time-consuming. The criminal investigation tracker system provides two major modules- Admin module and User module where new cases can be added, cases can be assigned to police officers, various departments can work together on a case, evidence can be uploaded, and a prediction engine for predicting suspects with a probability based on logical evidence provided. The prediction engine uses bayesian networks to predict the suspects by modeling conditional dependencies between features based on type of relationship, previous criminal record, and presence on crime scene. This platform allows for speedy investigation along with proper logging of case details making the entire investigation process efficient.

title: Class-AB Power Amplifier for IoT Based Healthcare System
authors: Puja Priya, Deeksha Sankrit, Avinash Kumar Pandey, Avadhesh Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This work proposed a Class-AB Power Amplifier (PA) design for IoT-based healthcare applications, exploiting GaN HEMTs technology obtained from CREE for improved performance. The final aim for such applications is to optimize the stability between performance and power efficiency, crucial for portable healthcare applications. This paper introduces the design a Class-AB power amplifier of 1.5 GHz operating frequency, consisting of a single stage topology with input and output matching network. The proposed amplifier achieves an output power of up to 42.360 dBm with a power gain of 14.360 dB, a power-added efficiency of 63.637% and a drain efficiency of 64.88%. The performance of the power amplifier aligns with the stringent requirements of IoT-based healthcare systems, ensuring reliable and efficient operation in wearable and portable devices. Design and simulation are performed using Advance design system (ADS) software from Keysight technologies.

title: SIW Circulator with a Smoothed Constant-Width Triangular Central Junction
authors: Kang Yang, Di Jiang, Guhaolan Zhao, Weifeng Su, Jiahao Zhao, Jing Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: To meet the demand for low-loss microwave components and drawing on waveguide circulator design theory, we present an X-band single-surface-field substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) circulator that exhibits high isolation, low loss, and wide bandwidth. Based on an analysis of waveguide field modes and ferrite geometry, a smoothed constant-width triangular (SCWT) junction is proposed. Measurements show that the X- band SIW circulator employing an SCWT-shaped ferrite junction achieves an insertion loss below 0.2 dB, a voltage standing-wave ratio (VSWR) below 1.2, and an isolation exceeding 20 dB from 9.5 to 10.8 GHz, meeting the design requirements.

title: Spatiotemporal Optimization in AI-Enhanced Climate Modeling using Deep Gaussian Processes
authors: Tina Babu, Rekha R Nair, Thrilok Kolla, Aarthi Sivasankaran, Balamurugan Balusamy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Climate modeling is essential for anticipating environmental risks, yet conventional methods fail to capture localized non-linear spatiotemporal variations. This paper proposes an AI-enhanced climate modeling framework based on Deep Gaussian Processes (DGPs) for spatiotemporal optimization. The novelty lies in a multilayer Gaussian architecture with separable spatiotemporal kernels that model hierarchical dependencies while quantifying predictive uncertainty. The methodology integrates standardized feature representation, posterior inference, spectral analysis, and predictive entropy mapping. An acquisition-based optimization strategy balances predictive mean-variance trade-offs to locate high-information spatiotemporal points. Results demonstrate improved accuracy in uncertainty visualization, identification of dominant frequency structures, and detection of dynamic patterns across space and time. The model highlights regions of high epistemic uncertainty, enabling targeted monitoring and intervention. By enhancing predictive fidelity and interpretability, the framework supports climate change mitigation, disaster preparedness, and policy formulation. The proposed approach delivers a scalable and interpretable solution, bridging advanced probabilistic modeling with actionable decision support for sustainable environmental planning.

title: One- and Two-Stage Ka-Band MMIC Power Amplifiers in 90nm GaN
authors: Benjamin Rautio, Adam Der, Taylor Barton
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: This paper presents the design of two Ka-band power amplifiers in a 90-nm Gallium Nitride (GaN) process. The first design is a broadband 25–45 GHz driver stage with 25 dBm output power. In the second design, the same driver is used as the first stage of a two-stage power amplifier with a 1:2 staging ratio. Simulated and measured continuous wave (CW) performance are compared for both designs. The two-stage PA is characterized under modulated excitation at a 40 GHz center frequency with 12.4% average power-added efficiency (PAE) for a 24 dBm average output power for a 10-dB peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) signal with 100 MHz bandwidth.

title: Tailored Reflection Network Design for Class-F Power Amplifiers
authors: Fabian Schneider, Maik Ehses, Benjamin Mydla, Alexander Ruderer, Alicia Westphal, Gianluca Simone, Christof Pfannenmüller, Fabian Lurz
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: Harmonic reflections primarily influence the performance of class-F power amplifiers, particularly when implemented in practice, with non-ideal lossy components. To analyze these limitations, the impedance transformation capabilities of harmonic reflection networks is compared between lumped elements and transmission lines, using S-parameters of real components and substrate parameters, respectively. A mixed network of lumped elements and transmission lines is introduced and validated through the design and implementation of a 10W class-F power amplifier operating at 144 MHz. Measurements demonstrate a peak efficiency of 75.9% at 20.39 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{dB}$</tex> gain. The results conclude a practical design methodology for selecting suitable harmonic termination networks based on frequency and implementation constraints.

title: AI-Driven Agriculture System for Crop Recommendation and Farmer Decision-Making Support
authors: Priyanka Kaushik, Suchita Yadav, Rushan Kumar, K Kiran, Minu Balakrishnan, Kochumol Abraham
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Agriculture is a significant factor in the realization of food security and economic stability especially in the countries where many individuals rely on farming. Nevertheless, the conventional methods of farming are usually tainted by issues like poor choice of crops, poor management of fertilizers, epidemics, and uncertainties in the market prices. The proposed project is a holistic solution that uses machine learning and deep learning to transform agriculture. The smart agriculture system proposed will have applications of crop recommendation, fertilizer guidance, prediction of plant diseases, and forecasting of crop prices. The apps give farmers practical information according to the soil data, crop details and visuals. By tying the strength of data-driven decision-making, this project will contribute to the improvement of agricultural productivity, promote sustainability, and empower the farming communities. The project will be successful due to continuous improvement, user feedback, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

title: Predicting Breast Cancer Survival: An Approach using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Soma Sekhar Kolisetty, Parlapalli Haseena, Chennupalli Chandrika Tirumala, Kodavati Jayamma, Kathi Chandra Mouli, Anitha Vulugundam, K.V.Narasimha Reddy
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Accurate prediction of breast cancer survival is a critical task for guiding treatment planning and clinical decision-making. In this study, we present a comparative analysis of classical survival models, machine learning techniques, and deep learning architectures using the METABRIC dataset. Traditional approaches, including the Cox Proportional Hazards (CPH) model and Random Forest Survival (RFS), achieved the highest predictive performance, with C-indices of 0.8719 and 0.8249, respectively. We further introduce SA-DGNet, a novel hybrid deep learning framework that integrates gated neural networks with self-attention mechanisms to capture both short-term and long-range temporal dependencies. While SA-DGNet obtained a lower C-index (0.7590) compared to CPH and RFS, it demonstrated enhanced interpretability, personalized risk stratification, and temporal attention visualizations that are clinically meaningful. Our findings highlight that classical models remain highly competitive for structured datasets, but deep learning models such as SA-DGNet open new avenues for multimodal integration, time-varying patient trajectories, and explainable prognosis tools. This work contributes a systematic benchmark across modeling paradigms and lays the groundwork for future research in interpretable deep survival prediction.

title: Compliance to Stimulus: A Mixed Method Study
authors: Rithika Sivasankar, Reena Monica P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Excessive noise in high school study halls can disrupt focus and learning, yet interventions often depend on active supervision. This study tested a passive, psychology-based approach by displaying a “watching eye” poster, adapted from Haley and Fessler's (2005) Watching Eye Theory, to examine whether subconscious cues could reduce noise. Over 20 days, sound pressure levels were recorded at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex>-second intervals during study hall sessions with and without the poster. Quantitative analysis showed a statistically significant 2.91 dB reduction in mean noise levels when posters were displayed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(t=-6.82, p<0.001)$</tex>, further supported by two-way ANOVA results showing main effects for group <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{F}(\mathbf{1, 1 1 8 0})=\mathbf{7 5. 4 8}, p<0.001)$</tex>, day <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{F}(\mathbf{9, 1 1 8 0})=\mathbf{3 1. 3 1}, p<0.001)$</tex>, and interaction effects <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{F}(\mathbf{9, 1 1 8 0}) =54.48, \mathrm{p} 0.001)$</tex>, with a medium effect size <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\eta^{2}=0.34)$</tex>. Logistic regression models achieved 58% accuracy in classifying control versus poster conditions, demonstrating modest predictive value. Qualitative observations documented mixed student responses, ranging from compliance to mockery, highlighting the complexity of real-world behavioral change. By integrating traditional inferential statistics with machine learning methods, this work illustrates how advanced computational analysis can be applied to behavioral psychology.

title: Carbon Nanotube Mediated Hyperthermia for Cancer Therapy
authors: B. Zeinali, A. Mojra
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Carbon nanotube (CNT) mediated hyperthermia has emerged as a promising approach for cancer therapy, offering localized heating with high thermal efficiency. This study presents a computational framework that systematically evaluates the effects of CNT concentration and applied power on tumor temperature distribution and necrosis development. An axisymmetric tumor model incorporating interstitial fluid flow, nanoparticle transport, bioheat transfer, and Arrheniusbased damage assessment was employed. The results demonstrate that increasing CNT concentration and power density significantly elevates maximum tumor temperature (up to 52% increase) and accelerates necrosis onset, reducing the time to irreversible tissue damage from over 1000 s to less than 200 s. However, excessive escalation was shown to extend irreversible necrosis into adjacent healthy tissue, highlighting the delicate balance between treatment efficacy and safety. These findings provide clinically relevant insights that support the optimization of CNT dose and power modulation to achieve effective and safe tumor ablation.

title: Drone-Based Thermographic Inspection for Preventive Maintenance of High Voltage Substations: A Case Study in Indonesia’s IKN Supporting Grid
authors: Chandra Lima Silalahi, David Silalahi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Drone-based thermographic inspection has emerged as a promising method to enhance the reliability and efficiency of high-voltage substations. Conventional thermovision techniques have proven effective for preventive maintenance by detecting early-stage hotspots in both substations and transmission networks. However, manual inspections are time-consuming, pose safety risks, and often require partial equipment shutdowns. This paper presents a case study on the integration of drone-borne infrared thermography for preventive maintenance at the Karang Joang Substation, a critical node in Indonesia’s new capital (IKN) supporting grid. The approach demonstrates a substantial reduction in inspection time (1 hour versus 16 hours using manual thermography), improved personnel safety, and higher anomaly-detection accuracy. The study further discusses UAV reliability aspects, thermal monitoring of electronic components, and hotspot-mitigation strategies, thereby providing insights into the integration of unmanned aerial systems into substation asset management. The proposed method underscores a scalable pathway toward the digitalization and automation of preventive maintenance in high-voltage infrastructure.

title: Low Complexity Block Modified LSMR Signal Detection for MIMO-OTFS
authors: Yuan Qu, Meng Tang, Lei Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: This paper proposes a low-complexity block modified least squares minimum residual (B-MLSMR) signal detection strategy for multi-input multi-output (MIMO) systems employing orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation. To alleviate performance degradation caused by spatial correlation between MIMO antennas, we have designed an optimized estimation algorithm for the exponential correlation matrix based on samples. Incorporating a regularization parameter λ, the proposed B-MLSMR detection algorithm serves to enhance robustness against non-convergence and severe error degradation. Furthermore, we adopted a block-wise implementation by leveraging the sparsity diagonal characteristics of the time-domain channel. Compared with other detection methods, simulations demonstrate that the proposed B-MLSMR approach achieves superior efficiency and enhanced diversity gain.

title: Fused Deposition Modeling in Bone Tissue Engineering: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Parsa Doaguie, Shima Mirzaie Parsa
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Bone tissue engineering has emerged as a promising approach to address the limitations of conventional bone grafting methods, such as autografts, allografts, and xenografts, which are hindered by issues like immunological responses, disease transmission risks, and limited donor availability. Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM), a costeffective and versatile 3D printing technique, has gained significant attention for fabricating bone scaffolds with precise control over geometry, porosity, and mechanical properties. This paper reviews the role of FDM in bone tissue engineering, focusing on its ability to produce scaffolds that mimic the extracellular matrix (ECM) of bone, facilitating cell migration, proliferation, and differentiation. Key scaffold requirements, including biocompatibility, biodegradability, mechanical stability, and optimal pore size, are discussed alongside the materials used, such as polylactic acid (PLA), polycaprolactone (PCL), and their composites with bioactive ceramics like hydroxyapatite (HA) and tricalcium phosphate (TCP). The FDM process, from CAD design to G-code conversion and layer-by-layer deposition, is detailed, highlighting its accessibility through open-source platforms and low-cost materials. Recent advancements, including the development of bio-based filaments and hybrid polymerceramic composites, are explored for their enhanced bioactivity and sustainability. The paper also addresses challenges, such as limited material options and high extrusion temperatures, which restrict direct cell incorporation. By offering patient-specific scaffold designs with tunable properties, FDM demonstrates significant potential for bone regeneration, paving the way for advanced clinical applications in addressing complex bone defects.

title: Deep Learning-Based Lung Tumor Detection and Classification in CT Imaging
authors: Srinivasulu Raju S, Mahitha Krishna P, Meghana V, Nauseen SK
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Lung tumors may originate within the lungs or spread from other parts of the body, and they pose a serious health risk as they can be either benign or malignant. Early detection and precise identification of lung tumors play a critical role in supporting pathologists to initiate timely treatment and improve patient outcomes. Among imaging modalities, computed tomography (CT) is most widely used, as it provides radiologists with detailed visualization of lung structures. In this study, a deep learning–based approach is proposed, employing a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to effectively analyze CT scans for lung tumor detection and classification. The model categorizes CT images into either normal (no cancer present) or abnormal, with suspected cancer cases further classified into major subtypes of lung cancer, including adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, and large cell carcinoma. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed CNN model achieves a detection accuracy of 97%, highlighting its potential as an effective tool for assisting clinicians in the early diagnosis of lung cancer.

title: Development and Classification of Manipuri databases for healthy and Parkinson’s disease patient of Manipur
authors: Ranita Khumukcham, Kishorjit Nongmeikapam
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Parkinson’s disease is a neurological movement disorder which affects the brain by stopping the dopamine production in the brain. Some common health problems of Parkinson’s disease are body movement difficulty due to stiffness of muscle, loss of memory, depression, sleeping problem and speech problem which is difficulty in pronouncing words. The paper presents the development of database of Parkinson’s disease (PD) patient of Manipur and classification of PD patient from healthy ones. The voice of 50 people of PD patient and 50 people of healthy ones is collected from the people of Manipur. The voice of English vowel \a\, \e\, \i\, \o\, \u\ are recorded and used here. An accuracy of 67.08% is achieved which classifies the voice recorded as PD and healthy ones while implementing this database.

title: Dynamic Modeling of a Cable-Driven Series Elastic Upper Extremity Exoskeleton for Post-Stroke Rehabilitation
authors: Ali Selk Ghafari, Omid Kalantari
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: This study presents a dynamic modeling framework for simulating human-exoskeleton interaction in upper-extremity movements using Simscape. An open-loop inverse dynamics simulation was conducted with a one-degree-of-freedom exoskeleton designed to assist elbow flexion/extension while allowing natural shoulder motion. The shoulder joint was modeled with compliant properties, and the exoskeleton was actuated via a series elastic actuator (SEA). Simulation results demonstrated close tracking between human and exoskeleton joint angles, with mean errors below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm \mathrm{3}^{\circ}$</tex>. The SEA provided compensatory torque closely matching the estimated human torque deficit at the elbow, while human-robot interaction forces and torques remained within physiologically acceptable ranges. A cyclogram analysis of shoulder-elbow coordination yielded a correlation coefficient of 0.92, with a 15 % reduction in loop area compared to unassisted motion, indicating improved efficiency of assisted movements. These findings validate the proposed model as a reliable tool for analyzing upper-extremity human-exoskeleton dynamics and provide insights for controller design and optimization of assistive strategies.

title: From Theory to Practice: Experiential Learning in Analog Electronic Circuits Lab
authors: Remya Jayachandran, Salila Hegde
abstract: Experiential learning in the context of an analog electronics lab involves immersing students in hands-on circuit design, testing, and troubleshooting—moving beyond traditional theory-centric instruction. Conventional methods often emphasize theoretical concepts at the expense of practical understanding, limiting the student’s ability to grasp the complexities of analog circuits. This article presents an innovative methodology for conducting analog electronics laboratory sessions, aimed at promoting experiential learning and increasing student engagement. The approach integrates real-time simulations, project-based learning, and collaborative problem-solving to bridge the gap between theory and application. Implemented over two consecutive semesters with a cohort of 71 second-year engineering students, the redesigned lab encouraged critical thinking, creativity, and deeper conceptual understanding through hands-on experiments and interactive demonstrations. Student feedback indicates notable improvements in conceptual clarity, motivation, and practical skills. The findings suggest that this innovation-driven and experiential framework can significantly enhance the effectiveness of analog electronics education within engineering curricula.
