title: Enhancing AES Security Through Simulated Power Trace Analysis and DPA Resistance
authors: Chella Amala, Jayaram Kandukuri, Burra Subbarao, Trinadh Sundar Katta, Jayendra Kumar, Priyanka Singh, Banee Bandana Das, Saswat Kumar Ram, Saraju P Mohanty
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This research presents a simulated AES encryption and decryption system designed to protect against side-channel attacks using advanced masking and redundancy techniques. Two power trace generators were developed: one simulates power consumption with adjustable noise and scaling, and the other enhances security through masking and least significant bit (LSB) randomization. A Differential Power Analysis (DPA) attack model was used to test the system's resistance by analyzing differences in power trace patterns. Implemented in Python, the simulation results show that the proposed system effectively hides power consumption behavior and significantly lowers the success rate of DPA attacks. By shuffling bits during each encryption round and spreading power usage across multiple paths, the system makes it harder for attackers to identify keyrelated activity. These countermeasures provide a strong defense compared to standard AES and show promise for future realworld and hardware-based cryptographic applications.

title: Bridging First Aid Guidance with Real-Time Community Assistance for Public Health Emergencies
authors: Sundaram Raj, Soumya Singh, Nidhi Agarawal
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Public health emergencies occur suddenly and demand swift, informed response. Indeed, the vast majority of people do not have immediate access to accurate first aid advice or real-time support from the community in such circumstances. This study presents the design of a conceptual web application to complement verified first-aid instructions and offer live help from community to enhance emergency response system. The service that was built is an emergency system to help real-time step by step "walk" the user through common emergencies, the ability for offline access as well as live chat to interact with nearby volunteers or trained responders. This article investigates the theoretical framework, application designs and community-based support structure for enhancing health resilience in the grass roots. This project aligns with Sustainable Development Goal 3 - Good Health and Well-Being through the incorporation of healthcare information systems within social assistance networks.

title: Engineering Injectable Gelatin-Tyramine/Alginatetyramine Hydrogels for Bone Tissue Engineering: A Ratio-Dependent Study of Structure, Mechanics, and Biocompatibility
authors: Melika Mansouri Moghaddam, Soroush Ghofrani Beiragh, Elaheh Jooybar, Rana Imani
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: The development of injectable hydrogels with tunable physicochemical and biological properties is critical for advancing minimally invasive strategies in bone tissue engineering (BTE). In this study, enzymatically crosslinked hydrogels composed of tyramine-modified gelatin (GEL-TA) and alginate (ALG-TA) were engineered at three volumetric ratios (70: 30,50: 50,30: 70) and systematically evaluated for their structural, mechanical, rheological, and biological performance. The optimized GEL50/ALG50 formulation exhibited a balanced gelation time, an interconnected porous morphology, and superior mechanical properties (Young's modulus: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$185.3 \pm 12.7 \text{kPa}$</tex>), which can be attributed to enhanced polymer entanglement and crosslinking density. All hydrogels exhibited shear-thinning behavior, desirable for injectability. Human osteosarcoma cell line (MG-63)-based assays confirmed high cytocompatibility, with the GEL50/ALG50 formulation supporting the most favorable cell adhesion, viability, and morphology. These findings highlight the potential of GEL-TA/ALG-TA hydrogels, particularly the 50:50 ratio, as injectable and biocompatible scaffolds for bone regeneration applications.

title: During Sequential Hand Movements Using Neurophysiological Analyses of EEG Signals
authors: Elnaz Eilbeigi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Background: A brain-computer interface (BCI) is considered a promising tool in neurorehabilitation. For this purpose, movement intention can be detected from brain signals and used as a control command for a robotic device. In most existing studies, a rest interval is provided before each movement to allow the generation of movement intention. However, daily life activities are performed continuously, without such intervals. Method: This paper focuses on detecting movement intention during sequential movements, using neurophysiological analyses that include movement-related cortical potentials (MRCP), event-related desynchronization/synchronization (ERD/ERS) patterns, and, for the first time, inter-trial phase coherence (ITC) analysis. Results: The results showed that during sequential hand movements, including reaching, grasping and lifting objects of various weights, holding the object, lowering it, replacing it, releasing it, and returning the hand to its initial position, two movement intentions were present: the first before starting the hand movement, and the second during the holding phase of the object. Conclusions: In this study, we demonstrated that it is possible to decode the next movement intention while performing the current movement, which can lead to the development of a new generation of intelligent neural prostheses.

title: EnergiVerse: AI-Powered Decentralized Energy Marketplace System
authors: Sangeeth M.S, Neha Lilly Kishor
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Peer-to-peer (P2P) renewable energy trading is emerging as a promising solution to decentralize power distribution and enhance renewable energy utilization. However, several critical barriers limit its real-world adoption, including the absence of real-time energy tracking, lack of grid safety mechanisms, potential DISCOM revenue loss, and fraud risks. Many existing models depend heavily on smart meters, which are not widely accessible or affordable in several developing regions. This paper presents EnergiVerse, an AI-powered decentralized energy marketplace that combines blockchain smart contracts, real-time energy tracking through low-cost IoT-based solutions, AI -driven demand forecasting, and dynamic grid safety layers. The system introduces a trading fee mechanism to fairly compensate DISCOMs and ensure grid sustainability. The design incorporates strict fraud prevention through real-time seller-side energy updates and buyer storage capacity limits. The entire system was developed and rigorously tested in a simulated environment using Remix IDE, with backend-controlled dynamic trade limits emulating real-time grid responsiveness. The results demonstrate EnergiVerse's ability to deliver a secure, scalable, and economically viable energy trading platform for communities worldwide.

title: SCADA System Analysis for Load Control Using Decision Tree C4.5 Method at Public Facility
authors: Yudi Andika, Cahya Karisma Akbar, Dimas Pristovani Riananda, Wibowo Arninputranto, Aulia Rahma Annisa, Mirza Ardiana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This research presents the implementation of the Decision Tree C4.5 algorithm for optimizing mosque lighting control at Mosque. The system utilizes prayer schedules and motion sensor inputs to determine lighting activation, processed by a microcontroller unit and transmitted to a PLC for execution. Simulation results show that the proposed system achieves a 95% success rate in decision-making accuracy, with an average system response time of 42.6 ms. Additionally, energy efficiency analysis indicates a 28.2% reduction in power consumption, demonstrating the system’s capability in reducing unnecessary energy usage while ensuring proper illumination. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in enhancing automation and efficiency. Future improvements could include incorporating additional environmental parameters for more adaptive and intelligent lighting control.

title: Improvement of Leakage Current of Short Channel Effect in FinFET
authors: Amit Singh, Tilak Kumar Saxena, Shyam Bahadur Tiwari
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: A fresh application of FinFET MOSFET technology is paving the way for advancements in the nano era. The simulated results provide insights into the scalability of the FinFET structure as it dips below the 10 nm mark. The ultra-thin fin formulation effectively mitigates short channel effects. FinFET stands out as a worthy to the traditional single gate MOSFET, thanks to its good properties like enhanced electrostatics and improved manufacturability. While boosting circuit performance is essential, cost considerations also play a crucial role. FinFET-based MOSFETs are not only cost-effective but also exhibit low leakage currents. The leakage current has been significantly reduced, and the roll-off is well managed due to DIBL.

title: An Autonomous Agentic Framework for Interactive Code Mentoring and Error Resolution using Large Language Models
authors: Seetaram Rao Rayarao, Nilesh Dnyaneshwar Bhandarwar, Rahul Dattangire, Sudhakar Vuriti
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Revolutionary advances in large language models have ushered in the age of intelligent agents. The development of artificial general intelligence may hinge on Large Language Model (LLM) agents, which are capable of goal-driven behavior and dynamic adaptability. The purpose of this study is to bridge this gap with an autonomous Agentic framework for interactive code mentoring and bug fixing powered by LLMs, specifically GPT-4o-mini and built on the MERN stack. The software platform enables authenticated users to pose code-specific issues in a structured, prompt-response manner and receive an intelligent response, while logging all their interactions, including prompt content, response time, and token utilization. During testing, the system processed over 150 user prompts, with 71% of responses categorized as slow (above 1000 ms), 29% moderate (500-1000 ms), and none classified as fast (below 500 ms). Token analysis showed an average usage of 480 total tokens per prompt, with notable spikes at prompts #3 and #5. The platform comes equipped with a simple user interface, history management of sorts, and an admin tool to visualize data captured at the prompt level. The Agentic framework and development may not only assist individual learning but may produce organized information to analyze their beliefs and morals.

title: Predictive Biomarkers
authors: Sayed Reza Ramezani, Afsaneh Mojra
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Cancer, as one of the most complex and multifactorial diseases, remains a major challenge in modern medicine. Progress in therapeutic approaches requires a deeper understanding of cellular behavior and the influence of various factors on cell dynamics. In this context, the mechanical properties of the cellular microenvironment play a critical role in identifying the behavioral patterns of cancer cells and in designing novel therapeutic strategies. The findings of this study demonstrate a strong correlation between biological responses, cellular mechanical behavior, and the predictions obtained through mathematical modeling using the Kelvin-Voigt Fractional Derivative model. This correlation highlights the potential of employing mathematical models based on mechanical parameters to predict the outcomes of biological tests before their actual implementation. Furthermore, such modeling approaches allow the estimation of anticancer drug efficacy on cells in advance of experimental validation. Consequently, integrating mechanical insights with mathematical modeling can significantly reduce the cost and time associated with biological testing while improving the accuracy of designing targeted therapies. These results underline the importance of interdisciplinary strategies that combine biology and engineering, opening new horizons for the development of personalized cancer.

title: A Monitoring Method Based on EEG Data and Deep Learning
authors: Yijie Yang, Chongming Mu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: In the context of modern education, the pursuit of effective teaching and learning has been constantly evolving. With the increasing emphasis on personalized education and the need to understand students' cognitive processes in real-time, the limitations of traditional teaching assessment methods have become more prominent. This paper presents a monitoring method for real-time assessment of students' classroom understanding based on EEG data and deep learning. By addressing the limitations of traditional teaching assessment methods, this approach aims to provide educators with more accurate and timely feedback. The proposed method utilizes specific EEG data, advanced data processing techniques, and deep learning algorithms to achieve high-accuracy monitoring.

title: Compact Multiband Adjustable and Gain-Enhanced Microstrip Antennas
authors: Karima Bencherif, Mohammed Titaouine, Awatef Djouimaa, Asma Bounouara, Ibtissem Adoui
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: In this paper, a novel compact multiband microstrip antenna is proposed for the C and X-band applications. The antenna has the characteristics of having a simple structure with small dimensions of 20 × 20 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, printed on an FR4 substrate with a dielectric constant of 4.4 and a thickness of 1.6 mm. The antenna operates at two resonant frequencies around 6.3 GHz and 9.36 GHz, with corresponding bandwidths of 0.3384GHz and 0.3225GHz respectively defined at -10 dB reflection coefficient. The insertion of strips filled with PIN diodes leads to a shift in the first resonant frequency to 4.69 GHz or 5.87 GHz, and the second to 8.75 GHz or 9.08 GHz. The original resonances can be restored when all the diodes are turned ON. To enhance the antenna gain, a metallic plane is inserted beneath the planar antenna to reflect the radiated electromagnetic energy. This reflection enhances the antenna's radiation efficiency, resulting in a gain increase from 5.18 dB to 6.454 dB. Finally, to introduce an additional resonance frequency, a strip is placed vertically near the antenna patch. The antenna's design, analysis, and optimization were performed using the High Frequency Structure Simulator (HFSS) software, which is based on Finite Element Method (FEM) numerical method.

title: Optimization method for power supply restoration under flood disasters
authors: Dexiang Li, Ling Li, Tianlu Luo, Milu Zhou, Shanpeng Huang
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: In the face of power outages caused by typhoons and floods in isolated distribution networks, this paper proposes an optimized method for power supply assurance and restoration in such networks, which includes distributed power sources and energy storage. By reconstructing the distribution network, the method takes into account the recovery of critical loads and is based on an improved binary particle swarm optimization algorithm. A model of typhoon and flood disasters is constructed, and post-disaster fault scenarios are simulated using Monte Carlo sampling. Considering the output of wind-drivens, the goal is to maximize load recovery and minimize outage losses. The method optimizes the regulation of energy storage and controllable loads, proposing a power supply assurance and restoration optimization model. The reconstruction model is simplified using second-order cone relaxation techniques. Finally, using an improved IEEE 33 node isolated distribution network as a case study, the advantages of this method in mitigating disaster impacts, enhancing power supply reliability, and reducing losses are verified through simulation comparisons.

title: Research of Station Area Line Loss Anomaly Detection Based on LSTM-VAE and Microservices
authors: Hongwei Pan, Min Zhu, Duliang Lv, Ruixiang Xu, Junling Wan, Peisen Yuan
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional station line loss management, such as high false alarm rate of fixed threshold method, dependence of supervised model on labeled data, and the existing system architecture cannot support concurrent analysis across tens of thousands of station areas, this paper designed and implemented an intelligent anomaly detection system for line loss based on unsupervised learning and microservice architecture. First, we propose a hybrid model integrating LSTM and VAE to learn normal patterns from massive unlabeled line loss data. The system identifies anomalies by leveraging reconstruction errors in conjunction with dynamic quantile-based thresholds, which significantly enhances detection accuracy. Second, a microservices cluster built upon Spring Cloud and Kubernetes enables elastic scaling and high-concurrency processing. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieves an Accuracy of 0.9849, Precision of 0.9853, Recall of 0.9529, and F1-score of 0.9691, effectively capturing the coupling patterns between line loss anomalies and load fluctuations. Furthermore, the system integrates anomaly monitoring, log recording, and governance maintenance into a closed-loop management workflow, providing power utilities with a lightweight and scalable solution for line loss management.

title: Benchmarking Generative Models for Traffic Scene Image Synthesis A Comparative Study
authors: Yujun Geng, Lei Chen, Bo Qiu, Yunjie Wang
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Automated traffic scene generation is vital for autonomous driving and urban planning. While advanced generative models excel in general image synthesis, their performance in traffic-specific domains with strict rules and constraints remains underexplored. Off-the-shelf models exhibit critical flaws, with rule violations exceeding 30% in certain scenarios and notable physical inconsistencies in safety-critical contexts. This study benchmarks top generative models (SDXL, DALL•E 3, Midjourney, ERNIE-ViLG, Doubao) for text-to-traffic-image synthesis, evaluating semantic fidelity, traffic compliance, and physical realism through a novel comparative framework that integrates FID/CLIP metrics with expert-annotated safety criteria. The findings offer practical insights into model selection and reveal substantial gaps in rule comprehension among current models.

title: Leveraging Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Cybersecurity Policy Optimization in AWS-Based IoT Cloud Infrastructures
authors: Sumanth Bontha, Kiran Kumar Reddy, Tejas Vepala, Sharath Reddy Gade, Narendra Balaji Medikonda, Pell Reddy Rajender Reddy
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The use of reinforcement learning techniques for cybersecurity policy optimisation is examined in this research article. Because cyber-attacks are becoming more sophisticated and complex, traditional rule-based techniques are unable to properly adjust to changing attack strategies. By enabling an autonomous agent to discover the best cybersecurity strategies through trial-and-error interactions with its surroundings, reinforcement learning presents a prospective answer. The dataset used in this research was created using real-time attack simulations and security logs. In order to provide RL models for anomaly detection and dynamic security enforcement, the component includes both benign and malevolent traffic patterns. The Greedy Epsilon reinforcement learning algorithm serves as the foundation for our suggested AEGRL method. The RL models are implemented using Open Al Gym, TensorFlow/PyTorch, and Python. The models were tested against a variety of attack scenarios, including SQL Injection Attack, Denial-of-Service (DoS), and Man-in-the-Middle (MitM). They were also contrasted with earlier RL-based research and conventional security measures.

title: A 164-GBP 87-101 GHz Low-Noise Amplifier in 40-nm CMOS for Broadband Applications
authors: Wei-Chih Chang, Zih-Han Jian, Po-Yuan Chen, Po-Lei Lee, Hong-Yeh Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE 26th Topical Meeting on Silicon Monolithic Integrated Circuits in RF Systems (SiRF)
abstract: This paper presents a W-band multi-stage low-noise amplifier (LNA) with high gain and low de power consumption for broadband applications, implemented in a 40-nm CMOS process. To reduce noise figure and improve circuit stability, source degeneration inductors combined with transmission lines are employed in the first stage. The proposed LNA achieves a minimum measured noise figure of 7 dB. It demonstrates a peak small-signal gain of 21.6 dB at 91.5 GHz and a 3-dB bandwidth of 13.6 GHz, covering the frequency range from 87 to 100.6 GHz. The resulting gain-bandwidth product (GBP) is 164 GHz. The dc power consumption is 68 mW with a supply voltage of 1.2 V. Furthermore, the exhibits an input 1-dB compression point <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{IP}_{\mathrm{l}\mathrm{d}\mathrm{B}})$</tex> of -17.5 dBm and an input third-order intercept point (IIP3) of - 12.5 dBm at 91.5 GHz.

title: Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on Acoustic Vibration Signals and Deep Learning
authors: Zhihao Hu, Jiaming Han, Fang Ting, Xianzhong Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: In industrial equipment, rolling bearings serve as critical rotating components whose health status directly impacts the safety and reliability of mechanical systems. However, in complex industrial environments, factors such as strong noise interference and insufficient single-sensor information pose challenges for capturing fault characteristics. To fully leverage the potential of multi-source information, this paper proposes a feature-level fusion fault diagnosis method based on acoustic-vibration signals and a TCN-Attention network. This method first inputs acoustic and vibration signals into parallel Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN) to extract multi-scale temporal features. Subsequently, the dual-branch features are concatenated in the channel dimension, and a Channel Attention module is introduced to perform adaptive weight allocation. This approach highlights key modal features, suppresses redundant information, and further enhances deep-layer complementarity. Using the University of Ottawa acoustic-vibration bearing dataset for validation, experimental results demonstrate that the proposed acoustic-vibration fusion model achieves superior fault recognition accuracy and robustness compared to single-modality diagnostic methods.

title: Monte Carlo Assisted Analysis of $2 \times 4$ Low-Power Decoder Implementations in 45 nm CMOS Technology
authors: Katyayani Hukeri, Arpita Patel, Pallavi Darji, Tejas Darji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Decoders are fundamental digital building blocks used in memory addressing, instruction decoding, and data routing, where efficiency in power, delay, and area is critical. This paper presents a comparative analysis of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 4$</tex> decoder implementations using three logic styles-Static CMOS (28T), Transmission Gate Logic (TGL, 20T), and Pass Transistor Logic (PTL, 12T)-are analyzed with GPDK 45 nm CMOS technology. The designs were simulated in Cadence Virtuoso at a supply voltage of 0.8 V with a load capacitance of 1 fF. Input signals were applied with periods of 20 ns (A0) and 40 ns (A1) to exercise all output states. Performance was evaluated in terms of power consumption, propagation delay, power-delay product (PDP), and transistor count, while Monte Carlo simulations were carried out to assess robustness under process-voltagetemperature (PVT) variations. Results demonstrate that PTL achieves the lowest power and PDP due to its reduced transistor count, whereas TGL provides a favorable balance between efficiency and voltage swing restoration. Static CMOS consumes higher power but remains the most robust under variability. This analysis highlights critical trade-offs among logic styles and provides design guidelines for low-power and area-optimized decoder architectures at deep-submicron technology nodes.

title: Blockchain in Education: Leveraging Blockchain for Immutable Certifications
authors: Vedant Patel, Vraj Patel, Vidisha Pradan, Jigar Sarda, Avani Khokhariya, Muskan Dave
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: In today's digital world, validating education credentials is important as traditional means of record management and validation represents slower, more expensive, and easily fraudulent methods of accessing credentials. This paper proposes a security, process-efficient, and user-friendly blockchain-enabled method for issuing and verifying educational certificates. Our method utilizes smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain and utilizes MetaMask to streamline and enhance our system with an authentication layer. Under our proposed model, institutions can mint certificates in bulk requests, choose to revoke them, and easily allow users to verify their certificate without any other third-party. Academic learners can also have more power over what data they share. This proposed model aims to alleviate much of the burden on institutions, reduce testing time for employers in verifying certificates, and importantly give learners the strong, well-designed, proof-notampering evidence of achievement. Some prominent institutions have already begun the process of blockchain-based integration into their credentialing processes. A prime example of this is the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), which offers blockchain-based digital diplomas enabling graduates to securely store and share their credentials. Likewise, other universities and online learning platforms are beginning to explore blockchainenabled approaches.

title: Research on the Digital Preservation of Shadow-Puppet Character Styling Based on a Low-Rank Adaptation Model: A Case Study of Shadow Puppetry in Haining, Zhejiang
authors: Xuefang Zhang, Yiran Cheng
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: This study examines the distinctive character designs and visual semantics of Haining shadow puppetry and explores a generative AI-based approach to stylized image creation. By developing a low-rank adaptation model tailored to shadow-puppet character styles, this study demonstrates the feasibility of achieving high-fidelity character transfer with limited training samples. It further identifies image quality and semantic annotation as critical determinants of generative performance. The findings offer both a technical pathway and a theoretical reference for the digital preservation and innovative development of traditional shadow-puppet art.

title: Mapping the Blockchain Landscape: A Systematic Review of Frameworks, Consensus Models, and Research Directions
authors: Oshima Sharma, Naveen Kumar, Shilpi Garg, Smriti Sachan
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Blockchain is a revolutionary technology now a days. It has opened various platforms for research too. Blockchain is decentralized distributed network system with no central authorization. Because of its inherent features it has been used in various applications. Features are immutability, anonymity, transparency, decentralized etc. This study gives the basic idea about blockchain technology. The study also investigates consensus mechanisms to add transactions in the network. This study gives an idea about what the various frameworks are and how consensus protocols work under various frameworks. Bitcoin, Ethereum are the common frameworks used in blockchain technology. Proof of Work (PoF) and Proof of Stake (PoS) are the commonly used consensus types in blockchain. The analysis includes considerations about blockchain process, types of blockchain, layered architecture of blockchain, consensus protocols, blockchain framework, applications of blockchain. This study emphasizes the need for better scalability, security and integrity options. In future, Blockchain technology has the potential to integrate with many technologies like Artificial Intelligence (AI), Cloud Computing, Internet of Things (IoT) etc.

title: Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Resilient and Trustworthy V2X Communications
authors: XiaoYu Zhou, Raj Sonani, Noman Mazher, Zillay Huma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Vehicular-to-everything (V2X) communications are vital part of transportation systems that enable the exchange of information between vehicles, infrastructure, pedestrians, and networks. This system pays prime role for road safety and cooperative driving, but it also suffers from problems such as cyber-attacks, fake data, and lack of reliable information. Artificial Intelligence (AI) provides methods (anomaly detection, adaptive defense, trust management) to fill this gap. This study highlights a hybrid edge-cloud framework that enables lightweight intrusion detection modules on vehicles and roadside side units as well as improved privacy-preserving models through federated updates. Key points of research include taxonomy between AI techniques and V2X threats, an integrated trust story mechanism, and a comprehensive evaluation framework based on accuracy, latency, and robustness.

title: Aligning Large Vision-Language Models by Deep Reinforcement Learning and Direct Preference Optimization
authors: Thanh Thi Nguyen, Campbell Wilson, Janis Dalins
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) or multi-modal large language models represent a significant advancement in artificial intelligence, enabling systems to understand and generate content across both visual and textual modalities. While large-scale pretraining has driven substantial progress, fine-tuning these models for aligning with human values or engaging in specific tasks or behaviors remains a critical challenge. Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) offer promising frameworks for this aligning process. While DRL enables models to optimize actions using reward signals instead of relying solely on supervised preference data, DPO directly aligns the policy with preferences, eliminating the need for an explicit reward model. This overview explores paradigms for fine-tuning LVLMs, highlighting how DRL and DPO techniques can be used to align models with human preferences and values, improve task performance, and enable adaptive multimodal interaction. We categorize key approaches, examine sources of preference data, reward signals, and discuss open challenges such as scalability, sample efficiency, continual learning, generalization, and safety. The goal is to provide a clear understanding of how DRL and DPO contribute to the evolution of robust and human-aligned LVLMs.

title: Research on Abnormal Detection of Rolling Bearings Based on Kolmogorov–Arnold Network
authors: Mengran Xie, Xinchun Ma, Fangqi Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Rolling bearing faults are typically manifested through vibration signals, but traditional detection algorithms face two main issues: first, they struggle to effectively distinguish spectral characteristics in non-stationary or multi-frequency signals; second, they have poor generalization performance when dealing with complex fault modes. To address these problems, this paper proposes an industrial equipment anomaly detection method based on the integration of KAN network (KolmogorovArnold) and Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT). First, the bearing signals are processed using STFT to extract timefrequency domain features and distinguish spectral characteristics. Then, the KAN network captures the complex features in the signals through nonlinear mapping, improving fault recognition. Experimental results show that on the CWRU and PU datasets, the accuracy reaches $99.87 \%$ and $96.38 \%$, respectively, outperforming traditional methods and demonstrating stronger generalization ability.

title: Cloud-Enabled LSTM-Based Framework for Real-Time Diabetes Risk Prediction Using MIMIC-III Clinical Data
authors: Sampada Bharara, Shubh Pradhan, Prakash T. Raut, Sadikshya Bhattarai, Arya Jagdale, Abhinav Pathak
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is one of the most important health disorders worldwide that is becoming more and more common and leading to various life-threatening complications. The situation calls for early prediction. Traditional methods like Logistic Regression and Random Forest were reported to have an accuracy of about 89%, but they cannot detect time- dependent features in electronic health records (EHRs). Due to this limitation, a Cloud-Enabled Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model has been created, using the MIMIC-III dataset. LSTM is a unit that effectively handles the sequential nature of data, and cloud integration is scalable, interoperable, and can support real-time analysis. The results of the experiments show that the performance metrics have been improved to 93.7% accuracy, 93.1% precision, 94.4%recall, 93.7% F1- score, and 0.96 AUC, which are higher than those of the traditional methods. The method introduces LSTM with cloud integration as a scalable and feasible solution for better chronic disease management and precision medicine.

title: A Review of Automatic Speech Recognition Approaches for Bridging Communication Gaps in Clinical Handover
authors: Sasikala D, Siva Sathya S, Baghavathi Priya S, Niranjan Kumar D, Vignesh S
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Communicating patient information during nursing handover is a critical and vital task for ensuring patient safety by maintaining continuity and quality of care. However, communication gaps and documentation inefficiencies during this process can result in medical errors, treatment delays, and adverse outcomes that directly affect both patients and healthcare providers. In many medical settings, automatic speech recognition (ASR) is used in real-time for documentation, which reduces information loss and eases the cognitive burden on healthcare professionals. This survey paper focus on recent developments in clinical ASR, highlighting the role of pre-trained ASR with fine-tuning on clinical data to improve transcription accuracy. The paper also investigates the challenges of clinical speech data availability, the use of synthetic text and audio to address privacy and data scarcity. The paper discusses in detail the usage of deep learning techniques for clinical ASR and the integration of large language models for error correction. Further, the paper also discusses the various metrics for the assessment of the models. This work provides a comprehensive overview of the current landscape and identifies the research directions for developing safe, reliable, and clinically aligned ASR systems for clinical handovers.

title: Augmentation-Free Contrastive Learning for Few-Shot Hindi Fake News Detection
authors: Sherin Mary Rajee, Amit Praseed
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: The proliferation of fake news has increased substantially in the post-pandemic era. As a major threat to society as a whole, there have been considerable research efforts into identifying and limiting the dissemination of fake news, particularly using transformer based Large Language Models (LLMs). However, the efficiency of these models for detecting fake news on regional languages or dialects continues to be sub-par, particularly due to the lack of pre-training and fine-tuning datasets in resource-constrained languages like Hindi. Transformer models are also particularly data-intensive, and necessitate substantially large datasets for fine-tuning to perform well. There is a urgent requirement for efficient Hindi fake news detection mechanisms which can work in few-shot settings, with limited training data. In this work, a contrastive learning approach is proposed for detecting Hindi fake news. Contrary to existing contrastive learning approaches in literature, the proposed approach works exclusively on text data, and does not require supporting data from other modalities. Additionally, the proposed approach does not use data augmentation unlike other frameworks. Experiments indicate that the proposed approach can outperform transformer based models in few-shot settings, and thus, serves as an effective approach for fake news detection in Hindi and other resource-constrained languages.

title: Improved Detection and Prevention of Phishing URLs Using Ensemble Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Tomiwa Kunle Oluwadare, Pantaleon Lutta
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Software Defined Systems (SDS)
abstract: Phishing is a cyberattack that uses deceptive emails, websites, or messages to trick individuals into revealing sensitive information like passwords or financial details. It often involves imitating trusted entities to exploit user trust. Researchers have tackled phishing by developing machine learning and deep learning algorithms to analyse patterns in URLs, emails, and website features. Recent advancements include using ensemble methods to enhance detection accuracy and efficiency. However, the literature reveals a gap in effectively combining ensemble learning techniques to overcome individual model limitations. The goal of this research is to enhance the detection of phishing URLs by harnessing the strength of ensemble machine learning. To combat this, the proposed solution involves using a dataset with phishing URLs from a reliable UCI dataset repository, consisting of 134,850 legitimate URLs and 100,945 phishing URLs. It employs multiple machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, Multilayer Perceptron, and Gradient Boosting Machine, each handling different aspects of phishing detection such as URL-based, HTML-based, and derived features respectively. By combining these models through an ensemble approach, the system can mitigate the weaknesses of individual algorithms and improve predictive performance. Key features such as URL length, the presence of HTTPS, the number of subdomains, and 45 other features were extracted for analysis making it total of 47 features after data pre-processing. The results show that the ensemble model outperformed traditional methods, achieving a high level of 99.6% accuracy in detecting phishing URLs.

title: Load Resilient Double Balanced Sequential Power Amplifier for MIMO Transceivers
authors: Michael Loose, Alexander Deublein, Christian Musolff, Norman Franchi, Georg Fischer
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: This work presents a power amplifier (PA) demonstrator targeting circulatorless base stations to facilitate dense MIMO integration. Implemented using a novel RF GaN-on-Si technology developed by Infineon, the demonstrator addresses the inherent mismatch resilience required in such frontends, which conventional Doherty power amplifier (DPA) or load modulated balanced amplifier (LMBA) lack due to their mismatch phase sensitivity. The double balanced sequential amplifier (DBSA), also referred to as PD-LMDBA, was selected for its superior mismatch resilience. A design technique is presented, which makes use of non-mutual load modulation, which enables a sequential design flow: First the main branch is optimized independently, subsequently used for nonlinear analysis of the auxiliary branch under unidirectional load modulation. A hardware demonstrator covering the 5G NR n104 band (6.425 to 7.125 GHz) was implemented using a novel RF GaN-on-Si technology developed by Infineon and verified via pulsed CW measurements. At 6.8 GHz, 68.7% drain efficiency and 42dBm output power were achieved at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P_{2\mathrm{d}\mathrm{B}}$</tex>. The average efficiency under a clipped OFDM waveform with 8 dB PAPR is estimated to 42%.

title: Replicating Extreme Maneuvers of Anna’s Hummingbirds Using a Small Indoor Quadrotor: A Simulation and Proof-of-Concept Approach
authors: Puthalapattu Sukanya
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This research investigates the capability of a small indoor quadrotor (Crazyflie 2.1) to autonomously execute extreme aerial maneuvers inspired by the courtship display dives of Anna’s Hummingbirds (Calypte anna). Utilizing a multi-camera motion tracking system (OptiTrack), the study aims to replicate these high-speed, high-acceleration dives through precise trajectory tracking and control. The primary objective is to assess the quadrotor’s ability to reproduce these maneuvers by minimizing the time-scaled root mean square error (RMSE) between the hummingbird and quadrotor trajectories to less than 10 cm, with a standard deviation of 5 cm. The research explores the engineering challenges associated with replicating such complex movements, considering factors like speed, acceleration, and physical constraints. By leveraging simulation tools (Matlab and Simulink) and subsequent proof-of-concept demonstrations, the study seeks to advance the field of unmanned aerial systems (UAS) by improving their maneuverability and understanding the biomechanical limits of extreme aerial maneuvers. Potential applications include enhanced autonomy in navigation, obstacle avoidance, and behavioral playback studies for biological research.

title: Image-Based Detection and Classification of Skin Ulcers Using Deep Learning
authors: Patatri Acharya, Sudipta Acharya
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Automated detection and classification of skin ulcers is crucial in supporting clinical diagnosis and treatment, especially in resource-constrained environments. This study proposes a deep learning approach for the multi-class classification of three clinically significant skin ulcer types - arterial ulcer, venous ulcer, and neurotrophic ulcer - using photographic images, a task not previously addressed in the literature. Individuals are first classified as either having skin ulcer or having normal skin (non-disorder) state. If any kind of skin ulcer is detected, the model further classifies the condition into one of three abovementioned skin ulcer classes. A dataset with 187 skin ulcer images was obtained from the Kaggle website. Data preprocessing was done by rescaling the images, thus normalizing the pixel values. Data augmentation was applied to expand both the size and diversity of the training dataset. Three pre-trained CNN-based architectures - VGG16, VGG19 and ResNet50 - were applied to the preprocessed and augmented image dataset through transfer learning approach. Experiments were carried out using an 80:20 train–test split. Among the models, VGG16 achieved the highest accuracy of 91.43%, followed by ResNet50 with 88.57%. VGG19 showed the lowest performance, reaching an accuracy of 85.71%. These findings highlight the capability of CNN-based deep learning models to support clinical diagnosis and decision-making.

title: Electrospun Chitosan-Gelatin/ZIF-8 Nanofibers Scaffolds for Enhanced Wound Healing
authors: Maryam Nosrati Hashi, Maryam Tajabadi, Fateme Mirzajani, Alireza Khavandi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Wound healing poses a major public health challenge, exacerbated by the growing burden of chronic diseases and aging populations. Electrospun nanofibrous dressings offer unique advantages enhanced surface-to-volume ratio, controllable porosity, and the potential to administer bioactive compounds to accelerate tissue repair. Herein, we describe the preparation and characterization of chitosan-derived nanofibers incorporating zinc imidazolate framework 8 (ZIF-8) nanoparticles to improve antibacterial activity. ZIF-8 particles were synthesized via coordination of zinc nitrate with 2 methylimidazole, yielding uniform nanoparticles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$138 \pm 34 \text{nm}$</tex>). Subsequent electrospinning under optimized conditions produced smooth, bead free nanofibers averaging <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$213 \pm 31 \text{nm}$</tex> in diameter. Structural and compositional analyses SEM, FTIR, EDS, and elemental mapping confirmed nanoparticle incorporation without compromising fiber morphology. Antibacterial assay revealed 90 % inhibition of Escherichia coli and 10 % inhibition of Staphylococcus aureus. Collectively, the present data endorse the suitability of chitosan/ZIF 8 electrospun mats as materials for wound therapy with both infection-preventing and healing-promoting properties.

title: An Extensive Machine Learning Approach for Crop Prediction
authors: Ratan Lal, Saivivek Reddy Kusukuntla, Kamalini Pradhan, Prasad Chetti, Chandra Mouli Madhav Kotteti
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: In this paper, we consider the problem of finding a suitable crop to maximize the crop yield based on soil attributes analysis. Our broad approach is based on machine learning techniques. First, we use feature selection methods to identify a subset of soil attributes that are most relevant to the models. Subsequently, we train machine learning models both with and without feature selection techniques. Our experimental results suggest that the Bagging model consistently outperforms the other models in terms of accuracy regardless of whether feature selection is employed. Furthermore, when using feature selection, Recursive Feature Elimination enhances the performance of the Bagging model compared to the other feature selection methods. The Bagging model's accuracy for both Soil and Environmental characteristics is 9 0. 6 2% before applying feature selection and improves to 92.72% after applying Recursive Feature Selection.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Model for Fungal Disease Detection in Apple Plants Using Textural Feature Analysis
authors: Shivani Gupta, Pooja Rani
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Infections in apple leaves are often caused by unexpected weather conditions such as rain, hailstorms, droughts, and fog. This results in significant productivity losses for farmers. Early detection of the disease in the apple crop can therefore prevent the disease, and it can help to avoid the economic losses caused by it. Plant disease detection includes several steps, including pre-processing, segmentation, extracting the relevant features, and classification. The feature selection algorithms are broadly classified into colour and textural features. In the previous year’s various classification models are proposed for plant disease prediction. The traditional classification models are unable to achieve good accuracy, due to which a hybrid classification model is proposed. The proposed model is developed using Python, and its performance is evaluated based on accuracy, precision, and recall.

title: Heel-Raising Motion Using OpenSim-MATLAB
authors: Mohammad-Reza Sayyed Noorani, Roghaiyeh Ahmadian Sarand, Nakisa Farshforoush
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: In this study, we address muscle-driven simulation for heel-raising motion and estimate the activation of the soleus muscle using a fuzzy inference system (FIS). Surface electromyography (sEMG) signals of the major shank muscles are recorded experimentally, while optical markers attached to anatomical landmarks on the leg are simultaneously tracked using a conventional camera to extract ankle joint kinematics via the Kinovea motion analysis software. Two separate FISs are designed in MATLAB: one based on ankle joint kinematic data and the other on sEMG signals of agonist-antagonist muscles. Both systems were able to estimate the soleus muscle activation. Next, the movement is simulated based on the sEMG signals of four muscles (medial and lateral gastrocnemius, soleus, and tibialis anterior), using the Forward Dynamics tool of OpenSim, with the ankle plantarflexion angle as the output. The error between the maximum experimental and simulated angles serves as the evaluation metric. Although both FISs successfully estimate soleus activation, the EMG-based FIS demonstrates a more physiologically plausible signal pattern and marginally superior performance. It should be noted that without stimulating the soleus, the simulated movement fails to execute correctly.

title: HEALTH: Hyperbolic Embedding and Acoustic-based Learning for Topological Hierarchies in Parkinson's Disease
authors: Saghar Shafaati, S. Hossein Erfani
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disorder with heterogeneous motor and non-motor features that complicate early diagnosis. Acoustic biomarkers have emerged as non-invasive predictors, yet classical models often fail to capture the hierarchical nature of progression. We introduce HEALTH (Hyperbolic Embedding and Acoustic-based Learning for Topological Hierarchies), a framework that integrates graph-based similarity modeling, hyperbolic geometry, clustering, and explainable classification to analyze dysarthric speech in PD. Sustained phonation recordings were embedded in a two-dimensional Poincaré disk, where hyperbolic distances reflected latent acoustic dissimilarities. Optimization reduced reconstruction loss by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>, and silhouette coefficients stabilized near 0.44, indicating robust cluster separation. SHAP analysis highlighted pitch entropy, amplitude variability, and frequency measures as key determinants, enhancing clinical interpretability. Compared to Euclidean approaches, HEALTH achieved superior stratification and explainability. These findings demonstrate the potential of hyperbolic embeddings as scalable, interpretable tools for non-invasive monitoring and precision diagnostics in PD.

title: Coordination of SVC and SWC Control Systems to Enhancing Power Grid Stability During Disturbances Caused by Electric Arc Furnace
authors: Marwan Rosyadi, Syamsir Abduh, Radpandji Edy Widjaja, Ignatius Rendroyoko, Ibnu Hajar, Bayu Aji Soedibyo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Electric Arc Furnaces (EAFs) are widely used in industrial processes, but they pose significant challenges to power system stability due to their nonlinear, unbalanced characteristics and rapidly fluctuating power consumption. These fluctuations can cause voltage sags, frequency deviations, and overall power quality degradation. This study proposes a coordinated control approach between a Static Var Compensator (SVC) and a Static Watt Compensator (SWC) to mitigate the negative impacts of EAF operation. The SVC regulates reactive power and stabilizes voltage, while the SWC compensates for active power fluctuations to maintain frequency stability. Through this coordinated control strategy, these two devices are not only able to stabilize the system against dynamic disturbances but are also effective in addressing the reactive and active power unbalances generated by EAF loads. Simulation results show that this approach significantly improves the voltage and frequency profiles during EAF operation, thereby strengthening the overall reliability and resilience of the power system.

title: Optimization of Braking Parameters for High-Power Wind Power Disc Brake Based on Response Surface Methodology
authors: Jie Hao, Kunzhu Han, Xin Li, Xueren Wang, Meiyu Guo, Xiaowei Yin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: The disc brake of wind turbine is a key device to ensure the safe operation and stable braking of wind turbine generator sets. However, under the braking condition of high-speed and heavy-load, the poor braking stability will seriously affect the braking performance. A rigid-flexible coupling dynamic model of the wind turbine brake is established and its accuracy is verified through the inertia test bench of the wind turbine brake. According to the braking stability determination method of the wind turbine brake, the relationship between braking speed, braking force, friction coefficient and braking stability is analyzed. Based on the response surface method, the optimized braking parameter combinations under different initial speeds are obtained. The accuracy and rationality of the optimized parameters are verified by simulation.

title: Lung Cancer Cell Detection Using YOLOv8: A Deep Learning Approach for Medical Imaging
authors: Prince Kumar Yadav, Mohammad Ashif Iqbal, Wasiullah Aman, Javed Ahmad
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Lung cancer remains a leading cause of cancerrelated mortality globally, necessitating the development of fast and accurate diagnostic systems. In this work, we propose an advanced lung cancer cell detection framework using the YOLOv8 object detection algorithm. Our model is trained on a Kaggle dataset comprising 12,000 annotated medical images, including CT and histopathology scans. Preprocessing techniques such as image resizing, augmentation, and noise reduction were applied to enhance model robustness. The trained YOLOv8 model achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>, significantly outperforming over 50 previously published models in accuracy and inference speed. These results demonstrate the superiority of YOLOv8 in real-time cancer detection tasks and its potential for integration into clinical decision support systems.

title: Application of Machine Learning on Diabetic Prediction for Pregnant Women
authors: Sitakanta Parida, Rashmita Mohanty, Rekha Sahu, Bikram Keshari Mishra
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Diabetes is a persistent medical condition attributed to insufficient production of insulin. When diabetes is not managed properly, variety of complications are shown in the human body like cardiovascular disease, foot ulcers, nerve damage, etc. Diabetes in pregnancy can occur in two main forms gestational diabetes and pre-existing diabetes. Both must be closely monitored to safeguard the health of both maternal and infant. If this diabetes mellitus is not well controlled, birth defect, miscarriage or stillbirth, preterm birth etc. are shown and so, early detection is needed. A survey is performed on prediction of diabetics using Machine Learning. To get an appropriate model, fourteen traditional machine learning approaches are implemented on a dataset of pregnant females and compare their performance to find a better model in predicting diabetic for the pregnant women. In conclusion, the logistic regression has given better performances, but not a satisfactory performer for which more experiments are required.

title: A Novel Approach to Image Steganography in Spatial Domain: Using Pixel Value Differencing, Autoencoder, and Custom D-CNN with Data
authors: Dipanshu, Gureshpal Singh
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Image steganography is an imperative security application in modern digital communication, enabling the concealment of sensitive information within images in a way that prevents detection by unintended recipients. This paper proposes a robust image steganography detection framework integrating Pixel Value Differencing (PVD), an autoencoder-based feature extractor, and a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (D-CNN) classifier. The PVD technique is employed for data embedding, an autoencoder learns compact feature representations of both clean and stego images, enhancing discriminative power for classification, and D-CNN exploits these features to achieve precise detection. Embedding quality of PVD is evaluated using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM), achieving mean values of 27.70 dB and 0.9027, respectively, indicating high visual similarity despite PSNR values below the conventional 30 dB threshold. The proposed detection system attains 99.85% validation accuracy, with perfect precision, recall, and F1-scores for both classes. Experimental results confirm that the combination of PVD embedding, autoencoder-driven feature learning, and DCNN classification delivers a high-performance, and imperceptible, steganography detection approach suitable for real-world applications in secure communication and digital forensics.

title: A Hybrid Mobile System for Indoor Navigation and Product Localization in Supermarkets
authors: Leonardo Bazán Picinin, Luisa Jorge, Paulo Melo
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and evaluation of an integrated mobile system to support product localization and indoor navigation within supermarkets. Using augmented reality for real-time visual guidance, computer vision for shelf-level recognition, and QR-code-based markers for accurate spatial initialization, the system bridges the gap between virtual and real store environments. The solution utilizes a React Native frontend and a backend built with FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and PyTorch. Experimental results validate its technical viability, demonstrating world alignment and shelf detection, while addressing BLE signal instability and image processing latency. The proposed architecture proposes a foundation for retail navigation systems, aimed at delivering scalable, accurate, and user-friendly in-store experiences.

title: A Comprehensive Security Framework for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) in Multi-Agent AI Systems
authors: Om Narayan, Ramkinker Singh, Praveen Baskar
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables seamless AI agent-tool interactions but introduces distinct security challenges such as prompt injection, impersonation, and supply chain vulnerabilities. These risks are amplified by the autonomous and complex reasoning of GenAI agents, necessitating a robust threat model. Prior research has tackled some of these issues through prompt filtering, architectural isolation, authentication, secure installation, monitoring, and defensive agent design patterns. However, critical gaps persist in state consistency, multi-tenant isolation, context integrity, and comprehensive authentication/authorization within dynamic MCP environments. As MCP-based multi-agent systems become integral to enterprise operations, they present unique security considerations. This paper introduces a comprehensive security framework addressing these challenges, including solutions for standard authentication and authorization, robust supply chain defense, and enhanced observability, aiming to establish a secure, scalable, and resilient foundation for MCP-based multi-agent systems suitable for sensitive applications. Specifically, we propose the MCP Server Standard Framework as a foundational architecture designed to mitigate these vulnerabilities through a combination of secure by design principles and advanced threat detection mechanisms.

title: Food Nutrition Recognition Using Robotic Arm with Integrated Camera and Mobile Application
authors: Achmad Farizul Islam, Muchamad Wahyu Prasetyo, Aripriharta, Titi Mutiara Kiranawati, Mazarina Devi, Satia Nur Maharani, Gwo-Jiun Horng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The increasing prevalence of chronic diseases such as obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders highlights the importance of accurately and efficiently monitoring diet and nutritional intake. Unfortunately, traditional methods of food identification still rely on manual input that is slow, impractical, and prone to errors, especially for ordinary users. To overcome this challenge, this study developed an integrative system based on a robotic arm with a high-resolution camera and a mobile application to automatically recognize food and estimate its nutritional content in real-time. The system utilizes the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) algorithm to classify food images captured by a robotic camera, then forwards the classification results to a mobile app to display nutritional information. The dataset contains $\mathbf{2, 1 8 6}$ images of Indonesian food used for model training and evaluation, equipped with a preprocessing process and data augmentation to improve the model’s resilience to visual variation. Test results show that the system can achieve a recognition accuracy of $91 \%$ under normal lighting conditions and maintain an accuracy above $85 \%$ under extreme lighting conditions. Estimates of nutritional values show a high level of precision when compared to trusted nutrition databases such as USDA and Fat Secret, with low MAPE and RMSE values. The mobile app shows a fast response time (10 seconds on average) and a user satisfaction score of $\mathbf{4 . 6}$ out of $\mathbf{5}$. This approach shows great potential in daily nutrition monitoring, nutrition education, and therapeutic dietary support. This system is expected to contribute to preventive efforts in public health through smarter, faster, and more accessible food monitoring.

title: Explainable Deep Learning for Kidney CT Scan Classification
authors: Artham Vasu, Suzaan S Srivastava, Diana Olivia, Divya S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This study presents a clear and accurate framework for automatically classifying kidney computed tomography (CT) scans into four important categories: normal, cyst, stone, and tumour. While current methods show high diagnostic accuracy, their lack of interpretability and confidence calibration limits their use in clinical settings. To address these issues, the proposed system uses an improved image analysis architecture along with three supportive interpretability methods: Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), and Score-CAM. These methods help visualize decision patterns at different levels. A realtime reliability diagram is included to regularly evaluate and ensure probability calibration for each classification. This helps provide reliable confidence estimates. The framework was built and tested using a publicly available dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2, 4 4 6}$</tex> kidney CT images that cover different anatomical orientations and contrast phases. It achieved a classification accuracy of 94.2%, a macroaveraged F1-score of 0.925, and an Expected Calibration Error (ECE) of 2.0%. The system shows strong ability to differentiate and maintain robust confidence. These results emphasize the importance of combining explainability and calibration to develop transparent, trustworthy, and clinically valuable diagnostic systems.

title: Cyber Security Awareness Analysis of School Teachers in India
authors: Atul Kumar Srivastava, Ajay Vikram Singh, Subhranil Som
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: As the use of technology in education has increased recently, so too has the risk of fraud, abuse, mistreatment, and security problems; in this case, the educational environment is especially susceptible to cyberattacks. Due to the widespread use of digital technologies and online resources, educators are responsible for both teaching knowledge and ensuring the safety of their students in virtual learning settings. In order to determine the level of cyber security awareness among teachers in both government and non-government schools in India, a sample of 249 teachers was chosen from a total of 90 schools. In addition to demographic data, a quantitative study approach was used with a questionnaire of 27 questions. The results of the project show a statistically significant difference in the degrees of cyber security awareness among teachers in government and non-government schools. While nongovernment school teachers have demonstrated inattention, government school teachers are more concerned with cybersecurity standards. However, since there hasn't been any statistically significant difference, both government and nongovernment school instructors need to participate in training and skill sessions. Both government and non-government school teachers should have recourse for using safe and secure platforms, and school administration should be more involved in taking responsible action.

title: An Adaptive Interval Type-2 Fuzzy Fast Terminal Sliding Mode Controller for Fault-Tolerant Actuator Control in Omnidirectional Vehicles
authors: Abdelkader Djerad, Samir Zeghlache, Khaled Benzaoui, Ghellab Mohamed Zinelaabidine
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel fault-tolerant control (FTC) framework for Mecanum wheel omnidirectional vehicles (MWOVs) using an adaptive type-2 fuzzy global fast dynamic terminal sliding mode (AT2-FGDTSMC) methodology. Distinguishing itself from conventional approaches, the proposed controller is specifically designed to manage concurrent multi-actuator failures without the need for system reconfiguration. The scheme ensures robust trajectory tracking under model uncertainties, external disturbances, and complete actuator malfunctions. Extensive simulation studies, including scenarios with abrupt rotor faults, confirm the efficacy of the control strategy, showing preserved tracking performance indistinguishable from nominal fault-free operation.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy-PI/PID Tether Control for a UAV-UGV System
authors: Ammar Selim, Joshua D'Souza, James E. Pickering
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a modelbased design approach for a tethered unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) and unmanned ground vehicle (UGV) system. An integrated dynamic model is formulated to capture the coupled dynamics of the UAV, tether, and winch mechanism. A Sugenotype fuzzy inference system (FIS) is employed to adaptively adjust the reference tether length based on wind velocity and the desired geometric separation between the UAV and UGV. A dual-loop PID/PI control strategy with switching logic is implemented to regulate both tether length and winch motor velocity, enabling robust tracking of the reference tether length. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the integrated.

title: A Model on Gen AI and NLP Based Multi-Purpose Meeting Summariser
authors: J. Peter Praveen, B. Sai Sasank, B. Mahaboob, V. Siva Ram Kamal, Md. Sana Farheen, S. Uday Bhaskar
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Meeting organizers need to be able to capture important information and generate concise summaries. We present a Gen AI and NLP-based meeting summarizer that analyzes, extracts and generates succinct summaries of meeting content. The tool transcribes the audio from the meeting in question live or recorded into text, with summaries consisting of action points, decisions, insights, and context. It can translate summary to different languages, recognizes context and participants’ intent and proposes tailored summaries for different audiences. We can connect this product to Skype and Microsoft Teams in which we offer an AI-powered analytics and assure data privacy and security. This eliminates manual note taking and improves efficiency and alignment with the agreed objectives.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)

title: Virtual Reference-Based Predictive Coding for V-PCC Attribute Compression
authors: Yu Liu, Shuyuan Zhu, Qiang Zhu, Siu-Kei Au Yeung, Bing Zeng
abstract: Video-based point cloud compression (V-PCC) has achieved remarkable performance in dynamic point cloud compression. However, due to the 3D-to-2D projection in V-PCC, the temporal continuity across projected video frames is broken, limiting inter-frame coding efficiency. In this paper, we propose a virtual reference-based predictive coding (VRPC) scheme for V-PCC attribute compression to improve coding efficiency. This coding scheme uses the temporal correspondence between point clouds to guide the alignment of attribute video frames for virtual reference frame generation. Specifically, we first construct a neural deformation field-based geometry alignment module to align the reference and target point clouds to reduce the effect of motion on temporal correspondence estimation. Then, temporal correspondence is obtained in 3D space and used to align the reference and target video frames to generate the occupied region of the virtual reference frame. Finally, after padding the unoccupied region, the resulting reference frame is added to the reference picture list and used by the target frame for inter-frame coding. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively improves the attribute coding efficiency of V-PCC.

title: How Can We Establish Trustworthiness in Satellite Networks? Certificate Issuance, Checking, Revocation, and More
authors: Yali Wang, Yuan Zhang, Jingwen Lu, Dairu Han, Ruijin Sun, Zhisheng Yin, Nan Cheng
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Certificate management is needed for securing certificates which has been widely deployed in satellite networks to support security-related services. However, directly utilizing existing certificate management mechanisms in satellite networks would cause critical issues in terms of security, privacy, and practicality. Typically, the trustworthiness of certificate revocation checking (CRC) cannot be guaranteed in the presence of active adversaries; The certificate to be checked contains the satellite’s identity, which is sensitive in some applications but could be exposed during CRC; CRC cannot be trivially launched when the satellite is under constrained networks (e.g., it enters dead zones where direct communication with base stations fails).Worse still, compromising the secret key of a single certificate authority (CA) leads to certificate forgery. In this paper, we propose a forward-secure and privacy-preserving certificate management scheme, dubbed SNCM, for satellite networks, where a forward-secure signature algorithm is used to issue certificates. We utilize a neighboring-assisted forwarding paradigm in SNCM to support CRC in constrained networks. SNCM is secure against adversaries who invalidate CRC results or violate related sensitive information about the satellite, which is achieved by utilizing authenticated encryption with associated data (AEAD). Furthermore, SNCM utilizes a 2-layer revocation checking protocol to perform lightweight CRC, where the CA and base stations handle CRC tasks from satellites in a cooperative way, which frees the CA from heavy costs and reduces CRC delay significantly. We analyze the security of SNCM, implement an SNCM prototype, and conduct a comprehensive performance evaluation, which demonstrates its security, efficiency, and practicality.

title: ReToCue: Reliability-Aware Task Offloading and Caching in UAV-assisted LEO Satellite Edge Computing_supp1-3668646.pdf
authors: Yao Xin
abstract: The integration of Space-Air-Ground Networks with mobile edge computing represents a key 6G paradigm enabled by advances in low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and terrestrial networks. However, several critical challenges emerge when handling delay-sensitive services in rescue missions and infrastructure-less scenarios, where terrestrial network layers are absent. In such scenarios, the limited battery capacity of UAVs and satellites can be exhausted quickly. These factors jointly increase the probability of task delay violations and degrade the quality of service. To address these challenges, our work first investigates the joint optimization of edge caching and computation offloading in the UAV-assisted LEO Satellite edge computing (UAV-LEC) system. The primary objective in our formulation is to minimize total energy consumption while maintaining strict service reliability requirements. Given the distributed and stochastic characteristics of the UAVLEC system, we utilize a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP) to model the system. Furthermore, martingale theory is incorporated to analyze the service reliability bounds of the tandem queue in the proposed framework. Building on these theoretical foundations, we propose a novel asynchronous interactive multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithm to address the distributed caching and computation optimization problem in the UAV-LEC system. Finally, we conduct comprehensive simulations to demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves significant improvements compared to the baseline.

title: Ultrafast laser-fabricated three-dimensional photonic chips empowering diverse applications
authors: Jian Wang, Jue Wang, Chengkun Cai, Kangrui Wang, Shiao Ding
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) photonic integration is becoming a key enabler for future optical technologies, driven by the need for greater functionality, higher compactness, and superior interconnect density. In response to these demands, ultrafast laser processing has emerged as a powerful technique for realizing 3D photonic chips with enhanced design and fabrication flexibility—while avoiding the complexity of mask-based or cleanroom-dependent fabrication. This review begins by outlining the fundamental mechanisms of femtosecond laser–matter interactions, which enable precise energy deposition and spatially confined material modification. It then delineates three primary processing routes—direct modification, additive processing, and subtractive processing—alongside essential beam-shaping strategies. The compatibility of various material systems and structural platforms, including glasses, polymers, crystals, bulk substrates, thin-film layers, and optical fibers, is further discussed. Subsequently, the review details how these 3D laser-fabricated photonic structures underpin a wide range of applications, such as optical field manipulation, optical communications, optical interconnects, optical computing and processing, optical data storage, quantum information, nonlinear optics, optical trapping, and imaging. Finally, it concludes with a perspective on the prevailing challenges and future trends toward scalable, multifunctional, and hybrid-integrated photonic systems enabled by ultrafast laser microfabrication.

title: Time-Polling Multisource KPI Forecasting Using Channel-Dependent Source-Independent Strategy and Sourcewise Mean-Initiated Adaptive Fusion
authors: Hu Zhang, Zhaohui Tang, Yongfang Xie, Zhoushun Zheng, Weihua Gui
abstract: Key performance indicator (KPI) forecasting is crucial for industrial process operation and decision-making. Existing methods usually assume real-time synchronization of all data variables. However, due to cost constraints in practice, time-polling detection schemes create extremely sparse, asynchronous data. Therefore, we introduce a novel KPI forecasting framework for time-polling multisource data through three key innovations: 1) a data reconstruction strategy transforming sparse time-polling data into source-independent time series; 2) a channel-dependent source-independent (CDSI) module enabling intrasource fusion while maintaining intersource separation; and 3) a sourcewise mean-initiated adaptive fusion (SMIAF) mechanism for stable weight learning under sparse data conditions. Experiments on a lead-zinc flotation process demonstrate KPI prediction improvements: 9.42% MSE reduction, 12.84% mean absolute error (MAE) reduction, and 2.64% <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> improvement.

authors: N/A

title: A Strong Sense of Curiosity [Pathways]
authors: Nehad El-Sherif

title: A Text-to-3D Framework for Joint Generation of CG-Ready Humans and Compatible Garments_supp2-3668900.mp4
authors: Yong-Jin Liu
abstract: Creating detailed 3D human avatars with fitted garments traditionally requires specialized expertise and labor-intensive workflows.
