While recent advances in generative AI have enabled text-to-3D human and clothing synthesis, existing methods fall short in offering accessible, integrated pipelines for generating CG-ready 3D avatars with physically compatible outfits; here we use the term CG-ready for models following a technical aesthetic common in computer graphics (CG) and adopt standard CG polygonal meshes and strands representations (rather than radiance field representations like NeRF and 3DGS) that can be directly integrated into conventional CG pipelines and support downstream tasks such as physical simulation. To bridge this gap, we introduce Tailor, an integrated text-to-3D framework that generates high-fidelity, customizable 3D avatars dressed in simulation-ready garments. Tailor consists of three stages. (1) Semantic Parsing: we employ a large language model to interpret textual descriptions and translate them into parameterized human avatars and semantically matched garment templates. (2) Geometry-Aware Garment Generation: we propose topology-preserving deformation with novel geometric losses to generate body-aligned garments under text control. (3) Consistent Texture Synthesis: we propose a novel multi-view diffusion process optimized for garment texturing, which enforces view consistency, preserves photorealistic details, and optionally supports symmetric texture generation common in garments. Through comprehensive quantitative and qualitative evaluations, we demonstrate that Tailor outperforms state-of-the-art methods in fidelity, usability, and diversity.

title: Bio-Inspired Tensegrity Robotic Wrist With Tendon Sheath Functionality_supp3-3664932.mp4
authors: Meiling Zhang
abstract: Conventional robotic wrists predominantly employ rigid structures, which exhibit significant limitations in adaptability, while flexible robotic wrists often face challenges, such as insufficient stability and complex actuation system. Inspired by the bone-ligament composite structure of the human wrist, this study proposes a bio-inspired tensegrity mechanism that replicates functionality of wrist through kinematic mapping. By constraining the degrees of freedom (DOF) of the structure, we designed a biomimetic wrist with a workspace covering the human wrist’s motion range, achieving a balance between flexibility and stability. Furthermore, we investigated the role of tendon sheaths in wrist motion and simulated their function using eyebolt to guide tendon routing, enabling effective motion control. Impact test demonstrated the wrist’s rapid recovery (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.6 \, \mathrm{s}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) to equilibrium after disturbance, with steady-state recovery rates of 99.73% (coronal plane) and 99.86% (sagittal plane), highlighting its superior stability. Laser-tracing experiment further validated the wrist’s capability to execute complex trajectory-tracking tasks. Finally, the potential of this robotic wrist for practical applications was demonstrated through both load-bearing and dynamic variable loading tests.

title: Data-Efficient Real-Time Control of an Artificial-Muscle-Driven Continuum Robot with Physics-Informed Koopman Operator_supp1-3668444.mp4
authors: Jiefeng Sun
abstract: Data-driven techniques enable modeling of nonlinear continuum robots directly from input-output data without relying on physics-based modeling. Among these techniques, the Koopman method has been particularly successful as it enables the application of linear optimal control techniques such as model predictive control (MPC). However, this approach requires a large amount of experimental data to capture the complex dynamics of continuum robots. To reduce the data requirements, we implemented the physics-informed Koopman operator (PIKO) based on Strang splitting that combines simulation data from a static rod-muscle coupling model. Experimental results demonstrate successful trajectory tracking with reduced experimental data requirements. Comparing models trained on different amounts of simulation and experimental data shows the advantage of this method in a low experimental data scenario.

title: Full-space Four-channel Independent Transmit and Receive in-band Full-duplex Antenna System with Enhanced Throughput
authors: Chong-Zhi Han, Shengnan Liu, Shuo Teng, Qiming Wang, Jun Xiao
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this letter, a full-space four-channel in-band full-duplex (IBFD) antenna system is presented with independent transmit and receive beams. It employs a forward structurally multiplexed Fabry–Perot cavity transmitter, a backward folded transmitarray, and two reflectarrays for the four independent channels. The unit cell design adopts a cascaded multilayer metasurface scheme, which is developed to realize independent phase responses for transmission (Tx) and reflection (Rx), whereas the isolation between Tx and Rx is achieved through orthogonal circular polarizations. The proposed architecture therefore ensures four degrees of freedom in phase and two degrees of freedom in polarization. Experimental validation confirms high gain, low axial ratio, and channel isolation exceeding 35 dB across all operating modes. Enabling simultaneous co-frequency full-space transmission and reception, the antenna offers a theoretical fourfold throughput enhancement.

title: Modular Reservoir Echo State Network based Adaptive Consensus Control of Nonlinear Multi-Agent Systems under Time Varying State Constraints, Delays and Disturbances
authors: Simi Raj, K.V. Shihabudheen, Jeevamma Jacob
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper focuses on the adaptive neural network based leader follower consensus (LFC) tracking problem for a class of nonlinear multi-agent systems (MAS) with unknown dynamics, in the presence of time varying state constraints, unknown time varying delays and disturbances in a unified framework. A hybrid Modular Reservoir Echo State Network (MRESN) based adaptive control law is developed to achieve LFC with reduced tracking error and faster convergence. Here, MRESN is used to approximate the unknown agent dynamics and system uncertainties. Distinct from the existing studies, in this work, a disagreement function based backstepping-free control design is developed that utilizes a Time Varying Barrier Lyapunov Function (TVBLF) to constrain the states within prescribed bounds. The unknown time varying delays are compensated by utilizing an Exponential-type Lyapunov-Krasovskii functional (LKF) in the design process, resulting in the proposed control law that does not necessitate prior knowledge of the maximum time delay. Lyapunov analysis confirms the bound-edness of all closed-loop signals and guarantees that the system states are within defined bounds. The effectiveness and practicality of the developed control law are demonstrated through illustrative simulations and hardware-in-the-loop tests along with comparative studies against state-of-the-art controllers.

title: Optimized Authentication for WBANs Using Hyperelliptic Curve Cryptography in Cloud-Aided Medical Systems
authors: Pabitha B, V. Vani, Shridhar Sanshi
publish: IEEE Canadian Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering
abstract: Wireless body area networks (WBANs) are one of the most essential technologies for today’s electronic healthcare to achieve real-time monitoring and remote medical treatment in cloud-assisted environments. However, secure and efficient authentication is still a big challenge due to the constrained capabilities of WBAN devices. While many existing solutions use elliptic curve cryptography (ECC), it might introduce excessive computational and communication overheads. The work describes a lightweight authentication protocol for WBAN based on hyperelliptic curve cryptography (HECC), an emerging ECC substitute to address these limitations. HECC ensures the same security with shorter key sizes and reduced computation overhead, and thus is even more appropriate for resource-constrained environments. The proposed protocol is comprehensively analyzed for security and has been proven secure against various known attacks while fulfilling the necessary authentication requirements. Performance analysis indicates that the presented scheme attains considerable computational time savings, communication overhead, and storage, which indicates its feasibility and efficiency in secure healthcare systems.

title: Sentinel-5P Atmospheric Air Pollutants Forecasting Using Temporal Fusion Transformer
authors: Hana Najiyya, Suryo Adhi Wibowo, Runik Machfiroh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Rapid industrialization and reliance on fossil fuels have significantly increased air pollutant emissions, directly contributing to air quality degradation and public health issues. Accurate prediction of atmospheric air pollutant concentrations is crucial for effective environmental management and mitigation strategies. This study presents an in-depth approach using the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) model to predict atmospheric pollutant levels in Indonesia, leveraging high-resolution satellite data from the Copernicus Sentinel-5P satellite. The study integrates multivariate time series data across Jakarta and Banten. The TFT model, known for its ability to capture long-term dependencies, can provide interpretable results that can be used to analyze historical patterns and predict future air pollution trends at granular spatial resolutions. Model performance is evaluated using metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and Coefficient Determination ($R^{2}$), and it achieves the best performance with the lowest Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.0016, the lowest Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.0020, and the higher $R^{2}$ of 0.962. The results of this study indicate that the TFT model effectively predicts the temporal dynamics of air pollutants, providing information that can assist policymakers in making policy decisions for environmental monitoring in Indonesia.

title: Microplastic Detection in Glass Containers Using Circular Hough Transform and YOLOv8n
authors: Manan Jindal, Sayak Ghorai, Yash Upadhyay, Vikas Upadhyaya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This paper serves as a medium to present a novel approach combining Circular Hough Transform in the pre-processing phase with YOLOv8n architecture for microplastic detection in glass containers. Our proposed system is lightweight in nature which processes a dataset of 781 images along with a csv file containing the information of the microplastic fragments present in the respective images, using CPU-based training. The methodology integrates traditional algorithm from the field of computer vision which is the Circular Hough Transform to isolate the necessary region thereby providing a more focused area to work on with a pre-trained neural network from the domain of deep learning to detect and classify microplastic within the samples. The empirical results of the model were as follows: achieved $71.55 \%$ precision, $30.44 \% \mathrm{mAP}$ @ 0.5, and $\mathbf{2 8 . 5 3 \%}$ recall, showcasing a conservative detection behavior suitable for quality control applications. While the performance of the model is not up to the level of GPU-based systems, yet our approach still offers accessibility and deployment efficiency with $\mathbf{8 4 . 7 m s}$ CPU inference time with $\mathbf{2 . 6 9 M}$ parameters.

title: Comparative Analysis of Hybrid Machine Learning and Time-Series Models for Product Demand Forecasting Using XG-Boost, SVM, GRU, and FCNN
authors: Pratibha Rajwat, Mandeep Mittal, Biswajit Sarkar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of product demand and carbon emissions is critical for optimizing supply chain management and promoting sustainable business practices. Conventional forecasting methods often struggle to capture complex non-linear relationships, and temporal dependencies present in logistics data. This study conducts a comparative evaluation of advanced machine learning models Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Fully Connected Neural Network (FCNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XG-Boost) for forecasting demand and carbon emissions within a third-party logistics (3PL) system. An enhanced pre-processing framework is introduced to transform raw data into structured representations, enabling robust classification into Low, Medium, and High categories. Empirical analysis on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2}$</tex>-month dataset reveals that the GRU model consistently achieves the highest performance, with forecasting accuracy of 73 % for demand and 80 % for carbon emissions, surpassing FCNN, SVM, and XG-Boost by effectively capturing sequential and multivariate dependencies. These findings underscore the advantages of recurrent deep learning architectures in complex supply chain forecasting and highlight their potential to enhance operational efficiency and sustainability in modern logistics systems.

title: Optimization of Magnets And Minimisation of Torque Ripple in Brushless AC Embedded Permanent Magnet Motor
authors: Assam Zorig, Khaled Belhouchet, Noureddine Hamouda, Belkacem Bouacherine, Ismail Ghadbane, Badreddine Babes, Anwar Zorig, Rabhi Abdelhamid
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: The work presented in this paper relates to the optimization of a 50 Kw Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (IPMSM) used in an electric vehicle. The shape of a IPMSM was optimized for torque ripple reduction using optimization procedure and finite element analysis to improve the noise and vibrational performance of the motor. In the optimization stage geometry of IPMSM machine was considered, concerning magnet volume value and torque ripple minimisation with physical and technological constraints.This paper employs a strategy of the Global Response Surface Method (GRSM) optimization, which is on account of the impact levels of the design parameters on the objective functions, and a parallel computation, which is a valid method to shorten the computing time. As an application example, a PMSM is optimally designed. Its simulation results and prototype experiments are provided to showcase the effectiveness of the proposed method. The geometry found by the optimisation tool reduces by 9.8% magnet volume and The torque Ripple reduce by 19 % and keeps the same average torque in comparison to the initial design.

title: CF-SDALR: Confidence-Filtered Self-supervised Domain Adaptation Learning for Robust Transfer in Mechanical Fault Diagnosis
authors: Xudong Liu, Chuansong Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Bearing fault identification is crucial for rotating machinery reliability and efficiency, yet accurate diagnosis under varying running conditions is challenging due to vibration feature domain changes. The current work develops a framework named Confidence-Filtered Self-supervised Domain Adaptation Learning for Robust Transfer (CF-SDALR), integrating confidence filtering and self-supervised learning methodologies to improve mechanical fault diagnosis. The validation experiment carried out on the JNU bearing dataset shows that CF-SDALR achieves exemplary performance, yielding an average accuracy rate of $\mathbf{9 8. 6 8 \%}$ on six transfer tasks featuring different rotational speeds and exceeds state-of-the-art methodologies, namely Source-Free Cluster Adaptation and SDALR, which yielded $97.41 \%$ and $98.50 \%$ accuracy, respectively. The confidence filtering mechanism is particularly effective on demanding tasks, with accuracy improvements of $\mathbf{2. 3 4 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{8 0 0} \rightarrow \mathbf{6 0 0 ~ r} / \mathbf{m i n})$ and $\mathbf{1. 9 5 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{1 0 0 0} \boldsymbol{\rightarrow} \mathbf{6 0 0}$ r/min). Future work will focus on extracting subtle fault features and extending to complex industrial conditions with multiple domain shifts.

title: A Review of the Design and Performance of Passive 5 Gmm Wave Multi-Port Antenna on Printed PCB
authors: Malini Soman, Manish Sharma, Seema, Pradeep Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Passive multi-port antennas that are designed for 5G millimeter-wave (mmWave) applications, specifically on printed circuit boards (PCB) have been thoroughly analyzed and reviewed in this paper. It is highly notable that these antennas operate in the mmWave spectrum (30–300GHz) used for 5G communication. "Passive" means they do not amplify signals on their own, relying instead on the surrounding hardware to boost signal strength. Particularly, the paper has been focused on the performance of MIMO (Multiple-Input Multiple-Output) antennas that operate in the millimeter-wave (mmWave) spectrum for 5 G networks, with a specific focus on the 28 GHz and 38 GHz frequency bands. The paper has been focused thoroughly on main performance metrics such as bandwidth, gain, isolation, and efficiency as well. By conducting a thorough review of 20 studies, existing strengths and weaknesses in current MIMO antenna designs have also been identified. In the paper, the design of a novel MIMO antenna has been proposed and the process of simulation, characterization, and performance validation has been explained. Antennas for 5 G mmWave applications, specifically targeting the 28 GHz and 38 GHz bands, which are very crucial for 5 G networks have been highlighted in the paper. The results from both simulated and measured performance data have been compared, with the specific aim to improve the understanding and design of high-performance MIMO antennas for the 28 GHz and 38 GHz bands used in 5G.

title: SmartTAS-DRL: A DRL-Based Adaptive Scheduler for Deterministic Communication in TSN-Enabled In-Vehicle Networks
authors: Lanting Pang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: With the development of automobiles toward software-defined vehicles, the integration of functions such as autonomous driving has placed higher demands on the determinism and low latency of data transmission in in-vehicle networks. The Time-Aware Shaper (TAS) in Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) ensures the transmission of critical traffic through Gate Control Lists (GCLs), but traditional static scheduling methods struggle to adapt to dynamic environments. To address this, this paper proposes an adaptive scheduler named SmartTAS-DRL based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), which employs the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm to dynamically adjust scheduling strategies according to network states. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms the compared approaches in terms of scheduling success rate and packet loss rate across various scenarios, particularly excelling in complex high-load environments, thereby offering an effective solution for the efficient operation of in-vehicle networks.

title: A Hybrid Neural Network and Heuristic Based Task Scheduler for Realtime Uniprocessor Systems
authors: Premkumar Balani, Apurva Shah
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Reliable task scheduling is critical for uniprocessor real-time systems that underpin safety-critical and latencysensitive applications such as industrial control, healthcare monitoring, and autonomous devices. Classical algorithms such as Earliest Deadline First (EDF) are provably optimal under feasible processor utilization but degrade sharply when the system becomes overloaded, leading to cascading deadline misses and throughput loss. This paper introduces EDF_NN_LET, a hybrid scheduling framework that integrates a supervised multi-layer feedforward neural network with a lightweight heuristic fallback. The neural network, trained offline on EDF-labeled scheduling traces, learns to replicate EDF decision-making and serves as the primary runtime scheduler. When the predicted task is unavailable or the system encounters overload, a Least Execution Time (LET) heuristic selects the next job, ensuring continued operation and improved resilience. The proposed method was evaluated across sixteen CPU-load levels from 0.50 to 3.00 using 800 synthetic task sets. Experimental results show that EDF_NN_LET achieves near-perfect deadline adherence under feasible loads and sustains significantly higher success ratios and processor utilization compared to pure EDF in overload scenarios. The study demonstrates that lightweight learning-based augmentation of classical scheduling algorithms can provide robust and practical solutions for embedded real-time systems while preserving low computational overhead.

title: Integrative Approaches to Landslide Prediction: From Geospatial Techniques to Risk Mitigation
authors: Jyoti Arora, Anupam Mittal, Geetika Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Landslides are a major hazard to human life, infrastructure, and the environment, particularly in mountainous and high-rainfall areas. Predicting landslides accurately is essential for successful risk reduction and management measures. This paper provides a thorough analysis of landslide prediction techniques, highlighting machine learning (ML) and data-driven approaches. Through the combination of topographical, hydrological, geological, and meteorological information, a number of ML models including Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, and Neural Networks have been trained and tested against historical landslide datasets landslide4Sense and Bijie. The performance of these models is assessed in the work using accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC-ROC measures. Results prove the better performance of ensemble-based methods for landslide-prone area prediction. Furthermore, the paper discusses real-world applications such as early warning systems, urban development, infrastructure planning, and disaster response planning. The combination of remote sensing and GIS data also increases the spatial resolution and accuracy of the predictions. The work identifies the potential for AI-based tools to improve proactive landslide risk management and decision support systems.

title: Stochastic Differential Equation-Based SRGM of Deep Neural Networks to Study the Reliability Growth on Medical Imaging Data
authors: Suneeta Chaudhary, Bhushan Borotikar
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Software reliability checking is one of the crucial steps in the software development process. There are several software reliability growth models (SRGMs) developed to date, and many more are in process that employ various assumptions to study the reliability growth of software developed by companies before releasing it to the market. However, to date, SRGMs have not been applied to deep neural networks to study their reliability growth on medical imaging data. In this paper, we investigate the reliability growth of three deep neural networks (MobileNetV2, VGG16, and EfficientNetB0) used for classifying magnetic resonance imaging data using SRGM. Here, we consider three types of error: simple, hard, and complex. We used a stochastic differential equation-based SRGM and found that EfficientNetB0 has lower reliability growth as compared to the other two networks. The proposed SDE-based SRGM framework can be effectively employed to understand the reliability performance of networks, even without using expensive training procedures.

title: Four Port High Isolation Planer Antenna with Capacitive Inductive Decoupling with Ground Shorted Patch Antenna
authors: Shalni Chandan, Vandana Nath
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-band four-element MIMO antenna for sub-6 GHz systems. The designed antenna resonates at 2.49 GHz with a bandwidth of 0.11 GHz (2.43GHZ-2.54GHz), while the second resonating band frequency ranges from 5.34 to 6.69 GHz with a bandwidth of 1.5 GHz. The isolation between the ports varies between 15 and 30 dB across the resonating band. A capacitive slot is used to decouple the two elements, with an additional patch to ground shorting via that provides inductive coupling. The maximum gain in the first resonating band is 4.66 dB, while in the second resonating band it is 7.94 dB.

title: MedVox-AV: A Noise-Robust Medical Voice Assistant for Autonomous Vehicles Using Edge—Cloud Deep Learning
authors: Sriramkumar R, Vaishnavi R, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, A.Vegi Fernando, Mithaguru Mithaguru, M.Lakshmanan M. Lakshmanan
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles are being designed with passenger safety in mind beyond driving assistance capability, especially for passengers in need of medical assistance on board. This study develops a deep-learning enabled medical voice assistant for autonomous automotive environments that understands health-related voice commands, monitors the mental/physical state of the passenger, and takes automated emergency responses. The system integrates a noise-robust speech front-end and Transformer-based medical speech recognition module that contains training to respond to automotive noise, user accents, and specific vocabulary pertaining to physical symptoms. A medical intent understanding network is capable of recognizing emergency urgency phrases such as "chest pain," "difficulty breathing," or "need doctor" and correlating them to physiological information collected from wearable sensors. A hybrid decision model based on neural inference and rule-based medical triage interprets urgency and makes autonomous decisions to take actions such as contacting emergency services, providing vehicle and passenger vitals, and navigating the vehicle nearest to a biomedical facility. Edge-optimized deployment provides for low latency deployment and privacy compliance, while cloud deployment options allow for larger medical conversational potential. Experimental evaluation demonstrates reliable performance under dynamic in-cabin conditions and real-world noise conditions demonstrating strong enabling technology for passenger safety in autonomous mobility ecosystems.

title: RESSFN: Reliability Estimation of Stochastic Sensor Flow Network in IIoT Environments
authors: Deepika Rani Sahu, Niranjan Kumar Ray, Pradyumna Kumar Tripathy
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Reliability in sensor networks based IIoT is the critical aspects of determining the efficiency of the network. One of the prominent parameter in the sensor networks is the reliable transmission of data packets. Two-terminal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{s} \sim \mathrm{t}$</tex> reliability is the fundamental measure in network reliability analysis that ensures the reliable transmission of data packets from source node to destination node under the presence of link failures, random failures and behavioral pattern. It computes at least one functional link exists between the two terminals <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{s} \sim \mathrm{t}$</tex> under the presence of link failures. The network is modeled as a stochastic sensor flow network and incorporating failure probabilities of individual link capacities. This ensures that the system will be robust and resilient communication between the sensor nodes. In this work, a Reliability Estimation Stochastic Sensor Flow Network (RESSFN) is designed to assess the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{s} \sim \mathrm{t}$</tex> reliability of the system. A descriptive analysis of RESSFN will be performed on simpler and complex network topologies and compared with other existing works. The simulated model shows that the system achieves an improvement of more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 5 \%}$</tex> compared to existing work on different network topologies with dynamic links failures.

title: Seconds of Meeting Using NLP
authors: K. Jaspin, M. Jagan, N. Iniyaaraja
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative paradigm that would reshape the consumption and retrospective analysis of meetings by implementing the Natural Language Processing (NLP) and other advanced machine learning paradigms. The system proposed is called Seconds of Meeting, and it represents a smart meeting summarization system, which abridges the lengthy discussions in concise and impactful summaries. In the case where some meetings last more than a few hours, practitioners that cannot be present during them or have limited time to do so can have on the spot a one- page summary that will capture the main arguments, decisions, and action plans. Using state of the art applications like Pegasus, LongT5 and Whisper, the system translates verbal dialogue into accurate textual information, which is then processed into a summarized form, which in turn, can be understood in a minimum of 59 seconds. The empirical evaluation shows that the system achieves a total transcription and summarization fidelity of approximately 80, thus, providing reliable performance in a variety of meeting settings. At the end of the session, the summary is automatically created as a PDF document, which allows free documentation and availability. Such combination of NLP, Automatic Meeting Logging (AML), and Large Language Models (LLMs) promotes effective information storage, temporal reduction, and boosts productivity at both the business and academic level.

title: Latent Space Exploitation through Convolutional Autoencoders for Enhanced Image Denoising on Structured Datasets
authors: P.D.V.S.K. Kishore, Navdeep Singh, B Ammani, Gotlur Karuna, Dinesh Kumar Yadav, Tarunika Sharma
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: There is a rising necessity to enhance advanced methods of image processing through the noise reduction using improved neural network topologies. Autoencoder networks, untied types of neural networks, are relatively proficient in data representation learning and concretely superior to other types of networks in denoising. This paper consists of an application of the noise reduction technique based on autoencoder using the CIFAR-10 set with TensorFlow and Keras. The method under consideration uses a deep architecture of the autoencoder, which is trained to minimize the mean squared error for reconstructing clean images from noisy data. Details of implementation are declaring Gaussian noise to the dataset, using convolutional layers for building the autoencoder, and fine-tuning for better performance. The performance of the model can be considered through experiments and shows the model’s capability in maintaining image details as well as removing different types of noises. Thus, this paper fills the gap in the research on image processing by presenting an efficient approach to noise reduction with applied perspective for a wide range of fields, such as medicine, remote sensing, and digital photography.

title: Automated Diagnosis of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: V. Lakshmi, B. Pushpa
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: PCOS is the most common endocrinological disorder and one of the main reasons why women worldwide experience infertility after their first period. Recent research investigations and medical recommendations state that the utilization of clinical symptoms, laboratory investigations, and imaging by ultrasound can be used to diagnose PCOS. Major symptoms, including excessive weight or hair on the face, serious pimples, darker skin spots, or unscheduled periods that persist for more than six months following menstrual cycles, should all be evaluated even more. Hormonal characterization, ovarian ultrasonography, health information, and inspection of the body can all be used as initial monitoring to establish a diagnosis before symptoms escalate. Therefore, in this research, 3856 trained and tested ovary ultrasound images were used, where the deep learning techniques ResNet50 and InceptionV3 are used to predict the infected uterus. GLCM is used to extract the texture features like contrast, correlation, energy, homogeneity, and entropy, and VGG16 is used to extract the morphological features like follicle count, follicle size, ovarian volume, and follicle arrangement patterns. The ResNet50 performs with the best accuracy result of 94.44% compared to InceptionV3.

title: Modulation of EEG Connectivity by Insular Cortex Stimulation: Frequency-Specific Effects and Interoceptive Implications
authors: Ramin Aghili Karampour, Alireza Fallahi, Reza Kazemi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: This study examined the impact of targeted insular stimulation on EEG-based brain connectivity across multiple frequency subbands, using both correlation and coherence analyses. EEG data were collected from healthy participants both prior to and following transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) targeting the insular cortex. To identify significant channel-to-channel interactions, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex>-value matrices and heatmaps were generated for each subband. The results revealed that insular stimulation induced notable changes in connectivity, particularly within central and posterior regions, with the alpha and beta subbands showing the highest number of significant connections. Correlation analysis demonstrated greater sensitivity in the alpha and theta bands, while coherence was more effective in detecting changes within the beta and gamma bands, highlighting subband- and methodspecific modulation patterns. Beyond its involvement in cognitive and sensory networks, the findings underscore the insula's central role in interoception-integrating bodily signals such as cardiac and visceral inputs to support self-awareness and emotional regulation. Overall, this study demonstrates that targeted insular stimulation can enhance functional interactions within sensory, cognitive, and interoceptive networks, supporting its potential application in neuromodulation-based interventions.

title: A Non-uniform Subinterval Method for Interval Parameter System Response
authors: Jie Wu
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Due to measurement, processing, installation errors, and material aging, the true values of system design parameters fluctuate to varying degrees around their design nominal values, resulting in fluctuations in the true values of system performance around their design nominal values and causing design failure. To this end, a non-uniform subinterval method (NUSIM) is proposed to calculate the interval ranges of the uncertain system response. Interval numbers are utilized to characterize the uncertain system design parameters and the system performance function is expanded by a single point Taylor series for each interval parameter, providing an approximation for the system response that can significantly reduce truncation errors and does not require derivative calculations. For the large uncertainty interval parameters, the monotonicity of each parameter to the system response is determined and the stationary points of the system response to each non-monotonic parameter is calculated, and the non-monotonic uncertain parameters are divided into several non-uniform monotonic subintervals according to their respective stationary points. Based on the high-precision approximation of system response function, a high-precision calculation method for the system response interval is proposed by calculating the system response at the subinterval characteristic vertices. The calculation results show that compared with the traditional subinterval method (TSIM) based on common Taylor series expansion, the method proposed in this paper can significantly improve the calculation accuracy and efficiency of the system performance interval.

title: Analysis of RON90 Fuel Fulfillment in Balikpapan City Using Dynamic System Method
authors: Puspita Sari, Christopher Davito Prabandewa Hertadi, Mochamad Sulaiman, A. M. Adhitya A. Walenna, Zaenal Muttaqien
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The public demand for subsidized fuel such as RON90 in Balikpapan City continues to increase in line with growth in the number of vehicles from 481,922 to 642,409 and the population from 654,582 to 757,418, especially since the relocation of the national capital to East Kalimantan. This situation is exacerbated by distribution limitations and uneven fuel allocation policies, resulting in long queues and public concern. This study aims to analyze the factors affecting the availability of RON90, develop a dynamic system model, and formulate scenarios and policy recommendations to optimize its distribution. A dynamic systems approach is employed using STELLA software to construct a Causal Loop Diagram and a Stock and Flow Diagram. The model has been verified and validated using both ex post and ex ante methods. Simulation results show that without any intervention, RON90 distribution will remain unstable, while the number of vehicles continues to increase. The existing distribution volume reaches $25,400.23$ KL, which exceeds the actual data of 19,443.825 KL. To address this issue, four scenarios were developed, Scenario 1 involves increasing the number of gas stations (SPBU), Scenario 2 focuses on enhancing refinery capacity, Scenario 3 adds fuel imports to Balikpapan, and Scenario 4 combines all three measures by adding more SPBU, increasing refinery capacity, and boosting fuel imports. The best scenario is Scenario 4, which results in a distribution volume of 19,991.13 KL close to the actual data thereby improving the stability of fuel availability and distribution. This scenario offers a long-term and sustainable solution. With an efficient distribution system, the risk of long queues at gas stations can be minimized, as the fuel supply can be distributed evenly and sustainably until 2045.

title: Polysemy-Aware Probabilistic Measure for Interpreting Semantic Consistency in Domain-Specific LLMs
authors: Debatosh Chakraborty, Dwijen Rudrapal, Baby Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Large language models are often fine-tuned for specific domains to improve performance on downstream tasks. Beyond conventional accuracy metrics, a key question in interpretability research is how well these models capture domainspecific semantics. This paper introduces a method to assess semantic coverage in contextualized embeddings of fine-tuned encoder models. It examines whether domain-specific terms maintain consistent representations across diverse contexts, indicating meaningful semantic understanding. It is assumed that variation in embeddings correlates with consistency and that alignment with polysemic behavior indicates nuanced understanding. Using the financial domain for evaluation, the paper uses a semiautomatic approach to build a reference polysemy distribution and uses a probabilistic framework using log-likelihood to compare model consistency. Analysis shows that models with high polysemy divergence exhibit reduced prediction accuracy and semantic consistency, highlighting embedding stability as crucial for understanding. Findings also showed that high task accuracy may not reflect genuine domain understanding, suggesting the framework's utility for detecting and mitigating semantic underfitting and overfitting in fine-tuned large language models, particularly in low-resource settings.

title: A Performance Evaluation of Machine Learning Algorithms for Breast Cancer Detection Using PSO and CFS Feature Selection
authors: Priyanshu Raj, Shabnam Praveen, Shubham Kumar, Tulika Roy, Ranjita Kumari Dash
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the foremost global health problems, emphasizing the need for accurate and efficient diagnostic approaches. Machine learning has shown considerable promise in supporting clinical decision-making by enabling timely and reliable disease detection. This study presents a detailed comparison of seventeen machine learning algorithms applied to the Wisconsin Diagnostic Breast Cancer (WDBC) and Breast Cancer Coimbra (BCCD) datasets. The analysis spans traditional, ensemble, and advanced classifiers, with special attention given to two feature selection techniques: Correlation-Based Feature Selection (CFS) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The research workflow includes data preprocessing, systematic feature selection, and model training using 10-fold cross-validation to ensure robustness and generalizability. Results indicate that ensemble and boosting methods—specifically CatBoost, Random Forest, AdaBoost, and Gradient Boosting—consistently outperform simpler classifiers across both datasets. Incorporating PSO-based feature selection further improves classification accuracy, especially for the WDBC dataset, where CatBoost achieved a maximum accuracy of 98.25%. Although the Coimbra dataset presents greater classification challenges, the use of advanced models and effective feature selection still yields substantial performance improvements. These findings underscore the importance of careful algorithm and feature selection in developing reliable machine learning tools for breast cancer diagnosis, providing valuable insights for the advancement of automated clinical support systems.

title: Local Density Window Clustering: An Algorithm Based on Local Density Windows Density Clustering
authors: Zhan Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Density-based clustering effectively identifies arbitrarily shaped clusters and handles noise, but faces challenges with parameter sensitivity. Particularly with uneven density distributions, a single global parameter cannot adequately characterize both sparse and dense regions, often causing misclassification of clusters. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes a clustering method based on density windows. This method generates adaptive local density windows for each data point to identify high-density regions, thereby overcoming the reliance on global parameters and enabling flexible adaptation to the local distribution characteristics of the data. Building on this, the algorithm systematically merges overlapping windows based on the distance between windows and density connectivity, and ultimately forms the clustering result by optimizing the cluster structure. Comparative studies demonstrate that this approach provides a new perspective for addressing the challenges of parameter sensitivity and uneven density in density-based clustering.

title: Resilient SDR-Based Remote Sensing with Asynchronous Edge Upload and Cloud-Native Parallel Processing
authors: Sonny Wandel, Michael Einhaus
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Software Defined Systems (SDS)
abstract: This paper presents a scalable and robust architecture for remote wideband signal acquisition using low-cost SDR devices integrated with cloud-based processing. Leveraging asynchronous uploads of IQ samples, fault-tolerant cloud storage, and event-driven processing via AWS Lambda, the proposed system addresses key limitations of conventional SDR streaming, which rely on synchronous, high-throughput network links. By fully decoupling signal acquisition and processing in a software-defined, cloud-native manner, our approach enables reliable measurements in environments with unstable or low data rate connectivity and supports the deployment of multiple lightweight SDR nodes without local processing overhead. Evaluations demonstrate the system’s ability to maintain data integrity and support LTE/5G-NR measurements with high sampling rates under realistic network conditions, establishing a practical and cost-efficient solution for next-generation remote sensing and spectrum monitoring applications.

title: Language Prediction and Text Emotion Analysis by Utilizing Machine Learning Concepts and Algorithms
authors: Jyotiram Manik, Neelamadhab Padhy, Jogabrata Manik, Santosh Kumar Panda, Pradeep Kumar Mahapatro, Dasaradha Arangi
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: A branch of artificial intelligence called machine learning enables us to teach robots to anticipate and categorise data similarly to humans. We developed a robust model in this work to categorise user-inputted text data into language and emotion categories. Our primary objective is to classify text written in a specific Language and determine the emotion expressed in the text using machine learning algorithms. For language classification, we employed the Multinomial Naive Bayes algorithm due to its effectiveness in multi-class classification. For emotion classification, we utilised a Neural Network Model, specifically a Sequential Model. After testing on various models, we found that the Multinomial Naive Bayes performs better than the other models and has an accuracy and recall score of 95.27% which indicates that the model is more generalized and has better classification over other models. The Sequential model also got a test accuracy score of 85%. From all the above scores, we can tell that our model is a generalised and robust model for classification and emotion detection.

title: Developing a Strong End-to-End Semantic Approach for Transcribing Spoken Language to Text in Impaired Wireless Conditions
authors: Subhranshu Kumar Singh, Jibanananda Mehena, Sudhansu Bhusan Kar, Bhagirathi Behera, Suvasis Rout
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: As 6G networks advance, semantic communication offers a transformative approach by sending data's fundamental meaning instead of its unprocessed signals. In this context, we introduce DLSC-SR is an end-to-end semantic communication approach employing deep learning that is particularly suitable for speech recognition tasks. Instead of conveying the entire speech waveform, DLSC-SR identifies and sends only the semantic elements relevant to text, reducing bandwidth consumption significantly. The transmitter utilizes convolutional and recurrent neural networks to convert speech spectrograms into compressed semantic features, which are subsequently sent through a wireless communication channel. At the receiving end, a decoder rebuilds these features and retrieves the textual content using a greedy decoding algorithm. The model is tuned. with the connectionist temporal classification (CTC) loss function, enabling effective handling of variable-length sequences. Performance evaluations over AWGN and Rayleigh fading channels demonstrate that DLSCSR achieves improved character and word recognition accuracy, particularly in low-SNR scenarios. Moreover, the model shows resilience to changing channel conditions without needing retraining, making it a strong candidate for 6G applications.

title: Analysis of Cyber Attacks and Security Techniques for a IoT enabled Smart Grid
authors: Rashi Singh, Nasib Singh Gill, Preeti Gulia
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The rapid increase in the energy demand, the focus is shifting from energy generation to efficient energy management. Undoubtedly the energy generation can be enhanced by integrating existing power sources along with renewable energy sources that makes the grid capable to fulfill the demand. However, for the efficient generation and management of energy, a Grid 2.0 is introduced with a tittle Smart grid that is embedded with the Internet of Things (loT) and communication infrastructure. A smart grid is considered more rational in decision-making via two-way communication and reduces the energy loss to a nominal point. However, despite of all these benefits, the Smart Grid needs synchronization through the intranet-based communication channels. These communication channels have some weaknesses and vulnerabilities to cyber-attacks and penetration. In order to overcome these weaknesses, security related research also need attention of active researcher. This paper is aimed at exploring all the related vulnerabilities, and threats, and thereafter taking a stock of security techniques, and necessary countermeasures to overcome such types of vulnerabilities. This paper also provides further insights that helps in related areas in future.

title: An Improved YOLO11 Lightweight Detection Method for Routine Road Damage
authors: Xiaojun Guo, Limei Yu, Xue Liang, Xiao Ye
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: To address the trade-off between detection accuracy and limited computational resources in routine road damage inspection, this paper proposes a lightweight detection method based on the improved YOLO11s architecture. By incorporating the EfficientNetV2 backbone and the SlimNeck architecture, the proposed method significantly reduces computational redundancy while effectively optimizing the fusion efficiency of cross-stage multi-scale features. Furthermore, by integrating the dynamic non-monotonic focusing mechanism of the WIoU loss function, the model's ability to localize difficult samples, such as cracks, is further enhanced. Experimental results on the Jilin-RDD hybrid dataset demonstrate that the improved model reduces the parameter count and computational cost by 27.1% and 38.4%, respectively, while increasing the inference speed to 208 FPS and maintaining an mAP50 of 0.702. This method achieves an optimal balance between performance and efficiency, effectively meeting the requirements for real-time inspection on edge devices in complex scenarios.

title: High Frequency, Thermally Stable Dielectric Resonator Oscillators for New Space Applications
authors: Przemyslaw Kant, Piotr Szczepinski
publish: 2026 IEEE Space Hardware and Radio Conference (SHaRC)
abstract: This publication summarizes the design, fabrication and verification testing of a free-running frequency generator based on a dielectric resonator oscillator dedicated to the direct generation of pure local oscillator signals at high frequencies exceeding 14 GHz. The device includes a discrete design, which minimizes the cost and complexity of manufacturing and testing. Additional advantages of the design include radiation resistance despite the use of COTS and automotive-level components, low footprint and weight of the device, and low phase noise. The devices successfully passed qualification process following a typical ECSS approach to qualification testing.

title: SECUREAM: Advancing Cybersecurity in IoT-Enabled Additive Manufacturing
authors: Eun-Young Kang
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: This paper describes dissemination and exploitation of the SECUREAM industrial research project which directly addresses the evolving cybersecurity challenges in IoT-enabled Additive Manufacturing (AM) by developing a comprehensive security framework through close collaboration between academic and industry experts. While the primary focus of the project is on enhancing cyber resilience across digital AM processes and workflows, the framework is also designed to be adaptable to the stringent requirements of high-value and mission-critical component production, where robust security and traceability are imperative. Its aim is to secure AM supply chains, safeguard data integrity and confidentiality, and support compliance with emerging regulations, thereby meeting the rising demand for certified, secure AM solutions.

title: Advanced Techniques for Detecting Anomalies to Improve Security in Smart Networked Environments
authors: Saurabh Pratap Singh Rathore, Jay Singh, Kamlesh Kumar Sharma, Gotte Ranjith Kumar, Anandakumar Haldorai, Solleti Phani Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Network intrusion is one of the greatest threats to the security of the modern network communications and therefore, efficient intrusion detection systems (IDS) must be established to secure network services and data. Machine learning algorithms have emerged as a powerful tool of identifying the suspicious patterns of network traffic. In the given paper, we propose an ensemble learning technique that will combine SVMs) and Random Forest algorithm to identify network intrusion. The dataset we will be using is the NSL-KDD dataset which is a better version of the KDDCUP-99 dataset. Our intrusion detection algorithm is geared towards classifying the incoming network traffic either as a normal one or a malicious one, depending on 41 features that describe each traffic pattern. We achieve a high accuracy of over 95 percent and random forest is better than SVMs. The research is significant in enhancing network security and privacy in intelligent environments.

title: Gene Expression-Based Oral Cancer Prediction Using Ensemble Machine Learning Models
authors: Bhupesh Deka, Sujata Dash
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Early detection of Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma (OSCC) remains a critical challenge in clinical oncology. Traditional diagnostic techniques such as biopsies are invasive, time-intensive, and often fail to detect the disease in early stages. In this study, we propose a gene expressionbased predictive framework for oral cancer detection using ensemble machine learning models. Gene expression data from the TCGA-HNSC cohort is preprocessed through normalization, feature selection, and batch correction techniques. Models including Random Forest, XGBoost, and CatBoost were trained and evaluated using stratified crossvalidation and independent testing. The proposed system achieved a test accuracy of 99.52% and an F1-score of 1.00, demonstrating exceptional predictive capability. Furthermore, model explainability was incorporated via feature importance analysis to highlight biologically relevant gene markers. This research highlights the potential of ensemble learning models in transforming genomic data into actionable diagnostic tools for early and interpretable oral cancer detection. The code is available at: https://github.com/Bhupeshdk/OralGen

title: Domain-Specific Keyphrase Extraction: A Specialized Named Entity Recognition and Multi-Stage Filtering Approach
authors: Nikita, Kavya Fagun Shah, Dipti P. Rana, Rupa G. Mehta
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Keyphrase extraction enables retrieval systems, similarity and precedent analysis from complex Indian judgments. Traditional NLP algorithms cannot analyze phrases from Indian context. This research proposes a keyphrase extraction methodology based on a specialized Indian Legal NER (InLNER) model. The proposed method involves: specialized InLNER model generation, contextual text filtering, multi-technique keyphrase extraction and weighing & selection to yield the final keyphrases. The InLNER model uniquely identifies Indian entities including statutes, case citations etc., allowing systematic filtration of generic keywords and contextual entities. InLNER improves keyphrase quality by avoiding words with minimal context for analysis while retaining substantive concepts. This method transforms information processing by curating precise, informative keyphrases that accurately expresses context, improving information retrieval systems and performing sophisticated similarity analysis.

title: Artificial Intelligence Methods for Improving Trust and Cooperative Interaction Between Humans and Robots
authors: Priyanka Kaushik, R K Yadav, Abhishek Kumar Singh, R Sujitha, Anandakumar Haldorai, Mahendran Rajamanickam
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: HRI is a new field that aims to improve human-robot interactions in efficiency, effectiveness, and trust. Artificial intelligence technology is making robots increasingly popular in the workplace and home. This research analyzes the challenges of applying AI to increase human-robot cooperation and trust, as well as the latest methods. The study begins with HRI's fundamental challenges, such as language, emotional, and physical risks. Researchers use AI technologies including natural language processing, computer vision, and emotional recognition algorithms to help robots comprehend and respond to human emotions. The second half of this article examines HRI trust and the need to build trust between humans and robots. Trust fosters acceptance, teamwork, and task completion. explore trust-building tactics like open decision-making, understandable AI, and feedback loops for robot behavior to satisfy human demands. The research also examines collaborative frameworks that let humans and robots work together. From task allocation and coordination to joint action planning, AI technologies maximize cooperation. study how human intent detection, multi-agent systems, and reinforcement learning promote fruitful human-robot partnerships. The ethical challenges of implementing AI technology into HRI include privacy, prejudice, and employment. Responsible AI practices and ethical standards are needed to maintain AI-powered robot legitimacy and promote HRI system acceptance.

title: Research on Knowledge-Enhanced Large Language Models for Legal Document Synthesis
authors: Kaixuan Xing, Yu Yang, Zheyan Wang, Jie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: The size and complexity of legal corpora continue to increase, making the efficient and accurate synthesis and organization of large document collections a central challenge for legal practice and the development of intelligent legal systems. General-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle with long-context understanding and domain-specific knowledge, leading to coverage gaps, fragmented reasoning chains, and limited controllability, and thus fall short of the rigor required for legal document synthesis. This paper proposes a knowledge-enhanced LLMs framework for legal document synthesis. The framework introduces a Modular Cognitive Processing (MCP) mechanism that decomposes document understanding and synthesis into interpretable subtask and combines it with a curated legal knowledge base to support the systematic consolidation and structured organization of case facts, statutes, and judicial interpretations. The system generates structured outputs with explicit and traceable links between facts and cited provisions, thereby improving organization, interpretability, and controllability. Without relying on task-specific heuristics, the proposed approach provides practical support for building intelligent legal systems and lays the foundation for applications such as judicial decision support and the construction of legal knowledge graphs.

title: Design and Analysis of a Miniaturized Multi-Band Ultra-Wideband Microstrip Patch Antenna for 5G Millimeter-Wave Applications
authors: D. Haimoune, B. Guenad, H.A. Bakir, A. Zidour
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a compact, multi-band ultra-wideband microstrip patch antenna for 5G millimeter-wave (mmWave) systems. The antenna is fabricated on a Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate with a relative permittivity of ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> = 2.2 and a thickness of 0.254 mm. It incorporates a square slot and an inverted L-shaped strip, while the partial ground plane includes narrow slots to enhance radiation. The antenna resonates at 39, 47, 58, and 70 GHz, achieving return losses of −30 dB, −47 dB, −23 dB, and −20 dB, respectively. It achieves an impedance bandwidth of 66.67% and a peak gain of 6.7 dBi. These results confirm its suitability for 5G mmWave communication applications.

title: Conductive Hydrogels in Biomedical Engineering: Current Status and Challenges
authors: Elham Amiraslani, Zahra Mohammadi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Hydrogels are rapidly emerging as a pivotal class of materials across diverse disciplines, largely owing to their exceptional attributes such as biocompatibility, biodegradability, and inherent hydrophilicity. Furthermore, their notable adhesive qualities and impressive elasticity enable seamless integration with myriad surfaces. Within their extensive range of applications, particular interest has been directed towards how these substances influence vital biological phenomena, including cellular migration, neovascularization, and the moderation of inflammatory responses. The present paper is dedicated to elucidating the action mechanisms of conductive hydrogels in promoting wound recovery. Concurrently, it offers an overview of advancements in relevant materials and methodologies, discusses prevailing challenges, and forecasts future directions within this specialized domain.

title: Fabric Defect Detection Based on Computer Vision
authors: Shuhan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Fabric defect detection plays a crucial role in modern textile manufacturing, as traditional manual inspection is inefficient and susceptible to subjective bias. Although convolutional neural networks (e.g., ResNet-50) have demonstrated strong performance in image classification tasks, their accuracy in complex texture scenarios remains limited by insufficient data diversity and structural constraints. To address these issues, this study uses ResNet-50 as the baseline and introduces two major improvements: (1) employing multiple data augmentation strategies-including geometric transformation, color variation, and channel manipulation-to enhance sample diversity; and (2) integrating the SE channel attention mechanism together with a two-layer MLP classifier with dropout to strengthen feature representation and mitigate overfitting. Experiments conducted on the Fabric Defects Dataset show that the baseline model achieves an accuracy of $88.5 \%$, the dataenhanced model reaches $93.25 \%$, the structurally improved model yields $85.22 \%$, and the final model combining both improvements achieves $\mathbf{9 5. 0 7 \%}$. The results demonstrate that appropriate data augmentation and architectural optimization can significantly improve the robustness and generalization capability of convolutional neural networks in fabric defect classification, providing a feasible and efficient solution for intelligent visual inspection in the textile industry.

title: Polynomial Regression-Based Modeling and Optimization of Output Voltage in Moist Electric Generators under Varying Humidity Conditions
authors: Septianissa Azzahra, Rudina Okvasari, Iwa Garniwa, Samsurizal, M. Zulham Kentjie, Yulisya Zuriatni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This study investigates the effect of air humidity (% RH) on the output voltage (Voc) of a Moist Electric Generator (MEG) for three different material compositions: A, B, and C. The modeling was conducted using second- and third-order polynomial regression. Model performance was evaluated based on the coefficient of determination ($R^{2}$), optimal humidity (H_opt), and maximum voltage (V_max). The results show that the third-order model provides higher accuracy for all compositions. Composition $\mathbf{B}$ yielded the highest maximum voltage of 5.40 V at 78.48% humidity, followed by compositions $\mathbf{C}$ and $\mathbf{A}$. This model can serve as a foundation for the development of humidity-based MEG systems in tropical environments.

title: Emotion Recognition System for Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder Using Image data
authors: Aiswarya K S, Leena V A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) of ten struggle to recognize and express emotions, causing challenges for their social interactions and effective support by caregivers. This paper presents an image-based emotion recognition system tailored for children with ASD, leveraging deep learning models to classify facial expressions into four categories: happy, sad, angry, and neutral. Three architectures were developed and evaluated: A CNN based on MobileNet enhanced with a SE block,a ViT model for capturing global facial features, and an ensemble that combines MobileNet+SE, ResNet18, and ViT out-puts. A custom dataset of ASD children's facial images which has augmented and balanced was used for training the model. The ensemble model achieved the highest test accuracy of 98.16%, outperforming the individual models (MobileNet+SE: 88.5%, ViT: 95.4 %). Precision, recall, and f1 scores for the ensemble reached 0.98. Grad-CAM is employed to visualize facial regions crucial to the models' decisions, enhancing interpretability. The results demonstrate a highly accurate and explainable emotion recognition approach that could aid caregivers and professionals in monitoring and understanding the emotional states of children with ASD in real time.

title: An Extensive Exploration Of Deep Learning Methods for Raga Recognition in Indian Classical Music
authors: Amrita Jiju, Akhil V V
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Indian Classical Music or Shastriya Sangeet, an essential aspect of Indian cultural heritage, is a rich and intricate art form encompassing two major traditions: Hindustani and Carnatic. A fundamental aspect of Indian Classical Music is the raga, which provides the melodic structure for compositions and improvisations. Raga recognition, a vital task in Music Information Retrieval (MIR), facilitates applications such as music recommendation, education, archival, and content-based music retrieval. This paper reviews state-of-the-art methodologies for raga identification, focusing on approaches utilizing deep learning and signal processing techniques. Methods explored include feature extraction and classification models which include Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), spectrograms, scalograms, and temporal sequence modeling via LSTM, BiLSTM, CNN, and RNN techniques. Additionally, this work highlights the importance of open-access datasets for large-scale analysis and the challenges inherent in Indian Art Music research, such as sociocultural context, data annotation, and model generalizability. Future prospects for improving raga identification are explored, emphasizing hybrid models, multi-modal techniques, and real-time applications to blend tradition with technology.

title: Multi-Objective Optimal PMU Placement Using Binary Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm
authors: Aishwarya Premnath, Divya N A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper presents a multi-objective optimal Phasor Measurement Unit (PMU) placement strategy using the Binary Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (BAOA), aimed at enhancing power system monitoring while minimizing associated costs. The proposed approach addresses the key trade-offs in PMU placement by simultaneously optimizing multiple objectives, including minimization of total installation cost and maximization of system observability. To ensure practical applicability, the method incorporates critical constraints, specifically: (i) Full system observability, ensuring that the entire network remains observable under normal operating conditions, and (ii) Cost constraints, limiting the total expenditure based on a predefined budget. The BAOA, a recent metaheuristic inspired by arithmetic operators, is adapted into a binary framework suitable for solving the discrete PMU placement problem. The algorithm effectively explores the solution space and balances the conflicting objectives using a weighted fitness function or Pareto-based approach. Simulation results on standard IEEE test systems demonstrate that the proposed BAOA-based method achieves high-quality solutions with fewer PMUs and lower cost, while maintaining full observability, validating its robustness and efficiency for real-world Power system applications.

title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for EEG-Based Parkinson's Disease Classification
authors: Santosh Sah, Rupsa Mukhopadhyay, Rakesh Kumar Rai, Saptarshi Dutta, Deependra Singh
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) affects neural motor control and its early identification is crucial for clinical intervention. In this study, we investigate the effectiveness of deep learning architectures for PD classification using electroencephalography (EEG) signals. A publicly available EEG dataset from Kaggle was preprocessed using bandpass filtering and normalisation to remove noise and enhance discriminative patterns. Five models-Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Residual Network (ResNet), and DenseNet-were implemented and trained for binary classification. Model evaluation was performed using standard metrics, with accuracy as the key performance indicator. Experimental results show that the CNN model achieved the highest classification accuracy of 0.97, demonstrating strong feature extraction capability from EEG signals. RNN and ResNet also produced competitive performance with accuracies of 0.95 and 0.92, respectively, while DenseNet showed comparatively lower accuracy. These findings indicate that CNN-based frameworks are well-suited for EEG-driven PD detection and motivate future exploration of hybrid deep learning approaches for enhanced diagnostic reliability.

title: Enhancing Vehicle Seat Belt Usage Detection in Low-Light Conditions Using YOLOv11 and EnlightenGAN
authors: Erika Devi Udayanti, Pulung Nurtantio Andono, Abdul Syukur, Moch. Arief Soeleman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The number of vehicles operating in Indonesia continues to increase significantly, necessitating stricter enforcement of traffic discipline to reduce the potential for accidents due to violations. One important aspect is seat belt compliance. Indonesia has begun implementing an electronic violation detection system based on CCTV installed at strategic points on highways and toll roads. However, detecting seat belt use in real-world conditions remains a challenge, particularly in low-light conditions such as at night or in bad weather. This study proposes an approach that combines the YOLOv11 object detection algorithm with a deep learning-based image enhancement technique, EnlightenGAN. The dataset was obtained from the Roboflow platform with a total of $\mathbf{2, 4 6 3}$ images representing various low-light conditions and camera angles. Experimental results show that the application of EnlightenGAN significantly improves the visual quality of images and has a direct impact on detection performance. The YOLOv11 model integrated with EnlightenGAN is able to achieve $\mathbf{m A P} \text{@} 0.5$ values close to 0.90 and mAP@0.5:0.95 stable above 0.82, with precision and recall around $0.89-0.91$ and an F1-score of $\mathbf{0 . 8 8}-\mathbf{0 . 9 0}$. The best performance was achieved in the seatbelt class, where detection results were more consistent and average confidence increased to more than 0.9.

title: Performance Enhancement of Transformer based Stacked Cascade Multilevel Inverter Using Modified SPWM Control
authors: Anirban Chatterjee, Lalit Mohan Saini, Shivam
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: In this paper, an improved Stacked Cascade Multilevel Inverter (SCMI) topology obtained by reorganizing the switching truth table and the added Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM) in place of the traditional round-control method is proposed. The adapted switching scheme removes redundant conduction states, and produces symmetric voltage level generation without adding additional power semiconductor devices or transformer windings. The architecture is tested in MATLAB/Simulink in the following conditions: no-load of the architecture, single- phase (1600 W) and three-phase (1.8 kW/phase) operation. The performance indicates a great decrease in Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), of 4.31% in single- phase and 3.03% in three-phase operation, which is lower than the above 6.63% in the reference SCMI topology. Also, behavior under no-load startup of THD is studied, which has not been considered in previous studies, to investigate the stability of inverters during light-load or under standby conditions. The results prove the fact that harmonic suppression is possible with the optimization of switching states and modulation relining without the collaboration of external filters or hardware enhancement. The SCMI offered is therefore best suited in transformer-isolated renewable, UPS and standalone inverter applications.

title: Edufit – Career Catalyst: AI-Powered Resume Analysis and Adaptive Learning for Career Development
authors: Aman Nair, John B Varghese, Nikita Anna Ajith, R Sidharth, Anjali S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This project presents an innovative platform that streamlines the transition from education to employment by integrating a comprehensive suite of tools tailored for job seekers. Existing platforms typically include an Application Tracking System but lack mechanisms to analyze users' skills or provide guidance for improvement based on job recommendations. Similarly, current educational systems often fail to offer personalized learning experiences, resulting in disengagement and limited learning outcomes. The proposed platform addresses these gaps through a placement assistance system that connects users with potential employers via a curated job-matching service. By leveraging advanced algorithms, it aligns candidate profiles with relevant job openings to enhance the accuracy of recommendations. Additionally, the platform features an intuitive resume creation tool that assists users in crafting professional and impactful resumes through customizable templates and guided support. Furthermore, a skill-enhancement module promotes continuous professional development by identifying individual skill gaps and recommending personalized learning resources-such as courses, tutorials, and practice exercises-based on user profiles and current job market trends.

title: Optimization of Scenario-Adaptive Vectorization for Cholesky Decomposition on DSP
authors: Minghao Qiu, Yue Yang, Qingyi Zhang, Li Feng, Hongyang Liang, Yaokun Wang, Siyi Guo, Huan Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: To address memory bottlenecks and vectorization adaptability challenges in Cholesky decomposition on high-performance vector DSPs, this paper proposes an optimized vectorization methodology. Firstly, we design an iterative instruction compaction technique that eliminates redundant memory accesses to diagonal elements in existing vectorized methods. Secondly, leveraging the evolving characteristics of decomposed submatrices, we introduce a phased optimization strategy that overcomes limitations of single vectorization approaches and achieves dynamic adaptation to varying submatrix scales. Finally, we optimize compute-transfer overlap mechanisms for diverse data scales. Experiments demonstrate that this integrated approach delivers over 40% performance gain versus state-of-the-art methods and achieves 2.1 speedup over DSPLIB library functions.

title: User Requirement Understanding Based on Large Language Models for Large Constellations
authors: Peiyun Li, Zhanpeng Xu, Xiaohan Sun, Xi Cai, Song Xie, Linghui Yu
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: With the widespread application of large constellations in the remote sensing field, traditional manual parsing of user requirements has become inadequate to meet the demands of massive and time-sensitive task scheduling. This paper proposes a user requirement parsing method based on large language models. By constructing a tree-like decomposition structure between user natural language requirements and formatted input for task planning, the method achieves precise extraction of requirement elements. To address the issue of sample insufficiency, an innovative reverse synthesis strategy is designed to generate natural language requirements from formatted outputs. Through training, the model's response accuracy is enhanced. Experimental results show that the method achieves over 90% accuracy in key elements such as target location, payload requirements, priority levels, and time allocation, validating the effectiveness of large language models in parsing user requirements for remote sensing constellations.

title: A Comprehensive Review of AI-Driven Emotional Companions in Digital Reading
authors: Barnwal Jyoti Kumari, Surya Veer, Nikita
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Involvement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its features in platforms which are used for reading in the digital space has provided new ways and techniques for personalized learning without outsiders' participation through the invention of Self-Regulated Learning also known as SRL. AI involving in emotions and feeling commonly referred as emotional AI evaluate, predict and respond to learners' affective and cognitive conditions and supply relatable and relevant guidance, viewpoints, and motivational help and support.
