authors: N/A

title: HeadRouter: A Training-free Image Editing Framework for MM-DiTs by Adaptively Routing Attention Heads
authors: Yu Xu, Fan Tang, Juan Cao, Xiaoyu Kong, Yuxin Zhang, Jintao Li, Oliver Deussen, Tong-Yee Lee
abstract: None

title: Hybrid Wavelet Bi-LSTM Model for Stable Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting
authors: Fedora Dias, Anant Naik
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Accurate short-term wind power forecasting underpins reliable scheduling, reserve allocation, and economic dispatch in renewable-rich grids. This paper proposes a hybrid Wavelet Bi-LSTM (Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory) framework that combines Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) denoising with Bi-LSTM for robust temporal learning. Using hourly meteorological and power data from Kanyakumari (India), ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average), LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory), Bi-LSTM, and Wavelet BiLSTM is benchmarked under identical preprocessing, lookback, and horizon. Accuracy via MSE, RMSE, MAE, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and robustness via multi-horizon RMSE (t+1 to t+3) is evaluated. The hybrid model achieves competitive point errors and the smallest RMSE growth (5.4%), evidencing superior stability. Diagnostics ACF/PACF plots, correlation heatmap, and actual–predicted overlays explain how denoising and bidirectional temporal learning interact to improve performance.

title: Zero-Shot Knowledge Graphs from Handwritten Notes with LLMs and OCR
authors: Tanush Sharanarthi, Mahesh Ganesamoorthi, Shreyas Shrinivas Kallapur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Knowledge graphs are valuable tools for organizing and analyzing relationships in unstructured data, improving search, recommendation, and decision-making systems. However, existing approaches largely depend on well-structured digital text, leaving handwritten documents such as medical records, historical manuscripts, and research notes underutilized. This paper introduces a knowledge graph construction framework that extracts structured information directly from handwritten text by combining Optical Character Recognition (OCR) with Large Language Models (LLMs). The system integrates OCR-based text extraction with transformer-based entity recognition and relationship inference to generate structured knowledge graphs without requiring domain-specific training. The methodology evaluates handwritten and typed text extraction accuracy, examines the impact of handwriting styles on entity recognition, and visualizes knowledge graphs from both data sources. Experimental results show high recall for typed text while revealing challenges in handwritten text extraction due to OCR limitations. However, LLM-based inference improves structural accuracy by mitigating errors introduced during text recognition. These findings demonstrate the potential of LLM-assisted knowledge graph generation for handwritten documents, enabling new applications in archival analysis, medical data digitization, and historical document processing.

title: An Ensemble Based Predictive Analysis for Chronic Kidney Disease with Quantum Neural Networks
authors: Mukesh Kumar, Dharmendra Kumar, Dipti Dash, Rasheswari Bhramar Ray, Niranjan Kumar Ray, Saroj Kumar Mohapatra
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Chronic kidney disease is a lifelong hazardous disease caused by decreased kidney function or renal pathology. In this context, accuracy is crucial for disease prediction. In this work, we examine the prediction ability of different ensemble-based models and machine-learning approaches for chronic renal disease. This work examines many machine learning techniques as well as an ensemble-based classifier for the prediction of chronic kidney disease based on clinical data. Additionally, we analyzed and contrasted the outcomes using Quantum Neural Networks (QNN), Support vector machines (SVM), logistic regression (LR), voting, stacking, decision tree classifiers, and many more machine learning techniques and ensemble based algorithms are used for the performance analysis. The performance comparison and analysis have done on four dataset with four evaluation metrices with various traing testing ratios (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5,0.6$</tex> and 0.8).

title: Feature-Conditioned WGAN for Generating Alzheimer's EEG: Enabling GAN-Based Synthesis Under Data Scarcity
authors: Parsa Bahramsari, Alireza Taheri
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) significantly impairs cognitive function, making early detection and personalized care crucial. Electroencephalography (EEG) provides a noninvasive, low-cost window into cortical oscillations and is sensitive to AD-related spectral slowing and reduced temporal complexity. However, acquiring high-quality EEG data is often limited by factors such as patient fatigue, session variability, and logistical challenges, especially in environments like socially assistive robots (SARs). These constraints make it difficult to gather sufficient data for training reliable deep models for AD detection. To address this challenge, we propose a feature-conditioned Wasserstein generative adversarial network (fc-WGAN) that generates class and subject specific EEG segments from minimal training data. We first analyze a broad set of time-domain and frequency-domain EEG features to identify those most discriminative between AD and cognitively normal groups. Notably, features like nonlinear energy and band powers consistently demonstrate high separability. fc-WGAN aligns the mean and variance of these features between real and generated EEG batches, enhancing physiological realism and class consistency. Starting from only 200 overlapping 3second segments per subject, our method improves EEGNet classification accuracy from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87.5 \pm 4.5 \%$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.2 \pm 4.4 \%$</tex> by effectively augmenting the training dataset. These results underscore the power of feature-aligned generation in overcoming data scarcity and demonstrate the practical utility of fc-WGAN for SAR-based cognitive assessment and early AD detection in real-world settings.

title: An Efficient Path Planning Approach with Improved A* Algorithm based on DEM
authors: Xuwen Mao, Junjie Wang, Qingbo He, Hongrui Zhang, Zhen Liu
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Low-threat and Low-cost paths are an important guarantee for realizing combat missions. Traditional path planning algorithms focus on the path cost, lack of consideration of weapon deployment patterns and geographic information, additionally, when dealing with high-dimensional complex matrix data, there are problems such as low search efficiency, and difficult to reach the destination point. To address these problems, this paper proposes an improved A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup> algorithm path planning method based on DEM. The algorithm comprehensively considers multiple threat factors such as exposure risk index and lethal threat index in typical enemy weapon deployment mode, adopts dimensionality reduction technology to deal with high-dimensional matrix data, designs the heuristic guidance function of direction vector to search for the optimal route search, and generates final feasible paths by mapping the optimal paths to high-dimensional geography through index mapping. Simulation results show that the improved A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup> algorithm proposed in this paper significantly improves the search efficiency. The path planning time of the traditional algorithm is reduced from hours to 47.693 seconds and the computational memory is reduced while the path planning accuracy is guaranteed.

title: Estimating Ground-Level Air Quality Index from Satellite Imagery: A Data-Driven Approach
authors: Yuval Mehta, Vaishnavi Kamath, Nisha Kini, Aryan Haraniya, Supriya Agrawal
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Appropriate modeling and prediction of air quality are critical to alleviate the health effects of air pollution, especially in fast-growing urban areas. Ground-based monitoring networks, though accurate, are hampered by the high cost of deployment and poor spatial coverage. Here, we introduce a multimodal deep learning model that combines satellite and street-level images to forecast air quality indices and primary pollutants, such as Particulate Matter with aerodynamic diameter less than 2.5 micrometers, Ozone, and Nitrogen Dioxide. Using an open-source dataset with India and Nepal air pollution data, we augment the dataset with associated satellite images secured through Google Earth Engine. The model involves the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs)-based encoders (e.g., EfficientNet) and utilizes diverse attention mechanisms in integrating heterogeneous visual features. Elaborate preprocessing is pursued by the framework, such as K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) imputation of absent values and modality-specific normalization. Model training is engaged about with PyTorch and experiment tracking is done by Weights & Biases (wandb). Experimental results showcase that the multimodal strategy works significantly better in prediction, particularly in regions with little sensor infrastructure. The paper illustrates the potential for hybridizing satellite and ground-level images toward more durable air quality monitoring and reveals the issues and possibilities for implementing such systems in real-world environmental monitoring contexts.

title: Comparative Analysis of Preictal–ictal Synchronization Techniques for EEG-Based Seizure Classification
authors: Jayalakshmi P K, Manimegalai P, Lavina Joseph, Minu C Davis, Supriya Mary Sunil, Rani Varghese
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: An estimated 65 million people worldwide suffer from epilepsy, a neurological disorder brought on by aberrant electrical activity in the brain. Large amounts of data are required to train machine learning algorithms, enabling the creation of seizure prediction systems that are accurate and have low false alarm rates. The dataset for this study is the EEG scalp signals obtained from 14 epileptic patients at the University of Siena's Neurology Unit in Italy. With an emphasis on identifying synchronization patterns in EEG signals, a patient-specific seizure prediction method is proposed and assessed. The objective is to determine whether non-invasive EEG data can be utilized to create a reliable seizure prediction, monitoring, and control tool. The prediction framework employs a straightforward, computationally efficient threshold-based classifier and uses synchronization metrics calculated across all pairs of EEG channels. Four distinct classification techniques are evaluated for seizure detection and prediction based on the synchronization between preictal and ictal states.

title: Quantitative Assessment of Data Analytics Applications in Remote Patient Monitoring and Management: A Case-Based Experimental Framework
authors: Sarika Shingate, Shilpa Nawale, Nikita Chavan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) has transformed healthcare delivery by enabling continuous observation with near real-time insights of patient health parameters outside clinical settings. This transition is especially important to patients with chronic illnesses, post-surgery care requirements, or those living in rural and underserved areas. The use of data analytics, especially machine learning (ML) and visualisation tools, as part of RPM systems has proved to be promising in improving predictive care, early intervention, and operational efficiency. This paper includes a quantitative experimental design which assesses the contribution of data analytics tools in improving outcomes in RPM. The approach implies the use of real-life data sources such as Electronic Health Records (EHRs), wearable sensors, and RPM platforms based on the cloud. Logistic regression, decision trees and anomaly detection models were used and evaluated on typical classification and regression metrics (e.g. F1-score, MAE, RMSE). Power BI dashboards were used to visualise insights that were actionable. The outcomes indicate a statistically significant 23% decrease (p < 0.001) in 30-day readmissions, patient engagement, and statistically significant model performance. The study confirms the effectiveness of data analytics in RPM implementation and provides an expandable model that can be used by healthcare systems to modernize remote care by intelligent automation.

title: Sentiment Analysis in Finance: Predicting Market Trends with Artificial Intelligence
authors: Reena S. Satpute, Divyani V. Chatarkar, Nura Muhammad Sani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Financial sentiment analysis plays a critical role in understanding market behavior and supporting data-driven investment decisions. This study proposes a comprehensive methodology that evaluates machine learning, deep learning, and transformer-based models for classifying financial text into positive, neutral, and negative sentiment categories. A dataset of 17,200 financial text samples was collected from publicly available sources, including financial news outlets, market reports, and Twitter posts. The text underwent extensive preprocessing involving cleaning, tokenization, stopword removal, and lemmatization to ensure high-quality input for model training. Feature extraction techniques were applied using TF -IDF for classical machine learning models, word embed dings for deep learning models, and specialized transformer tokenization for BERT and FinBERT. Multiple models, Naive Bayes, logistic regression, SVM, random forest, XGBoost, LSTM, BERT, and FinBERT, were evaluated using accuracy, F1-score, RMSE, and ROC-AUC metrics. The results consistently show that transformer-based architectures outperform traditional approaches, with FinBERT achieving the highest performance across all indicators. FinBERT recorded an accuracy of 0.95, an F1-score of 0.94, the lowest RMSE value of 0.25, and a ROC-AUC of 0.96, demonstrating strong generalization and minimal prediction error. The confusion matrix further highlights FinBERT's effectiveness in correctly classifying the majority of sentiment categories, confirming its ability to capture complex contextual cues in financial language. This research demonstrates that domain-specific transformer models offer substantial improvements in financial sentiment classification and provide a reliable foundation for developing intelligent market analysis and prediction systems.

title: Experimental Investigation of Mass Removal Methods for High-Performance Cantilever Sensors
authors: Shubham Kumar Mishra, Abhinav Sharma, Mohd. Zahid Ansari, Afzal Husain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Cantilever-based sensors are widely used for fluid density and viscosity measurements, but their sensitivity is often limited by geometric constraints. This study explores a mass-removal approach to enhance performance by comparing a conventional rectangular cantilever with non-uniform designs featuring either linearly varying thickness or linearly varying width. Analytical models based on Hoffman's formulation were developed to predict resonance frequency, and three-dimensional eigenfrequency simulations were performed in COMSOL Multiphysics 5.6 to validate the theory. Stainlesssteel prototypes of the baseline and the optimized varying width design were then fabricated, integrating piezoelectric (PZT) patches for experimental frequency characterization under vacuum, air, water, and water-glycerine mixtures. Analytical, numerical, and experimental results showed close agreement, with the varying width profile consistently yielding the highest fundamental frequency. Sensitivity measurements, defined as frequency shift per unit density change, demonstrated that the varying width cantilever achieved an 18.75 % improvement in sensitivity compared with the rectangular baseline. These findings confirm that linear width tapering provides a robust, dimension-independent strategy for enhancing the sensitivity of cantilever-based sensing devices.

title: LLMs Powered Storytelling Frameworks Using Text Generation and Image Generation Models
authors: Deepika Pantola, Madhuri Gupta, Arya Sunil, Anvi Kohli, Arushi Dadhich
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Recently, multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable ability in the creation of text-to-images, making comic storytelling an essential component of content production. Nevertheless, current models frequently have trouble preserving narrative, chronological, and spatial coherence between image sequences and usually fall short of the complexity and interest of stories written by humans. In this work, we have conducted a comparative analysis of 3 text-to-comics frameworks, each of which consists of a large language model (LLM) for text generation and image generation. These three frameworks are: Gemma 3 with Grok 3, Mistral with Flux. 1 Schnell, and LLaMA 3.1 with Stable Diffusion. This study provides a comparative examination of these frameworks, detailing their design, workflow, and characteristics in generating AI-driven comics. The data used for demonstrating these frameworks is a famous short story from the renowned Panchatantra Tales, named The Brahmin's Dream. The experimental results showed that although Mistral and Gemma 3 showed adaptability in managing a variety of storytelling approaches, LLaMA 3.1 showed good logical organization in panel generation with the best FID and SSIM scores.

title: Multiband Terahertz MIMO Antenna with Enhanced Isolation for IoT and 6G Wireless Communication
authors: Farha K A, Jasmine P M, Smitha N M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This work proposes a compact 2-element inset-fed MIMO antenna featuring multi-band operation within the terahertz spectrum. The proposed antenna is implemented on a polyimide substrate, with a relative permittivity of 3.5. The designed antenna demonstrates effective performance across three operational bands: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.91-3.54 \text{THz}$</tex> with a bandwidth of 0.63 THz and an average gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.24 \text{dBi}, 4.55-4.78 \text{THz}$</tex> with 0.23 THz bandwidth and 8.25 dBi average gain, and 5.26-5.36 THz with 0.1 THz bandwidth and an average gain of 8.38 dBi. It also exhibits inter-element isolation exceeding 35.63 dB, −35 dB and −30.1 dB in the respective bands. With its compact structure, the proposed design is well-suited for high speed IoT and short-range terahertz communications.

title: Energy-Efficient Motion Detection Algorithm for Intelligent Security and Surveillance Applications
authors: Devireddy Srinivasa Kumar, Durgesh Nandan
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Now a day’s security is one of the main concerns in large communities, business areas and apartments. Monitoring for intruders or suspicious or unauthorized movement of people day and night is a big challenge for security persons. Appointing more number of security persons leads to financial burden to owners as the maintenance charges exceeds their budget. Automatic detection of objects from video streams is useful for security and surveillance systems where constant human monitoring is not possible. A simple, low-cost and light weight solution for object detection system has been proposed. This object detection system doesn’t need any deep learning or heavy computational resources. Simple digital image processing techniques like frame differencing, thresholding and contour detection have been applied to detect objects. This object detection system has been developed using Python programming language along with OpenCVlibrary. The input of this system is a CCTV video stream and audio file for alert. This object detection system compares the video frames to identify the movement of objects in the video stream and plays an alert sound when ever motion of an object detected. Each video frame is converted into grayscale and blurred to reduce noise. The first frame is taken as reference background. This system detects the motion of an object by comparing the current frame and reference frame. The difference of these two frames is identified as a moving object. When a moving object is identified an alert sound is played automatically to alert the user. This object detection system is a practical and easy to implement computer vision application.

title: A Memory-Augmented Regression System for Concept Drift Adaptation
authors: Wanqi Gu, Wei Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Data streams evolve continuously with their distributions potentially shifting unexpectedly, known as concept drift. A primary issue in this context is the scarcity of samples from the new distribution immediately after a drift occurs, which hinders the model's ability to rapidly capture the new concept. In this work, we propose the Memory-Augmented Regression System based on Broad Learning System (MARS-BLS). In MARS-BLS, a memory-augmented module composed of a Global Monitor Window (GMW) and Local Memory Pools (LMPs) is employed. The GMW actively detects concept drift in real-time. Upon drift detection, the LMPs dynamically retrieve historically stored data that is distributionally similar to the new data. By fusing these relevant historical data with the new data, the model gains richer information and updates more effectively. Experimental results demonstrate that MARS-BLS achieves rapid responsiveness to concept drift across both real-world and synthetic data sequences. It successfully alleviates the difficulty models face in swiftly adapting to concept drift when new samples are scarce.

title: Enhancing Brain Tumor Classification Accuracy through Multi-Modal MRI Analysis and Advanced Ensemble Deep Learning
authors: Ayyoub Berra, Oussama Bouguerra, Bilal Attallah, Youcef Brik, Mohamed Djerioui, Ibraheem Shayea, Saleh Ibrahim Al-Zahrani, Youssouf Bouzidi, Abdelwahhab Boudjelal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This paper focused on the multi-class brain tumor classification problem, using deep learning methodologies based on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images. Two state-of-the-art deep learning models, EfficientNet-B3 and ConvNeXt-Tiny, were fine-tuned through transfer learning with custom classifier heads, and used a publicly available dataset from Kaggle, leveraging various data augmentation techniques to help reduce class imbalance. These two models were trained to the same hyperparameters and had the same metrics applied to the evaluation. Both models were assessed using Accuracy, F1-Score, Recall, and Precision. EfficientNet-B3 achieved an accuracy of 96.85% and ConvNeXt-Tiny achieved 95.88%. After observing the strengths of both models, a weighted ensemble (60% EfficientNet-B3 and 40% ConvNeXt-Tiny) was created, achieving a final accuracy of 97.07%. Based on the results of the study, it demonstrates how deep learning and ensemble techniques can improve diagnostic accuracy in a complex multi-class brain tumor classification task.

title: Improving Clinical Trust via SHAP-Based Interpretability in Early Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Detection
authors: Lov Kumar, Vikram Singh, Debasish Senapati, Rajesh Kumar Panda
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is a multifaceted endocrine disorder prevalent among women of reproductive age, often remaining underdiagnosed due to its heterogeneous clinical manifestations and the absence of standardized diagnostic procedures. This study presents a robust and interpretable machine learning pipeline for early PCOS prediction, leveraging both clinical and symptomatic data. To identify the most informative features while reducing dimensionality and noise, we employed a statistical feature selection approach based on the Wilcoxon rank-sum test. We then evaluated a diverse collection of 20 classifiers, including traditional machine learning models, ensemble methods, and deep learning architectures, using three-fold cross-validation. Model performance was assessed across multiple metrics (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, and AUC). The bestperforming model was a linear-kernel Support Vector Classifier (SVC) with probability estimation, achieving 93.92% accuracy with the selected features. To enhance clinical trust, SHAPbased explainability techniques were applied at global and local levels, identifying critical predictors such as follicle count, hirsutism, irregular menstrual cycles, and weight gain. These findings confirm that methodologically rigorous yet computationally lightweight models can deliver high accuracy while preserving interpretability and clinical relevance, making them well-suited for real-world deployment.

title: MAGA, Trump, and Tweets: Analyzing Public Opinion Ahead of the 2024 U.S. Presidential Election Using LDA and Dendrogram Analysis
authors: Shreya Srivastava, Seba Susan
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Social media platforms like <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{X}$</tex> (formerly Twitter) provide useful information on public sentiments and online discussions related to events of national/international importance such as elections. In this study, we analyzed tweets related to the recently concluded 2024 U.S. Presidential election, collected between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1{ }^{\text {st }}$</tex> May to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$31{ }^{\text {st }}$</tex> July 2024. Using Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA), we identified three prominent topics within this timeframe. For each topic, we extracted the most frequent keywords and visualized them using word clouds for interpretability analysis. Our analysis revealed that “Trump” and “Make America Great Again (MAGA)” were the most frequently occurring terms within the topic that had the highest semantic coherence. This topic also formed a more distinctive cluster within the dendrograms derived by hierarchical agglomerative clustering, as compared to the other topics that represented scattered discussions. Given the close association of Donald Trump's election campaign with the MAGA slogan, our findings reveal that the idea resonated well with public opinion on Twitter, even five months prior to the election date, giving a strong indication that he could potentially win the 2024 U.S. Presidential election.

title: An Optimized SVD-Based Spread Spectrum Audio Watermarking Scheme for Enhanced Robustness and Imperceptibility
authors: Nur Said, Gelar Budiman, Sofia Sa’Idah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This paper proposes an optimized hybrid audio watermarking technique that integrates the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT), Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), and Spread Spectrum (SS). The proposed framework aims to achieve a balanced tradeoff between robustness and imperceptibility through parameter optimization involving block size, subband selection, and spreading factor ($\alpha_{\mathrm{ss}}$). Unlike previous hybrid watermarking approaches, the inclusion of SS enhances correlation-based detection, while the optimization process ensures adaptability to different types of audio content. The embedding process is performed in the transform domain, where watermark bits are spread across singular values using a pseudo-random sequence, and then reconstructed through inverse transforms. The system is evaluated using multiple audio hosts under various common signal-processing attacks, including MP3 compression, filtering, resampling, time scaling, and additive noise. Experimental results demonstrate strong robustness and high perceptual transparency, achieving SNR values above 23 dB and BER below $10 \%$ under light to moderate distortions. The payload capacity reaches 43.06 bps while maintaining imperceptibility, indicating a practical balance between embedding strength and audio quality. Comparative analysis with existing DWT-DCTSVD methods confirms improved resilience to compression and resampling attacks. Overall, the proposed DWT-DCT-SVDSS hybrid watermarking framework offers a computationally efficient and adaptive solution for secure audio ownership authentication in modern multimedia environments.

title: A Freezing Transfer and Coordinate Attention Based Method for PCB Defect Detection
authors: Zhengping Li, Saiyan Saiyod
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Printed circuit board (PCB) defect detection is crucial in the electronics manufacturing process. However, due to the diversity of defect types, small scales, and complex background interference, the detection task still faces significant challenges. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight PCB defect detection method based on YOLOv8n. First, the CoordAttention mechanism is introduced into the model architecture, which encodes both spatial positional information and channel features to effectively enhance feature representation for small defects and complex scenarios. Second, a frozen transfer training strategy is designed: in the early training stage, the backbone network is frozen to maintain stable low-level features while training only the detection head and attention module; in the later stages, the backbone is gradually unfrozen, and a higher learning rate is assigned to the CoordAttention module to accelerate convergence and alleviate overfitting. Experimental results on the HRIPCB dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves excellent performance with only 3.0M parameters, reaching mAP@0.5 = 0.967 and mAP@0.5:0.95 = 0.544, while maintaining a lightweight structure. Compared with mainstream detection methods, the proposed approach exhibits a better trade-off between accuracy and efficiency. These results validate the effectiveness and application potential of the proposed method in PCB defect detection tasks.

title: Evaluating Communication Models for Scalable IoT-Cloud Integration
authors: Samuel Horáček, Roberto Rossi, Ahmad Al-Shishtawy, Vladimir Vlassov
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: The increasing scale and complexity of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems demand communication architectures that balance performance, scalability, and maintainability. Although publish-subscribe communication is widely regarded as the de facto standard for decoupled IoT-cloud integration, a comprehensive empirical comparison with direct request-reply architectures remains limited, particularly under realistic IIoT conditions. In this work, we evaluate three IoT-cloud architectures: a direct HTTP-based model, a single-protocol MQTT-based model, and a hybrid model combining MQTT and Apache Kafka. Using a custom testbed with up to 40 virtual IoT devices and four network profiles, we assess latency, throughput, and CPU usage. The evaluation results show that MQTT outperforms HTTP in latency and throughput, while the hybrid model provides extensibility at the cost of higher latency.

title: 26.6-33.6 GHz CMOS Up-Conversion Mixer for Ka-Band LEO SATCOM UT Transmitter
authors: Yo-Sheng Lin, Chung-Ta Huang, Yu-Rong He
publish: 2026 IEEE Space Hardware and Radio Conference (SHaRC)
abstract: This paper presents a CMOS up-conversion mixer for Ka-band low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite communications (SATCOM) user terminal (UT) transmitter. The mixer constitutes a balun-QC-based differential I/Q LO generator, a three-stage-PPF-based differential I/Q IF generator with following buffer amplifier, a dual-double-balanced-Gilbert-cell-based mixer core, and an active RF adder. DTMOS-with-Rb topology is utilized in LO switch transistors for achieving better on/off switch properties. B-to-D-with-RB transistors with inductive peaking are adopted in the active RF adder to achieve higher conversion gain CG (due to reduction of the threshold voltage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{th}}$</tex>) under the same or lower power consumption. The mixer consumes 24.6 mW, and achieves decent LO matching bandwidth of 13.6 GHz (21.9–35.5 GHz), CG of 11.6 ± 1.5 dB for 26.6–33.6 GHz (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{f}_{\text{3dB}}$</tex> of 7 GHz), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{OP}_{1dB}}$</tex> of 0.8–3.9 dBm and OIP3 of 9.1–15.1 dBm for 25–35 GHz.

title: A Decentralized and Secure IoT Access Control Model Using Blockchain, Federated Learning, and Cryptography
authors: Vijilius Helena Raj, Venkata Siva Bathula, K.Pithambar, Dinesh Kumar Yadav, Amit Dutt, Rallabandi Venkata Santoshi Saraswati Swetha Nagini
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Centralized access control architectures demonstrate serious scalability problems and vulnerability to single points of failure along with privacy risks during IoT network expansion. The recently developed Blockchain and Federated Learning based Access Control Technology (BFL-ACT) serves as a distributed access control solution for IoT networks that uses blockchain technology combined with federated learning and cryptographic methods to enhance participant privacy protection and manage vast network sizes effectively. The protection of sensitive information and model updates during both training and inference phases utilizes integrated homomorphic encryption alongside lightweight cryptographic primitives. This paper demonstrates the system design and protocol flows for BFL-ACT and develops its threat model before constructing a prototype IoT testbed for performance benchmarking. The BFL-ACT approach demonstrates superior intrusion detection capabilities and increased robustness against attacks by malicious actors compared to standard centralized and non-blockchain federated learning systems while maintaining low communication costs and near real-time inference speeds ideal for practical IoT systems.

title: Recent Breast Cancer Categories, Detection Techniques, and Recommended Treatments
authors: Zarrish Iqrar, Avinash Kumar Pandey, Puja Priya, Sumit Kumar Mishra, Akanksha Kumari, Shruti Sharma
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Unfortunately, breast cancer (BC) challenge has displeased the modern worldwide medical researchers community, because of its complexity, commonly known as a heterogeneous disease. Here, the comprehensive review actually provides a current BC panorama brief overview, which classified multiple associated categories, newly advanced detection techniques, and the recently recommended treatment methods to overcome such heterogeneous disease issues. Renowned BC assortment simply allows primary categories, including ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS), invasive ductal carcinoma (IDC), invasive lobular carcinoma (ILC), and triplenegative, as well as HER2-positive BC, which only focus on some possible successful customiseds steps. Each specific category is different and required depending on the individual diagnosis for treatment techniques. An advanced detection approach has quietly come into existence, which suggests early diagnosis and improved patient results. However, ultrasound, mammography, and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) are still crucial while screening despite several evolving technologies such as molecular imaging and liquid biopsies that not only allow early detection and its overall monitoring. Whether it is surgery or therapy like chemotherapy, radiation therapy, hormone therapy, targeted therapy, or immunotherapy, these noticeable advancements in BC treatment are true evidence of hope. Besides, the primary attention of this paper will be on BC categories, renowned detection techniques, and possible treatments. Recent advancements with future directions for the highlighted topics are further elaborated.

title: Data-Driven Classification and Spatial Clustering of Stunting Cases in Rural Indonesia
authors: Abdurrasyid, Herman Bedi Agtriadi, Rima Rizqi Wijayanti, Aisyah Ragillia, Mochamad Farid Rifai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Stunting remains a public health priority in Indonesia, with prevalence rates in Bengkulu Province reaching 18.8%(2024). Current monitoring still relies on static reports, limiting timely pattern recognition. This study applies two machine learning approaches to analyze stunting in North Bengkulu Regency: K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) for classification and K-Means clustering for spatial grouping. The objectives are to predict toddlers’ nutritional status and identify areas with elevated stunting prevalence to guide targeted interventions. The KNN algorithm was applied to classify the nutritional status of children under five, while K-Means clustering grouped sub-districts into distinct spatial clusters based on stunting prevalence. Secondary data were obtained from the North Bengkulu Health Office. The KNN model achieved high predictive accuracy (96.86%), and K-Means effectively partitioned sub-districts into two clusters with markedly different stunting rates. Model performance was rigorously evaluated using a confusion matrix for classification and the Davies-Bouldin Index (DBI) for clustering, confirming the robustness and reliability of both approaches. These findings highlight the potential of integrating classification and clustering techniques to support evidence-based policymaking, helping local governments design more effective interventions to reduce stunting rates.

title: Designing Hyperchaotic Robust 2D Chaotic Map Using 1D Sine Map for Image Encryption
authors: Manish Kumar Thukral, Jyoti Grover, Meenakshi Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This research proposes a novel two-dimensional (2D) chaotic map, termed the Exponential Modulo Sine Map (EMSM), designed for IoT applications using a one-dimensional (1D) sine map. Unlike the 1D sine map, which exhibits a limited chaotic range and intermittent periodic windows, the proposed 2D EMSM is robust, offering a wide chaotic region. The map demonstrates hyperchaotic behavior, with more than one positive Lyapunov exponent, indicating high sensitivity to initial conditions. These properties make it suitable for developing secure image encryption algorithms. A three-layered scrambling-based image encryption algorithm, followed by a diffusion stage, is proposed based on the EMSM. Security analysis, including histogram, correlation, and key sensitivity tests, confirms the algorithm's robustness. Simulations are conducted using MATLAB R2023a.

title: A Comparative Study on QPSK, 8QAM, and 16QAM Modulation Schemes for Optical Fiber Communication
authors: Zonghao Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Optical fiber communication systems rely heavily on modulation scheme selection to balance spectral efficiency, transmission reliability, and distance performance. This paper presents a simulation-based study to assess the performance of QPSK, 8QAM, and 16QAM modulation formats under practical optical transmission conditions. Key performance metrics include signal-to-noise ratio (SNR)-bit error rate (BER) characteristics, and maximum transmission distance supporting a BER threshold of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–3</sup>. We investigate the effects of Gaussian noise, chromatic dispersion, and dispersion compensation on constellation, and quantify how fiber segment count and single-segment length influence long-haul transmission capability. Results indicate that QPSK, despite lower spectral efficiency (2 bits/symbol), demonstrates exceptional robustness—achieving 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–3</sup> BER at ~7 dB SNR and supporting ultra-long distances up to 16,440 km. Higher-order formats (8QAM, 16QAM) offer improved spectral efficiency (3 and 4 bits/symbol, respectively) but require higher SNR (>10 dB for 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–3</sup> BER) and suffer from severe transmission range limitations, restricting their application to short-reach or high-SNR environments. This work provides data-driven guidance for selecting appropriate modulation schemes based on specific system requirements, aiding in the design optimization of optical communication networks.

title: DMLLNet: A Structural Complexity-Driven Registration Network for High-Resolution SAR-Optical Images
authors: Simin Peng, Hongxiang Lv, Fei Wu, Hai Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: High-resolution optical–SAR registration remains challenging due to pronounced cross-modal discrepancies and substantial computational overhead. This paper presents a complexity-aware image registration network named DMLLNet. The method enhances local structural representation through image partitioning and structural complexity modeling, and designs a two-dimensional direction-decomposed large-kernel feature fusion module, LL (Long-range and Local Feature Aggregation), to strengthen cross-modal feature alignment. In addition, a complexity-driven smoothness loss function, CARS Loss (Complexity-Aware Regularization for Smoothness Loss), is constructed to impose adaptive constraints on deformation fields across regions of varying complexity. Experiments conducted on the SOPatch and QXS-SAROPT datasets show that DMLLNet outperforms comparison methods in both corner error and displacement accuracy, validating its effectiveness in high-resolution cross-modal registration.

title: Skin Thermomechanical Modeling: Assessing the Influence of Water and Ambient Air
authors: Pezhman Namashiri, Akbar Allahverdizadeh, Fatemeh Khodadoost, Farid Vakili-Tahami
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Skin burn is one of the dangerous incidents that threatens human life on a regular basis. In this study, a thermomechanical model is developed to simulate skin burn by utilizing a multilayer three-dimensional framework of skin that includes the stratum corneum, epidermis, dermis, and subcutaneous tissue. The Pennes model describes bioheat transfer of the tissue. Linear elasticity was utilized for defining skin mechanics. Thermal expansion was incorporated to define the coupling between heat transfer and skin mechanics. In order to investigate the skin cooling response, the influence of ambient air and water on temperature distribution and mechanical response of the skin were analyzed. The findings revealed that that heating causes the top layers of the skin to increase in temperature, but has a minimal impact on the temperature increase of the subcutaneous tissue. Moreover, when water was employed for cooling, the temperature of the skin surface reduced significantly in comparison to the case where ambient air was used. The mechanical behavior illustrated the significant effect of temperature on thermal response. The presented thermomechanical model provides readily implementation with straightforward reproducibility.

title: Biomechanical Analysis and Comparison of Lumbar Spine Fixation: A Finite Element Analysis
authors: Nima Moazed, Mohammad HaghPanahi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Treatment of fractures, deviations, and abnormalities of the spine requires precise and appropriate tools based on various concepts such as rigid stabilization systems, semi-rigid stabilization, and dynamic stabilization. In this study, the biomechanical evaluation of different stabilization systems in the lumbar region (L3-L4) was performed using finite element analysis. A healthy spine model without stabilization and three stabilized models with different rods were considered: a conventional simple rod (rigid stabilization), a curved rod (dynamic), and a notched rod. All spinal models were loaded with a compressive force of 400 Newtons and a moment of 10 Newton-meters in four movements: flexion, extension, lateral bending, and axial rotation. The results for range of motion (ROM), percentage reduction in range of motion, von Mises stress, and percentage reduction in von Mises stress were compared. Precision biomechanical tools for the spine are significantly sensitive to geometric features. The obtained results showed that all three rod models used resulted in the fusion of the lumbar vertebrae (L3-L4). However, considering that the stress and strain on the pedicle screw are critical factors in reducing complications and failures of the spinal stabilization system, the notched rod, due to its lower stress compared to other models, has the best performance.

title: Construction of an indoor natural light target position illuminance prediction model based on machine learning
authors: Ji Yao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: This study addresses the issue of limited prediction accuracy in indoor natural-light illuminance by constructing a machine learning–based model for predicting illuminance at target locations. It systematically evaluates linear regression, SVR, XGBoost, and MLP using sensor-derived data, meteorological parameters, feature engineering, and multi-model training. Experimental results indicate that XGBoost and MLP exhibit consistently strong performance across lighting conditions, with MLP achieving the highest accuracy at R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.92. The findings demonstrate that well-designed feature selection and model optimization substantially enhance prediction performance and offer valuable support for building energy efficiency and daylighting design.

title: Evaluating the Challenge of RE Integration in Small Power Grid by Optimizing Unit Commitment
authors: I Putu Rastaman Putra, Sarjiya, Yusuf Susilo Wijoyo, Edo Adrianto, Yuana Putra Adianto, Faris Fitrianto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The Lombok Power Grid has an installed capacity of 463 MW, while its peak load reaches 385 MW. This system comprises an interconnected system that includes distributed generation units on both the high voltage (HV) and low voltage (LV) sides, in addition to 13 substations and transmission networks. In the global energy transition era, it is essential for the power system to increase the amount of energy mix derived from Renewable Energy (RE) resources. The integration of a substantial volume of RE into a small scale power grid will likely result in an imbalance concerning spinning reserve balance. Continued occurrence may lead to reliability issues stemming from insufficient inertia or spinning reserves within the system. To prevent this issue, it is essential to perform an optimization step in scheduling generators to maintain the availability of spinning reserves within a system that will be integrated with substantial amounts of RE. This study aimed to formulate a mathematical model for unit commitment combined with frequency and inertia constraints to determine system readiness for the implementation of large amount RE penetration. The case study examined the Lombok Power Grid, assuming RE penetration of up to $10 \%$ of the installed capacity. The results of this study demonstrated an increase in system frequency strength of 324 mHz following the disturbance condition, compared to the prior generator scheduling pattern.

title: Research on Attention-enhanced Pavement Pothole Instance Segmentation
authors: Ronggui Ma, Huanhuan Li, Meiyu Xu, Xinyuan Wang, Xiaohui Yang, Yu Li
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: To enhance the detection accuracy and computational efficiency of pavement pothole instance segmentation, an improved method for the YOLO11 lightweight network is proposed. Specifically, the Area Attention mechanism is incorporated into the backbone of YOLO11n-seg to construct an ABlock module, enabling focused attention on pothole core regions and effective suppression of pavement clutter. Tests conducted on the self-collected pavement pothole dataset indicate that the improved model achieves a detection mAP50 of 0.813 and a segmentation mAP50 of 0.821, with notable improvements in recall and precision for both tasks. Furthermore, generalization validation on the open-source POTHRGBD dataset demonstrates that the model attains a detection mAP50 of 0.967 and a segmentation mAP50 of 0.960. While maintaining its lightweight nature and a high inference speed of 303.0 FPS, the proposed model validates the effectiveness and practicality of the developed method for pavement pothole detection and segmentation tasks.

title: Guided Perception and Adaptive Dual-IMM Filtering for Robust Extended Object Tracking
authors: Gongding Chen, Bin Rao, Mingkai Zheng, Dongyang Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Radar seekers of anti-ship missiles face a severe threat from countermeasures like chaff during terminal guidance. When decoys and ship targets are spatially close and kinematically similar, conventional point-target trackers are prone to failure from target confusion and centroid drift. To alleviate the challenges, this paper proposes an integrated framework for extended object tracking and recognition. The framework's main contributions are twofold: First, a prediction-guided dynamic clustering algorithm utilizes track feedback to improve measurement segmentation at the source, particularly when the target and interference coalesce. Second, an integrated filtering architecture is designed where recognition results adaptively modulate the kinematic filter's measurement noise, enabling robust information fusion. Monte Carlo simulations demonstrate that, compared to traditional coupled IMM architectures, the proposed framework improves overall tracking accuracy, while increasing computational efficiency significantly.

title: A Pretraining-Based Deep Learning Framework for Protein Mutation Effect Prediction
authors: Pengyan Zhang, Juntao Deng, Miao Gu, Min Liu
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Accurately predicting the effect of amino acid mutations on protein–protein interaction is essential for understanding molecular mechanisms and guiding protein engineering. Recently, many structure-based methods have been proposed to predict the changes in binding affinity caused by mutations. However, how to effectively exploit the interface features of protein complexes to capture the protein-protein interactions remains a major challenge. In this study, we propose InterDDG, a pretraining-based deep learning framework for predicting the mutation effect. We construct a large-scale, non-redundant dataset of protein–protein interfaces from high-resolution structures in the RCSB PDB and perform self-supervised pretraining using a masked-residue prediction task to learn geometric and compositional patterns at the interface level. The pretrained interface encoder is then combined with an anti-symmetric differential network to effectively capture the structural differences between wild-type and mutant protein complexes. Experimental results on the benchmark dataset and external validation dataset demonstrate that InterDDG achieves significantly improved performance in mutation effect prediction.

title: Deep Learning and Fuzzy Entropy in Parkinson's Diagnosis: A Framework Based on Task-Based EEG Signals
authors: Amir Hossein Tajarrod, Tania Hossein Khani, Asghar Zarei, Mousa Shamsi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is the second most common neurodegenerative disorder worldwide, characterized by reduced dopamine levels in the central nervous system. Electroencephalography (EEG) signals have emerged as a promising tool for diagnosing PD due to their non-invasive nature, low cost, and high temporal resolution. This paper proposes a framework for diagnosing PD in healthy individuals. The proposed framework involves the extraction of fuzzy entropy from sub-bands of wavelets, combined with deep learning networks to classify EEG signals obtained under an auditory oddball paradigm. The deep learning networks used in this study include the EEG Network (EEGNet), Residual Networks within EEG (ResNetEEG), EEG Transformer, and Long Short-Term Memory Fully Convolutional Network (LSTMFCN). Four classification scenarios were explored: healthy control (CTRL) vs. PD patients off medication (PDOFF), CTRL vs. PD patients on medication (PD-ON), PD-ON vs. PD-OFF, and a multi-class. The results indicated that the ResNetEEG network achieved the best average accuracy of 99.78 % for the CTRL vs. PD-OFF classification. In contrast, the LSTMFCN network demonstrated optimal performance for the other classifications, with average accuracies of 99.81 % for CTRL vs. PD-ON, 99.38% for PD-ON vs. PD-OFF, and 99.85% for the multi-class scenario. Both the EEGNet and EEG Transformer networks also showed comparable performance. Even the ROC curves for these networks showed AUC values of 1.0, further confirming the effectiveness of the implemented networks. These results emphasize the significant potential of utilizing EEG-derived features and deep learning techniques for the accurate detection of PD across various clinical scenarios.

title: A study of inertial navigation road detection by unmanned multi-source heterogeneous sensors
authors: Lili Wei, Xingzhi Lin, Tao Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Aiming at the demand of road detection in unmanned complex scenarios, this study proposes a multi-source heterogeneous sensor inertial navigation fusion method. Through the hierarchical progressive fusion architecture, combined with $\mathbf{L i}$ group flow shape optimization and dynamic federal filtering algorithm, the multimodal data of IMU, LiDAR, BeiDou and magnetometer are tightly coupled. A two-stage error compensation mechanism is designed, and a sliding window factor graph is optimized to suppress the accumulated inertial navigation error. The simulation results show that the system lateral positioning error standard deviation is $\leq 0.31 \mathrm{~m}$, the heading angle drift rate is $\lt 0.02^{\circ} / \mathrm{s}$, the road drivable area segmentation intersection and merger ratio (IoU) reaches 92.7%, and the end-to-end delay is controlled within 58 ms. The method provides an effective solution for unmanned high-precision navigation in complex environments.

title: Tomography Versus Conventional Pulse-Echo Technique
authors: Elnaz Rostami Siahpoush, Haniye Fathi, Zahra Kavehvash
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Conventional pulse—echo ultrasound limits boundary depiction and contrast. We propose a dual-probe, phased-array reflection ultrasound computed tomography (RUCT) scheme in which one probe transmits diverging waves and the opposing probe records oblique echoes, providing near 360° angular coverage. The approach is evaluated in numerical simulations on generic heterogeneous 2-D phantoms under matched acquisition budgets. Images are reconstructed with delay-and-sum beamforming and compared with linear and single-probe phased acquisitions. The dual-probe, diverging configuration yields crisper delineation of convex and concave boundaries, stronger target-to-background separation, and more uniform intraregion intensity. The region-of-interest SNR and CNR increase consistently, while the diverging transmission reduces the number of shots, thereby decreasing the scan time and data volume required for comparable coverage. Although limited to simulations, the results indicate a practical route to fast, high contrast reflection tomography compatible with clinical arrays.

title: AI Powered Multiagent Workflow for Vernacular Technical Short Film Production
authors: Padma Adane, Nachiket Deshpande, Aditya Chavhan, Mandar Gurjar, Utkarsh Lambade, Aniket Pingley
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-powered multiagent workflow for producing vernacular technical short films aimed at children aged 5 to 12. The system transforms simple text prompts in regional languages into fully animated and narrated educational videos, emphasizing historical and cultural relevance. It comprises three core agents: a Visual Agent for scene generation, an Audio Agent for natural-language narration, and an Integration Agent for synchronization and assembly-all coordinated by a lightweight orchestrator. Designed for scalability and accessibility, the pipeline runs efficiently on modest hardware and supports eight Indian languages. A case study on the Makar Sankranti festival demonstrates its ability to generate high-quality, localized content in minutes, offering a scalable solution for democratizing educational storytelling.

title: Evaluation of DDoS Attack Detection in IoT Systems using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Putu Handayani, Helena Widiarti, Syafrudi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The security of Internet of Things (IoT) systems is becoming increasingly critical due to the growing number of connected devices and the rising complexity of cyber threats, such as Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks. In this work, DDoS attacks on IoT systems using machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Decision Tree were evaluated. The dataset used is CICIoT2023, which contains network traffic data from both normal and attack conditions. The research process includes data preprocessing, feature selection using the Random Forest Classifier, model training, and performance evaluation. The dataset was split into 70% training data and 30% testing data. Model performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The results show that the Random Forest algorithm achieved the highest performance with an accuracy of 99.96%, followed by KNN and Decision Tree with accuracies of 99.91% and 99.93%, respectively. Feature importance analysis revealed that the most influential features in detecting DDoS attacks are syn_flag_number, Header_Length, syn_count, Number, and Protocol Type. Based on the running time evaluation, Random Forest demonstrated the fastest computation time at 53 seconds, with a model file size of $\mathbf{1 K B}$. These findings indicate that selecting relevant features significantly contributes to detection accuracy. Therefore, the Random Forest algorithm is proven to be effective and reliable in detecting DDoS attacks on IoT systems.

title: Dempster-Shafer combination rule for multi-sensor data fusion driven by Zero Trust Security over IIoT (DS-MSDF)
authors: Anamika Singh, Puneet Sharma, Durgesh Nandan, Paresh Nasikkar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) plays a pivotal role in smart data collection and retrieval across interconnected devices. It establishes seamless communication among heterogeneous devices using internet connectivity, resulting in the generation of substantial volumes of unstructured data through embedded smart sensors. With the advancement of digitization, IIoT has emerged as a dominant framework that leverages intelligent devices to autonomously monitor, manage, and execute operations, ensuring optimized performance across industrial environments. In IIoT systems, multiple smart sensors collaborate to gather raw data from various sources distributed across the network. This interconnected ecosystem leverages diverse sensing units to enable multi-source data collection, offering richer, more accurate, and context-aware insights compared to single-sensor systems. Unlike traditional systems, the use of multiple data sources in IIoT enables cross-verification, enhances compatibility, and delivers richer information, which ultimately contributes to improved service quality and operational efficiency. Such multi-perspective data acquisition supports more reliable decision-making and enhances service delivery with improved quality and standardization. The proposed study introduces a robust data fusion model that significantly enhances the performance of IIoT networks. It outperforms conventional techniques such as "Single Source Data Fusion" (SSDF) and "Bayesian Estimation Data Fusion" (BEDF), achieving a 6% and 2% in accuracy and data confidentiality rate, respectively.

title: A Fine-Tuned GoogleNet and ShuffleNet V2 for Non-Invasive Diabetes Detection Using Photoplethysmography Signals
authors: G. Praveena, Ramkumar Natarajan, B. Girirajan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The accurate interpretation of raw Photoplethysmography (PPG) signals has always remained difficult because signal artifacts, motion noise, and bodily variability intervene, severely affecting classification in medical diagnosis. The problem of accurately detecting diabetes through a non-invasive method is addressed via a robust Deep Learning (DL) approach, wherein a fine-tuned GoogleNet, coupled with ShuffleNet V2, is proposed to extract complex features from preprocessed PPG signals. The publicly available PPG-BP dataset is preprocessed for noise and baseline drift removal using the Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT), and six statistical features are derived to improve the representation of the data. The patients are clustered into diabetic and non-diabetic groups based on clinical labels using the developed fine-tuned GoogleNet along with ShuffleNet V2. The model employs convolutional, pooling, and inception layers to extract features at multiple scales, followed by dense and dropout layers for learning in a robust manner. The ShuffleNet V2 block is introduced to learn a lightweight channel-wise feature representation, and finally, a dense layer with a sigmoid activation function outputs the binary classification result. The model greatly outperformed conventional models with an accuracy of 98.45%, precision of 96.31%, recall of 96.30%, specificity of 98.45%, and an AUC of 0.9845.

title: A Novel AR-Based Kalman Filtering Framework for ECG Enhancement
authors: Hamed Danandeh Hesar
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Model-based Bayesian methods, while effective for preserving ECG morphology, often demand extensive preprocessing (e.g., R-peak detection, phase assignment, offline parameter optimization), hindering their real-world use. Our prior adaptive Kalman filter bank reduced preprocessing to Rpeak detection but introduced computational complexity and iterative parameter adjustments via expectation maximization. To address these limitations, we introduce a novel approach: an autoregressive (AR) state model embedded within a two-stage Kalman filter framework for ECG denoising and parameter estimation. Critically, this method eliminates the need for any preprocessing, including R-peak detection. It achieves this by adaptively estimating model parameters and dynamically adjusting state and measurement noise covariances based on intrinsic noise characteristics. Extensive evaluations on benchmark datasets (MIT-BIH Normal Sinus Rhythm, Arrhythmia; Creighton University Ventricular Tachyarrhythmia; Sudden Cardiac Death Holter) reveal that our proposed AR-based Kalman smoother (AR-KS) surpasses state-of-the-art model-based adaptive Kalman filter banks, particularly for signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs) above 0 dB.

title: Design of Multi-Source Data Fusion Target Recognition System Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jinli Che, Liqing Fang, Qiao Ma, Guibo Yu, Xinyue Wang, Xiaoting Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: To address the issues of low target recognition accuracy and poor robustness of single sensors in complex environments, a multi-source data fusion target recognition system is designed and implemented by combining multi-sensor fusion technology with deep learning algorithms. The system integrates complementary data from visual sensors, lidar, and infrared sensors, achieves spatiotemporal alignment of multisource data through precise joint calibration, and completes feature-level and decision-level fusion using deep learning models. Ultimately, it realizes all-weather and high-precision target recognition, which can meet the environmental perception needs of the system in scenarios such as severe weather and low visibility.

title: S-PPR: A Semantic-aware Personalized PageRank Framework for Academic Author Recommendation
authors: Yazhe Li, Qizhe Li, Zhijie Miao
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: The rapid growth of scholarly publications has made it increasingly challenging for researchers to identify suitable collaborators. This paper presents a Semantic-aware Personalized PageRank (S-PPR) framework that integrates structural co-authorship information with semantic signals extracted from publication titles. Unlike conventional structure-based PPR approaches, S-PPR constructs a semantic-informed teleportation vector via cosine similarity between author text embeddings and reweights the transition matrix by jointly incorporating co-authorship frequency and semantic proximity. This dual integration enables random-walk propagation that is sensitive to both topical relevance and collaboration patterns. Experiments conducted on a public dataset demonstrate that S-PPR substantially improves semantic alignment and recommendation relevance compared with standard PageRank. The results confirm the effectiveness of semantic-aware propagation for generating accurate and interpretable academic author recommendations.

title: Optimized Hybrid Framework of DenseNet121 and MPCNN for Early Gastric Cancer Detection in Histopathological Imaging
authors: Radhika Shetty D S, D Dhrusha Ghate, Kavya Nayak K, Pooja P C, Pranamya B
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Gastric cancer is among the most aggressive and prevalent malignancies worldwide, with early detection being critical to improving survival outcomes. This study proposes an optimized hybrid deep learning framework integrating DenseNet121 and a Multi-Perspective Convolutional Neural Network (MPCNN) for histopathological classification of gastric tissues. DenseNet121 captures hierarchical and global representations, while MPCNN extracts fine-grained, multi-scale local features. Their feature maps are fused through concatenation before classification, enabling comprehensive representation of tissue morphology. The model was trained using data augmentation and cross-validation on GasHisSDB and SEED datasets. The hybrid model achieved 88.6% accuracy, outperforming individual DenseNet121 85.8% and MPCNN 82.0% models. These results confirm the effectiveness of feature-level fusion in improving early gastric cancer detection.

title: A Robust Feature Selection Model for Adaptive E-Learning Recommender Systems
authors: Rakesh Chandrashekar, Manish Gupta, V.Arun Kumar, Sk Mahammad Shareef, Nadimpalli Venkata Ganapathi Raju, Abhishek Kaushik
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: E-learning platforms are growing daily to help users. This study introduces an upgraded strategy for feature selection to enhance the reliability of e-learning system recommendations. The recommended approach utilizes feature selection algorithms such as ICA, Chi-Squared, Simulated Annealing (SA), and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to enhance input data quality while reducing dimensions without sacrificing vital information. Developing a content matrix for material classification and recommendation purposes utilizes Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF). The recommendation model integrates Logistic Regression with a Deep Neural Network (DNN) based on a four-layer feed-forward Multilayer Perceptron (MLP). The system learns from user-generated information which includes details about pages accessed and ratings plus learning preferences. The system utilizes deep learning algorithms to capture intricate non-linear patterns in user behavior for personalized recommendation purposes. The hybrid feature selection method results in both better classification accuracy and improved computational efficiency. The recommended approach aims to refine e-learning recommender systems by providing personalized material suggestions that create more effective and engaging learning experiences. The suggested approach achieves 90% accuracy compared to other methods.

title: Touchless Control Interface Using Mediapipe and Kalman Filter
authors: Aldrin Binu, Aneena M S, Namitha Anna Koshy, Rithika Krishna, Elvin Kuruvilla
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a cutting-edge, contact-free interface that enhances digital interaction through sophisticated hand gesture recognition. By harnessing computer vision techniques via OpenCV and MediaPipe, we introduce a unified framework that seamlessly translates manual motions into accurate, real-time system commands. The proposed method addresses key challenges in human-computer interaction by enabling recognition of seven distinct gestures, including pinch, toggle, scroll, drag, eraser, and copy paste which function as intuitive alternatives to traditional input tools, particularly in environments where touch is impractical or unhygienic. Achieving a gesture detection accuracy of 93.75% and an average response time of 13.98 milliseconds, the system operates on standard webcams, eliminating the need for specialized hardware while ensuring cost efficiency and responsiveness. Comprehensive testing confirms its applicability across diverse scenarios such as classroom engagement, professional presentations, and assistive interfaces. By merging advanced visual processing with an instinctive mode of control, this work represents a meaningful advancement in the development of natural user experiences. The results highlight the transformative potential of gesture-based technologies in reshaping digital communication and fostering more intuitive human-machine synergy.

title: Design of High-Speed Communication Simulation Interface System Based on FPGA
authors: Zengchao Liu, Xianguo Zhang, Youwen Zhang, Fanjun He, Chunchen Dong, Zhuang Wang, Wenbo Zhang, Duo Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: In response to the issue that the RTDS simulation device and the control and protection device in the DC transmission control and protection system verification cannot be directly and effectively connected, this paper conducts research on PCIe communication, Aurora communication and IEC60044 communication based on the FPGA platform, proposes the design method of Aurora high-speed communication, data packet buffering and flow control mechanism and the custom IEC60044 communication protocol, and designs a high-speed communication simulation interface system based on FPGA. The experiment-al results show that through the Aurora input communication and IEC60044 output communication test and CRC check monitoring, the system can accurately receive RTDS simulation data, and can flexibly configure the data channel and the number of sending routes according to the settings of the upper computer, effectively meeting the diverse configuration requirements of users.

title: Comparative Analysis of Dataset Balancing and Augmentation Techniques for Pedestrian Action Recognition Using the JAAD Dataset
authors: Rajasree R, Sreelatha G
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Recent innovations in autonomous driving and automated driver assistance systems have highlighted pedestrian action recognition as a crucial research area. The Joint Attention in Autonomous Driving (JAAD) dataset is a popular publicly available dataset for pedestrian action recognition. However, this data set suffers from severe imbalance between classes of different attributes, leading to reduced performance. This paper presents five data balancing and augmentation techniques, namely conditional augmentation (Method I), conditional over- sampling (Method II), targeted augmentation for all attributes (Method III), targeted augmentation for selected attributes (Method IV), and targeted augmentation with cross attribute re-balancing (Method V), that can be applied to the JAAD dataset to alleviate the class imbalance problem. The effectiveness of these approaches are evaluated using a CNN-GRU architecture with dual attention mechanism, utilizing precision, recall, f1-score, accuracy, Categorical Cross Entropy (CCE) loss, and Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) as the evaluation metrics. The results demonstrate that the proposed methods improve performance, especially by reducing the CCE loss. Method IV offers a 11.68% reduction in average CCE loss and 6.94% and 2.41% increase in the average f1-score and AUC, respectively, compared to results without balancing. Method V gives the best results for cross attribute, with 18.25% reduction in CCE loss and 18.03% and 9.72% increase in f1-score and AUC, respectively.

title: Vision Transformer with Mixture-of-Head Attention and Memory Bank for Industrial Anomaly Detection
authors: Yi Fu, Yuxin Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Industrial anomaly detection faces challenges in capturing both global contextual dependencies and defect-typespecific patterns using traditional CNN-based methods. We propose a novel framework integrating Vision Transformer (ViT) for hierarchical feature extraction with a Mixture-of-Head (MoH) attention mechanism for adaptive defect modeling. Our approach treats attention heads as specialized defect detectors, employing dynamic routing to assign patches to relevant heads based on anomaly characteristics such as scratches, contaminations, and structural distortions. A memory bank of normal features enables efficient anomaly scoring via nearest-neighbor distance computation, while shared heads maintain global coherence across product categories. Load balancing regularization ensures diverse head specialization without redundancy. Experiments on the MVTec AD dataset demonstrate superior performance, achieving $99.1 \%$ image-level AUROC and 98.6% pixel-level AUROC, outperforming state-of-the-art methods including PatchCore, EfficientAD, and ViTAD by $2.3-4.7 \%$. Ablation studies confirm the critical roles of dynamic routing and memory bank integration in robust anomaly localization.

title: Who Knows What? Semantic Negotiation for Human-Supervised RAN Agentic Coordination
authors: Eduardo Baena, Ankita Mandal, Abhiram Elango, Phuc Dinh, Dimitrios Koutsonikolas
publish: Proceedings of the 27th International Workshop on Mobile Computing Systems and Applications

authors: Yawen Liu, Swarun Kumar
publish: Proceedings of the 27th International Workshop on Mobile Computing Systems and Applications

title: DEMO: CellWatch
authors: Beatriz Palacios Abad, David Peeler, Amanda Meng, Ellen Zegura, Elizabeth Belding, Morgan Vigil-Hayes
