title: A Real-Time Textile Defect Inspection System for Industrial Polyester Reinforcement Layers Based on YOLO and PLC-Driven Closed-Loop Control
authors: Xin Wang, Yuzhuo Bi
abstract: Manual inspection of textile fabrics remains common in industry, yet it is labor-intensive, subjective, and difficult to scale to high-speed production lines. This paper presents a real-time fabric defect inspection system for the middle reinforcement layer of high-strength industrial polyester filaments. The system integrates a YOLO-based detector with a Mitsubishi FX-series programmable logic controller (PLC) to enable closed-loop defect-triggered stopping of a rotary table or conveyor in an industrial setting. Five defect categories are considered: CL (disordered pattern), BJ (tight warp), TS (shuttle skipping), Short (short loose end), and Long (long loose end). To reduce production-specific false alarms, we propose a task-specific multi-stage post-filtering strategy for Short defects, combining an edge-based boundary constraint, class-specific confidence thresholding, and a minimum area constraint, with parameters selected on a held-out validation set and fixed for testing and deployment. The method achieves strong overall performance on the test set (precision 94.3%, recall 97.7%, mAP@0.5 98.9%, mAP@0.5:0.95 71.9%) and improves precision to 95.2% after applying the full post-filtering pipeline. We further benchmark YOLOv5s against recent lightweight variants (YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s) under the same protocol, reporting both detection metrics and PLC-integrated end-to-end latency (mean and P95) to quantify the accuracy–latency trade-off. The deployed system runs on an industrial PC (HIKROBOT MV-VC3704X-128G60) and maintains an average processing time of approximately 315 ms per frame on CPU-only inference. In on-line operation, the closed-loop system achieves an average end-to-end stop latency of about 420 ms with a low false stop rate of 0.35 per hour over 120 hours of continuous production, demonstrating robust long-term stability.

title: Privacy-Preserving Multi-Class Skin Lesion Classification using Single-Shot Machine Unlearning and Noise Maximization
authors: Aryan Nikul Patel, Ramalingam Murugan, Gokul Yenduri, Praveen Kumar Reddy Maddikunta, Bharath Bhushan Savarala
abstract: The fusion of data analytics, machine learning, and artificial intelligence has transformed various sectors, notably healthcare. This rapid advancement has also revolutionized dermatology, offering remarkable accuracy in skin lesion classification. In highly confidential domains like dermatology, the task of unlearning the model’s training data becomes crucial. Unlearning optimizes data processing by deleting unnecessary information, thereby lowering the burden on healthcare devices. However, the integration of these models into clinical environments presents a unique challenge: the need to balance model performance with patient privacy. This paper presents a novel approach addressing the imperative task of unlearning specific classes from a pretrained model, focusing on pigmented skin lesions. We present a one-epoch method that adds noise to trained models using the HAM10000 dataset, strategically targeting the forgetting of specific classes. Then, we use a fine-tuning phase with a single epoch to re-calibrate the model for the remaining classes. This provides a full framework for perturbation and recovery. We evaluate the efficiency of this approach across four distinct model architectures: MobileNet-V2, EfficientNetB2, ResNet-50, and VGG-19. We demonstrate the robustness and adaptability of the proposed method, providing insights into its applicability in real-world clinical environment for dynamic data management and model refinement.

title: On-Line Distributed Model Predictive Scheduling for Multi-Vehicle Routing Problems With Lane-Change and Platoon Maneuvers
authors: Nianhua Zhang, Fernando Viadero-Monasterio, Jicheng Chen, Hui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) have garnered significant attention in the field of smart logistics due to their potential to greatly enhance transportation efficiency. While significant focus has been placed on the capabilities of individual AVs, less attention has been given to the complexities of coordinating multiple vehicles within dynamic traffic environments. Key factors such as combined traffic flow, varying traffic signals, and complex cooperative maneuvers play a major role in effective multi-vehicle scheduling. This paper aims to address these overlooked challenges by exploring dynamic traffic conditions and micro-level cooperation within multi-vehicle routing systems. The large-scale multi-vehicle routing problem (MVRP) is reformulated into a series of smaller-scale cooperative vehicle routing problems (VRPs). A distributed model predictive control-based (DMPC-based) planner is constructed and applied in parallel across connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs). Each instance of distributed model predictive scheduling incorporates a closed-loop vehicle dynamics to predict lane-changing and platooning maneuvers, where feedback controllers are introduced. To optimize routes toward destination, a destination-oriented search space is rebuilt with the filtered feasible routes. By accessing real-time traffic lights, a model predictive control (MPC) problem is formulated, where the vehicle-lane distribution is predicted with a sub-optimization of the shortest queue and combined with the predictive scheduling. Comparisons against existing methods reveal that the proposed planner exhibits significant improvements in time efficiency and successes to avoid traffic congestion, which is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ZNianHua/DMPC-planner-for-VRP</uri>

title: Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Prediction Based on Continuous Conditional Random Field
authors: Miao Yu, Jinyang Han, Huiran Zhang, Ahmed Abu-Siadad, Bin Song
abstract: Accurate photovoltaic (PV) power prediction is essential to facilitate the integration of renewable energy sources. Among other things, the results of PV power prediction depend heavily on weather conditions. However, current methods either construct Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) for prediction through physical models only or use machine learning methods to create time series for prediction. The aim of this paper is to combine accurate NWP information with machine learning methods to fulfil the task of PV power generation prediction using high-precision NWP data. To this end, we propose a Continuous Conditional Random Field (CCRF) model integrating Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) and contrast learning, which first generates NWP data with high spatial and temporal resolution as input features by constructing a weather research and prediction model. Then, we use KAN to train a unitary potential function in a stochastic conditional field, and then learn the time-dependent information from the pairwise potential function through contrast learning modelling. Finally, prediction results are produced from a real photovoltaic power plant in Australia. Compared to existing methods, our proposed approach not only improves the accuracy of PV power prediction, but also reduces the complexity of the model and improves the overall efficiency.

title: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
authors: N/A

title: In-Memory-Computing For Machine Cognition at the Edge
authors: Andreas G. Andreou, Michael Tomlinson, Richard Niu, Adil Malik, Kwesi B. Debrah, Georgios Psaltakis, Daniel R. Mendat, Philippe O. Pouliquen, Jamal Molin, Steven Bottone, Sergio Montano, Louise Sengupta, Isidoros Doxas
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: In-Memory-Computing (IMC) is an alternative computer architecture to the standard von Neumann machine, in which compute functionality is incorporated with the state holding elements - memory. This idea is inspired from biology, where memory and processing are integrated in a single structure, the synapse. In this paper we explore the sometimes counterintuitive implications of integrating processing within the memory cells demonstrate how the latter can be leveraged to dramatically increase energy efficiency of computing hardware for machine cognition. We demonstrate the performance in real-world application examples on actual scaled up physical hardware (a 79 core IMC System On Chip (SOC). We finally show how recent developments allow IMC computers (as opposed to von Neumann computers with IMC accelerators) have the weights storage density necessary for embodied intelligence and machine cognition at the edge.

title: Sustainable Edge Deployment of Consumer-Centric Digital Twins for Healthcare 5.0: A Resource-Efficient Optimization Framework
authors: Jialin Li, Rahul Kumar, Sachin Kumar, Keqin Li, Jianhui Lv
abstract: Healthcare 5.0 emerges as a transformative paradigm positioning patients as active participants through consumer-centric digital twin technologies that enable proactive health management and personalized interventions. Consumer-centric digital twins (CCDTs) function as high-fidelity virtual patient replicas continuously processing physiological data streams from wearable sensors and medical devices to predict health trajectory deviations and recommend timely corrective actions. While patient CCDTs exhibit heterogeneous requirements for processing electrocardiograms, glucose monitors, and mobility data. This study formulates sustainable CCDT deployment as a binary integer programming problem minimizing total system latency under computational capacity, storage limits, synchronization requirements, and collaboration needs, proving its NP-hardness. The proposed sustainable patient-centric edge deployment (SPCED) algorithm combines branch-and-bound initialization through linear relaxation with matching-theory refinement using move and swap operations, achieving bilateral exchange stability. Simulation results demonstrate that SPCED outperforms six baseline algorithms, including state-of-the-art decentralized federated learning approaches, achieving 99.81% computational efficiency improvement while maintaining superior latency performance across varying patient populations, resource configurations, and collaboration patterns, validating its effectiveness for sustainable healthcare 5.0 CCDT ecosystems.

title: ECroA: Efficient Cross-Domain Authentication in Dynamic Digital Twins of Wireless Industrial IoT
authors: Yingjie Xia, Xuejiao Liu, Yidan Yin
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: The development of wireless industrial internet of things (IIoT) has facilitated the widespread deployment of dynamic digital twins across multiple factories and regions. However, the wireless communication between digital twins and their physical devices takes place over public channels, and the dynamic behavior of digital twins makes them susceptible to forgery and impersonation. As a result, data exchanged between digital twins is exposed to risks of tampering, fabrication and corruption. Moreover, real-time data sharing among a large number of digital twins incurs significant computational and communication overheads, thereby necessitating a secure and efficient cross-domain authentication scheme to ensure trustworthy identities and data integrity. To address these challenges, we propose ECroA, an efficient cross-domain authentication in dynamic digital twins of wireless IIoT. Specifically, we employ a distributed key management mechanism to establish trust across domains while reducing communication overhead. Additionally, we design an identity-based signature and pseudonym-based authentication scheme to provide conditional privacy preservation, ensuring secure data exchange without exposing sensitive data. To further enhance efficiency, we integrate signature aggregation and batch verification techniques, significantly reducing computational costs for large-scale cross-domain authentication. Performance analysis demonstrates that ECroA effectively enhances authentication efficiency while ensuring secure cross-domain authentication in dynamic digital twins of wireless IIoT.

title: Preparation and Performance Optimization of ZnO Nanorod/PVDF-TrFE Composite Material and Its Application in Flexible Piezoelectric Sensors
authors: Shi Feng, Rui Li, Quanbin Ma, Juqi Wang, Haoyang Chen, Hongjun Qian, Fengjuan Xu, Shishang Guo
abstract: The development of flexible wearable sensors in health monitoring has garnered significant attention, and piezo electric pressure sensing technology has emerged as a piv otal research direction. This study successfully fabricated a n ovel ZnO NRs/PVDF-TrFE nanocomposite material for construct ing high-performance piezoelectric nanogenerators (PENG). Zn O nanorods (ZnO NRs) were synthesized via high-temperature hydrothermal synthesis and integrated into the PVDF-TrFE poly mer matrix through electrospinning. The effects of ZnO content on the piezoelectric performance and β-phase crystallinity of th e composite were systematically investigated. Experimental res ults demonstrated that a 5 wt% ZnO content yielded optimal co mposite performance: achieving a β-phase content of 87.6% an d a significant voltage output increase to 12 V. The composite fi lm exhibited excellent mechanical stability, maintaining stable e lectrical output after 3000 consecutive compression cycles, pro ving its potential as a durable wearable device. Finally, when ap plied to monitor standing long jump movements in adolescents, the sensor demonstrated outstanding dynamic response capa bility and sensitivity, validating its practical value in real-time hu man motion monitoring.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Mask Reconstruction via Kernel-Based Surrogate Modeling for Automated Circulator Design
authors: Marco Atlante, Riccardo Trinchero, Igor S. Stievano, Mihai Telescu, Vincent Laur, Noël Tanguy
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter introduces a mask-based surrogate modeling framework that generalizes the feature-based paradigm for efficient microwave design exploration using a limited number of full-wave simulations. Unlike feature-based approaches, which extract scalar performance metrics, the proposed method reconstructs the complete <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-parameter boundary contour within the operating band. This preserves each design’s parametric signature and enables on-demand evaluation of multiple performance specifications without model retraining. The framework employs two vector-valued kernel ridge regression models to predict boundary coordinates from geometric parameters. Validation on a three-port circulator demonstrates accurate reconstruction and rapid exploration of thousands of candidate designs.

title: Robust and Efficient Cooperative Perception Under Vehicle-to-Vehicle Communication Impairments
authors: Tao Tang, Chaokun Zhang, Gong Chen, Jinlong E, Pengcheng Lyu
abstract: Cooperative perception facilitated by vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) data sharing has emerged as a crucial enabler for safe and efficient autonomous driving. However, the current state-of-the-art algorithms are unable to resolve severe performance degradation caused by communication impairments in realistic V2V scenarios. This paper models the V2V communication quality and confirms their fragile robustness under loss conditions. To this end, we propose a robust and efficient cooperative perception framework. Specifically, we propose RoCooper, a robust fusion algorithm. It leverages the lossless ego feature as an anchoring foundation, then utilizes multi-dimensional feature correlations and dynamic regional selective cross-learning. This allows it to perform multi-scale feature recovery and judiciously fuse multi-view features from neighboring vehicles. In addition, we design ReduAdapt, an efficient redundancy-aware scheduling scheme that first constructs hierarchical metadata to decompose 3D perception space, then applies dynamic multi-factor thresholds for region-specific cropping, and finally performs retention-driven adaptive compression, enabling connect vehicles to prioritize critical data streams while dynamically suppressing redundant transmissions, thereby maximizing effective throughput. Extensive evaluations of real-world datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in varying impairment scenarios, while delivering more efficient compression-transmission at comparable levels.

title: Encoder Circuit Optimization for Non-Binary Quantum Error Correction Codes in Prime Dimensions: An Algorithmic Framework
authors: Aditya Sodhani, Keshab K. Parhi
abstract: Quantum computers are a revolutionary class of computational platforms with applications in combinatorial and global optimization, machine learning, and other domains involving computationally hard problems. While these machines typically operate on qubits—quantum information elements that can occupy superpositions of the basis <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$|0\rangle$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$|1\rangle$</tex-math></inline-formula> states—recent advances have demonstrated the practical implementation of higher-dimensional quantum systems (qudits) across various hardware platforms. In these hardware realizations, the higher-order states are less stable and thus remain coherent for a shorter duration than the basis <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$|0\rangle$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$|1\rangle$</tex-math></inline-formula> states. Moreover, formal methods for designing efficient encoder circuits for these systems remain underexplored. This limitation motivates the development of efficient circuit techniques for qudit systems (d-level quantum systems). Previous works have typically established generating gate sets for higher-dimensional codes by generalizing the methods used for qubits. In this work, we introduce a systematic framework for optimizing encoder circuits for prime-dimension stabilizer codes. This framework is based on novel generating gate sets whose elements map directly to efficient Clifford gate sequences. We demonstrate the effectiveness of this method on key codes, achieving a 13–44% reduction in encoder circuit gate count for the qutrit (d = 3) <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[[9,5,3]]_{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[[5,1,3]]_{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[[7,1,3]]_{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula> codes, and a 9–21% reduction for the ququint (d = 5) <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$[[10,6,3]]_{5}$</tex-math></inline-formula> code when compared to prior work. We also achieved circuit depth reductions up to 42%.

title: Uncertainty Propagation in GaN HEMT Small-Signal Models for Millimeter-Wave Circuit Design Applications
authors: Jason Shell, Edward Gebara, Nicholas C. Miller
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of S-parameter measurement uncertainties on broadband modeling and circuit design using gallium nitride (GaN) high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) from 0.5–110  GHz. A process for creating a small-signal model (SSM) for the GaN device is described, as well as a method for propagating uncertainties through SSM simulations. This work identifies two salient SSM parameters to which uncertainties can be applied to replicate the trends in the measurement uncertainties. A comparison of the propagated uncertainties in the measurement and the model data is analyzed. Two matching network circuits were designed utilizing the SSM, and the effect of uncertainties on these circuit designs is discussed. These results indicate that measurement uncertainty has a notable impact on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S_{11}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S_{22}$</tex-math></inline-formula> in both conjugate match network designs and specified gain network designs, with more variation present in conjugate match designs. From these results, we conclude that there is an inverse relationship between the amount of mismatch in RF matching network circuit designs and the impact of uncertainties on their input and output matching conditions. Even in circuits designed for no mismatch, there is still a noticeable variation in S-parameters, particularly in the phase of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S_{22}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The findings of this investigation could be useful for RF engineers to better understand uncertainties in measurements and extracted models, as well as integrated circuit designs that utilize them.

title: An Accurate Conformal Weakly Conditionally Stable FDTD Scheme and its Application on Electromagnetic Shielding Effectiveness Analysis
authors: Kaikun Niu, Junqiang Wang, Jirui Zhang, Yingsong Li, Zhixiang Huang, Xianliang Wu, Atef Z. Elsherbeni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a conformal weakly conditionally stable finite-difference time-domain (WCS-FDTD) method, termed the C-WCS-FDTD method, to overcome accuracy limitations in simulating complex electromagnetic structures. The WCS-FDTD method provides significant advantages for modeling practical structures with very fine features in one or two directions, as its time step is determined solely by the coarsest spatial discretization. However, like the conventional FDTD method, it suffers from staircase error when handling complex geometries. This study presents the first implementation of conformal techniques into the WCS-FDTD method, significantly improving simulation accuracy for perfect electric conductor (PEC) structures while maintaining the original Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy (CFL) stability condition of the WCS-FDTD scheme. The study provides detailed derivations of the C-WCS-FDTD iterative equations and rigorously examines the method’s numerical stability through von Neumann analysis. Comprehensive numerical validations, including scattering characteristics of canonical shapes (sphere and cylinder) and electromagnetic shielding effectiveness (SE) analysis of practical enclosure structures, demonstrate that the C-WCS-FDTD method not only achieves superior computational accuracy but also substantially reduces simulation time for complex models compared to the traditional WCS-FDTD method. These significant improvements position the proposed method as an efficient and accurate computational tool for practical engineering applications involving intricate structures.

title: Poster: Bridging the Gap: E2SM-SENS for ISAC-Native O-RAN Architectures
authors: Rajesh Krishnan, Eduardo Baena, Dimitrios Koutsonikolas
publish: Proceedings of the 27th International Workshop on Mobile Computing Systems and Applications
abstract: None

title: Machine Learning Models for Optimizing Industrial Defect Prediction: Enhancing Accuracy and Computational Efficiency
authors: Said AlMaqbali, Asma Marhoubi, Oualid Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: One of the most important goals of modern engineering is to reduce production defects which directly influence operational efficiency, quality of products and the satisfaction of customers. The proposed work examines how to apply a supervised machine learning model in predictive defect detection using an open-access dataset collected from kagle.com, which contains $\mathbf{3, 2 4 0}$ records and 17 productionrelated features. This work test the 12 classifiers, Random Forest, LightGBM, Bagging, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), Decision Trees, Naive Bayes, AdaBoost, and Logistic Regression, and others in both an imbalanced and balanced version with the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). The performance of each classifier was tested by Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC and computing time. The obtain results in this work show that Random Forest and LightGBM displayed the highest accuracy (96.1%) on an imbalanced dataset but with modest ROC-AUC ($\sim 0.89$). The Random Forest and LightGBM models were both affected with the discriminative power (ROC-AUC) increasing by 0.991 after the SMOTE-balancing procedure and by 0.989 after the Post-SMOTE-balancing procedure. LightGBM outperformed Random Forest and represented the favorable model in implementing the application in real-life practice due to its higher runtime efficiency ($\sim 1.3$ seconds), compared to a similar parameter of Random Forest ($\sim 11$ seconds). The proposed work provides an effective and scalable way of incorporating predictive analytics in industrial processes to allow proactive quality control and mitigation of defects.

title: A Modular Architecture for Inter-Fog Environments
authors: Henrique Buzin, Alysson Oliveira, Mauro Marques, Patricia Della Méa Plentz
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: Fog computing brings processing and storage closer to data sources, aiming to reduce latency and communication overhead in distributed systems. Although reference models such as IEEE 1934 (OpenFog) provide high-level guidelines, practical architectures that address interoperability, scalability, and co-ordination across fog domains remain limited. Previous studies have examined container placement, hierarchical scalability, and multi-layer orchestration, but direct inter-fog communication and modular abstractions are less explored. This work presents a modular architecture that organizes fog nodes into protocol, processing, and service layers, supporting configurable services and request redirection between domains. A prototype was implemented using Docker containers representing smart city devices operating with HTTP and CoAP protocols. An experimental evaluation illustrates how the architecture manages request distribution, protocol handling, and data consolidation across environments. These results show the adequate performance of the proposed fog computing modular architecture for smart cities.

title: Multi-Objective Adaptive MPC for Integrated Path Tracking and Obstacle Avoidance in Mobile Robots
authors: Taoxiang Cai, Guangping Qiu, Weiming Chen, Qingyuan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Achieving high-precision tracking and reliable obstacle avoidance remains a challenge for Model Predictive Control (MPC) due to its typically static parameterization. To address this, this study proposes a Multi-Objective Adaptive MPC (MO-AMPC) framework that unifies path tracking and avoidance. The architecture integrates three key innovations: Bézier curvebased path smoothing for trajectory continuity, a dynamic mechanism for tuning control weights and prediction horizons, and a risk-gradient avoidance strategy embedded directly within the cost function. Simulation results demonstrate that MO-AMPC significantly outperforms traditional benchmarks, reducing average tracking error by $23.5 \%$ and improving control smoothness by $31.7 \%$ while maintaining a stable 30 Hz control frequency. This approach offers a robust, real-time solution for enhancing robot autonomy in complex environments.

title: Gravity-Directed Growth of ZnO Nanorods: Morphological Control via Chemical Bath Deposition
authors: Masoud Ghashami, Melisa Daryayi, Mohammad Abdolahad
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: The morphology of Zinc Oxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{ZnO})$</tex> nanorods(NRs) strongly influences their performance in biosensing and cancer cell culture applications. Although chemical bath deposition (CBD) is a cost-effective method for ZnO synthesis, the influence of gravity on nanorod growth remains underexplored due to difficulties in controlling substrate orientation. In this study, we developed a custom-built holder that enables precise upward (against gravity) and downward (with gravity) growth orientations during CBD. Using this setup, we demonstrate that gravity significantly impacts NR morphology, downward growth produced nanorods with smaller, more uniform diameters, improved vertical alignment, and eliminated parasitic microrod clustering. The resulting nanostructured surfaces enhanced cancer cell culture interfaces, highlighting the importance of substrate topography for biological interactions. Our results introduce both a practical tool for orientation, controlled CBD and new insights into gravity, directed nanostructure growth.

title: Ensemble Learning Framework for Predicting Medication Effectiveness based on Drug-Disease Association
authors: Dipali Dakhole, Lahitanshu Das, Aryan Kore, R Yogesh, Smriti Sachan, Kailash Sharma
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The ability to assess medication efficacy based on patient test results can significantly help with diagnosis, therapy planning, and patient outcomes in the modern medical era. This study uses machine learning (ML) to predict how medications like aspirin, paracetamol, ibuprofen, etc. affect conditions like cancer, arthritis, asthma, diabetes, etc. Based on the outcomes of diagnostic tests, the drug's effectiveness is indicated by the categories of Normal, Abnormal, and Inconclusive. The incorporation of predictive modeling for decision-making using Random Forest (RF), Extra Tress, XG Boost, and Voting Ensemble is new. In ensemble framework, 55,500 records and 15 characteristics were preprocessed. With an accuracy of 71.69%, recall of 89.7%, and F1 score of 67.7%, the voting ensemble model performed better.

title: LDAF-Net: A Lightweight Dual-Stream Attention Fusion Network for Ore Image Segmentation in Harsh Mining Environments
authors: Yuanxi Zhang, Xueyu Huang, BinHui Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: A Lightweight Dual-stream Attention Fusion Network (LDAF-Net) is proposed for ore image segmentation on high-speed conveyor belts under harsh mining environments. LDAF-Net embeds a LightGGM bottleneck into an improved ResUNet to reassemble ASPP with depth-wise separable dilated convolutions, capturing 5–200 mm particles in one forward pass; channel-attention re-weighting reduces computation by 89%. A SimpleEAM edge branch is plugged into the decoder to form a closed-loop reinforcement with the segmentation backbone, suppressing boundary degradation caused by dust and glare. Dual-task learning jointly optimises region segmentation and edge detection using a weighted combination of binary-cross-entropy and edge-aware losses. The whole model occupies only 2.1 MB after INT8 quantisation and runs at 23 FPS on Jetson Xavier. Evaluated on 3 000 real production images from China Minmetals graphite mine, LDAF-Net achieves 92.3% mIoU and 88.7% edge F1-score, outperforming the best lightweight competitor by 6.8% mIoU and 2.9× speed (mean±std, n = 3, p < 0.01). Edge integrity remains > 85% even under heavy dust or over-exposure, offering an accurate, low-latency and deployment-friendly paradigm for intelligent mining.

title: Network for Predicting Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy Response in Breast Cancer Patients
authors: Parisa Donyaei, Javad Haddadnia
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Among women worldwide, breast cancer continues to represent one of the most critical and challenging medical conditions, demanding ongoing improvements in diagnostic and therapeutic strategies. Due to factors like tumor adhesion or large volume making some patients initially ineligible for surgery, neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NAC) aims to shrink tumors and enable breast-conserving surgery while assessing early treatment response. However, accurately forecasting an individual's response to NAC remains a major clinical challenge. It critically impacts personalized treatment planning, patient prognosis, and survival rates. It is also essential for minimizing unnecessary treatment-related toxicity. In this work, we designed an attention-based dual-branch convolutional network (AG-CNN) that analyzes dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI scans to forecast the likelihood of achieving a pathological complete response (PCR) before therapy. Our framework integrates global contextual learning with region-specific analysis by employing attention maps that guide the network toward clinically informative tumor subregions. When evaluated on the selected dataset, the architecture demonstrated strong predictive power, reaching an overall accuracy near 90.76 %, with sensitivity exceeding 96.66 % and specificity close to 89.95 %. The model outperformed baseline methods lacking attention mechanisms, demonstrating the significant advantage of integrating attention in clinical outcome prediction. These results highlight the potential of attention-driven MRI analysis to guide personalized therapy planning.

title: Mapping Epileptic Networks: IED-Triggered Hemodynamic Changes Identified via Simultaneous EEG-fMRI Recordings
authors: Elias Ebrahimzadeh, Hora Amoozegar, Mostafa Asgarinejad, Melika Akbarimehr, Hamid Soltanian-Zadeh
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: A central goal of the presurgical evaluation for refractory focal epilepsy is the accurate delineation of the epileptogenic zone (EZ)-the cortical region indispensable for generating epileptic seizures. Given that electroencephalography (EEG) provides superior temporal resolution while functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) offers enhanced spatial localization, integrating these modalities holds significant promise for improving epileptic focus identification. In this study, we first derived characteristic spike patterns by detecting and averaging interictal epileptiform discharges (IEDs) from extraoperative EEG recordings. These patterns were then correlated with intracranial EEG data to develop an automated system for precise temporal mapping of seizure activity. Finally, we convolved the resulting temporal regressor with the hemodynamic response function (HRF) within a general linear model (GLM) framework to achieve robust localization of epileptic foci. This study was performed on five medication-resistant epilepsy patients whose neuroimaging and electrophysiological data were acquired at the National Brain Mapping Lab (NBML). Our proposed methodology demonstrated strong concordance with clinical EEG findings across all five cases. Notably, for the three surgical candidates, the approach provided additional localizing information beyond conventional EEG data. Quantitative analysis revealed statistically significant enhancements in both localization accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) and spatial precision (mean ± SEM: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.3 \pm 0.4 \text{mm}$</tex>) compared to current gold-standard techniques reported in recent literature.

title: Depression and Psychosis Using EEG-Based Machine Learning Models
authors: Vahid Asayesh, Mehdi Dehghani, Majid Torabi, Sepideh Akhtari-Khosroshahi, Maedeh Akhtari-Khosroshahi, Sebelan Danishvar
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Accurate differential diagnosis of major depressive disorder, psychosis, and psychotic depression remains a major challenge in psychiatry due to overlapping clinical symptoms and the reliance on subjective assessment tools. This study aimed to identify objective EEG-based neuromarkers that distinguish between these three diagnostic groups using quantitative EEG features and machine learning. Resting-state EEG was collected from 60 medication-free adult patients clinically diagnosed via DSM-based psychiatric interviews. A total of 26 linear and nonlinear features, including relative power, cordance, alpha peak frequency, Lempel—Ziv complexity, largest Lyapunov exponent, and sample entropy, were extracted across 19 scalp channels. Statistical group comparisons with FDR correction identified significant feature—channel combinations, which were then ranked using the MRMR algorithm. Stepwise feature inclusion and classification with SVM, KNN, and MLP were performed. Results showed that 42 selected features achieved optimal accuracy, with KNN yielding 96.53 ± 0.20% accuracy. These findings highlight the potential of EEG neuromarkers to support precision psychiatry and improve diagnostic specificity.

title: Vision Transformer-Based Framework for Automated Melting Point Determination from Chemical Substance Images
authors: Anurag Shrivastava, Bhavana Shrivastava, Animesh Srivastava, Anil Dahiya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: The Proposed work utilized Vision Transformer (ViT) to create a complex and robust deep learning framework that can automatically find the melting points of chemical compounds by categorizing high-resolution images. The proposed model uses self-attention mechanisms in transformer designs to record specific visual cues that show when a solid changes into a liquid. A specific collection of high-resolution images of powdered chemical compounds shot in a controlled temperature range and labelled with the correct temperature values and physical states. The ViT architecture looks at images as sequences of patches of a certain size and uses multi-head selfattention to find the contextual dependencies and global spatial correlations that are necessary for phase recognition. After finding the visual characteristics that go along with the liquid state, the temperature at which this classification happens at that instant, the present time is recorded as the melting point of the chemical compound. Tests indicate that the ViT model attains an accuracy of 99.87 %, markedly surpassing that of conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and AlexNet architectures. The model is quite good at generalizing, doesn't get affected by noise in the environment, and works well in different lighting circumstances. These results show that transformer-based visual models are good at collecting and analyzing thermal transition data. This makes it possible to quickly, automatically, and on a large scale estimate thermal properties in chemical analysis.

title: Efficient Vector Search for RAG and Document Classification Using Principal Vectors
authors: Ritesh Karval, Kamakhya Narain Singh
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Classification algorithms like K-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), SVM, Random Forest, and neural networks are widely used to group data into similar classes. However, these classification algorithms don't consider the direction of the data where the vectors have a significant effect, and focus on the nearby neighbors, or features, which result in misclassification for edge case samples. This paper proposes an approach to categorize datapoints with the principal vectors of class distribution acting as the class identity. We combine this classification method with a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) framework to retrieve related vectors quickly and precisely for each new search. This combination has shown faster results and more relevant matches with diverse vectors.

title: State of the Art an Hybrid Fast Deep Learning Approach:h-QGAN for Healthcare Image Data
authors: Chapala Maharana, Ch. Sanjeev Ku. Dash, Ajit Ku. Behera
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: We find machine learning applications in healthcare industry, business intelligence, natural language processing, image processing, robotics, production industry management and monitoring, space craft analytics. We have used one of the hybrid deep learning models for image data analytics in healthcare. This implementation is made using quantum GAN (generative adversarial network). Quantum computer has the advantage of processing data ata faster rate than classical computers. It uses qubits than bits to represent the data in a smallerdimension than the existing higher dimension of the original data and found effective than classical computers in terms of data processing speed. The healthcare image data is found in fewer amounts and of low quality. Generative adversarial network is used to generate synthetic data for rarehealthcare image data. GAN with quantum latent space called quantum generative adversarial network (QGAN) is used to generate high amount of image data ata faster rate. It makes the decision making with better accuracy than classical convolutional neural network approach. The proposed work is a combination of QGAN which is combination of QGAN and QNN called hybrid QGAN (h-GAN) learning from Covid-19 lungs image datasets from kaggle repository.

title: Communication Security: Quantify Clock Jitter-Based Countermeasures Against Deep Learning Side-Channel Attacks
authors: Chao Zhou, Caijian Luo, Huanyu Wang, Junnian Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: With the rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT), Side-Channel Attacks (SCAs) increasingly threaten communication security by exploiting leakages from devices (e.g., electromagnetic radiation, power consumption). Combined with the data analysis capabilities of deep learning, SCAs expand their threats to more communication security domains. To mitigate SCAs, clock jitter-based countermeasures are widely proposed and applied, yet their precise effectiveness in combating SCAs lacks sufficient evaluation. This paper assesses the side-channel resistance of clock jitter countermeasures through experimental comparisons using deep learning-based SCAs. To ensure the validity of results, experiments are conducted across three AES implementations and two side channels (electromagnetic radiation, power consumption). We also perform extensive quantitative ablation tests to evaluate the countermeasure resistance at varying strengths, and conduct tests across two attack scenarios (attackers with/without countermeasure information) to gauge the impacts of scenarios. Our findings provide insights into the resistance of clock jitter under different adversarial intensities and scenarios, offering valuable data for countermeasure selection and laying a foundation for side-channel security applications.

title: MediDT: Digital TwinBased Predictive Analytics for Personalized Patient Recovery
authors: Mahek Mukadam, Mohammed Nadeem Shaikh, Mariyam Usmani, Shreeya Kalwar, Mohammad Sami Ansari, Arshi Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: In this paper, we present MediDT, a Digital Twinbased predictive analytics framework designed to forecast patient recovery trajectories and assess individualized risk profiles. Unlike conventional clinical prediction models that provide static estimates, MediDT dynamically simulates patient health progression by integrating real-world clinical records with digital twin forecasting. Our framework employs supervised machine learning models trained on synthetic patient datasets, incorporating features such as demographics, comorbidities, activity levels, sleep quality, and vitals. MediDT generates forward-looking "what-if" scenarios, allowing clinicians to evaluate the impact of lifestyle changes (e.g., increased physical activity or improved sleep) on recovery outcomes. Furthermore, the system merges predicted recovery curves with actual patient data in unified Excel exports, enabling physicians to compare real and simulated trajectories for clinical validation. Experimental evaluation shows that MediDT achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.3 days, an RMSE of 3.8 days, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.91 in recovery prediction, while risk classification attains 89.7% accuracy and an F1-score of 0.88. These results demonstrate that MediDT provides reliable, interpretable, and actionable forecasts for personalized healthcare monitoring. By offering a scalable and interactive decision-support tool, MediDT bridges the gap between predictive modeling and practical clinical application.

title: Computational Aspects of Nonlinear, Stochastic and Complex Pandemic Models with Vaccination
authors: Saptarshi Das, Lamia Alyami
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has stimulated extensive research on nonlinear, stochastic, and complex epidemiological models capable of explaining recurrent waves, uncertainty in disease transmission, and the impact of vaccination strategies. In this paper, we investigate the computational aspects of a six-dimensional epidemiological model with vaccination, fitted to COVID-19 data from Saudi Arabia. Particular emphasis is placed on bifurcation analysis with respect to the vaccination rate, which reveals that beyond a critical threshold, the system undergoes a Hopf bifurcation, giving rise to oscillatory epidemic dynamics. These oscillations are validated using long-horizon time-domain simulations and confirmed through phase-portrait visualizations, providing insight into the mechanisms behind recurrent waves observed in pandemic data. To contextualize this study, we conducted a literature survey of nonlinear, stochastic, fractional-order, and agent-based models, highlighting how different modeling frameworks capture memory effects, demographic stochasticity, network heterogeneity, and individual-level interactions. We also review advanced computational tools for analyzing nonstationary epidemiological time series, including spectral and bispectral methods, Lyapunov exponents, detrended fluctuation analysis, recurrence plots, Poincaré maps, and multivariate techniques for cross-country comparisons. The study highlights the importance of combining dynamical systems theory, stochastic processes, and data analytics for epidemic forecasting. We also discuss how these computational approaches can inform vaccination policy design and emphasize their relevance for preparedness against future pandemics, particularly when data availability is limited and pathogen characteristics are initially uncertain.

title: Role of Big Data Analytics in the Business Arena
authors: Satyam Prakash Srivastava, Rupa Khanna Malhotra, Mukul Bhatnagar, Amit Kumar Uniyal, Ranjit Kumar Mukherji, Riya Sharma
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Over the span of years, data analytics have brought significant changes in the business world. In recent years, the role of data analytics has gained impetus in terms of growth and has eventually made businesses take strategic decisions. Through Big voluminous data, insightful information is derived from the raw data set, and this has become a game-changer for most of the business tycoons. It’s more about knowing about the potential taste and preferences in advance and assisting companies and organisations to do promotions effectively. Giant companies are managing to save their significant amount of cost and making the investments more effective. In this paper, a glimpse of big data analytics and its journey in different arenas is reflected through different updates. Further, a holistic approach is followed through data analytics to elaborate its role.

title: A Novel Wideband Circular Microstrip Antenna Employing Slots and Parasitic Elements for 0.1 THz 6G Applications
authors: Yashika, Joel Saxena, Vaishali Kikan, Ashwni Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: In this paper, circular microstrip patch antenna formulated for terahertz (THz) 6G communication systems is presented. This antenna is developed on low-loss Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate (ε_r = 2.2) and integrates parasitic circular stubs along with precisely shaped slots on both patch and ground plane. The features included to improve impedance matching, wide bandwidth and increase the radiation efficiency. This antenna indicates dual resonance at 99.8 GHz & 100.0 GHz, attaining return loss of-51.62 dB & -37.23 dB. Moreover, it reports a wide impedance bandwidth from 95.6 GHz to 121.7 GHz. Peak gain of 10.93 dBi is noted, confirmed by stable impedance values (50.229 Ω real and 0.141 Ω imaginary) and very low VSWR levels of 0.0456 and 0.2388 at both resonant frequencies. Radiation patterns in both E- and H-planes remain stable & symmetric across operating band, confirming strong directivity and high radiation efficiency. Thanks to its small design, broad bandwidth and excellent overall performance, the proposed antenna is best choice for next generation 6G and THz communication, imaging and sensing applications.

title: Automated Text-to-Video Integration with Audio and Question Answer Generation
authors: G Stalin Babu, Swathi Lenka, V Yamuna, G Vijay Kumar, S N Tirumala Rao, Palo Sangeeta
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Automatic text-to-video integration with audio and query generation the paper focus on converting text into video content with synchronized audio narration and comprehension questions. Using MoviePy to generate videos, API to store relevant text, and T5 model to generate queries, the system is easy to create multimedia educational content. In this function, the script is launched from the API and used as the script of the video. MoviePy place this text in a movie, image, or background, combine transitions and effects to create a composite video. The gTTS and pyttsx3 convert text into beautiful speech, then synchronize with the video to keep the audience engaged. To facilitate comprehension, T5 model automatically generates questions that stimulate thinking and enhance the understanding of key concepts in the content. Employees save a lot of time and resources. Combining audio, video and interactive content, the system provides a high-quality, efficient solution for creating rich content, making it useful in education, training and entertainment areas where educational value is important.

title: A Survey on Machine Learning-Based Fault Detection and Correction in Lithium-Ion Batteries using CatBoost Algorithm
authors: Logesh K S, Karunamoorthy B
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The growing need of the stable lithium-ion batteries in electric cars and power storage systems requires smart fault management systems. Conventional Battery Management Systems (BMS) use set thresholds and therefore cannot adjust to nonlinear battery characteristics. The current paper consists of a hypothesis concerning the implementation of a data-driven framework of fault detection and correction based on the CatBoost machine learning algorithm along with an IoT-based sensing platform. The voltage, current and temperature data are detected in real-time to categorize faults like thermal stress, overcurrent, short circuit and cell imbalance. When this is detected, the system takes automatic corrective measures to avoid degradation and increase safety. CatBoost has been experimentally validated to have an accuracy of over 97% which is higher than SVM, Random Forest and XGBoost. The offered framework will make the traditional BMS a predictive and self-correcting system, enhance the reliability of the battery, operational safety and life as well as allow scaling smart energy management.

title: Crisp Attention: Regularizing Transformers via Structured Sparsity
authors: Sagar Gandhi, Vishal Gandhi
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: The quadratic computational cost of the self-attention mechanism is a primary challenge in scaling Transformer models. While attention sparsity is widely studied as a technique to improve computational efficiency, it is almost universally assumed to come at the cost of model accuracy. In this paper, we report a surprising counter-example to this common wisdom. By introducing structured, post-hoc sparsity to the attention mechanism of a DistilBERT model during fine-tuning on the SST- 2 sentiment analysis task, we find that model accuracy improves significantly. Our model with 80% attention sparsity achieves a validation accuracy of 91.59%, a 0.97% absolute improvement over the dense baseline. We hypothesize that this phenomenon is due to sparsity acting as a powerful implicit regularizer, preventing the model from overfitting by forcing it to make predictions with a more constrained and robust set of features. Our work recasts attention sparsity not just as a tool for computational efficiency, but as a potential method for improving the generalization and performance of Transformer models.

title: Multi-Objective Deep Reinforcement Learning with Fuzzy Evolutionary Reward Shaping for Pareto-Optimal Policy Optimization
authors: Tina Babu, Rekha R Nair, Firoz Khan, Thrilok Kolla, Balamurugan Balusamy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Multi-objective reinforcement learning (MORL) seeks to optimize multiple conflicting goals simultaneously, but conventional fixed-weight scalarization methods often bias learning toward limited regions of the Pareto front. To address this, we propose a novel fuzzy evolutionary reward shaping framework for multi-objective deep reinforcement learning that dynamically adapts scalarization weights to the environmental context. The core idea integrates fuzzy inference with genetic algorithm (GA)-based parameter evolution, enabling adaptive mapping of normalized spatial depth into objective weights that balance treasure collection and time efficiency. The technology leverages deep reinforcement learning agents guided by fuzzy GA-shaped scalarization, contrasted against fixed-weight baselines across 500 episodic experiments. Results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves robust convergence with an average scalarized return above 15, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.91, and greater dispersion across the Pareto front, ensuring diverse policy outcomes. Error distributions concentrate in high-return regions, validating the resilience and adaptability of the method. This hybrid fuzzy-evolutionary framework introduces novelty by embedding interpretability and adaptability directly into reward shaping, overcoming static bias. By advancing reliable and adaptive policy optimization, the proposed work can support societal needs in autonomous navigation, robotics, resource allocation, and smart decisionmaking systems, contributing to efficiency and sustainability in real-world environments.

title: YOLOv8 Optimization and Implementation for Small-Scale Object Detection in Drone Aerial Images
authors: Zishuo Wei, Kun Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Small object detection in UAV aerial imagery remains challenging due to small object scales, dense distributions, and complex backgrounds, making it difficult for existing detection methods to balance accuracy and real-time performance. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved small object detection model, termed SOD-YOLOv8, based on the You Only Look Once (YOLOv8) framework. The proposed method introduces targeted optimizations in multi-scale feature modeling, detection head design, and loss function formulation. Specifically, an enhanced multi-scale feature fusion strategy is employed to emphasize high-resolution features, thereby improving the representation capability for small objects. The detection head architecture is further refined to bias detection toward shallow, high-resolution feature maps, which effectively enhances small object localization and recognition. In addition, a quality-aware loss function is incorporated to reduce the adverse effects of low-quality samples and noisy annotations during training, improving model robustness in complex UAV aerial scenes. Experiments conducted on the VisDrone-DET2019 dataset demonstrate that SOD-YOLOv8 achieves significantly improved small object detection accuracy while maintaining high inference speed. These results validate the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed method for UAV-based small object detection tasks.

title: Enhanced YOLOv11 for small object detection in UAV images
authors: Guangcan Jiao, Hongwei Gao, Jian Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: To address the challenges in small object detection for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), including limited pixel representation, large-scale variations, and high sensitivity to background interference, this paper proposes an improved algorithm based on YOLOv11. First, a novel CMUNeXtBlock module is introduced to enhance the feature extraction capability of the backbone network. Second, a new Feature Fusion Module (FFM) is designed with a Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN)based neck structure, and a Normalized Wasserstein Distance Loss (NWDLoss) is incorporated to improve the stability of small object detection. Experimental results on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves a $2.2 \%$ improvement in detection accuracy, measured by mean Average Precision (mAP50), compared with YOLOv11n.

title: Postural Responses to Mediolateral Rotations: Contributions of Surface, Vision, and Cognitive Load
authors: Haniyeh Zahra Budaqi, Ali Mojibi, Saeed Behzadipour
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Maintaining postural stability is a complex process influenced by sensory, motor, and cognitive factors. Dynamic balance tests provide valuable insight into these mechanisms by challenging balance under controlled conditions. This study investigated the effects of surface type, visual availability, and cognitive load on postural control during mediolateral rotational disturbances in healthy young adults. Fifteen participants completed trials on a robotic balance platform under eight conditions combining rigid or foam surfaces, eyes open or closed, and with or without a cognitive task. During each trial, ML platform rotations were applied while Center of pressure (COP) responses were recorded. COP data were analyzed across position, velocity, and frequency domains to capture both static and dynamic aspects of postural control. Analysis of variance revealed that vision significantly affected all COP domains, surface type primarily influenced dynamic and frequency measures, and cognitive load altered static and frequency measures. No significant interactions were observed, indicating that each factor contributed independently to balance regulation. These findings suggest that surface, vision, and cognitive load each impose distinct challenges to postural control during ML disturbances, highlighting the importance of evaluating multiple task constraints when assessing balance performance.

title: Task Allocation for Dual-Robot Cooperative Disassembly of Retired Power Battery Modules Using an Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Xinying Li, Wenhua Ye, Weifang Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: With the rapid growth of retired power batteries, efficient and safe robotic disassembly has become a critical challenge in automated recycling systems. This paper investigates the dual-robot task allocation problem for cooperative disassembly of surface components in power battery packs. An optimization model is formulated by jointly considering disassembly sequence constraints, spatial collision avoidance, and operational safety. To solve the resulting combinatorial problem, an improved genetic algorithm is developed by incorporating a rational initial solution strategy, directional crossover, and elitist preservation mechanisms to enhance convergence efficiency and solution stability. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior task allocation solutions with faster convergence, while effectively reducing the overall disassembly time and idle traveling cost of robots. These results indicate that the proposed approach is effective and suitable for practical applications in automated power battery disassembly systems.

title: Efficient Hydroporation in Immunotherapy Applications
authors: Soheil Mahdavi, Zohre Nazemi Dehkordi, Ali Abouei Mehrizi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Efficient intracellular delivery of therapeutic biomolecules is a critical challenge in biomedical applications, including cancer immunotherapy, gene therapy, and drug delivery. Hydroporation, a mechanoporation-based microfluidic technique, has emerged as a promising method to transiently permeabilize the cell membrane through controlled hydrodynamic stresses. In this study, we present a novel circular cross-junction microchannel with non-colinear inlets designed to generate a mixed extensional-rotational flow, enhancing both shear and vorticity at lower Reynolds numbers compared to conventional square cross-junctions. Three-dimensional numerical simulations were performed across Reynolds numbers <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-366$</tex> to analyze flow behavior, including vorticity distribution, recirculation zones, and vortex formation. The results demonstrate that the circular geometry induces stable vortical structures and a well-defined central stagnation point, enabling simultaneous extensional and torsional deformation of cells. Shear stresses in the circular junction were found to be nearly three times higher than in conventional geometries at comparable flow rates, suggesting potential for high-efficiency membrane poration while maintaining cell viability. These findings indicate that the proposed design can achieve enhanced hydroporation efficiency with reduced mechanical stress, offering a scalable platform for high-throughput intracellular delivery. Future experimental validation and further optimization of junction geometry may expand its applicability to diverse cell types and therapeutic cargos.

title: An 8-Way E-Band GaAs Power Amplifier Utilizing Shared-Ground Vias
authors: Zhiyi Liu, Göksu Kaval, Rob Vissers, Gregor Lasser
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: In this paper, an 8-way 3-stage E-band gallium arsenide (GaAs) power amplifier utilizing shared-ground vias is presented. To save area, the ground vias of neighboring transistors are shared, while keeping the gate and drain connections separate. This requires careful de-embedding of the via influence in the transistor model as is outlined in this paper. In measurements, the PA has more than 10 dB small signal gain covering from 60 to 81.4 GHz and at 78 GHz produces 28 dBm of output power with a maximum PAE of 27.1%.

title: Performance Assessment of Diverse Artificial Intelligence Driven Water Quality Prediction Procedures
authors: Mamata Garanayak, Pankajini Tripathy, Suvendu Kumar Nayak, Subhra Gantayat, Bijay Kumar Paikaray, V.S. Damodharan
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: A very basic fundamental human right is the access to clean and safe drinking water. Water potabilty testing needs lab facilities and often takes time and also inaccessible in remote regions. We depicted a performance assessment of various artificial intelligence driven machine learning prototypes to predict the potability of water. The dataset collected consists of 9 important features of water. Several machine learning procedures are implemented and compared including decision tree, random forest, SVC, GradientBoosting and bagging classifier. These prototypes were evaluated using precision, recall, F1 score, accuracy, and ROC-AUC, which are the standard performance metrics. These prototypes exhibit accuracies of 60 \%, 68 \%, 69 \%, 78 \% and 75 % respectively. Among all of them, the GradientBoosting classifier delivered the highest accuracy i.e. 78 %. The findings specify that artificial intelligence driven machine learning prototypes can play a substantial character in predicting water potability. Integrating IoT data, optimization of prototypes as well as developing user friendly app is the future extension of the proposed assessment.

title: Analysis and Designing of a Novel Compact 2-Port MIMO Antenna for n260 band 5G Applications
authors: K. Vijayakumar, Brijesh Mishra
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This manuscript focuses on the design and performance evaluation of a novel compact 2-port MIMO antenna that is designed for 5G frequency applications in the n260 band, starting from 37 to 40 GHz. In the new antenna design, an effective and compact radiating structure is proposed, which has been optimized for reduced size, wide bandwidth, and good radiation performance. To enhance the isolation between the ports, the antenna exploits the spatial diversity technique that reduces the ECC and enhances the DG. In this way, the optimum functioning of MIMO is guaranteed. The antenna element comprises a circular radiating patch that is excited with uneven power by a coplanar feed. The antenna exhibits a wide bandwidth that covers the n260 band, while showing adequate gain and high radiation efficiency, rendering it suitable for 5G frequency communications at very high data rates. Simulation results have shown a compact structure featuring a high level of isolation and exhibiting very good performance for a wide range of signals, and thus it is a potential candidate for next-generation mm-wave MIMO applications.

title: A multimodal biometric system based on multi-level fine-tuning using score fusion
authors: Cheyma Nadir, Youcef Brik, Bilal Attallah, Abdelwahhab Boudjelal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: Multimodal biometric recognition has gained significant attention as it substantially improves personal recognition performance by leveraging multiple data sources. This paper presents an approach to biometric recognition utilizing both unimodal and multimodal systems, with a focus on finger knuckle prints (FKP) and palmprints. We use the IIT Delhi datasets for both modalities and investigate the performance of three prominent Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures: VGG19, VGG16, and MobileNetV2. Our methodology comprehensively evaluates three learning strategies: transfer learning, full fine-tuning, and fine-tuning of the top layers. Through extensive experimentation, we identify the optimal CNN model and learning strategy for each biometric modality. In our unimodal experiments, the best-performing model achieves state-of-the-art accuracies of 98.10% for FKP and 99.13% for palmprints. Subsequently, we develop a multimodal system that employs score fusion techniques to combine the outputs of the best-performing model for each modality. This combination generates a powerful and accurate multimodal biometric system with a precise recognition rate of 100%. This significant improvement over the unimodal systems demonstrates the effectiveness of our approach in leveraging the strengths of multiple biometric traits.

title: Multi-Organ Plant Disease Detection Using CNN and Machine Learning: A Root-to-Leaf Approaches
authors: Shibi B, Shyam Sundar N, Abhinav George, Saran R, Bhavanshree U, Nagasuri Meerash
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Detecting crop diseases is critical but labor-intensive task in agriculture, often requiring expert knowledge and manual inspection. This paper describes an efficient technique for automated disease using computer vision and Machine learning. The system analyzes images of plant leaves, stems, and roots to identify symptoms with high accuracy using Otsu's thresh-olding. A structured data acquisition process ensures quality input, while convolutional neural networks (CNNs) enable robust classification. This approach reduces reliance on skilled labor, supports early disease intervention, and improves overall crop health monitoring. The solution is designed for scalability and real-time use, including mobile-based applications for on-field diagnosis.

title: Exploring Sentiment Dynamics in the Bhagavad Gita Using Zero-Shot Learning and BART
authors: Vikram Singh, Bhavya Gaur, Lov Kumar
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The Bhagavad Gita is a rich source of philosophical and spiritual wisdom, expressing deep moral reasoning and emotional insight. Unlike social media data, it presents abstract, context-rich emotions that go beyond typical sentiment labels like positive, negative, or neutral. Its 700 verses reflect diverse human sentiments relevant to emotional intelligence use-cases such as stress, anxiety, and self-regulation. However, the absence of annotated emotional labels makes verse prediction a complex task. This study explores the sentiment landscape of Gita's verses using a learning-based system. We propose a Zero-Shot Learning approach with Bidirectional and Auto-Regressive Transformers (BART) to assign emotion labels and assess sentiment without relying on large annotated datasets. Validated through open-source models, our approach captures a wide emotional spectrum, including devotion, peace, love, etc, highlighting its potential in affective computing and emotion-aware applications.

title: Development of a 3D Simultaneous Localization and Mapping System for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Navigation
authors: Fethi Demim, Aimen Abdelhak Messaoui, Souhila Benmansour, Ali Zakaria Messaoui, Yamina Aouimer, Abdenebi Rouigueb, Ouail Moumeni, Akrem Boutaghan, Abdelkrim Nemra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This article presents the design and implementation of an autonomous navigation framework for a Quadrotor-Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) based on Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM). The proposed system leverages the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)-SLAM algorithm, operating solely on LiDAR data and without reliance on GPS, making it highly suitable for navigation in GPS-denied or cluttered environments such as indoor settings, tunnels, and urban canyons. A dynamic model of the UAV is developed to describe its motion under 3-DoF, forming the basis for both simulation and control. The EKF-SLAM algorithm is implemented within the Robot Operating System (ROS) and tested using the Gazebo simulation environment. Experimental results confirm the algorithm’s effectiveness in real-time localization and 3D environment mapping using LiDAR data, even in the absence proprioceptive input sensor.

title: The Right to See and Be Heard: Assessing the Legal and Technological Framework of AI Driven Visual and Sign Language Assistants in India for Independent Living
authors: Girish Kulkarni, Vijayata G Uikey, Dipali Mankar
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This research paper examines the intersection of artificial intelligence (AI) and the rights of persons with disabilities in India. This paper argues AI based assistive technologies are not mere technological conveniences but necessary to uphold fundamental rights. In this research paper we are going to examine the contribution of AI and ML based solution for realisation of accessibility and full Participation of persons with disabilities in the real life. This paper analyse the role of legal mandates like United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (UNCRPD) and India's Rights of Persons with Disabilities (RPwD) Act, 2016 for ensuring accessibility for visual and hearing disabilities. The AI solutions which are present in real world are assessed on the basis of their affordability, reliability, availability and efficiency for democratic users. Only AI solutions can not be possible on its own but it is enforced in the form of digital accessibility through activist model of judicial behaviour. Recognition of constitutional guarantees for the disability rights is a tool for substantive and transformative equality for which the papers provides practical policy based and efficient funding recommendations

title: Research on Improved YOLOv11s for Aerial Drone Recognition and Detection
authors: Yanyang Li, Jie Zheng, Shengzhe Wang, Huaqian Xi, Yi Zhang, Xiao Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Aiming at the technical challenges in the detection of low, slow, and small (LSS) unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in the air, such as small target pixel ratio, insufficient feature information, and susceptibility to environmental interference, this paper proposes an improved lightweight YOLOv11s algorithm for UAV detection. Based on the YOLOv11s model, the proposed algorithm designs an SC-DSCONV context information extraction module, integrates a spatial-channel attention mechanism module and a lightweight module, and adds a small-target detection layer to expand the model’s recognition range for extremely small-sized targets. Experimental validation on the self-constructed dataset shows that, compared with the baseline YOLOv11s, the improved model achieves a 7.8% increase in mAP50 and a 0.81M reduction in the number of parameters. While maintaining lightweight characteristics, it realizes a significant improvement in the detection performance of LSS UAVs, making it more applicable to UAV recognition and detection in real-world scenarios.

title: Human Resource Demand Prediction Based on Particle Swarm Optimization and Convolutional Neural Network Model
authors: Kezia Rani Burgula, Md. Yusuf Mulge, Ambrayya, A. Thangam, Swati Kunwar, Monika Gulati
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The impact of human resource demand projections on company operations is investigated in this study. Human resources departments need to recruit new employees every year to meet the changing demands of organizations brought about by scientific and technological advancements. Accurately evaluating HR requirement and linking it to business goals is a challenge for many firms. To maximize HR allocation, a realistic and accurate prediction model is required, particularly when making hiring decisions at the beginning of the year based on historical data. We propose a new model, the CNN-PSO, to address this problem. Data collection and normalization preprocessing are the first steps of the methodology. Feature extraction via principal component analysis (PCA) is then used to identify significant variables. Preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification are the three stages that make up the CNN-PSO model. Results from an experimental evaluation using a standard HR dataset show that the CNN-PSO model outperforms state-of-the-art approaches to HR demand forecasting. Validating its durability and applicability, the model achieved a high prediction accuracy of 95.61%. To help businesses with strategic planning and effective labour management, this study provides a realistic and efficient method for forecasting the demand for human resources.

title: A Reverse Firewall & Re-Encryption Model Supported Peks Model for Security in Health Based Applications
authors: Krishna VN Addapalli, Ancy P. R.
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Combining reverse firewalls and reencryption in a PEKS (Public Key Encryption with Keyword Search) model introduces a powerful way to mitigate malicious client behaviour and enhance privacy, especially in untrusted or semi-trusted environments. A reverse firewall is a client-side monitor or proxy that sits between a cryptographic application and the network. Its goal is to ensure that compromised software does not leak any information, even if the software is compromised. In this context a reverse firewall supported with Proxy re-encryption (PRE) supports a secured scheme for applications which require high level of security in public domain scenarios like Cloud for data sharing forms. For schemes which require good level of Identity applications and also needs the support of data Integrity, reverse firewall associated with Re encryption may be a suitable choice. The model is refined with conditional time stamp in accepting the keys for re encryption process. As this model can use different random numbers at different levels of Reverse firewall and re encryption, makes the work free from Chosen cipher text attacks. Also, the proposed model supports Design, Modelling and security analysis in a real time environment.

title: Universal, Topological Approach to Black Hole Phase Transitions
authors: Krishnakanta Bhattacharya, Kazuharu Bamba, Douglas Singleton
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
