Model evaluation was conducted using Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) and Mean Squared Error (MSE). The results show that LSTM outperformed in predicting electricity consumption (KWh) with a MAPE of $0.04 \%$ and MSE of 0.0677, while Linear Regression performed better in predicting power (VA) with a MAPE of $0.0018 \%$ and MSE of 0.0000047. These findings indicate that LSTM is more suitable for complex and fluctuating data, whereas Linear Regression is better suited for stable and linear data.

title: In Synthetic and Natural Bone: A Comparative Study
authors: Mahdi Farrokhi Kashtiban, Gholamreza Rouhi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Rigid polyurethane (PU) foam, commonly referred to as synthetic bone, is widely used in orthopedic and dental implant research due to its uniform properties and ease of use and handling. However, its ability to accurately represent natural bone behavior remains a subject of debate. The aim of this study was to evaluate if PU foam produces comparable primary stability outcomes to those of natural bone. Forty implants from two manufacturers, i.e., Cowellmedi and DRI, with identical dimensions (4 mm diameter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 10 ~\text{mm}$</tex> length), but different thread and conical designs, were inserted into PU foam blocks <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.2 ~\mathrm{g} / \text{cm}^{3})$</tex> and also into fresh bovine ribs. Primary stability was assessed using maximum insertion torque (MIT) and resonance frequency analysis (RFA), the latter of which provides implant stability quotient (ISQ). The findings revealed that in natural bone, Cowellmedi implants demonstrated significantly higher MIT compared to DRI, but ISQ values did not differ significantly. In contrast, in synthetic bone, both MIT and ISQ showed no significant differences between Cowellmedi and DRI implants. These findings highlight that PU foam may not always replicate outcomes observed in natural bone, likely due to different mechanical characteristics and behavior of natural- and synthetic bone. While PU foam remains valuable for controlled testing due to its homogeneity, natural bone surrogates, such as bovine rib, can provide more reliable insights into dental implant performance.

title: Conditional Variational Autoencoder for Exoplanetary Radiance Spectrum Modeling
authors: Subhayan Mukherjee, Soumyodip Banerjee, Anupam Roy, Siddhartha Ghosh, Khushbu Chandrakar
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: We introduce a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) surrogate for exoplanet spectral modeling, trained on a wide grid of synthetic spectra spanning key planetary regimes. By tuning latent dimensionality and network hyperparameters, we jointly minimize reconstruction and Kullback–Leibler divergence losses to ensure accurate reconstructions and a well-regularized latent space. Quantitative metrics and visual inspections confirm that our CVAE yields tighter error distributions and faith-fully preserves critical molecular absorption features across variations in planetary mass, radius, equilibrium temperature, stellar effective temperature, and host-star radius. Compared to an unconditional VAE, the CVAE produces more precise, physically plausible spectra whose slopes and intensities respond appropriately to underlying physical parameters, making it well suited for data augmentation and forward modeling.

title: Exploring AI-Enhanced User Experience Monetization: A Multi-Task Learning Approach for Streaming Service Tiered Pricing Optimization
authors: Sabar Aritonang Rajagukguk
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This study examines how user reactions to Amazon Prime Video’s shift from an ad-free to an ad-supported model influence upgrade behavior. We analyze 47,832 Englishlanguage app-store reviews as the sole dataset for machine-learning training and testing, while 23,451 Twitter/ $\mathbf{X}$ posts and Google Trends data provide contextual and temporal validation. A multi-task BERT framework predicts complaint categories and upgrade propensity simultaneously, achieving accuracies of $94.3 \%$ and $89.7 \%$, which outperform the best single-task baselines by $7.2 \%$ and $12.1 \%$ respectively. The approach incorporates Expectation-Confirmation Theory to link service expectations, perceived performance, satisfaction, and intention to continue subscriptions. Ethical considerations focus on responsible use of customer data and on optimizing tiered service design to balance revenue goals with user autonomy. Limitations include an eight-month data-collection period, a focus on U.S. English reviews, and analysis of a single streaming platform, which may limit generalization to other markets or languages.

title: A review paper on design of chaotic oscillator using current-feedback operational amplifier and its implementation in secure Communication
authors: Amit Singh, Mohd. Rehan Haider
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Due to significant advantages of current-feedback operational amplifier (CFOA) over operational amplifiers (opamps), CFOA is used in secure communication. The present scientific paper reveals on the different analysis of superior contributions in secure communication which exhibits a chaotic nature. Chaotic The secure communication which is Chaotic, has so many advantages because of good unpredictable system as compared to conventional save communications systems and simple in designing. In the communicated paper, different types of chaotic oscillator and their implementation in secure communication are discussed.

title: Ensemble and Boosting-Based Machine Learning Framework for Personality Prediction
authors: Abhishek Misra, Umidbek Abdalov, Adolatkhon Dzhabborova, Rayhon Sapaeva, Sapayeva Vaziraxon, Chetan Prakash Khurana
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This study uses ensemble and advanced methods of machine learning to predict traits in individuals. Multiple models, such KNN, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Cat Boost, XG Boost, and Stacking Classifiers, were employed to improve generalization and durability. Feature engineering and data preparation were included in the pipelines for effective handling. Trained with ideal hyperparameters and a batch size of 32 , the models have always achieved top performance. The greatest performance of the highest performing models was 93% accuracy, with precision of 91%, recall of 92%, and F1-score of 92%. Reduced overfitting was indicated by the lowest training and validation loss. ROC and PR curve tests were used to further indicate stable classification performance. The results show how effective ensemble and boosting techniques are in personality prediction tasks.

title: Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on SCSSA-CNN-Transformer
authors: Changxu Gui, Dongao Li, Ting Fang, Jiaming Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: To address the challenges of accuracy in bearing fault detection and model parameter optimization, this paper proposes a hybrid intelligent diagnostic model that combines a convolutional neural network (CNN) with a Transformer, optimized using an improved Sparrow Search algorithm. First, a one-dimensional CNN is employed to adaptively extract rich local fault features from raw vibration signals. Subsequently, a Transformer encoder models the feature sequences extracted by the CNN, leveraging its powerful self-attention mechanism to capture long-range dependencies between features, thereby providing a more comprehensive representation of fault information. To overcome the manual tuning of model hyperparameters, Sine-Cosine and Cauchy Mutation Sparrow Search Algorithm (SCSSA) is employed to automatically optimize the key hyperparameters of the CNN-Transformer hybrid model, ensuring optimal model performance. Experimental validation using the public dataset from Case Western Reserve University demonstrates that this method can identify and classify faults with high precision. Its diagnostic accuracy and stability outperform traditional single models like CNN and Transformer, as well as other unoptimized hybrid models, providing an effective solution for intelligent bearing fault diagnosis in complex industrial scenarios.

title: Multi-scale Facial Expression Recognition Based on Multi-Direction Local Binary Pattern
authors: Shaoqing Xu, Shan Xiao, Siwen Wang, Wenlong Zhang, Fanyu Zeng, Yang Yu, Shuyu Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Real-world facial expression recognition (FER) remains challenged by sensitivity to illumination changes and pose variations. To address this, we propose MD-LBP-MSNet, a novel framework integrating a Multi-Direction Local Binary Pattern (MD-LBP) with a Multi-Scale Network. The MD-LBP operator expands the receptive field and employs multi-directional, center-symmetric encoding to enhance texture discrimination and illumination robustness. This is coupled with a multi-scale CNN architecture, which hierarchically extracts features across spatial scales by processing channel-divided feature subsets through convolutional blocks of varying depths. Evaluated on the FER2013 and CK+ datasets, our method achieves competitive accuracies of 76.23% and 74.56%, respectively, demonstrating superior robustness and performance, particularly in complex conditions, by effectively combining engineered texture stability with deep feature adaptability.

title: Quantum-Inspired and Context-Aware Approach to Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning
authors: Samyuktha J, Dhivyasree T, Anitha G
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Cross-lingual voice cloning is plagued by issues such as phoneme entanglement between languages and artificial prosody in voice transfer to new linguistic contexts. To address both, the paper introduces Q-ContextTTS, a zero-shot cloning framework combining quantum inspired phoneme embeddings with discourse-guided prosody modeling. Phonemes are situated within a Hilbert-like space for the representation of cross-language superposition and entanglement, and a discourse-aware encoder modulates intonation, phrasing, and breathing patterns in response to sentence type and discourse markers. Developed on datasets such as MLS and LibriSpeech, the system disentangles speaker identity from linguistic content without fine-tuning to speakers. The experimental assessment shows strong performance with 0.91 cosine similarity, 4.1 MOS, 9.1% WER, and 91% accent accuracy. Zero-shot results on VCTK show 4.38 MOS-Naturalness and 0.17 FFE, outperforming baselines. Ablation experiments validate the utility of both the quantum embedding and the context-aware modules, with better performance than current state-of-the-art baselines like VALL-E and YourTTS. This research brings cross-lingual speech synthesis closer to merging quantum-inspired representation learning with discourse-guided prosody, making a good foundation for subsequent generations of more natural, controllable, and ethically constructed cross-lingual voice technologies.

title: Neural Network Based Digital Predistortion for Digital MIMO Arrays Under Load Modulation
authors: Yurui Wang, Joel Fernandez, Arne Fischer-Bühner, Lauri Anttila, Mikko Valkama
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: In MIMO transmitters, antenna coupling effects can cause load modulation in the active power amplifier (PA) array, changing the nonlinearity of the PAs and impairing the effectiveness of conventional digital predistortion methods. This paper presents a new technique to linearize nonlinear PA arrays in digital MIMO systems, utilizing a novel concept of dual-input time-delay neural networks (NNs). The effectiveness of the proposed dual-input NN approach in linearizing the PA array under load modulation is demonstrated by RF measurement-based experiments at 2.1 GHz. The proposed method shows clear improvements and enhanced performance-complexity tradeoffs over the existing NN-based state-of-the-art models.

title: Career Guidance System using Artificial Intelligence for Decision-Making based on the Interests of Adolescents
authors: Manisha Koranga, Arslonbek Nurjonov, Sokhibjan Muminov, Sherzod Sabirov, Gulnoza Sabirova, Adilbek Dauletov
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) to career planning education has revolutionized the field of career counseling globally. This method gives students access to a broad spectrum of information, resources, and tools based on different professions. An AI chatbot's round-the-clock availability might help in the field of career counseling, which would benefit from more support. To offer the suitable and fair use of this innovation, it is necessary to test the ethical concerns and gaps in professional counseling for both humans and AI. Using Google Forms, we gathered information from several public and private universities in Nepal. The labels were normalized, and non-numeric labels were changed to numeric labels using Label Encoder. Getting clean, prepared data that will produce the most accurate results is the goal of pre- processing. Data is analyzed using machine learning algorithms using performance measures.

title: Broadband Characterization of Newly Developed ABF Type A Material for Advanced Packaging and mmWave Applications
authors: Mahin Ahamed, Lakshmi Narasimha Vijay Kumar, Madhavan Swaminathan, Habib Hichri, Yoshio Nishimura, Takashi Yamanaka, Yuko Shibata
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: To meet the performance demands of 6G and sub-terahertz (sub-THz) systems, advanced packaging technologies require new materials with low dielectric loss and high stability. This paper presents the first broadband characterization of a newly developed Ajinomoto Build-up Film® (ABF) Type A laminated on glass substrates. The dielectric properties were evaluated from 10 GHz to 220 GHz using the microstrip ring resonator (MRR) method, while microstrip lines on 30 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> thick ABF A were fabricated via a Semi-Additive Patterning (SAP) process to assess insertion loss. The measured dielectric constant remained nearly constant at 3.45 ± 0.05, and the loss tangent increased gradually from 0.002 at 10 GHz to 0.009 at 220 GHz. The microstrip lines exhibited average insertion loss from 0.08 dB/mm to 0.39 dB/mm across the same frequency range. Compared to the widely used ABF Type B, Type A variant demonstrates significantly lower loss at higher frequencies. These findings highlight the potential of ABF Type A as a promising candidate for low-loss interconnects and high-frequency packaging in future 6G and sub-THz wireless applications.

title: Evaluation of Mechanical and Biological Properties of PCL-coated Magnesium Scaffolds
authors: Fatemeh Sharifabadi, Sayed Khatiboleslam Sadrnezhaad
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Magnesium scaffolds, owing to their mechanical properties similar to natural bone and their biodegradability, are considered promising candidates for tissue engineering applications. In this study, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Mg}-\text{Zn}-\text{Ca}$</tex> scaffolds were fabricated using the powder metallurgy method with different porosity agents (10% and 30% urea) and subjected to compression testing. The results showed that the 10% urea samples exhibited significantly higher compressive strength <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(105 \pm 5 \text{MPa})$</tex> compared to the 30% urea samples (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$68 \pm 4 \text{MPa}, \mathrm{p}<0.01$</tex>). Meanwhile, the higher porosity of the 30% urea samples provided more favorable conditions for tissue ingrowth. To enhance the mechanical and biological performance, polycaprolactone (PCL) coating was applied to the scaffolds. Findings revealed that a single-layer PCL coating increased compressive strength by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-20 \%$</tex>, while a threelayer coating led to an improvement of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0}-\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> compared with uncoated samples. Moreover, the PCL coating acted as a protective barrier, reducing the degradation rate and providing more favorable biocompatible conditions. Overall, the results indicate that the application of PCL coating is an effective approach to improve both mechanical stability and biological performance of magnesium scaffolds for bone regeneration applications.

title: Lightweight RTOS for Safety-Critical Smart Mobility and Healthcare Applications
authors: Manoj Mysore Veere Gowda
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a lightweight Real-Time Operating System (RTOS) designed for safety-critical embedded applications in smart mobility and healthcare devices. Implemented on the Texas Instruments TM4C123GH6PM (ARM Cortex-M4F), the RTOS supports up to 9 threads with hybrid preemptivecooperative scheduling across different priority levels, semaphore synchronization with priority inheritance, and a UART-based shell for commands like ps, ipcs, kill, reboot, pidof, priority, preempt, and pi. Interrupts include PendSV for context switching, SysTick for 1ms ticks, Timer1 for CPU profiling with IIR filtering and SVC for kernel services (yield, sleep, wait, post, create, destroy). Fault- tolerant mechanisms include watchdog timers, MPU stack isolation, and 0xDEADBEEF pattern-based stack overflow detection. The enhanced system demonstrates improved context switching, reduced latency, and high reliability compared to FreeRTOS and AUTOSAR OS. Experimental results show preemptive and cooperative switching, 99.8 % task completion under faults, and a 10 KB memory footprint. Thread behaviors, visualized via five LED patterns and controlled by push buttons, simulate automotive ECU actuator control and biomedical sensor sampling, contributing to sustainable smart societies.

title: Refinement of 41 GHz Planar Microstrip Radiating patch antenna for advancing Wireless Infrastructure
authors: Jiya Yadav, Kajal Goswami, Juhi Sharma, Lavanya Kumari, Vaishali Kikan, Ashwni Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: A compact microstrip V-band antenna is proposed for 5G applications operating at 41 GHz. The design implements a rectangular mono-pole patch with a circular slot, allowing dual-band capability with improved circular polarization. The antenna is compatible with MIMO configurations for enhanced diversity performance with higher channel capacity and reduced mutual coupling. Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 measuring 0.0254 mm thick is used as the substrate for stable high-frequency operation. The performance of the antenna is analyzed through HFSS simulation with parameters such as reflected incident to final power ratio, VSWR, electromagnetic emission pattern, higher gain with isolation. The simulation results show wide impedance bandwidth, high isolation, and excellent diversity characteristics, hence suitable for integration into 5G devices and modern wireless systems.

title: RGB-D Semantic Segmentation in Indoor Environments via Heterogeneous Dual-Branch Networks
authors: Shihong Wu, Biying Shi, Shuobin Wei
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Semantic segmentation technology aims to accurately identify and segment various objects or scenes in images. Traditional segmentation methods based on RGB images have limitations in information utilization. With the widespread application of depth-sensing technology, the introduction of depth images has enriched semantic segmentation algorithms with abundant geometric information, significantly improving segmentation accuracy.Due to the modal differences between RGB and depth information, this paper proposes a novel heterogeneous dual-branch architecture. For RGB images a base encoder and a detail encoder are employed to jointly extract local and global features. For depth images containing only geometric information, a lightweight hierarchical encoder is designed to efficiently extract feature information with fewer parameters. Additionally, the paper introduces a Cross-Modality Interaction Module (CMIM), which combines Transformer and large-kernel convolution to achieve efficient fusion of cross-modal global and local information. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves state-of-the-art performance on the NYUDepthv2 and SUN-RGBD datasets.

title: Secure Communication for Industrial Applications via Measurement-Based Distributed Quantum Computing
authors: Namisha Gupta, Divya Punia, Kumar Gautam, Geeta Sikka, Ajay K. Sharma
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Recent advances in quantum technologies are rapidly pushing the boundaries of computational capabilities, with the promise of significant speedups across a variety of applications. However, building large-scale monolithic quantum processors remains an immense engineering challenge, motivating the pursuit of alternative paradigms to realize practical quantum advantage. One such paradigm is Distributed Quantum Computing (DQC), which interconnects multiple quantum nodes via quantum networks to collectively perform complex computations. While Circuit-Based Quantum Computing (CBQC) has traditionally been the dominant model, Measurement-Based Quantum Computing (MBQC), with its one-way computation framework and pre-shared entangled cluster states, introduces distinctive benefits for distributed architectures. Importantly, within DQC, MBQC offers enhanced security over CBQC by naturally supporting protocols such as Blind Quantum Computation (BQC) and secure delegated processing. This study offers an application-oriented perspective on the role of MBQC in DQC, highlighting concrete industrial use cases alongside key implementation challenges. By bridging theoretical potential with practical deployment considerations, it aims to accelerate progress toward secure and scalable distributed quantum systems.

title: RegCheck-Hybrid: A Hybrid Rule-LLM Framework for Automated Multi-modal Fire Code Compliance Checking
authors: Zirui Chen, Wei Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of building designs, fire code compliance checking faces challenges such as numerous regulations, frequent updates, and low efficiency of manual audits. Existing automated methods are polarized: traditional rule-based methods struggle with unstructured text and high maintenance costs, while Large Language Model (LLM) based methods suffer from hallucination risks in numerical reasoning. This paper proposes RegCheck-Hybrid, a hybrid framework. It first constructs a high-precision domain knowledge base through “Regulation as Code” governance and multi-modal parsing. Secondly, it designs a “Rule-First, LLM-Fallback” hybrid judgment engine, combining the determinism of symbolic logic with the flexibility of neural networks. Experiments on a real-world fire audit dataset show that the system achieves an F1-score of $89 \%$, significantly outperforming single RAG systems or traditional rule engines, especially in scenarios involving tables and complex logic clauses.

title: Real vs. Fake News Classification with Logistic Regression and TF-IDF Feature Extraction
authors: Gaurav Singh, Yunuskhujaev Khabibulla, Hamidbek Yusupov, Elyorbek Yuldashov, Etibor Sariyeva, Harshit Pandey
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: In the study, a set of a combination of real and fake articles is used to give a classifier to identify fake news using the logistic regression tests. Preprocessing of the dataset includes the combination of titles and texts, lowercasing of words, the removal of punctuations and stop words, and TF-IDF vectorization with 5,000 features maximum. The data is further divided into 80-20 train/test and the classifier was trained on the default parameters used by the Logistic Regression, which yields the accuracy of about 99 percent on training and 99.01 percent on test. The classifier does not explicitly specify any batch size or epochs as it is a classical ML algorithm whereas the default solver along with the learning rate parameters has achieved rapid convergence, steady performance and is very effective in detecting fake news.

title: Polarization-Insensitive Terahertz Modulator Based on Reconfigurable Metallic Ring Arrays
authors: Sanaz Zarei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This paper reports the proof-of-principle of a free-space terahertz spatial light modulator based on extraordinary transmission effect through a planar array of metallic ring apertures. The modulation is achieved by the vertical movement of the metallic membrane perforated with hole array, with respect to the inner rods which are fixed to the substrate. The structure shows different transmission behaviors for various displacement values, which enables the intensity modulation of transmitted terahertz waves at 1.05THz. A maximum modulation depth of 85 % in the frequency range between 0.96THz and 1.15THz is demonstrated.

title: Deep Learning for Inclusive Communication: A CNN-Based Approach to Sign Language Recognition
authors: Beera John Jaidhan, Polinati Vinod Babu, Soujanya Ketha, D V H Venu Kumar, C P Pavan Kumar Hota, V. Priyadarshani
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Despite a wide social group that could profit from it, the idea of sign language recognition by technology is underutilized. Several technologies are available that can be helpful in establishing a link between this social group and the rest of the world. One of the main tools for enabling sign language users to communicate with the rest of society is understanding sign language. Computers can recognize sign language with the use of image categorization and machine learning, which can then be translated by humans. This study uses convolutional neural networks to identify sign language motions. The static sign language gestures were photographed using an RGB camera and comprise the image dataset used. The photos underwent preprocessing before being used as the input that had been cleaned. This dataset of sign language motions was retrained and tested using convolutional neural network model, the results of which are discussed in this study. The model has a number of convolution filter inputs that operate on a single input. Its ensuing validation accuracy was above 90%. In this work, the many attempts to detect sign language based on machine learning and image depth information are also examined. It assesses the different difficulties involved in solving the issue at hand and also describes the problem's potential future.

title: EfficientNetV2B0 Hyperparameter Optimization with Metaheuristic Algorithm on Lung Cancer Detection from Lung CT Scan
authors: Kaisar Kertarajasa, Untari Novia Wisesty, Mahmud Dwi Sulistiyo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Computed tomography (CT) scan is one popular non-invasive mean of lung cancer early diagnosis. Computer vision can aid radiologists to interpret the result of lung scanning to increase diagnosis accuracy. However, an appropriate hyperparameter configuration is required to achieve optimal performance of deep learning models. This research offers a solution of optimizing hyperparameters of an EfficientNetV2B0 model using various metaheuristic algorithm, such as Rao Algorithm and Slime Mold Algorithms (SMA). The dataset LIDC-IDRI was used in the experiment for training, testing, and validation. Several scenarios were made to investigate the effects of different algorithm and population size to the optimization process and the resulting model. By choosing population size $\mathbf{n} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1 0}$, the objective value converged slower than population size $\mathbf{n} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{2 0}$ that exhibits drastic convergence in early generations and became plateau in later generations. On the final result, the best performance was achieved by Rao-3 algorithm with $\mathbf{n} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1 0}$, with accuracy, precision, recall, and f1-score of $0.9602,0.9489,0.9139$, and 0.9290 respectively.

title: Voice-Based Parkinson's Disease Detection Using Ensemble Learning Techniques
authors: Peetabas Patro, Tapan Kumar Das
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease is a chronic neurological condition that impacts millions worldwide, often beginning with subtle early signs. Early and accurate detection remains a significant challenge for healthcare professionals. In this study, an innovative method is proposed for the early detection of parkinsons disease(PD) by leveraging ensemble learning techniques on voice data. In this study, we used the Oxford Parkinson's Disease detection dataset, which contains biomedical voice measurements of PD patients and healthy control patients. We utilize a combination of XGBoost, Random Forest, Linear Regression, Support Vector Machine models, along with a stacking classifier to analyze the dataset. The approach aims to improve classification accuracy and accessibility, offering non-invasive tools for early intervention. Our models achieved high performance, with stacking yielding an accuracy of 0.93 %, F1-score of 0.94 %, ROC-AUC of 0.97 %, and Recall of 0.96 %. Feature importance analysis highlights the nonlinear measures, such as spread1, PPE, as the key predictors. The results demonstrate that the ensemble method can improve the detection of Parkinson's disease and its performance compared to other models. The findings underscore the effectiveness of ensemble learning techniques in offering a non-invasive approach for clinical applications, demonstrating their potential to enhance diagnostic accuracy and accessibility in healthcare.

title: System Using 3D-Hydrodynamic Focusing
authors: Zohre Nazemi Dehkordi, Ali Abouei Mehrizi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Electroporation (EP) is a well-established, non-viral method of gene delivery that transiently permeates the cell membrane in response to high fields of applied electric field. Although a successful method, traditional bulk EP systems have major limitations including uneven distribution of the electric field, limited control over exposure of individual cells, and Joule heating which can limit cell viability. Microfluidics have become a valuable tool to overcome these issues of conventional EP by allowing more precise manipulation of fluid movement, localization of the electrified field, and control over individual cells. In this work, we describe a microfluidic electroporation platform that utilizes three-dimensional hydrodynamic focusing to concentrate cells in a narrow stream of fluid measuring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times 20 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> at the center of the channel. The vertical and horizontal sheath flow provides orthogonal flow confinement which provides uniform exposure of each cell to the applied electric field. Our electroporation platform has embedded planar electrodes beneath the sheath layers so that the electric field is localized across the focused cell stream while minimizing direct interaction of the electrodes with the cells. Based on numerical simulations in COMSOL Multiphysics it is predicted that flow confinement is stable; concentration profiles developed appropriately; and electric field strengths will be sufficient and under the optimal electroporation window <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(300-800 \mathrm{V} / \text{cm})$</tex> at an applied voltage below 2 V. The design will provide consistent exposure times on the millisecond time-scale, which is appropriate for mammalian cell electroporation, while avoiding unwanted effects like Joule heating, electrolysis, and pH changes. In comparison to previously documented devices, the system presented has increased field uniformity, lower operating voltages and has better scalability for ongoing flow processes. These results are indicative of the promise that the combination of 3D hydrodynamic focusing and low-voltage microfluidic electroporation has in furthering intracellular delivery for cell therapy production and drug screening, as well as single cell analyzers.

title: Hardware Accelerated Detection of Elevator Electric Bike Using Improved YOLOv4-tiny
authors: Yuning Lu, Feng Yu, Yunlong Zheng, Song Wang, He Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: To address challenges in detecting electric bike invading elevators, including insufficient detection accuracy, poor real-time performance, and constraints on deployment costs and power consumption, this study proposes an object detection algorithm based on an improved YOLOv4-tiny model alongside a hardware acceleration implementation. By incorporating improved SPP feature fusion and CBAM attention modules, the multiscale and feature extraction capabilities of the network are enhanced, thereby reducing the probability of missed and false detections of targets in elevator scenarios. High-level parallel optimization and block pipeline design were applied to the operators and modules of the improved model using HLS. Finally, elevator scenario testing and hardware-accelerated deployment were conducted. Results show the model achieves a mAP of $96 \%$, with single-frame inference time on FPGA at 0.397 seconds and average power consumption of 2.58 W, providing a high performance solution for elevator electric bike intrusion detection.

title: Enabling the Design of Robust Network Applications by Creating Realistic Scenarios
authors: Edgar S. Oliveira, Paulo H. L. Rettore, Clayson Celes, Leon Luca Lausberg, Rodolfo Meneguette, Nils Aschenbruck
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Software Defined Systems (SDS)
abstract: The evaluation of softwarized tactical networks, such as those based on SDN, critically depends on realistic simulations. However, existing mobility models for Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) often fail to capture the structured, hierarchical movements of military missions, compromising the validity of the results. This paper addresses this gap by conducting a statistical analysis of an operationally relevant military scenario, Vignette 2 of the Anglova framework, to characterize its movement patterns. As a key contribution, the analysis of spatiotemporal patterns and inter-agent interactions provides the foundation for developing more realistic and configurable mobility models. Consequently, this work enables a more accurate and reliable performance evaluation of network technologies in tactical scenarios.

title: Collaborative Optimization of 6G Edge Computing Networks Based on Intelligent Reflecting Surfaces
authors: Liang Zhao, Gang Yu, Zhe Wang, Kaixuan Wang, Dawei Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-assisted mobile edge computing (MEC) systems in sixth-generation (6G) networks have attracted considerable attention. The joint optimization of IRS phase shifts and computation offloading decisions is formulated as a weighted minimization problem of system delay and energy consumption, subject to the unit-modulus constraint of IRS elements and limited edge server resources. In this work, to address the resulting non-convex problem, a bi-level optimization approach is developed, where deep reinforcement learning is employed for IRS configuration and convex optimization is applied for computation offloading and resource allocation. The proposed framework provides an efficient and scalable solution for IRS–MEC integration, and simulation results validate its effectiveness in terms of performance improvement and computational efficiency.

title: Research on Methods of Vehicle Re-identification in Highway Scenes Based on Deep Learning Fusion Network
authors: Chihang Zhao, Ruxin Qing, Guiwen Zu, Qunle Du, Ping Song, Yashi Wang, Wei Yang, Yijun He
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Vehicle re-identification in highway scenarios is of great significance for vehicle information tracking in intelligent transportation systems, but factors such as lighting effects, motion blur, and vehicle occlusion can cause significant variations in the form of the same vehicle in different frames, presenting challenges for vehicle re-identification in highway scenarios. First, based on the YOLOV5 model, cars, trucks, and buses under daylight, evening, and night lighting conditions were detected to construct a dataset for vehicle re-identification in highway scenarios; Next, three models for vehicle re- identification in highway scenarios were constructed, namely ResNet50-VRHS, DenseNet121-VRHS, and ShuffleNetV2-VRHS; Then, a vehicle re-identification model for highway scenarios based on deep learning fusion networks, DNFM-RDS, was proposed; Finally, an experimental study was conducted based on the constructed dataset, and the results showed that the DNFM-RDS model achieved 93.6%, 97.2%, and 84.7% respectively in Rank-1 hit rate, Rank-5 hit rate, and mAP metric, significantly outperforming the other three single-network models.

title: Transform-based algorithms dedicated to ECG baseline drift cancellation: A comparative study
authors: Zaki Aissam Khezzar, Redha Benzid, Elhadi Kenane
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: Nowadays, Electrocardiogram (ECG) diagnosis stills to be very useful to inform about the cardiac health status of humans. However, acquiring an ECG signal in a correct manner requires that all noise sources should be reduced proficiently. Among such noise types, we attract the attention to the baseline wander (BW) that can be instigated, usually, by unreliable electrode contact placement, patient breathing, unclean skin and so on. In this context, three strategies dedicated to diminish BW contribution that are: Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT), Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), Fast Walsh-Hadamard Transform (FWHT) based algorithms, were experimented comparatively. Achieved simulations demonstrate, qualitatively and quantitatively, the high-performance capacity of the DWT-based strategy compared to the DCT-based technique and FWHT-based method.

title: Modeling Attention Performance Across Female Reproductive Aging Using Logistic Regression
authors: Zahra Zehtabi, Leila Mehdizadeh Fanid, Pedram Salehpoor, Mahdi Jafari Asl
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Reproductive aging in women is associated with cognitive decline, particularly in attention domains. This study investigates the use of machine learning, specifically logistic regression, to predict attentional performance during different reproductive aging stages. A total of 100 healthy women, 50 premenopausal (mean age <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=29.5)$</tex> and 50 postmenopausal (mean age <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=54.8)$</tex>, underwent neuropsychological evaluation using the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) and the Integrated Visual and Auditory (IVA-2) test. Among the applied supervised models, logistic regression achieved 95.0% accuracy in 10-fold crossvalidation and 100% on the test set. The analysis revealed a strong inverse correlation between reproductive aging and auditory attention subscores. These results suggest that logistic regression offers a reliable, interpretable, and clinically applicable model for early detection of attention decline, supporting its integration into biomedical screening tools.

title: IoT Security Landscape: Risks, Challenges, and the Road to Robust Countermeasures
authors: Durgesh Nandan, Sushma Parihar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: last 10 years, the Internet of Things is one of the most demanding area due to increase his demand at various applications and industry. As the utility of IoT device increase, it attracted significant security vulnerabilities that impact the entire system emerge. This research analyses IoT security concerns, including malware, data breaches, illegal system access, and instances of device takeover. Through this research article, it addresses several issues in these ecosystems, including inadequate authentication protocols, a deficiency of industry-standard security measures, and poorly functioning connected devices. This study investigates a variety of potential solutions, encompassing sophisticated encryption techniques, blockchain security systems, zero-trust security models, and improved threat detection technologies. This essay underscores the imperative for stakeholders to cooperate in the deployment of multi-tiered security systems to address increasing security challenges. This study assesses potential dangers and solutions to aid researchers, policymakers, and industry leaders in fostering safe Internet of Things (IoT) practices and a dependable, resilient ecosystem. This discovery exacerbates experts' concerns over the safeguarding of networked systems in an ever-expanding digital landscape.

title: Design of a high efficiency peak current-mode buck DC-DC converter
authors: Xiaolong Chen, Yanjuan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: A high efficiency synchronous buck DC-DC converter was implemented in SMIC $0.18 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ BCD technology. The converter utilizes peak current-mode control to achieve fast transient response. A comparator with cross-coupled load is employed to accelerate the comparison between the slopecompensated current sensing signal and the output of the error amplifier. Moreover, a capacitance multiplication technique is applied in the compensation network of the error amplifier to reduce the required capacitor area. The sampling speed and accuracy of the current sensing circuit are effectively improved by introducing an auxiliary compensation current. Post-layout simulation results show that with a 5 V input and 1.2 V output, the converter achieves a peak efficiency of $95 \%$ and an average efficiency of $92 \%$, while maintaining an output voltage ripple of 0.2 mV. The proposed design is suitable for a wide range of portable electronic applications.

title: Toward Scalable Quantum Computing: A Comparative Review of Qubit Platforms
authors: Yingchang Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Quantum computation has emerged as a prominent research field with the potential to revolutionize information processing. Recent advancements have focused on extending qubit coherence times and enhancing error correction and fault-tolerant protocols, achieving coherence time to gate time ratios as high as 10⁸ and reducing error rates to as low as 0.143% ± 0.003% per cycle for 101 qubit system. The next critical challenge lies in scaling up qubit systems to support large-scale quantum computation that exceeds the performance of classical supercomputer. In this study, we summarize the working mechanisms, physical properties, gate operations, and limitations of three leading qubit platforms—superconducting qubits, trapped-ion qubits, and neutral atom qubits. Through a systematic comparison, we identify the key challenges that hinder qubit system scalability. Finally, we outline future research directions to address these challenges, including exploring the fundamental physics underpinning long coherence times, improving control tools such as laser systems, enhancing material quality and fabrication precision, and investigating hybrid quantum architectures. We hope this study offers a valuable perspective for researchers working toward the development of scalable and reliable quantum computing systems.

title: Examination and Analysis of the Influence of Near-Infrared Light Absorption by Hair Melanin on fNIRS Signal
authors: Elmira Baghaeifar, Sina Shamekhi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: In recent years, the use of functional nearinfrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) technology has increased despite its limitations and weaknesses. However, further studies are still needed to assess the quality of the signals it produces. fNIRS utilizes near-infrared (NIR) wavelengths, which are known to be absorbed significantly by melanin, oxygenated hemoglobin (HbO), and deoxygenated hemoglobin (HbR). This presents a challenge for researchers employing fNIRS technology. In this study, we examined the average signal power feature, which changes in proportion to the absorption profile of various chromophores, to analyze how factors such as hair color, length, and density affect fNIRS signals. Due to abnormal data distributions within each group, confirmed by the AndersonDarling test, we employed the Wilcoxon statistical test to compare group similarities. The p-values obtained from the Wilcoxon test revealed significant differences between the groups: those with dark versus light hair, varying hair densities, and different hair lengths. Our results indicated a decrease in the mean power of HbO, HbR, and optical density (OD) signals in individuals with dark hair, high hair density, and long hair length. Furthermore, we found that hair length and density had a more pronounced effect on the average signal power.

title: Next Generation AI-Enhanced Intelligent Imaging for Automated Knee X-ray Interpretation in Osteoarthritis: Front office Integration and Employee Training
authors: Pavithra K, Balakrishnan R, Loveline Zeema.J
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Osteoarthritis is a long-lasting musculoskeletal ailment in which the degenerative alterations in the cartilage of the articles progressively arise. As the pathological anatomy develops to bone structure degeneration, it results in pain, stiffness and functional impairment. It results in immobility, reduced quality of life, more susceptibility to fractures, hospitalization, and mental health problems in the older adults and postmenopausal women. To address the increasing global prevalence of OA and the necessity to diagnose X-rays of the knee promptly, affordably and accessibly, this paper presents an AI-based imaging system based on the use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and compares their results with the existing machine learning models. The framework will be structured to integrate the front-office workflows and streamline the diagnostic process of patient registration to report delivery and establish a well-organized staff training ecosystem aimed at enabling clinical staff to operate the AI-enabled workflow and adapt to it. The study offers a comprehensive, deployment ready, diagnostic platform through advanced automation, user oriented front-office functionality and the consistent upskilling of the workforce, to provide scalable, precise, cost effective OA detection, clinical efficiency, interpretative variability reduction, and workforce transition to scalable intelligent, patient centred care.

title: An Open-Source Facial Recognition Based Attendance System
authors: Yatra A Vaghasia, Nikita, Rupa G. Mehta, Dipti P. Rana
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Manual and RFID-based methods for attendance tracking are often inefficient, error-prone, and susceptible to fraud, requiring active participation that hinders seamless automation. While facial recognition offers a more passive approach, existing commercial systems are typically proprietary, incurring high costs and lacking data sovereignty due to processing sensitive biometric data on third-party servers, raising privacy concerns. Furthermore, other reviewed systems showed limitations in scalability, real-world testing, and sufficient security or privacy measures like encrypted storage. To address these limitations, we propose a privacy-focused, open-source, cost-effective facial recognition attendance system designed for educational institutions, emphasizing on-premise processing using face-api.js to ensure sensitive data remains within the institution. This system utilizes robust security measures including AES256 encryption for biometric data and frames and role-based access control, and is built with a scalable architecture using a NoSQL database for flexibility. Preliminary testing successfully demonstrated real-time face detection and recognition, favorable accuracy in a real classroom environment, confirmed the viability of face-api.js, and showcased a scalable design capable of generating versatile attendance reports. The open-source nature underscores the solution's cost-effectiveness.

title: MRI to SPECT Image Translation for Parkinson's Disease Diagnosis
authors: Pegah Zandian Pour Esfahani, Abolfazl Adib Almojahedi, Seyyede Zoheh Seyyedsalehi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Parkinson's disease, the second most common neurological disorder worldwide, results from the degeneration of dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra, making early diagnosis complex and challenging. Although MRI images are widely accessible, they lack the sensitivity required to detect early changes. Conversely, DaTscan images provide higher accuracy by directly visualizing dopaminergic activity, but their use is limited by cost and complexity. This study employs a deep learning-based generative model, CycleGAN, to convert MRI images of patients and healthy individuals into DaTSPECT-like images. Subsequently, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifies patients and healthy subjects using the transformed images. This approach combines the advantages of both MRI and DaTscan modalities while addressing the scarcity of DaTscan data. Data were obtained from the PPMI dataset, which includes DaTSPECT and MRI images of patients and healthy controls. Results demonstrate that incorporating synthetic images with the original data increases classification accuracy to 85% and recall to 82.5%, reflecting improvements of 8.75% and 17.5%, respectively, over using only original DaTscan images. These findings confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method in enhancing Parkinson's disease diagnosis.

title: A Novel Transfer Learning Approach in HUMERAL fracture detection utilizing Pre-Trained Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Susmitha.N, Anuradha.T
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: This study evaluates MobileNetV2 and EfficientNetB3 for humeral fracture detection using the MURA dataset. These architectures were selected due to their computational efficiency, suitability for mobile deployment, and demonstrated success in medical imaging tasks, making them practical for real-world clinical settings. A subset of over 2000 humerus radiographs was used, divided into training and testing sets, with augmentation strategies (rotation, flipping, zoom) applied to improve generalization. An advanced transfer learning strategy was employed, involving layer-wise freezing/unfreezing of top layers with a reduced learning rate, combined with hybrid optimization. Results showed MobileNetV2 achieved 99% accuracy with Stochastic Gradient Descent, while EfficientNetB3 attained 97% accuracy under similar conditions. Beyond accuracy, we report sensitivity (98%), specificity (95%), precision (94–95%), recall (95–98%), F1-score (up to 95%), and AUC, ensuring a reliable diagnostic profile. MobileNetV2 demonstrated a favorable balance of efficiency and diagnostic performance, particularly for deployment in low-resource or mobile healthcare environments. This work highlights how optimized CNNs can assist radiologists by reducing diagnostic delays, improving consistency, and supporting decision-making in resource-constrained hospitals. The novelty lies in the customized fine-tuning strategy, hybrid optimization experiments, and domain-specific augmentation, which together enhance fracture detection robustness. The comparative analysis highlights strengths and limitations of both models, providing actionable insights for selecting architectures in medical imaging tasks.

title: Quantifying White Space Zone of Image Segmentation Task Based on Mask2Former
authors: Peng Zhang, Shuai Li, Junchao Liu, Na Li, Binxia Xue, Yu Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: White space is pivotal in forming and embodying the composition and artistic conception of Chinese classical landscape painting, and it also serves as a key element contrasting sharply with Western landscape painting. This study employs Golden Ratio—a universally recognized aesthetic principle—to determine whether Chinese classical landscape painters subconsciously adhered to this rule during their creative process. Therefore, this paper employs a deep learning semantic segmentation model—the Transformer-based Mask2Former—to randomly select 300 renowned Chinese classical landscape paintings as the training set and 261 as the test set. By calculating the pixel ratios of their white space zone and ink splash zone, it finds that 7.28% of the test set paintings approximate Golden Ratio. This finding shows no significant correlation with human-consensus aesthetic proportions. Based on the findings of latent Golden Ratio proportions in Chinese classical landscape paintings, this study concludes that aesthetic similarity does not consistently align with specific, widely accepted aesthetic ratios. However, this algorithmic model provides an effective computational approach for validating research questions specific to Chinese classical landscape painting, offering a deep learning perspective for addressing aesthetic similarity in this genre.

title: Multi-Modal Predictive Maintenance Framework for Telecommunications Infrastructure Using Environmental, Usage, and Performance Data
authors: Puttaswamy M R, Zainab Hatim Hussain Al, Muhammed Bashir, Santhosh John
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: This paper presents a novel multi-modal predictive maintenance framework that integrates infrastructure characteristics, environmental conditions, usage patterns, and service quality metrics to predict maintenance needs for telecommunications towers. The framework employs a weighted risk assessment model combining six key factors: infrastructure age, technology type, usage pressure, environmental interference, climate impact, and historical complaint patterns. Experimental validation on a dataset of 50 telecommunications towers across 10 regions in Oman demonstrates the framework's effectiveness with 56% prediction accuracy and potential cost savings of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\$ 208,000$</tex> annually through preventive maintenance strategies. The results show that 3G and mixed-technology towers exhibit the highest maintenance rates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(58-63 \%)$</tex>, while environmental factors significantly influence infrastructure reliability. This research contributes a scalable, data-driven approach to telecommunications infrastructure management with measurable economic benefits and practical implementation guidelines.

title: Exploring the Garden of Forking Paths in Empirical Software Engineering Research: A Multiverse Analysis
authors: Nathan Cassee, Robert Feldt
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Develop an Enhanced AI-Based Model to Predict Depression in Women
authors: Soni, Tejinder Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Computing, Communication and Automation (ICCCA)
abstract: As the prevalence of depression in women continues to increase, there is a need to improve early diagnosis and intervention methods. Using a novel multimodal deep learning architecture and combining multiple data sources, this study seeks to develop a better artificial intelligence (AI)-based model for predicting depression in women. Using dedicated deep learning encoders and attention-based synthesis methods, we believe that combining self-report questionnaires, physiological data, social media activity, and environmental factors will create a simpler and simpler, as well as more intuitive, predictive model. This study describes a hybrid deep learning architecture, data collection methods, appropriate behavior engineering procedures, and a robust assessment framework. The potential for this advanced AI model to help provide early diagnosis and tailored treatment and improve mental health outcomes for women is being seriously investigated.

title: Total-Ionizing-Dose Radiation Effect on Current Gain for Bipolar Junction Transistor Based 18 V BCD technology
authors: Xin Zhou, Hongze Chu, Yanfei Li, Shenghuai Liu, Hong Peng, Hao Jiang, Rubin Xie, Bo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: In this article, total-ionizing-dose (TID) radiation effect on current gain (hFE) for bipolar junction transistor (BJT) based 18 V BCD technology is investigated. For post-irradiation, base current (IB) is increased and collector current is almost unchanged, leading to hFE decreasing. Irradiation charge modulation mechanism is revealed by simulation and experiment. Irradiation charge in oxide layer would lower surface energy band, and promote surface depletion. Recombination of electron and hole is enhanced, which gives rise to IB increasing and hFE decreasing. Based on the mechanism, a radiation hardened structure with the P-top layer in PW region is proposed to suppress surface recombination rate. Experiment results show that TID tolerance exceeds 300 krad(Si) for the proposed radiation hardened BJT structure, beyond 10 times that for the conventional structure.

title: Immediate Aftereffect of tACS on Directed Functional Brain Networks in Subacute Stroke Patients_supp1-3668719.docx
authors: Guizhi Xu

title: Differential Pressure Sensor Based on Pore Electrolytic Conductance
authors: Ali Hossein Asli Ardebili, Hesameddin Ahmadi, Mohammad Shoghi Tekmedash, Mojtaba Taghipoor
abstract: The demand for a bio-compatible pressure sensors, characterized by low response times, high sensitivity, and robust durability, is experiencing significant growth. This study proposes a differential pressure sensor based on the micro/nanopore conductance concept. The pore used in this study is fabricated from polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), making it bio-compatible and tunable. Applied pressure alters the size of the embedded pore, affecting the ionic current, which can be employed to determine the applied pressure. The electrolyte used as the conductive solution is aqueous solution of potassium chloride (KCl). The fabricated sensor can achieve sensitivities up to 0.03 kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> and operates most effectively and reliably within the negative pressure range of –20 kPa to 0 kPa. With a fast average response time of 30 ms, it demonstrates decent stability and durability under cyclic testing, maintaining a consistent output signal during repeated loading.

title: Electromechanical Performance Evaluation of PLD-processed REBCO Tapes for Superconducting Magnets
authors: Michael B. De Leon, Richard Pascua, Hyung-Seop Shin, Jaehun Lee, Satoshi Awaji
abstract: This study assesses the electromechanical performance of eight practical REBCO-coated conductors produced via PLD under uniaxial tension. Continuous I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> measurements at 77 K in self-field served as the baseline, and high-field tests for operational purposes were conducted at 20 K under 3-5 T using a Katagiri type tension rig. The reversible strain and stress limits, determined by the 95% Ic0 retention criterion, ranged from 0.33% to 0.53% and from 410 to 760 MPa, respectively, influenced by substrate, slitting, and APC architecture. Despite the limited dataset, a comparison of the EMP for I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> degradation based on the irreversible limit was performed at both baseline and operational test conditions. These results will provide valuable benchmark data and practical insights for magnet design and EMP standardization.

title: Design and Performance Comparison of Single-Electrode and Vertical Contact-Separation Triboelectric Nanogenerators for Energy-Harvesting Applications
authors: Ili Farhana Mohamad Ali Nasri, Nuraliza Noorawzi, Wan Nursheila Wan Jusoh, Ridwan Yahaya, Shaiful Bahri Zainal Abidin
abstract: Triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs) have the potential to revolutionize energy harvesting and make electronics equipment more portable and sustainable. TENGs improve at converting wasted mechanical energy, such as movement, into valuable electrical energy. The aim of this project is to compare the performance of single-electrode mode triboelectric nanogenerator (SE-TENG) and a contact separation mode triboelectric nanogenerator (CS-TENG). Two positive triboelectric materials, cotton and nylon, and one negative triboelectric material, PTFE, were tested with copper foil as conductor materials. The CS-TENG configuration using PTFE-Nylon with the copper foil was able to generate an output voltage of up to 64.4 V and a short-circuit current of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.1~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>A without load. While SE-TENG uses PTFE and copper foil to generate a voltage slightly higher than 67.2 V with a low short-circuit current of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.7~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>A. This was proven by LEDs as light indicators, and a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.7~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>F capacitor was used as an energy storage. This project contributes insight into material selection for textile-based TENGs and support their integration into wearable energy harvesting systems.[2025-0118]

title: Toward Realistic and Efficient Cyber Deception
authors: Hind Alrubaish, Walid Aljoby, Ahmed Aldeek, Mohammed Alkubaish, Daisuke Mashima
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Honeypots are defensive mechanisms that deceive adversaries by deploying fabricated assets, systems, data, and credentials, as decoys or lures within a network. Existing honeypot frameworks often integrate open-source projects (e.g., Honeyd, Cowrie) to emulate common network services such as SSH and HTTP, which frequently serve as initial vectors of exploitation for adversaries. However, honeypot practical effectiveness remains constrained by the challenge of high-fidelity, i.e., accurately modeling the state space of real services, at reasonable resource cost. In the absence of adversary-centric fidelity evaluations and resource-aware deployment baselines, decoys are easily fingerprinted, thereby degrading the quality of threat intelligence and wasting valuable computational, memory, and address-space resources. We develop a quantitative framework using a honeypot test suite grounded in the MITRE ATT&CK framework and containerized resource benchmarking to address these gaps. Experiments were conducted across 15 honeypots covering the most common network services, including SSH, FTP, SMTP, HTTP, and DNS, with three distinct honeypot implementations evaluated per service. We analyzed the honeypots' responses against real system implementations to derive a quantitative realism score. Furthermore, we compared the realism score of each honeypot with its resource utilization in terms of CPU, memory, and response time. Finally, we identified the common fidelity breaches observed in each honeypot implementation. We further propose a refined honeypot taxonomy that align deception quality with resource-aware operation. To our knowledge, this is the first work to jointly provide a quantitative ATT&CK-mapped realism score, a cross-protocol discriminative test suite, and a containerized resource-deviation analysis to support the selection and deployment of realistic and low-overhead honeypots.

title: SecDiff: Diffusion-Aided Secure Deep Joint Source-Channel Coding Against Adversarial Attacks
authors: Changyuan Zhao, Jiacheng Wang, Ruichen Zhang, Dusit Niyato, Hongyang Du, Zehui Xiong, Dong In Kim, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Deep joint source-channel coding (JSCC) has emerged as a promising paradigm for semantic communication, delivering significant performance gains over conventional separate coding schemes. However, existing JSCC frameworks remain vulnerable to physical-layer adversarial threats, such as pilot spoofing and subcarrier jamming, compromising semantic fidelity. In this paper, we propose SecDiff, a plug-and-play, diffusion-aided decoding framework that significantly enhances the security and robustness of deep JSCC under adversarial wireless environments. Different from prior diffusion-guided JSCC methods that suffer from high inference latency, SecDiff employs pseudoinverse-guided sampling and adaptive guidance weighting, enabling flexible step-size control and efficient semantic reconstruction. To counter jamming attacks, we introduce a power-based subcarrier masking strategy and recast recovery as a masked inpainting problem, solved via diffusion guidance. For pilot spoofing, we formulate channel estimation as a blind inverse problem and develop an expectation-minimization (EM)-driven reconstruction algorithm, guided jointly by reconstruction loss and a channel operator. Notably, our method alternates between pilot recovery and channel estimation, enabling joint refinement of both variables throughout the diffusion process. Extensive experiments over orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) channels under adversarial conditions show that SecDiff outperforms existing secure and generative JSCC baselines by achieving a favorable trade-off between reconstruction quality and computational cost. This balance makes SecDiff a promising step toward practical, low-latency, and attack-resilient semantic communications.

title: SpectralZero: Text-Driven Spectral-Spatial Alignment for Zero-Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Yang Xia, Xia Yue, Xuanzhi Liu, Ning Chen, Hui Liu, Jun Yue, Leyuan Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Recently, deep learning-based hyperspectral image (HSI) classification methods have witnessed significant advancements. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on feature modeling for seen classes during the training phase, while the exploration of generalization and transfer mechanisms toward unseen land-cover categories remains limited. As a result, these methods exhibit poor adaptability and generalization performance in zero-shot scenarios. To address this challenge, we propose a novel zero-shot HSI classification framework called SpectralZero that leverages the strong generalization capability of large language models (LLMs) to compensate for the absence of semantic information in unseen classes. Moreover, our framework explicitly captures the synergistic relationship between spatial and spectral features to facilitate knowledge transfer from seen to unseen categories. Specifically, the proposed framework comprises two key modules: Semantic Prompt Enrichment (SPE) and Spectral-Spatial Union Extraction (SSUE). SPE utilizes LLMs to generate fine-grained semantic descriptions of land-cover classes, mitigating the semantic representation deficiency caused by the lack of samples in unseen categories. SSUE introduces two separate branches to independently model spatial structures and spectral reflectance characteristics, thereby enhancing the model’s capacity to extract joint spatial-spectral representations. A major advantage of our method lies in its ability to accurately classify unseen classes without requiring any samples during training or inference. Experiments on multiple public HSI datasets demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in zero-shot classification tasks, exhibiting superior recognition accuracy and generalization performance. The code will be available at https://github.com/xiayang124/SpectralZero.

title: Characterisation of the Radiation Field in LHC IR4 using a Timepix Radiation Monitor measurements and FLUKA simulations
authors: Daniel Prelipcean, Giuseppe Lerner, Rubén García Alía
abstract: The Timepix3 Radiation Monitor was deployed at the Large Hadron Collider in Interaction Region 4, aiming to measure the ionising dose and charged particle fluence generated by the operation of the Beam Gas Curtain instrument. Results were benchmarked against dedicated FLUKA simulations, yielding an agreement within the factor of 2. Beyond standard quantities relevant for Radiation to Electronics, the detector also demonstrated novel capabilities by exploiting the cluster morphology: namely, for the charged particles, the reconstruction incidence angles and the measurement of stopping power distributions. These results establish Timepix3 as a versatile tool for radiation field characterisation in accelerator mixed environments, complementing existing monitoring systems.

title: Verifiable Noise based Privacy-preserving Scheme for Trustworthy Consumer Applications
authors: Yuxin Liu, Ziyi He, Jian Xu, Miaojiang Chen, Ahmed Farouk
abstract: Many consumer applications are developed by collecting massive amounts of data from consumer devices. However, it also introduces complex challenges in safeguarding sensitive data, maintaining privacy, and defending against a wide spectrum of privacy leakage risks. Among the existing approaches, data perturbation is one of the most effective methods for protecting consumer data privacy. Nevertheless, two critical challenges remain unresolved: (1) how to ensure that the added noise strictly follows the required specifications; and (2) how to obtain accurate data. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Verifiable Noise-based Privacy Preserving (VNPP) scheme to improve data quality for trustworthy consumer applications. First, a Data Disturbance Verifiable (DDV) mechanism is designed to inject noise in a predetermined manner by employing blockchain technology. Second, a noise elimination approach is proposed to recover truthful data, thereby overcoming the limitation of traditional data perturbation methods in which noise cannot be effectively removed. Third, a data quality enhancement strategy is developed, which leverages interval verification to filter out low-quality data and select reliable users, thereby improving data quality under privacy protection. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed VNPP scheme outperforms existing methods in terms of both robustness and accuracy. Its advantages lie in being more effective at eliminating noise, filtering malicious data, and implementing a more rational strategy for weighting trustworthy data. On average, VNPP achieves performance improvements of 86.29% in MAE and 86.90% in RMSE compared to the baseline algorithms.

title: COMFORT: A Continual Fine-Tuning Framework for Foundation Models Targeted at Consumer Healthcare
authors: Chia-Hao Li, Niraj Jha
publish: ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: SCMIT: Style-Consistent Multi-Domain Image-to-Image Translation - REVISED
authors: Anh Q. Nguyen, Byeongkeun Kang, Yeejin Lee
abstract: Multi-domain image-to-image translation has become increasingly prominent in computer vision, addressing the demand for versatile models that can transform images across various domains. However, current image-to-image translation methods are often limited to transforming images between specific domains, making it difficult to extend these models to additional domains. In addition, existing methods using disentangled representation learning often fail to accurately separate features, resulting in low-quality generated images. To address these challenges, we propose a novel framework for multi-domain image-to-image translation that uses multi-scale feature extraction to improve the quality of disentanglement learning. Our framework introduces a novel loss function, called style consistency loss, which more accurately maps style features to style space.We evaluate the effectiveness of our framework using standard evaluation metrics for image-to-image translation by comparing it to benchmark models. Experimental results show that our framework can produce high-quality, artifact-free images on various datasets.

title: Direct Detection Φ-OTDR System Based on the Different Pulse Width Method
authors: Yuhuai Huang, Xiaopeng Dong, Peichao Chen
abstract: Conventional vibration demodulation typically utilizes nearby sample points in conjunction with pulses having relatively wide width for demodulation, which has drawbacks, including inconsistent spatial positions corresponding to adjacent sampling points and limited spatial resolution. This research proposes a novel quantitative vibration demodulation technique based on direct detection Φ-OTDR to address this issue. At a set repetition frequency, this technique alternately emits optical pulses with comparable but different pulse lengths. To precisely demodulate the vibration phase, it obtains two optical intensity signals at the same fiber position, which have different initial phases but virtually similar vibration-induced phase shifts. Compared with the conventional demodulation method, the proposed technique achieves a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improvement of 9.4 dB. By not relying on the data acquisition card's (DAQ) sampling rate, this technique reduces the acquisition system's cost and performance requirements while avoiding the spatial resolution deterioration associated with conventional demodulation using nearby sampling points.

title: Compositional Text-to-Image Synthesis with Training-Free Layout-Guided Diffusion_supp1-3668676.pdf
authors: Zizhao Wu
abstract: Recent text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models have made significant strides in generating high-quality images from diverse textual prompts. Despite this progress, these models often face challenges in accurately understanding and synthesizing complex prompts, primarily due to their limited compositional capabilities. In this study, we propose a novel approach for compositional T2I synthesis using layout-guided diffusion models, which do not require additional training. Specifically, we leverage the chain-of-code prompting technique of large language models to interpret textual prompts and generate object layouts with spatial coherence. To enhance the alignment between generated images and textual descriptions, we introduce two innovative layoutguided loss functions: Patch-oriented Cross-Attention (PCA) loss and Region-oriented Cross-Attention (RCA) loss. The PCA loss emphasizes high activation values for image patches that attend to all tokens in the prompt across the layout. The RCA loss enhances the average attention within the layout, thereby increasing the accuracy of generating objects and their associated attributes within specified regions. These proposed loss functions reassign cross-attention in diffusion models during the denoising process. Our comprehensive experiments consistently demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in improving semantic alignment between generated images and a diverse range of textual prompts, while ensuring high usability as a ready-to-use plugin. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/gxl-groups/Compositional-T2I</uri>.

title: Grad-CAM Guided Occlusion Adversarial Training
authors: Leiji Lu, Hongxia Yu, Linlin Hao, Linjun Zhang, Lei Bao, Xiaoyan Qin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)

title: A Preliminary study for estimating Solar Energy in Tropics Using A Semi-empirical model
authors: Indra Ardhanayudha Aditya, Pranda M. P. Garniwa, Intan Pamungkas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The adoption of solar energy as a sustainable and renewable energy source has significantly grown due to its abundant availability and global reach. However, the intermittent nature of solar irradiance presents a notable disadvantage for electricity grids that depend on photovoltaic (PV) systems. Hence, obtaining precise solar irradiance data is essential to maintain a steady level of energy production. Geostationary satellite imagery has become a practical solution to tackle this issue, as it provides a comprehensive collection of solar irradiance data on a large scale and over a long period of time.
