Satellite imagery has been predominantly used to estimate Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI) by employing physical and semi-empirical models. Nevertheless, there is a restricted quantity of research that has specifically concentrated on constructing models for GHI in Indonesia using semi-empirical methodologies. Therefore, this study conducted a feasibility assessment to evaluate the most suitable semi-empirical model for estimating GHI in Indonesia. Hence, Perez model was selected to estimate the GHI in the Jakarta region of Indonesia, based on their appropriateness and applicability. After conducting simulation, it was determined that the Perez model can be candidate, as indicated by its lowest relative root mean square error (rRMSE) of around 58.6%.

title: AI-Driven Symptom-Based Disease Prediction for Efficient Healthcare Diagnosis
authors: Harshit Budhraja, Madhuri Gupta, Nipun Bhardwaj, Lakshit Agarwal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: To improve the patient outcomes through timely diagnosis and treatment, disease prediction has become an essential part of modern healthcare systems. Research on disease prediction has found that symptom-based methods, which take use of symptom data's accessibility and early diagnostic potential, are a promising direction. Many nations have an overburdened and sometimes costly medical system due to the yearly increase in people and ailments. It can be expensive and time consuming to consult with doctors when using traditional techniques of disease diagnosis. Nonetheless, illness prediction algorithms provide an economical and effective substitute if there is enough data available. We created a model in this project which uses patient symptoms to reliably forecast illness. Our accuracy range was 94%, thanks to the use of four separate algorithms. Through the provision of an automated, dependable approach to early illness identification, this model shows promise for improving future medical treatments. Our research indicates that the usage of these predictive models in the healthcare systems may reduce the workload of medical staff members and lower healthcare expenses, hence will increase the availability of high-quality healthcare. To improve and verify these models for use in real-world scenarios, it is needed that research and development efforts in symptom-based illness prediction continue.

title: Bokeh Rendering via Diffusion Models without Depth Priors
authors: Linxiao Shi, Shifeng Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Bokeh rendering has traditionally been limited by the lack of high-quality ground-truth data. Most existing bokeh rendering datasets provide only a small number of image pairs captured with different apertures, often taken in street environments, and lack sample diversity. As a result, previous methods generally exhibit poor generalization performance in real-world scenarios. In this work, we address this issue from two perspectives. First, we argue that large-scale data pre-training is crucial, and we propose a scalable synthetic data generation pipeline that leverages a de-bokeh model to create datasets. Second, we introduce a novel bokeh rendering approach that effectively utilizes the rich prior information from a pre-trained stable diffusion model. This architecture has two main advantages. First, the prior knowledge from the diffusion model plays a key role in bridging the gap between simulated and real-world scenes. Second, unlike most methods that rely on depth priors, our model can retain complex foreground details, such as hair, while accurately rendering highly intricate bokeh effects, without the need for depth maps or other auxiliary inputs. We conduct extensive quantitative evaluations on benchmark datasets, demonstrating that our method outperforms approaches and exhibits strong generalization capabilities.

title: Exploring Human-in-the-Loop Integration in the Operation of Digital Twin Applications
authors: Johannes Deufel, Sanja Lazarova-Molnar
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: Human-in-the-loop integration in the operation of Digital Twins has emerged as a critical research direction to advance their fidelity in representing real-world entities. Although a wide range of frameworks, interaction models and architectures exist for integrating humans into information systems, the systematic involvement of humans in the operation of Digital Twins remains limited. In this paper, we present a structured literature survey focusing on the current state of human-in-the-loop integration within Digital Twin implementations. We systematically analyze peer-reviewed studies that incorporate human-in-the-loop approaches into real implementations and prototypes of Digital Twins, and identify the types and characteristics of these integrations. We compare human roles, interaction elements, degrees of human involvement (according to an established Digital Twin framework), and the interfaces for human interaction. As an additional outcome of the survey, we identify key challenges and open research questions that inform the development of a roadmap for future work in this evolving interdisciplinary field.

title: IoT-Enabled Anti-Poaching Sensor System for Sandalwood Trees Using Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Akshatha Bhat, Archana Bhat
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Illegal logging of high-value species such as red sandalwood has emerged as a significant environmental and economic concern. Conventional surveillance techniques, including CCTV, RFID tagging, and manual patrolling, are costly, limited in coverage, or provide only post-event information. The work presents a novel IoT-enabled wireless sensor network (WSN) for anti-poaching and fire prevention in forest environments. The proposed system integrates tilt, sound, and flame sensors with an ESP32 microcontroller to detect tree felling, acoustic cutting signatures, and fire outbreaks. Sensor data are transmitted in real time to a cloud-connected Android/web dashboard, enabling remote monitoring, rapid alerts, and preventive actuation through alarms and automated water pumps. Prototype validation demonstrates reliable detection with high accuracy, low latency, and cost-effective scalability. The novelty of the work lies in its application-specific integration of multi-sensor fusion, real-time IoT dashboard monitoring, and preventive interventions, offering a proactive framework for sandalwood tree protection and forest conservation.

title: TACNet: Architecture for SAR Image Despeckling
authors: Hetvi Radadiya, Jigar Sarda, Amit Thakkar, Pia Patel, Rohan Vaghela, Kirtan Santoki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Due to its capacity to picture in any weather, synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images are widely used in the remote sensing community. Deep learning (DL) has demonstrated effectiveness as an efficient technique for despeckling SAR images. Currently, the majority of deep learning models are trained to minimize speckle adhering to a specific distribution, either through simulated noise or a particular collection of actual SAR images, hence constraining the applicability of these techniques to real SAR images with indeterminate noise statistics. Speckle is a condition depending on the target; hence, its estimate and mitigation need the identification of particular characteristics of the image's features. Speckle reduction has been a persistent issue in SAR imagery. Many different approaches have been developed for the rebuilding of intensity SAR images. The transformer-based encoder in the proposed despeckling network enables the network to recognize global dependencies between various areas of the image, which helps with despeckling. To capture every structural feature of the SAR image, the proposed model combines a SE module with a CBAM. From the testing results, the proposed model performs better than many cutting-edge techniques in terms of visual quality and quantitative metrics, and it has a greater capacity to restore the SAR image's fine texture and structure.

title: A Novel Indoor Intelligent Localization Strategy Based on NRBO-SVR Technology
authors: Xuyan Sa, Qinghai Li, Baofeng Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: WiFi indoor positioning attracts significant attention from researchers. It faces numerous challenges, and the core issue is its low positioning accuracy, which limits its widespread adoption. To improve accuracy, we propose a WiFi indoor positioning algorithm based on NRBO-optimized SVR. During the offline phase, the fingerprint database is first standardized by the algorithm, and then NRBO is used to optimize the parameters C and $\gamma$ in SVR to construct the algorithmic model. Experimental results in real-world scenarios show an average positioning error of $\mathbf{2. 4 8}$ meters. Compared to GA-SVR, SVR and WKNN, the proposed algorithm improves positioning accuracy by $11.1 \%, 32.2 \%$ and $17.61 \%$, respectively. Simulations demonstrate an average error of $\mathbf{1. 4 4}$ meters.

title: Geo-Temporal LSTM-Based Trajectory Prediction of Mobile Robots Using Sequential Multi-Sensor Data
authors: Sheeba Uruj, Avani Kottalgi, Kalaichelvi Venkatesan, Sujala Deepak Shetty, Karthikeyan Ramanujam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: To plan a safe path, mobile robots need to avoid collisions and navigate efficiently in dynamic environments. Reliable prediction of their next position is a crucial component for autonomous navigation. Nonlinearity of the robot motion and noise in sensors make traditional filtering based techniques and physics-based models difficult to apply. Although deep learning methods like Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) based on deep networks have performed well in modeling sequential representations, they are not capable of handling complex spatial dependencies successfully. Geo-Temporal Long Short Term Memory (GT-LSTM) provides a unified framework for considering temporal sequence learning along with encoded spatial structures that are capable of yielding better prediction in dynamic environment. This paper demonstrates the potential of the GT-LSTM framework in autonomous trajectory prediction which can find applications in multiple sectors like logistics, agriculture and many more.

title: From Frames to Fakes: Deep Learning and Classical Approaches for Video-Based Deepfake Detection
authors: Debasish Nayak, Bhabani Shankar Prasad Mishra, Satyananda Champati Rai
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: As AI-generated deepfakes become increasingly realistic, ensuring the authenticity and integrity of visual content has turned into a significant challenge. This paper presents a dual-strategy detection framework combining deep convolutional networks and traditional feature-based classifiers for framelevel deepfake detection. A hybrid dataset of 400 videos from FaceForensics++ and Celeb-DF was curated, yielding 16,000 facecropped frames. Three CNNs-MobileNetV2, EfficientNetB2, and DenseNet121 were fine-tuned using data augmentation and regularization. Handcrafted features (HOG, LBP) were used with SVM and Random Forest for baseline comparison. EfficientNetB2 achieved the highest test accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(92.19 \%)$</tex>, while DenseNet121 showed strong recall (95.83%) and AUC (0.9618). Results highlight the superior capability of deep learning models over classical methods and suggest future directions toward spatiotemporal integration for robust deepfake detection.

title: Panicle-Driven Rice Yield Estimation Using YOLO: A Data-Centric Deep Learning Approach
authors: Sujit Kumar Jena, Samaleswari Prasad Nayak, Ramakrushna Swain, Surajit Das
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Estimating rice yield accurately is essential for effective harvest planning and food distribution. Among the key indicators of rice productivity is the number of panicles per unit area. However, manually counting panicles is labor-intensive and inefficient for large-scale fields. To address this challenge, a framework has been proposed to automate panicle detection and counting using deep learning. The approach leverages a YOLOv5-based object detection model trained on labeled images of rice panicles captured using real-world conditions. The trained model demonstrates strong detection performance across varying orientations and panicle structures. By estimating panicle density from the images, the system enables scalable yield estimation through extrapolation, aiding in pre-harvest analysis. This method eliminates the need for manual counting and serves as a practical, non-destructive tool for field-level yield monitoring in rice agriculture to achieve significant results.

title: Beam-Squint-Aided Hierarchical Sensing for Integrated Sensing and Communications With Uniform Planar Arrays
authors: Jaehong Jo, Jihun Park, Yo-Seb Jeon, H. Vincent Poor
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical sensing framework for wideband integrated sensing and communications with uniform planar arrays (UPAs). Leveraging the beam-squint effect inherent in wideband orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) systems, the proposed framework enables efficient two-dimensional angle estimation through a structured multi-stage sensing process. Specifically, the sensing procedure first searches over the elevation angle domain, followed by a dedicated search over the azimuth angle domain given the estimated elevation angles. In each stage, true-time-delay lines and phase shifters of the UPA are jointly configured to cover multiple grid points simultaneously across OFDM subcarriers. To enable accurate and efficient target localization, we formulate the angle estimation problem as a sparse signal recovery problem and develop a modified matching pursuit algorithm tailored to the hierarchical sensing architecture. Additionally, we design power allocation strategies that minimize total transmit power while meeting performance requirements for both sensing and communication. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves superior performance over conventional sensing methods with reduced sensing power.

title: Topological and Entropic Analysis of Steganography and Steganalysis via AI-Driven Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Kuldeep Singh Kaswan, Jagjit Singh Dhatterwal, Varun Malik, Anupam Baliyan, Dinesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: In this paper we introduce a novel combination of Topological data analysis (TDA) and the informationtheoretic entropy measures integrated into an AI-based Multi-Agent System (MAS) framework to provide an enhanced steganography and steganalysis analysis. As a consequence of advanced hidden means of the communication and the increased requirements for smart adaptive destruction methods, the convergence of topology, entropy and AI in security is studied. Leveraging core concepts from steganography, steganalysis, and MAS design, we introduce a model formed by special agents—Embedder, Extractor, Detector, and Coordinator—that circulate in centralized, decentralized, and hybrid communication models. These agents are augmented with deep learning models (CNNs, GANs, Trans- formers) to extract features, predict payloads, and detect anomalies. We use persistent homology to extract topological features from high dimensional stego-object Feature Spaces, manifesting difference in the underlying manifolds of cover vs. stego data. In addition, we use entropic measures (Shannon, Rényi, KL divergence, predictive entropy) of security, agent uncertainty and information loss/gain in communications. Synergy of topological and entropic views enable us to define joint topological-entropic signatures that enhance readability and the steganalysis agents' performance. Experimental on image and audio steganography data sets (e.g., BOSSBase, ALASKA) demonstrates the proposed method's superiority of detection accuracy and robustness over baseline methods. Our findings convey that MAS dynamics particularly in adversarial training, largely decide the topological complexity and entropy landscape of learned feature space. The paper finishes with some discussion on these results when designing adaptive AI-based steganalysis systems.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Protocols for Secure and Scalable Data Transmission
authors: Ankita Kirti, Vineet Kumar, Meenakshi Kumari, Prakash Anand, Jyoti Nagpal, Rashmi Kumari, Nitesh Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Based on the methods discussed, a framework for integrating blockchain technology into networking protocols was created in this study. Some of the difficulties that conventional networking protocols face when integrating blockchain technology into communication networks are covered in this paper. This paper also provides case studies and examples to provide a clearer picture of how blockchain technology might be utilised to enhance security, privacy, and decentralisation in networking protocols. Based on the open and unchangeable nature of blockchain technology, this article offered suggestions for communication networks to achieve higher levels of integrity and trust while lowering any dangers related to centralised control and single points of failure. Furthermore, the study looks at advanced security feature which is SHA-256 cryptographic hashing to show how blockchain can maintain data integrity and guard against malicious attacks.

title: Triple Band Dual Element Bow-Tie Antenna for Wirelss Application
authors: Shanta Satyapathi, Gautam Kumar, Sachin Kumar, Kunal Srivastava
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This paper proposes a compact 3D design and analysis of a sub-6 GHz bow-tie antenna for wireless applications. The designed antenna worked on 2.45GHz, 2.95GHz and 4.63GHz resonating frequencies with the help of dual radiating elements. The all-resonating band has a good bandwidth that fits with the required applications. The designed bow-tie antenna gain varied from 2dB to 5dB and efficiency of more than 95%, which is quite good for a compact structure. The radiation patterns in both the E-plane and H-plane are stable in all the resonating bands. With these features, the proposed bow-tie antenna is suitable for multiple sub-6 GHz uses, including WLAN, IoT devices, and other communication systems.

title: Resolving Cyber Security Issues in 6G Networks Using Blockchain Technology and Quantum Computing
authors: Arun Agarwal, Akankshya Jena, Pratyusha Mohanty
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The sixth-generation wireless communication <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(6 \mathrm{G})$</tex> promises advancements in speed, latency, and connectivity to facilitate revolutionary technology like holographic communication, extensive AI, and Internet of Everything (IoE). Such breakthroughs come with sophisticated cyber security issues like ultra-dynamic attack surfaces, decentralized device systems, and quantum-vulnerable encryption protocols. To counter these challenges, this paper investigates the application of blockchain technology and quantum computing as solutions for 6G cyber security improvement. Blockchain provides a tamper-proofed, decentralized system that guarantees data integrity, secure authentication, and transparent access control among heterogeneous devices. However, quantum computing also provides solutions such as post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and quantum key distribution (QKD) that can enhance communication resilience against quantum and conventional assaults.

title: Sentinel Turret Rover: Intruder Defense System with Precision Shooting
authors: Abhishta H Mallaya, Vyshnav Kumar S, Gowtham S D, Kanishka S J, Abhishek S, Akhil V M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper presents the Sentinel Turret Rover, an advanced autonomous security system that integrates ArcFace with a Cross-Attention enhanced Convolutional Block Attention Module (ArcFace-CA-CBAM) for high-precision intruder detection and response capabilities. The proposed system combines a mobile platform with a turret mechanism, offering a more comprehensive security solution that overcomes the limitations of traditional fixed surveillance systems. The novel ArcFace-CA -CBAM architecture significantly improves feature extraction and contextual understanding through the fusion of channel and spatial attention with cross-attention mechanisms. Experimental results demonstrate high detection accuracy achieved by the integration of ArcFace-CA -CBAM in variable lighting conditions. The hardware architecture integrates a Raspberry Pi computing module with custom-designed mechanical components that provide 360° surveillance coverage. This research contributes to the advancement of intelligent security systems by establishing a framework for autonomous threat assessment and response with enhanced situational awareness. Integration of alert system that sends notification message when intruder is detected.

title: On the Impact of Chunking Strategies in NLP Pipelines: A Multi-Task Empirical Study
authors: Sawan Rai, Ramesh Chandra Belwal
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Chunking input text is a crucial preprocessing step when using Large Language Models (LLMs) for long or structured documents. However, its impact on downstream task performance remains underexplored. This study presents a comprehensive empirical analysis evaluating the effect of various chunking strategies: fixed-size, overlapping, sentence-based, and paragraph-based, across three fundamental NLP tasks: question answering, text classification, and abstractive summarization. Experiments were conducted using lightweight, open-access models such as Flan-T5, GPT-2, DistilBERT, and RoBERTa on benchmark datasets including SQuAD, CoQA, QuAC, IMDB, Amazon Polarity, CNN/DailyMail, and XSum. Performance was measured using task-appropriate metrics (ROUGE, EM, F1, precision, recall) along with latency. Results reveal that chunking strategies significantly affect performance and latency, with no single approach universally optimal. These findings highlight the need for task-specific chunking choices in practical LLM deployments, especially under resource constraints.

title: Intelligent Energy Prediction Mechanism for Smart Buildings Using CNN-BiLSTM and Occupant Count Data
authors: S.Naveen Kumar, Gotlur Karuna, A Sujin Jose, Venkata Siva Bathula, Ginni Nijhawan, Dinesh Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: A critical need for reducing resource abuse and waste creation and increasing sustainable is the correct identification of energy consumption in smart structures. Traditional energy forecasting systems lack both occupation data and temporal-spatial strategic data collection abilities. The proposed research implements a combination of CNN along with BiLSTM networks for building an advanced energy prediction system. The CNN part of the model extracts spatial features from the data inputs and BiLSTM analyses long temporal dependencies to make more accurate energy usage estimates. A key addition of occupant count as model attribute brings enhanced applicability to various buildings in different geographic locations. Experimental assessments of the anticipated system rely on real data to show better results than current techniques in prediction accuracy. The proposed model shows improved accuracy through its performance metrics including MAE and MSE together with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values. Analytical outcomes demonstrate that the CNN-BiLSTM hybrid model effectively decreases energy consumption discrepancies because it improves decision-making capabilities in smart construction liveliness supervision arrangements. The findings improve the development of intelligent automated energy management systems which help organizations achieve sustainable energy utilization goals. The proposed approach attains an overall accuracy of 98%, demonstrating its effectiveness relative to other models.

title: An Incremental Target Recognition Method for HRRP Based on Autoencoder Reconstruction and Multi-Level Knowledge Distillation
authors: Zhipeng Chang, Yuzhou Lv, Bo Tian, Xianjie Xiao, Shuai Guo, Penghui Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of catastrophic forgetting in HRRP-based target recognition under limited data availability and dynamically evolving target categories. An incremental learning framework, termed AE-MKD, is proposed, which incorporates an autoencoder-based task-agnostic feature branch, expandable task-specific branches, and multi-level knowledge distillation to enhance new-class learning while preserving previously learned knowledge. The framework further combines teacher–student knowledge distillation to suppress unbounded parameter growth during incremental learning under constrained storage conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that AE-MKD outperforms existing incremental learning methods in terms of overall recognition accuracy and old-class retention, indicating its effectiveness for dynamic HRRP target recognition scenarios.

title: an EEG Study
authors: Nasrin Dehghani, Negin Joghataei, Zahra Ghanbari, Mohammad Hassan Moradi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Analyzing brain signals can provide valuable insights into the complexities of anxiety and contribute to improve mental health. In this paper, we used the DASPS dataset, which contains EEG recordings from 23 healthy individuals in a resting state with eyes closed. Participants were presented with descriptions of 6 stressful scenarios and assessed their emotional states through questionnaires. Functional connectivity was estimated using the Weighted Phase Lag Index (WPLI), and dynamic analysis was conducted through clustering methods to detect change points between different mental states. Above static and dynamic features were used for statistical analysis and classification on the 6 scenarios. Statistical tests revealed significant differences across all frequency bands between the verbal abuse scenario and the witnessing a fatal accident, while no significant difference was observed among the remaining scenarios. Classification was performed using a Support Vector Machine (SVM), and model performance was evaluated through 5-fold cross-validation. The best accuracy in the six-class classification was achieved in the gamma band, 42.02 %, after applying a proposed majority voting approach. Our analysis suggests that static features showed relatively better classification performance than dynamic features in this dataset, although overall accuracies remained modest across all bands.

title: Data-Driven Techniques and Their Application in Agriculture Using Machine Learning
authors: TV Naveen, ArunKumar S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent Optimal Condition Suggestion System designed to support precision agriculture by recommending the ideal soil and climatic conditions for cultivating specific crops. The system surpasses traditional crop recommendation models by offering a detailed compatibility analysis, highlighting the necessary adjustments in nutrient levels (including calcium, zinc, magnesium, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, carbon, boron, pH, temperature, humidity, and rainfall) for growing a target crop under current conditions. The tool also predicts the most suitable crop for the given input, offering both forward (recommendation) and reverse (compatibility) inference capabilities. A parallel hybrid ensemble model combining Random Forest and XGBoost was developed, with each model achieving 90.72% and 92.88% accuracy, respectively. The combined model demonstrated superior performance with an accuracy of 99.05% supported by hyperparameter tuning using a hybrid search strategy. The training dataset, sourced from Kerala's agricultural college covering all districts and panchayaths, included 12 agronomic and environmental features along with geospatial tags. Public datasets were used for external validation. Exploratory data analysis and class imbalance correction were conducted to ensure robust modelling. A bilingual web application was developed using Streamlit, allowing users to receive not only crop recommendations but also actionable insights on what conditions must be optimised for successful cultivation. Feature importance analysis revealed that soil nutrients contributed more to prediction accuracy than climatic parameters, with calcium, magnesium, and phosphorus being the most significant. This system offers an interpretable, high-accuracy tool for farmers and agronomists, bridging the gap between predictive analytics and actionable crop planning in region-specific agriculture.

title: Voice-Driven Smart Home Automation System with AR-Enhanced Interfaces and Quantum Optimization
authors: Krish Sen, Tanish Kapoor, Ansh Walia, Aishwarya Mishra, Sudhanshu Ranjan, Pulkit Dwivedi
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Smart home automation is an interesting and useful field of study for new technologies that will make life easier, more comfortable and more energy-efficient. The available solutions suffer from some challenges, including accessibility constraints, poor user engagement, high cost, and poor efficiency of advanced technology integration. Thus, this paper addresses these issues by introducing the smart home automation system, incorporating voice command controls, augmented reality (AR) interfaces, and quantum computing techniques. The proposed model envisages Arduino UNO, a main control unit, integrated with Bluetooth-based interfaces for online operation of connected appliances. A 4-channel relay module works to imitate a wide variety of conditions present within households. Experimental evaluations demonstrate an accuracy of voice command recognition of 80%, while with support from quantum computing techniques, this level is expected to increase to above 90%. Moreover, overall energy efficiency enhancement recorded ranges from 70% to 90%. With the AR interface coming up to 85% of user satisfaction to 95%, doubled data processing speed on quantum neural networks estimated to bring about a 25% increase in total user satisfaction. The framework proposed will create the basis for future smart home solutions that are extendable, secure, and intuitive. Such solutions will be ready for augmented reality and quantum computing in future residential automation..

title: Performance Evaluation of QChainFog - Queueing Assisted Blockchain Enabled IoST-Fog-Cloud Framework for Geospatial Big Data Analytics
authors: Swarnakanti Samantaray, Veena Goswami, Nachiketa Tarasia, Meenakshi Kandpal, G.B. Mund, Rabindra Kumar Barik
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The amalgamation of the Internet of Spatial Things (IoST) with the Fog-Cloud computing architecture and Blockchain technology provides a robust solution for scalable and secure geospatial big data analytics. Coordinating data flow among resource-limited devices, latency-sensitive fog nodes, and robust cloud resources—while ensuring data integrity—presents considerable performance challenges. This paper introduces the novel QChainFog which assesses an innovative framework that utilizes strategic queueing models to manage data transfer and compute offloading inside the blockchain assisted IoST-Fog-Cloud architecture. It provides a comprehensive performance study emphasizing end-to-end latency, system throughput, resource utilization across all tiers, and blockchain efficiency. Our assessment reveals that the strategic implementation of queueing model with dynamic control disciplines not only averts system overload but also enhances the cost-performance balance, rendering the framework applicable to a diverse range of geospatial applications, including real-time trajectory tracking and long-term climate modeling.

title: GR-Moment: Quantifying Match Momentum for Real-Time Tennis Prediction via a Hybrid Fuzzy-Neural Framework
authors: Jiaming Liu, Xiaoyang Lu, Di Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Forecasting tennis outcomes is complicated by non-linear scoring and latent player psychology. Conventional static models often fail to capture these dynamics. This paper proposes GR-Moment, a hybrid framework that quantifies transient "momentum" via a Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process and integrates it into a Generalized Regression Neural Network. Leveraging GRNN’s rapid approximation for high-volatility scenarios, the proposed model achieves 86.64% accuracy on the Wimbledon 2023 dataset, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art ensemble baselines including XGBoost. Furthermore, statistical analysis uncovers distinct distributional asymmetries between momentum collapse and reversal, offering interpretable insights for tactical decision-making.

title: Implementation of a Student Psychological Crisis Early Warning System Based on Multimodal Data Fusion
authors: Jinran Kuang, Mingjing Li, Guonian Jin, Zhiyong Wang, Yang Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: To address the issues of lag, single-dimensional data, and weak privacy protection mechanisms in current university mental health crisis early warning systems, this paper implements a student mental health crisis early warning system based on multimodal data fusion. The system comprehensively utilizes existing campus surveillance cameras, audio equipment, and social media data to construct a data collection and processing pipeline for three modalities: video (facial expressions), audio (tone and semantics), and text (social media content). DeepFace and ArcFace are employed for facial identity and expression recognition; ECAPA-TDNN and Transformer technologies are used to achieve audio speaker identification, speech emotion recognition, and text transcription; and the BERT model is utilized for sentiment analysis of the transcribed text and social media content. An innovative dynamic weighted multimodal emotion fusion algorithm is proposed, which calculates single-channel and comprehensive emotion scores and introduces a baseline emotion and fluctuation detection mechanism to achieve dynamic, quantitative assessment and abnormal early warning of students' mental states. Role-based three-tier access control and data anonymization techniques are adopted to ensure data security throughout its lifecycle. Experimental results show that the system achieved a mental health crisis identification accuracy of 91.4% in a pilot deployment, with a significantly reduced false alarm rate. This research applies deep learning and multimodal information processing technologies to the field of mental health, providing a feasible technical solution for building an intelligent and proactive campus psychological protection system.

title: Machine Learning for Mental Health Illness Detection Based on Behavioral and Thought Patterns
authors: Sumathi Pawar, Ashwin Kumar M, Sunitha N V, Prashanthi K S, Rajermani Thinakaran
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Mental health illness is a consequence of increased pressure and stress. Mental health issues encompass a range of conditions, including anxiety, depression, stress, schizophrenia, and bipolar disorder, each characterized by distinct physical and emotional symptoms. This study aims to identify mental illness based on individuals' behaviors and thoughts, assessing symptoms such as panic attacks, sweating, palpitations, sadness, worry, excessive rumination, and severe indicators like delusions and hallucinations, which signal various mental disorders. Logistic Regression, SVM, KNN, and Decision Tree are techniques used for training and prediction of mental illness. The output of these models are taken as input to Stacking, Voting, XGBoost and LightGBM and output of ensemble models are analysed. Dominating features and coefficients of features are found during implementation. To evaluate the effectiveness of the machine learning models, metrics such as accuracy, recall, precision, and F1-score are calculated, allowing for a comprehensive comparison of the models. This system emphasizes workplace and lifestyle risk factors for depression. Unlike prior works that rely mainly on clinical or questionnaire-based datasets, this study focuses on professional populations. It is shown that linear models (SVM, logistic regression) generalize better than instancebased or tree-based methods, and provide feature-level insights (e.g., sleep duration, financial stress) that can guide workplace mental health monitoring tools. It is shown that stacking and XGBoost gives better accuracy compared to all other models ensemble models.

title: ILT: Advancing Indoor Localization with Transformer Learning on Wi-Fi RSSI Data
authors: Balaram Singh, Rajdeep Chatterjee
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Wi-Fi with radio fingerprinting is one of the most widely used methods for indoor positioning. Since its inception, people have approached it as a traditional machine learning problem, with an average accuracy of a few meters. With numerous publications claiming sub-meter positioning accuracy, deep learning (DL) has become a viable alternative strategy recently. This study describes indoor localization using a deep learning approach, that is, Transformer. In this paper, we introduce Indoor Localization using Transformer (ILT), a type of Transformer Neural Network that learns from examples to find a specific location by using the Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) measurements taken at that location. The paper compares and contrasts the performance accuracy of ILT with five distinct models using two benchmark datasets. Our ILT shows improved performance accuracy with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 5. 9 4 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 7. 6 0 \%}$</tex> on UJIIndoorLoc and MNAV datasets, respectively. This paper evaluates various SOTA algorithms and demonstrates superior accuracy and efficiency in several benchmark datasets.

title: Deep Learning for Abnormal Human Activity Detection: A Comprehensive Review of Technology, Evaluation parameters and Recent trends in Intelligent Surveillance
authors: Shweta S. Patil, U.L.Bombale
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The comprehensive integration of Closed-Circuit Television (CCTV) systems in combination with camera-enabled devices throughout public and private sectors has contributed to a substantial augmentation in the volume of video surveillance data. The process of evaluating this extensive array of video material to pinpoint abnormal activities continues to represent a considerable and intricate challenge. In reaction to this challenge, state-of-the-art deep learning frameworks, notably Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have come forth as robust solutions for the automated detection of anomalous behaviors in video streams. By employing their organized architecture, which features convolutional layers, max-pooling layers, Rectified Linear Units (ReLU), and fully connected layers, CNNs manifest the competence to efficiently handle video frames and discern irregularities from conventional patterns. These models exhibit particular relevance in high-stakes scenarios, such as examination locations, where the deployment of real-time anomaly detection can strengthen the effectiveness of surveillance, discourage unethical behaviors, and encourage equity. Even with their notable prospects, the deployment of such systems calls for a diligent focus on ethical implications, particularly concerning user confidentiality and data protection. This survey delivers an in-depth exploration of the contemporary field of research regarding video-based anomaly detection through the application of CNNs. It underscores pivotal datasets, recent technological innovations, and emerging trends that are influencing the evolution of more resilient and intelligent surveillance frameworks.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Enhanced Detection of Small and Dense Defects in Engine Oil Pipelines
authors: Penghui Xin, Hongwei Gao, Hongrun Luan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: The detection of defects in the inner wall of engine oil pipelines is critical for ensuring system reliability and safety. Traditional methods rely on manual inspection under natural lighting, which is prone to human error and environmental influences, leading to limited accuracy. Although deep learning techniques, such as YOLO, have made progress in detecting defects like cracks, wear, and corrosion, several challenges remain. CNNs struggle to capture global patterns, especially for pipe wall defects, which may lead to inaccurate results. While YOLO excels in speed, it faces limitations in detecting overlapping or small defects, impacting precision. To address these issues, this study proposes a Progressive Feature Pyramid Network (EPFPN). The network utilizes ConvNeXtV2 as the backbone, providing robust feature extraction capabilities, particularly for dense defect detection. Additionally, the study introduces the GS Bottleneck to improve the accuracy of small defect detection. The proposed method significantly enhances detection precision, especially for small and densely packed defects.

title: Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Trajectory Tracking Control of Manipulator Based on an Improved Dung Beetle Optimizer
authors: Jintao Ye, Lina Hao, Hongtai Cheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: To enhance the control performance and improve the disturbance rejection capability of the manipulator, a Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control (LADRC) method with self-tuning parameters is proposed, based on the improved dung beetle optimizer (LCDBO). LADRC can estimate and compensate for the unmodeled dynamics and external disturbances in the system as a total disturbance. However, its parameters are strongly coupled and difficult to tune. Therefore, this paper introduces the improved dung beetle optimizer. Lévy flight and Cauchy mutation strategies are incorporated into the standard dung beetle optimizer (DBO) to further boost the optimizer’s performance and realize the optimal adjustment of the LADRC parameters. Applied to the manipulator trajectory tracking control problem, the LCDBO algorithm achieves a faster convergence speed and higher solution precision compared to the original DBO algorithm. When the controller parameters optimized by the LCDBO algorithm are applied to the manipulator trajectory tracking problem with disturbance torques, it results in high trajectory tracking accuracy and excellent disturbance rejection performance.

title: High-Resolution Image Reconstruction Using Convolutional Neural Network-Based Deep Learning Models
authors: Saurabh Pratap Singh Rathore, R K Yadav, Prashant Dwivedi, Jayapal Lande, Surendarkumar S, Sheela S
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: This paper presents an optimized lightweight Super-Resolution Convolutional Neural Network (SRCNN) capable of reconstructing high-quality images with strong fidelity. The proposed framework introduces three key innovations: (1) a residual learning strategy with adaptive skip connections for preserving fine-grained details, (2) a data augmentation pipeline designed to mimic realistic degradation using bicubic downsampling and noise injection, and (3) a memory-efficient architectural design. All convolutional layers utilize 64 feature channels, while ConvTranspose2d-based upscaling combined with progressive residual blocks enhances texture recovery and reduces computational overhead. The training process leverages geometric transformations and photometric variations to improve generalization across diverse image domains. Experimental results demonstrate that the model reduces parameter count by 28% compared to standard SRCNN variants while achieving a 17.89 dB PSNR gain over bicubic interpolation on benchmark datasets. The proposed method processes 128×128 patches at 47 FPS on consumergrade GPUs, enabling real-time deployment. Ablation studies further show that integrating residual blocks with a global skip connection improves SSIM by 15%, especially in texture-dense regions. Overall, this work highlights an effective balance between computational efficiency and reconstruction quality for resource-constrained super-resolution systems

title: Salt Supply Chain Modelling: A Dynamic System Approach to Salt Price Prediction
authors: Titus Kristanto, Ahmad Saikhu, Bilqis Amaliah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Salt is a crucial commodity in Indonesia, necessary for household consumption and various industries, including food, chemicals, and pharmaceuticals. Between 2019 and 2024, national salt price exhibited significant fluctuations, largely influenced by production irregularities, weather patterns, dependence on imports, and distribution inefficiencies. The dynamics indicate that the formation of salt price is affected not only by the direct interplay of supply and demand but also by complex feedback loops and delays in the supply chain. This research develops a system dynamics model to analyze and predict the behavior of the national salt supply chain and its impact on price fluctuations. The research framework includes the creation of a causal loop diagram and a stock flow diagram, integrating variables such as salt production, harvested area, population growth, industrial and household demand, imports, and climate conditions. The model is calibrated using national salt data from 2019 to 2024 and is validated through error rate and variance tests, demonstrating high accuracy and reliability. The variables Salt Production, Population, and Area were used in the system dynamics modeling. The results obtained from the three variables showed a mean comparison value (E1) of $\leq 5 \%$ and an error variance value (E2) of $\leq 30 \%$.

title: A Low-Power High-Sensitivity 100-GHz 2:1 ECL Static Frequency Divider with 68-GHz SOF in 130-nm SiGe:C BiCMOS
authors: Eren Vardarli, Austin Ying-Kuang Chen, Michael Schroter
publish: 2026 IEEE 26th Topical Meeting on Silicon Monolithic Integrated Circuits in RF Systems (SiRF)
abstract: This paper presents a low-power, high-speed 2:1 ECL static frequency divider implemented in a 130-nm SiGe:C BiCMOS technology with an f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>/f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf> of 250/370 GHz. Design techniques. including asymmetric latch and inductive peaking, are employed to further extend the operating frequency beyond 100 GHz while maintaining low DC power consumption. For on-wafer characterization, Marchand baluns are integrated at both the input and output of the divider. The divider demonstrates wideband operation from 30 GHz to 100 GHz, exhibiting excellent input sensitivity and a single-ended output power of -6 dBm at 75 GHz. Through device and layout optimization, the divider achieves a self-oscillation frequency (SOF) of 68 GHz and a total DC power consumption of only 90 mW, including the output buffer, under a 3 V supply.

title: Application of Deep Q-Learning for Contamination Assessment in Water
authors: Shashi Bhushan, Aparna Sharma, Divya Rastogi, Manik Chandra Pandey, Bhavesh Kumar Sharma, Vishvendra Pal Singh Nagar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Water contamination is critical environmental and public health issue. Traditional contamination assessment relies on threshold-based monitoring and supervised learning techniques, which often fail to generalize to unseen contamination scenarios. This manuscript proposes a deep Q-learning framework for contamination assessment in water systems. By treating the assessment process as a sequential decision-making problem, the proposed system adaptively learns policies to classify and predict contamination events. The proposed system is evaluated on water potability dataset from Kaggle repository, which provides physicochemical properties of water samples. To observe the experimental results, the proposed model significantly outperforms traditional classifiers in term of adaptability, accuracy, and false-positive reduction. The experimental outcome demonstrate how useful reinforcement learning to facilitates intelligent water quality assessment.

title: Evaluation of Lightning Protection System on High Voltage Overhead Transmission Line 150 kV
authors: Andi Junaidi, Denny Setiawan, A. Agus Yogianto, Sugeng Purwanto, Agus Sudianto
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: A direct strike on the phase transmision line can cause a traveling wave which will also cause damage to the insulator, this is called a sheilding failure. On transmission lines, especially high-voltage 150 kV air transmission lines, there are still many disturbances due to lightning strikes. this can be seen in the results of the evaluation of the disturbances that occur, there are still many disturbances caused by lightning strikes. The frequent disturbances on the 150 kV high-voltage transmission line are due to flashback and shielding failure phenomena. These disturbances can cause damage to the insulator and can trip the circuit breaker which results in a loss of electric power or a black out in the system. In addition to that, there is a lack of an appropriate protection system against lightning strikes in Indonesia, including one of the areas with high lightning strikes, therefore a special handler is also needed for a protection system against lightning strikes on a 150 kV transmission line. To improve the performance of the channel against lightning, so that we usually optimize the reliability of lightning strikes.

title: Adaptation of Deep Learning Architectures for Bird Classification in Information Security Applications
authors: Sridhar Iyer, Narendra M. Shekokar, Kartikey Doshi, Akshay Kamath
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: This paper reviews deep learning architectures adaptation on fine grained bird species classification and how the models can be used in information security and visual encryption. We train seven different models, Swin Transformer, VGG19, InceptionV3, Xception, EfficientNet B0, GoogleNet, and a Custom CNN on a dataset of 400 bird species. Modern architectures-particularly transformer-based models and efficient convolutional variants-have achieved remarkable accuracy. For example, Swin-B reaches 99.60%. Rather than emphasizing benchmarking results alone, the focus here is on understanding how different architectural components influence performance and how they provide flexibility for integration into downstream secure systems. Substantial models like EfficientNet B0 are particularly attractive in actual deployment, where a trade-off between precision and computation cost is necessary, whereas deeper models, like Swin- B and Xception, have fine grained discriminative capability. This study highlights the potential of high-accuracy visual classifiers to serve as key components in cryptographic systems, secure authentication methods, and privacy-preserving AI applications.

title: Investigation of Microbubble Motion in a Microvessel with Various Obstructions Filled with Viscous Fluid: A Finite Element Modeling Study
authors: Mahdi Mirzaei, Afsaneh Mojra
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Microbubbles have emerged as powerful agents in biomedical imaging, targeted drug delivery, and ultrasound-mediated therapies due to their unique acoustic and transport properties. However, their motion within microvessels containing irregular structures remains insufficiently understood, particularly under viscous flow conditions where hydrodynamic and surface tension forces compete. Finite element modeling in COMSOL Multiphysics was used to analyze microbubble dynamics in viscous microvessels. Also, the Rayleigh-Plesset equation was applied to study microbubbles, capturing their radius dynamics under pressure, surface tension, and viscosity. The results reveal that obstruction geometry and position significantly influence microbubble trajectories and deformation, with asymmetrical obstructions inducing lateral migration. Changes in horizontal radius had little effect on bubble morphology. Shear stress analysis showed no consistent trend with vertical radius but indicated a clear increase with larger horizontal radii, where a threefold radius increase nearly doubled the maximum shear stress. During 4 microsecond interval period, the microbubble exhibits oscillatory behavior with a maximum radial displacement of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.4 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. These findings provide new mechanistic insights into microbubble-obstruction interactions, supporting the optimization of microbubble-mediated therapies and microfluidic biomedical device design.

title: VisionGuard: Anomaly Detection in Images with Hybrid Intelligence
authors: Dubba Hima Bindu, Siri M, Muvvala Sri Venkata Satya Shanvitha, Neetu Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Industrial quality control is intermittently confronted with challenges in coping with the visual uncertainty of manufactured items and the inherent limits of manual inspection. Feature-based image analysis, in conjunction with conventional machine learning methods, offers an effective solution over deep learning for flaw detection in resource-constrained environments. The system makes use of grayscale conversion, resizing, and flattening for image preprocessing, followed by standardization and dimensionality reduction utilizing PCA for better understanding of features. Covariance and distance matrices are calculated to measure correlations and spatial relationships between samples. Several models such as SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, and an optional autoencoder are trained and tested based on classification performance. Real-time interface enables one to enter new images, view them, and obtain instant predictions reflecting whether the object is ranked as "GOOD" or "DEFECTIVE." This process makes scalable industrial deployment possible along with interpretability, low computation expense, and applicability over a broad object variety. Performance measures like prediction accuracy, reconstruction loss (for autoencoders), and processing time are utilized to measure the efficiency and feasibility of the system for real-world use.

title: GelMA Synthesis and Experimental Challenges
authors: Mohammad Matin Shirzad, Zahra Mohammadi, Shaghayegh Kohzadi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Gelatin methacryloyl (GelMA) is a popular material in tissue engineering. It is made from gelatin and methacrylic anhydride (MAA) and capable of being photocrosslinked. GelMA is widely studied in tissue engineering due to its biocompatibility, tunability, and photocrosslinking capability. This study reviews the standard synthesis protocol and reports experimental challenges observed across three laboratory trials. In the first trial, rapid addition of MAA resulted in uneven reaction kinetics, and only 50% of the freeze-dried Falcon tubes yielded usable GelMA powder. In the second trial, dropwise addition improved reaction uniformity; however, overfilled tubes led to incomplete lyophilization, requiring a second cycle to achieve full drying. Re-drying recovered approximately 80% of the material as stable powder. In the third trial, casting into Petri dishes increased surface area and reduced thickness, enabling complete drying in a single cycle with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{> 9 0 \%}$</tex> recovery of dry GelMA. These results highlight the critical influence of reagent addition rate, pH stability, and mass-transfer efficiency on product consistency. The findings demonstrate that careful control of reaction and drying conditions is essential for reproducible GelMA suitable for biomedical applications.

title: Energy Dissipation Rate-Aware Clustering for Improving Wireless Sensor Network Lifetime
authors: Chandrashekhar Patel, Linesh Raja, Bhanu Priya
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: In the era of digital, Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) have been widely used and connected with the Internet of Things (IoT). However, WSNs have limits in battery power and sensor node life, so creating an efficient routing protocol is important. Cluster technology offers a promising solution to this challenge. This research work has proposed a new method of cluster head selection in wireless sensor networks. The proposed work selects the cluster head by considering the distance to the cluster centroid, residual energy, and EDR to reduce energy consumption and improve network lifetime. Simulations using NetSim 14.2 were conducted with 64 nodes. Compared to the traditional LEACH, TLEACH and KMEANS protocol. The analysis of the energy efficiency showed that the proposed is approximately 90.9% more efficient than LEACH, 91.7% more efficient than TLEACH, and about 90% more efficient than KMEANS. Throughput evaluation demonstrated that the proposed algorithm achieves the highest throughput. Compared to this, LEACH is about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5 \%}$</tex> lower, TLEACH is over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2 \%}$</tex> lower, and K-Means is nearly 89% lower. Furthermore, in the terms of network stability, the proposed algorithm outperforms compares to LEACH, TLEACH and KMEANS, as all the nodes are alive from 2000 to 5000 rounds. So, the proposed work ensures its effectiveness in enhancing energy-aware communication and extending WSN lifetime.

title: Dynamic Power Sharing Control for Magnetic-coupled Energy Router with Hybrid Energy Storage
authors: Yang Qi, Yexin Yan, Da Li, Ming Huang, Weilin Li, Xiong Liu, Frede Blaabjerg
abstract: Magnetic-coupled energy routers provide an effective solution for interfacing the load with hybrid energy storage systems. Particularly, it is important to achieve dynamic power sharing based on the characteristics of hybrid energy storage. This target is conventionally realized by the phase-shift control. However, the multiple-input and multiple-output (MIMO) feature of the energy router complicates the model and results in multiple control loops. Inspired by the virtual synchronous machine (VSM) control, this paper first proposes an emulated inertia and damping control for high-frequency magnetic-coupled energy routers. The proposed method can realize perfect dynamic power sharing in a simple and decentralized way, which improves the system modularity and flexibility. The principle is explicitly explained and validated by hardware experimental results.

title: Decentralized Stabilization of Smart Grids against Deception Cyber-attack via Active Disturbance Rejection Control
authors: Hisham M. Soliman, Sangkeum Lee, Farag A. El Sheikhi, Yeojin Jang, Ehab H. E. Bayoumi
abstract: Modern power infrastructure has evolved into Smart Grids (SGs), which are complex cyber-physical systems (CPS) integrating electrical networks with advanced communication and IT/OT. Due to their digitalization, SGs are particularly vulnerable to cyber threats such as deception attacks or fake data injection, which can cause system instability, widespread blackouts, cascading failures, and serious economic damage. This paper presents a novel decentralized stabilizer that strengthens the power grid's defences against these cyber-physical threats. This has been performed for the first time by applying the Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) approach to smart grid anti-attack control. The IEEE 39-bus, 10-generator New England test system, which is divided into three sub-networks, each consisting of coherent generators, is used to illustrate the stabilizer's efficacy. The robustness of the system against stochastic deception attacks, which are modeled using a Bernoulli distribution, is examined. Comparing the proposed ADRC approach to conventional H∞, linear quadratic regulator, and PID control schemes demonstrates its superiority.

title: An Intelligent Model to Predict Functional and Non-Functional Requirements from Software Requirements Specification
authors: Martina G. Said, Laila M. El-Fangary, Amr A. Abohany, Safaa M Azzam
abstract: Automated classification of Functional Requirements (FRs) and Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs) is a fundamental task in requirements engineering, yet it remains challenging due to the short length, domain specificity, and severe class imbalance of requirement texts. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid deep learning model that integrates BERT and RoBERTa through feature-level fusion, enabling joint learning of complementary contextual representations within a unified architecture. In addition, a targeted, semantics-preserving data augmentation strategy is employed to improve robustness and minority class recognition. The proposed approach is evaluated on two benchmark datasets, PROMISE and PROMISE_EXP. Experimental results demonstrate consistent and substantial improvements over state-of-the-art baselines. On the PROMISE dataset, the proposed model achieves an accuracy of 0.919, a macro-averaged F1-score of 0.84, and a weighted F1-score of 0.91. On the PROMISE_EXP dataset, it attains an accuracy of 0.93, a macro-averaged F1-score of 0.90, and a weighted F1-score of 0.93. Notably, the proposed model significantly enhances classification performance for minority NFR categories, highlighting the effectiveness of feature-level transformer fusion for fine-grained requirement classification. These results demonstrate the suitability of the proposed approach for practical and scalable requirements engineering applications.

title: Estimation of the uncertainty in the correction of differential slant ionospheric delay using Gaussian-Transformer neural network
authors: Yunri Fu, Ling Yang, Shaojun Feng, Nan Zhi, Yunzhong Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: State Space Representation (SSR) correction products, including satellite-related products (orbits, clock offsets, hardware biases) and atmosphere-related products (ionospheric and tropospheric delays), are typically broadcast to users to enhance the accuracy and integrity of GNSS positioning. All these SSR products require not only deterministic information for error correction but also uncertainty information for stochastic quality control. For regional high-accuracy and high-integrity applications, SSR services must specifically provide slant ionospheric delay information, including both deterministic corrections and associated uncertainty parameters. This paper proposes a deep learning-based Gaussian-Transformer neural network (GTNN) to generate two types of uncertainty parameters for regional differential slant ionospheric delays at the serviceend. One is the accurate Standard Deviation (STD) of the correction for enhancing positioning and the other is the conservative STD for enhancing integrity. Specifically, this model utilizes a Transformer network to capture complex spatial and temporal correlations in ionospheric behavior and employs a Gaussian likelihood function as a loss function to probabilistically represent the ionospheric stochastic component, providing an accurate STD of the ionospheric corrections. Then the accurate STD is inflated to account for non-Gaussian and spatial inconsistencies, providing a conservative STD. These two sets of STDs can compromise the balance between accuracy requirements and integrity assurance. This model has been verified based on the observational data from a GNSS network located in the lowlatitude region where the ionosphere is active. Experimental results demonstrate that the estimated accurate STDs precisely characterize the accuracy of ionospheric correction, responding promptly to modeling errors. Meanwhile, the estimated conservative STDs effectively bound ionospheric residual errors and meet the integrity requirement up to 99.999 conditions, the proposed method demonstrates resilience, maintaining a reliable error bounding probability of 99.9 despite the intensified ionospheric disturbances. Overall, this research highlights the potential of deep learning approaches to improve the accuracy and integrity parameters of ionospheric SSR products at the service-end.

title: Empirical Evaluation of AI-Assisted Risk Reasoning for ICPS Software Security
authors: Padma Iyenghar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: Operational Technology (OT) systems form the control and actuation layer of Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems (ICPS), linking computational intelligence with physical processes. Typical OT components (e.g., PLCs) with embedded dedicated control software operate in safety-critical environments where software reliability and cybersecurity are essential for overall system trustworthiness. However, existing OT security resources emphasize taxonomies and threat catalogs rather than reproducible benchmarks for empirical risk reasoning, particularly in evaluating Artificial Intelligence (AI)-assisted methods for cybersecurity risk assessment. In this context, this study introduces a reproducible, standards-aligned benchmark designed to evaluate AI-assisted risk reasoning in OT software within ICPS environments. This paper presents two main contributions toward advancing trustworthy and reliable ICPS. The first is the design of EmbSoftOTBench, a standards-aligned benchmark dataset for ICPS software risk reasoning that operationalizes IEC 62443-3-2 through machine-readable scenarios derived from MITRE EMB3D Embedded Systems Threat Matrix (EMB3D). The benchmark provides paired threat and benign scenarios with quantitative factors and expert rationales, enabling structured and reproducible evaluation of AI-assisted security reasoning within ICPS engineering workflows. The second contribution is an experimental evaluation of state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) employing the benchmark dataset. Results reveal systematic trade-offs between sensitivity, specificity, and calibration, establishing first baselines for AI-empowered ICPS software risk reasoning. The dataset is publicly released under the CC BY 4.0 license to support reproducible research, engineering practice, and education in safety-critical ICPS environments.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)

title: MEDCOM 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
