title: A Low-Cost IoT-Based Smart Weather Station for Real-Time Environmental Monitoring
authors: Abouchabana Nabil, Benmiloud Mohammed, Ameur Khaled, Hadjaissa Abobakeur, Zorig Abdelmalik, Benfarhat Sara
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a smart weather station using an ESP32 microcontroller and Internet of Things (IoT) technology. The system is designed to monitor key environmental parameters including temperature, humidity, pressure, altitude, solar irradiance, wind speed, and rainfall using dedicated sensors. Sensor data is transmitted to the IoT cloud platform for real-time monitoring and visualization on smartphones and computers. Moreover, the system is configured to trigger automated email alerts when predefined thresholds are exceeded (e.g., onset of rainfall or abnormal temperature rise), providing timely notifications to the user. This paper describes the system architecture, hardware integration, cloud-based connectivity, and alert mechanisms. This shows how low-cost microcontrollers and IoT platforms enable reliable and scalable environmental monitoring solutions.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization of the Impeller of a Mini Blood Pump: Balancing Outlet Pressure and Scalar Shear Stress
authors: Reza Sahebi-Kuzeh Kanan, Hanieh Niroomand-Oscuii, Habib Badri Ghavifekr, Farzan Ghalichi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases are one of the primary causes of mortality globally, and blood pumps along with ventricular assist devices play a crucial role in advanced medical care. To overcome limitations of prior studies, namely the small number of geometric parameters and single objective optimization, we present a hybrid framework that integrates response surface methodology, a neural network surrogate, and a multi objective genetic algorithm to design the impeller of a small blood pump at a specified operating point. Fifteen geometric parameters were varied and 290 computational simulations were performed to construct the database and train the surrogate. The optimization objectives were to maximize outlet pressure and to keep the mean scalar shear stress at 50 Pa.Compared with the baseline impeller, the optimized design achieved a 6–7% increase in outlet pressure and about a 15% increase in static head, while the mean wall shear stress decreased by about 17% and the hemolysis index dropped by nearly 50%. The optimized blade reduced high-shear stress near the leading edge, yielded a more uniform shear field, and exhibited smoother pressure growth and weaker jet—wake structures, indicating more uniform blade loading, better pressure recovery, and lower mixing losses. The neural network correctly captured the improvement trends but showed a modest discrepancy: it overestimated outlet pressure by about 13% and underestimated the shear-stress reduction by about 19% relative to simulation-verified values. Overall, the proposed framework simultaneously improves hydraulic performance and hemocompatibility and offers a practical route for multi-objective optimization of small blood-pump impellers.

title: Phase-I: Implementation of an FPGA-Enabled IoT Framework for Real-Time Wildfire Detection
authors: S.Dhivya, Kashish U, Jayasre S, Gorrela Pavani Mani Swathi
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Climate change, drought and human error are rising the frequency of wild fires. This will start early fire detection through timely identification of sources of ignition to save both the environment and the economy. The present paper presents the Phase-1 development of the Internet of Things (IoT) platform of wildfire detection based on field-programmable gate array (FPGA) hardware. This system was developed and based on a Nexys A7 FPGA development board containing a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, connected to an infrared (IR) flame sensor and built-in XADC temperature sensor. The IR flame sensor is sensitive to the presence of flames based on the infrared radiation nature, whereas the XADC provides temperature sensing digital binary numbers. Vivado 2021.2 RTL software was used with the Verilog hardware description language (HDL) to develop and implement the working design. The Hardware was proven and presented as having with the Nexys A7 the FPGA the capability to identify fire events in real-time with minimal latency and consistent temperature sensors. To identify the starting conditions of fire multi-parameter, a DHT11 temperature-humidity sensor will be installed shortly. The findings here demonstrate that FPGA edge processing can be used as an alternative to standalone microcontroller embedded pre-processing system in wildfire detection since it is more efficient and consumes less power.

title: Improvement of adaptive optics retinal image quality utilizing Dense Residual Adversarial Network
authors: Lijun Zou
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Although adaptive optics (AO) systems have significantly advanced retinal imaging capabilities, residual optical aberrations, intraocular scatter, and various complex imaging artifacts continue to compromise image quality and limit diagnostic utility in clinical applications. To address these limitations, this paper aims to develop and validate a dense residual adversarial network (DRAN) that is capable of restoring degraded AO retinal images under multiple residual aberrations and optical artifacts. Our DRAN architecture integrates dense residual blocks in the generator to strength feature propagation and mitigate gradient degradation, coupled with a patch-based discriminator. We comprehensively evaluate DRAN on synthetically generated AO retinal datasets under conditions including varying noise levels, different AO correction capabilities, and complex imaging artifacts encompassing light transmission disturbance, motion blur, and retinal artifact. Under the imaging condition with a signal to noise ratio of 38 dB, DRAN achieves the restoration performance with 37.42 dB PSNR and 0.9873 SSIM. More importantly, when subjected to multiple complex imaging artifacts, our method maintains robust performance with 29.90 dB PSNR and 0.9506 SSIM. Compared to existing learning-based AO restoration approaches, our DRAN achieves higher robustness and generalization under complex clinical artifacts, highlighting its potential significance for clinical ophthalmology and diagnostic analysis.

title: Leverage Blockchain in Embedded Systems for Decentralized Access Logging and Secure Data Exchange in Medical Equipment
authors: C. Anitha, Aaron James S, Kannaki, G.Sasikala, T. A. Sathish Shankar, Lakshana R
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Safe data interchange and decentralized access recording of medical equipment are proposed in this research for embedded systems using blockchain technology. An irreversible distributed ledger can be updated in real-time using a set of lightweight blockchain protocols that are already widely used in embedded medical devices; these protocols form the basis of the system. To guarantee that only authorized users can access data connected to medical equipment, this decentralized application utilizes smart contracts to automate access privileges. End-to-end data integrity and confidentially between devices, hospital servers, and remote monitoring systems is achieved utilizing public-key cryptography. This model is intended to avoid the inefficiency of consensus mechanisms like Proof of Work by using tuned forms of consensus like PoA or DPoS under heavy load that drastically cut down latency and required processing time. The proposed design is also compatible with IoT health-care platforms which allows secure interfacing into the larger hospital network with minimal performance degradation. Experimental simulations and prototype verification show that the blockchain-based embedded system can provide secure, transparent and efficient access logging and meet the real-time medical operation performance requirements. By simulating filed test results, it has been shown that the proposed system will successfully prevent most types of common vehicle borne attack scenarios, such as message tampering, message replay, and unauthorized remote access. What's more, this decentralized procedure is resistant to server failure, or distributed-denial-of-service attack on the central infrastructure.

title: A Spectrum Residency Adaptation Degree-Based Resource Allocation Algorithm for "IP+Optical" Networks
authors: Peng Xie, Zhijun Zhang, Guanglei Song, Yiyang Chen, Jingjing Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: This paper proposes a resource allocation algorithm based on spectrum state awareness in an "IP + Optical" Networks. Simulation results show that the algorithm exhibits superior performance in terms of network blocking rate and spectrum resource utilization.

title: Analysis of Microstrip Patch Antenna apt for Wireless LAN Applications
authors: Himanshi Aggarwal, Chanchal Kumari, Krishanu Kundu, Arshvardhan, Harshita Shandilya
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Current work provides design as well as optimization of a 2.401 GHz microstrip patch antenna suitable for WLAN, Bluetooth, as well as ISM band applications. Performance evaluation is done to determine performance requirements like Return Loss, VSWR, Bandwidth and Gain. Results show that this antenna has excellent resonance at 2.401 GHz Because of planar structure it is ideal for integration into Compact Wireless Devices. It has superb potential for development it into Antenna Arrays as well as Multiband Antennas.

title: How Geometric Asymmetry Impacts Aortic Valve Bioprosthesis Performance - A Finite Element Analysis
authors: Reyhaneh Mosaferchi, Nasser Fatouraee
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: A finite element analysis (FEA) was performed to compare the biomechanical performance of twelve asymmetric aortic valve designs against a symmetric reference, all sharing identical radius and height for geometric consistency. Using Grasshopper for parametric modeling, the study evaluated two key parameters: geometric orifice area (GOA), which affects flow during valve opening, and coaptation area (CA), which impacts sealing during closure. These were tested under simulated physiological conditions to assess the effects of asymmetry. Results showed significant differences in GOA and CA between asymmetric and symmetric designs, highlighting the role of asymmetry in valve opening and closing dynamics. The study identified valve asymmetry ranges (Area Diff% between - 9.848 and 31.312) linked to improved performance, offering practical insights for optimizing aortic valve designs. Natural aortic valves, which serve as the benchmark for optimal performance, exhibit inherent asymmetry, with studies showing that one of the three leaflets typically displays statistically significant differences compared to the other two, which have minimal variation. These findings underscore the importance of mimicking such asymmetry in prosthetic valves to enhance efficiency. This analysis provides valuable guidance for advancing prosthetic valve design in clinical and engineering contexts.

title: A 206-GHz Transformer-Based Super-Regenerative Oscillator with up to 39 dB Gain and 15 GHz Switching Rate in 130-nm SiGe BiCMOS
authors: Yu Zhu, Tilo Meister, Frank Ellinger
publish: 2026 IEEE 26th Topical Meeting on Silicon Monolithic Integrated Circuits in RF Systems (SiRF)
abstract: This paper investigates a super-regenerative oscillator (SRO) operating at 206 GHz that is based on a differential common-collector Colpitts oscillator topology and transformer. The SRO is used to regenerate a very small input signal into a substantially larger output signal by recovering its phase. An input stage utilizing an on-chip transformer is designed to feed the input signal to the differential oscillator. To prove the concept, the circuit is fabricated in a 130-nm BiCMOS technology with a total chip area of 0.88 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Measurement results demonstrate a minimum input sensitivity of −47 dBm for a phase-coherent output. The oscillator has a maximum output power of −3 dBm and achieves a maximum regenerative gain of 39 dB. A frequency tuning range of 3.6% from 204.2 GHz to 211.6 GHz has been realized. Furthermore, the circuit requires a dc power of only 12.4 mW leading to a DC-to-RF power efficiency of 4%. This work achieves a maximum quenching frequency of 15 GHz. Compared to the state of the art, this is the SRO with the fastest quenching frequency reported to date.

title: IoT-Based Food Freshness Monitoring and Environmental Control System
authors: Charvi Suri, Nikhil Mangrulkar, Sakshi Venu Vemuri, Shweta Ganeshrao Sorte, Yash Ankush Deshmukh
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Food spoilage continues to be a global issue whether it occurs in food storage, food transportation, or people's homes. It has detrimental effects on the economy and presents potential hazards to human health. The paper describes the design and creation of a low-cost Internet of Things (IoT) connected food monitoring system to help identify the early stages of spoilage in order to increase the shelf life of perishable goods. The monitoring system uses an ESP-32 microcontroller and gas sensors (MQ-135) to monitor for gases produced from spoilage, as well as a DHT11 sensor to monitor temperature and humidity. When temperature, humidity, or spoilage gases exceed predetermined levels, automated control systems, such as the Peltier cooling system or a fan, engage to regulate the storage conditions. The real-time monitored data is displayed on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 2$</tex> LCD and stored in a Firebase cloud database for remote monitoring. An experimental case study using bananas has shown that the monitoring system delayed food spoilage under controlled environmental conditions. The autonomous idea presented offers people a low-cost, automatic solution to food storage and can be used for homes, small businesses and, warehousing situations.

title: A CNN-Based Approach for Network Intrusion Detection in Cybersecurity
authors: Sunanda Senapati, Swarnakanti Samantaray, Pranati Mishra, Meenakshi Kandpal, Jyotirmayee Rautaray
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: In today's digital landscape, evolving cyber threats demand smarter network security solutions. Traditional intrusion detection systems often struggle with complex, high-volume network traffic and advanced attack patterns. This project presents a comparative analysis of conventional machine learning models and deep learning architectures for network intrusion detection. Evaluated across metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, deep learning, especially the CNN model, demonstrates superior performance in identifying intricate attack behaviours and reducing false alarms. The study highlights deep learning's potential to enhance adaptive, real-time threat detection and serves as a roadmap for evolving existing systems toward more intelligent, AI-driven security solutions.

title: An Intelligent Framework for Fake News Detection in Social Media with Machine Learning and NLP
authors: K Manimala, Sangeetha Subbaraj, S K Anulatha, R Ramya
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: In this manuscript, the rise of misinformation and harmful behavior on social media platforms has made it crucial to develop systems that ensure the authenticity of shared content and user accounts is presented. Parsing of social media feeds for forensic investigation aims to identify fake news and detect fake social media accounts (focusing on platforms like Instagram). The main goal of parsing of social media feeds for forensic investigation is to protect users from misleading information spread by fake accounts. To achieve this, various modules are integrated into the system, including Data Collection and Pre-Processing, Fake News Detection and Account Authenticity Verification. Web scraping tools such as BeautifulSoup are used to gather real-time data, while machine learning algorithms, including Random Forest and Large Language Model (LLM) are employed to classify fake news and detect suspicious account behaviors. The system also uses NLP models to identify fake news patterns and generate comprehensive reports summarizing the findings of each module. By combining Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning techniques, parsing of social media feeds for forensic investigation provides an essential platform for users to protect themselves from misleading information on social networks.

title: Optimizing Social Network User Experience Through Personalized Ranking Algorithms
authors: Tanish Gupta
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: This paper introduces SNSRouter, a personalized ranking algorithm designed to enhance user experience on social networks by optimizing content delivery based on individual preferences. Unlike static sorting methods, SNSRouter adapts to user behavior in real-time. Experiments on multiple datasets show significant improvements in user engagement and satisfaction. The system integrates a middleware (SNSAPI) and a web frontend (SRFE) to support cross-platform message aggregation and ranking. Results indicate that personalized, adaptive ranking can substantially increase content relevance and user efficiency.

title: Analyzing Blood Glucose Levels with Near Infra-Red Spectroscopy and Chemometric Multivariate Methods
authors: Hadi Barati, Arian Mousavi Madani, Soheil Moradi, Mohammad Mohsen Ebrahimi Seyghalan, Mehdi Fardmanesh
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: In this study, near-infrared (NIR) absorbance spectra of blood samples were obtained using the Fourier Transform Infrared (FT-IR) technique. It was observed that multiplying the absorbance spectra by the first derivatives of the water and glucose absorbance profiles efficiently suppresses the interference caused by water in the blood, thus enabling a clearer identification of glucosespecific peaks. When applied in principal component regression (PCR) analysis, this approach yields lower prediction errors compared to PCR based on raw absorbance data, while requiring fewer principal components. Moreover, estimating blood glucose concentration through a linear regression model constructed from glucose molar absorptivity values also provides satisfactory predictive accuracy.

title: Leveraging Operational and Environmental Data for Train Delay Prediction via Deep Learning Models
authors: Rama Krishana Eluri, Kotha Lahari, Appala Chandana Priya, Yarroju.Rekha Sri, Dharmapuri Siri, Kandukuri Swarnalatha
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Accurate prediction of train delay is important to enhance rail operations, increase passenger satisfaction, and facilitate enhanced traffic management. This study presents a comparison of four deep learning models: Deep Neural Network (DNN), Convocational Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM). We apply a dataset containing operating and contextual information to predict delays. The data set contains several attributes, including station-to-station distance, weather, weekday, time slots, train types, historical delays, and congestion degrees. These features were preprocessed and encoded for training. Every model leverages its strengths: DNNs handle non-linear data relationships, CNNs extract spatial-temporal features, and LSTM and BiLSTM are coded to capture long-term patterns.In order to measure how well each model performed, various metrics of evaluation were employed: Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Coefficient of Determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), and Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error (sMAPE). The results indicate that the BiLSTM model performs the best in all cases, producing the best predictions. This work emphasizes the extent to which BiLSTM networks are capable of learning intricate sequential dependencies, proving them fit for real-time forecasting of train delay. The findings emphasize the significance of deep learning algorithms in the design of intelligent railway systems and decision-making improvement.

title: Democratizing Legal Information in India through Multilingual Semantic Retrieval
authors: Ashish Bangwal, Anubhav Gusain, Chirag Rana, Neelam Sharma, Rajat Kumar
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The Indian court system is dominated by English, the language is spoken by less than ten percent of the population. Such a linguistic imbalance places significant barriers for citizens wishing to meaningfully understand or even receive information about the law, which is an area of highly technical employment of periodical judgments and dense conceptualisation of vocabulary. The state of Legal NLP research in India, while being richly multilingual, has seen a predominance of research concerning English, with most of the work within the country on multilingual challenges or inclusivity issues. This paper aims at bridging this accessibility gap by presenting a multilingual semantic legal information retrieval framework that allows the users to access the semantically relevant legal cases in twenty-two Indian regional languages. Based on the bidirectional neural translation model, IndicTrans2 and a transformer-based semantic representation embedding model, the proposed system can encode legal texts into the common English embedding semantic space. Language neutral queries posed in local languages are translated and embedded and the resulting vectors are semantically matched through HNSW-based vector similarity searching, thus guaranteeing efficient cross language retrieval. The framework was evaluated on a manually curated dataset taken from the LeSICiN corpus consisting of hundred queries in several languages with relevance-ranked case documents. Experimental results show good retrieval effects, such as Precision@1 of 0.9833 and NDCG@3 of 0.6203, which are much better than traditional lexical retrieval systems, such as BM25. The system combines semantic encodings with translation-based alignment, allowing retrieval of cases correctly even regardless of the language of the query. This is an attempt towards democratizing access to legal information, linguistic inclusivity and towards ensuring equitable access to justice in the multilingual context of the Indian judiciary.

title: Enhancing On-Demand Massive Connectivity: Cost-Effective Hetero-Granular Resource Allocation for DS2D Communication
authors: Jianxiong Pan, Xueqin Li, Qiaolin Ouyang, Neng Ye, Yiding Wang, Keshav Singh, Shahid Mumtaz
abstract: With great potential in providing global coverage and real-time service, recently, direct satellite-to-device (DS2D) communication has attracted considerable attention. However, how to effectively utilize the costly satellite resources to satisfy the on-demand massive connectivity remains a huge challenge. This paper proposes a cost-effective hetero-granular resource allocation framework that combines the advantages of ground-based scheduling and spaceborne scheduling. In specific, we aim to optimize both the cell-level and user-level scheduling in a beam-hopping system. The formulated optimization problem is first decomposed into a coarse-grained scheduling problem among cells using historical demand information on the ground station, and a fine-grained scheduling problem among users using real-time service demands on satellite. We solve the mixed-integer non-linear programming problem of coarse-grained scheduling with cross-entropy and quantum particle swarm optimization algorithms to find the global optimum, exploiting the adequate ground-based computational resources. The fine-grained scheduling problem is solved with a generalized-benders-decomposition-based algorithm to accommodate the limited spaceborne resources, which decouples power and bandwidth allocation based on a closed-form solution of optimal dual variables in the primal power allocation problem. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces the length of the waiting queue by up to 25.05% compared to the existing methods.

title: Functionally Graded Material Vertebroplasty Screws: A Finite Element Biomechanical Study
authors: Maryam Rahimi, Mohammad Hosein Zadeh-Posti, Aisan Rafiei, Nima Jamshidi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: The present study introduces a computational investigation into a newly developed vertebroplasty screw designed from functionally graded materials (FGMs). Osteoporotic vertebral compression fractures (VCFs) are commonly treated through vertebroplasty, yet titanium-based screws typically cause stress shielding and poor stress transfer across bone interfaces. To overcome these drawbacks, a screw integrating a Ti-6Al-4V metallic core with an outer hydroxyapatite (HAP) layer was proposed and analyzed using finite element methods. A three-dimensional model of the L2 vertebra, reconstructed from computed tomography images, was discretized into approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \times 10^{5}$</tex> elements. Two screw types—conventional titanium and FGM—were subjected to a 300 N vertical load to assess biomechanical performance. The stiffness gradient within the FGM screw was implemented through a power-law formulation incorporated in a UMAT subroutine coded in ABAQUS. Numerical outcomes revealed that the graded design lowered maximum von Mises stress by nearly 14% (from 12.19 to 10.45 MPa) and reduced peak elastic microstrain by about 32% (from 179.9 to 121.5). Cortical bone stresses remained essentially unchanged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 52 \text{MPa})$</tex>, while cancellous bone displacement declined by roughly 36% in the anteroposterior direction. Negligible variations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<0.00008 \text{mm})$</tex> were observed among directional displacements, confirming structural uniformity. Model verification with previously published simulations showed deviations within 10%. These findings demonstrate that the proposed FGM screw redistributes internal loads more evenly, diminishes localized stress accumulation, and offers improved mechanical compatibility and stability, which could potentially enhance postoperative outcomes and osseointegration in vertebroplasty applications.

title: Enhancing Stock Market Forecasting with a Dual-Attention LSTM Network: An Empirical Study on RELIANCE Industries
authors: Ningthoujam Avichandra, Rishabh Tripathi Singh, Shilpa Sharma, Vaibhav Bhatnagar
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Stock market trend forecasting is a difficult issue, primarily due to the inherent unpredictability, chaotic, irregular dynamics, and noisy nature of the market. This paper describes an advanced forecasting system based on a Dual-Attention Long Short-Term Memory (DA-LSTM) network to predict Reliance Industries Ltd. stock prices. The model is trained extensively by means of time-series information from January 2010 to March 2024. The architecture in question is defined by a sequential stack of LSTM layers, enhanced with both feature-level and temporal attention mechanisms and complemented with dropout regularization to avoid overfitting. The system performance is rigorously tested using a range of typical performance measures, comprising Mean Squared Error, Root Mean Squared Error, Mean Absolute Error, and the coefficient of determination (Rsquared, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex>). Experimental results show better performance, characterized by ultra-low MSE and RMSE of 0.0015 and 0.0387, respectively, showing high correlation between the forecasted model values and the real stock prices. This is supported by a high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> value of 0.94, suggesting that the model has indeed captured 94 % of the variance of the stock prices. The results show the efficacy of the model to extract insights in the form of complex temporal dependencies and patterns characteristic of financial time-series data, thereby justifying the application of advanced attention-based LSTM networks to high-risk financial forecasting applications.

title: DE-MKD+: Entropy-Aware Consistency Validation and Multi-Layer Feature Fusion for Enhanced Multi-teacher Knowledge Distillation
authors: Changzhi Bian, Liang Li, Wei Hu, Jun Fan, Lichun Wang, Jing Zhang, Xin Cheng
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Multi-teacher Knowledge Distillation (MKD) aims to enhance the performance of lightweight student networks by fusing diverse knowledge from multiple pre-trained teacher models. Current MKD approaches frequently rely on straightforward prediction averaging or subpar weighting strategies, which can mislead student training due to the unreliable outputs of low-quality teachers. To tackle this challenge, we present an enhanced method named DE-MKD+. Based on the original DEMKD framework, DE-MKD+ optimizes teacher weight allocation by integrating prediction entropy with inter-teacher consistency validation, thereby boosting the credibility of sample-specific weighting. Furthermore, we extend the utilization of intermediate layer features to encompass crucial shallow and deep layers, enriching the feature knowledge transferred to the student. Experimental results on the CIFAR-100 dataset demonstrate that DE-MKD+ outperforms baseline methods, delivering consistent accuracy gains across various teacher-student architecture combinations while preserving computational efficiency.

title: Hybrid Edge–Cloud Architectures for Vision-Based RAG in Emergency Medicine
authors: Abinaya Mettupatti Sivagnanam, Srikanth Singireddy
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Emergency rooms need fast and trustworthy decisions. We present a hybrid edge–cloud architecture for vision-aware retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). The edge (hospital or ambulance) provides low-latency, privacy-first image analysis, while the cloud handles long-term model training, analytics, governance, and model registry. Our blueprint shows data flows, safety and compliance controls, evaluation metrics, and a staged deployment plan for real-world emergency settings.

title: Ultrathin Synchronous Permanent Magnet Micromotor With 400 µm Outer Diameter for in-Body Health Applications_supp4-3660600.mp4
authors: Efren Diez-Jimenez
abstract: Micromotors can be used to build up complex microtools for health applications as for example steerable catheters or optical and ultrasonic imaging system. The thinner and smaller the micromotors are, the less invasive during the use or implantation. In this article, an ultrathin synchronous permanent magnet micromotor with 400 µm outer diameter and 800 µm length is presented. The main novelty of this article is the degree of miniaturization: this motor 2.5 thinner diameter and 15 times smaller volume than previous similar micromotor in the state of the art, while keeping high torque density. The micromotor demonstrated operation up to 27000 r/min, a torque density larger than 0.3 kN·m/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (twice larger than closest miniaturized motor) and a power consumption of 3 mW. The motor has proven to be durable over time demonstrating a lifetime of more than 70 h. The micromotor is capable to withstand and rotate loads one order of magnitude larger that its own rotor weight. Its ultrathin diameter and its high torque density permits to foresee its use in minimally invasive microcatheter tools or implantable devices with less damage during insertion processes.

title: SaHV: Spectral Composition-aware Visualization of Hyperspectral Image
authors: Jiahui Sheng, Shu Xiang, Xiaorun Li, Shuhan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral images (HSI), as a unique type of three-dimensional image, have a wide range of applications across various fields. However, their multichannel characteristics make them challenging for direct visual observation by humans, leading to significant inconveniences. To address this issue, the most common approach is to select three channels to generate a RGB image for visualization. However, this method neglects the most crucial spectral information in HSI, failing to represent the spectral composition of objects. Therefore, in this study, we proposed a HSI visualization method called SaHV, which aims to represent the spectral composition in the visualization results. The specific process of SaHV is as follows. First, the proposed method utilized spectral unmixing to obtain the abundance maps, which stands for the spectral composition. Then the obtained abundance maps were processed by our color mapping network to produce Hue and Saturation channels of HSV, in which the force-directed layout method was introduced to keep local consistency. Finally, the original HSI was used to obtain the value channel of HSV. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our method, we conducted experiments on three real-world datasets.

title: A Tool Posture Path Optimization Method for Robot-Turntable Surface Milling Process Based on 7-DOF Constant Tool-Tip-Stiffness_supp2-3662350.mp4
authors: lei lu
abstract: Robot-Turntable, with advantages such as extensive workspace, excellent flexibility, is gradually playing an important role in manufacturing process, especially suitable for large rotating parts such as propellers, impellers, etc. While providing sufficient degrees of freedom, thereby allowing greater flexibility in trajectory optimization, it also increases the difficulty of trajectory planning in joint-space. Moreover, insufficient stiffness further limits the widespread application of industrial robots in high-intensity machining. In response to this issue, this article proposes a tool posture path optimization method for 7-DOF robot-turntable milling process based on constant end-stiffness index of robot-turntable configuration machining equipment. First, the stiffness performance index of 7-DOF robot-turntable configuration machining equipment are established, and the stiffness performance index is introduced into the robot-turntable. Taking into account other constraints such as the dexterity index of 7-DOF robot-turntable, the tool posture path optimization model based on constant stiffness performance index is then established. Finally, four sets of trajectory machining experiments and two sets of metal surface machining experiments are conducted on the 7-DOF robot-turntable configuration machining equipment. The analysis of experimental data indicate that the joint trajectory planned using the constant stiffness index effectively reduces the robot’s vibration displacement during machining, thereby decreasing surface roughness.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: High-Performance Near-Infrared Photoresponsive Field-Effect Transistors Based on ZnO/PbPc Planar Heterojunction With Al-Buried Electrode and Au Electrode
authors: Lei Sun, Shuhuan Yu, Weijun Nie, Yingquan Peng, Sunan Xu, Wenli Lv, Ying Tang, Xiao Luo, Hailong Wang, Bo Yao, Shenghua Zhou
abstract: These ZnO–organic heterojunction photoresponsive field-effect transistors (PhFETs) offer enhanced near-infrared (NIR) sensitivity through synergistic organic absorption and ZnO charge transport. We engineer a ZnO/PbPc heterojunction device with buried Al electrodes (Al-buried-device), outperforming Al- and Au- electrode counterparts. The optimized architecture demonstrates 3.48-A/W responsivity and 534% EQE at 808nm (13.3mW/cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{{2}},{V}_{d} =50$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V), surpassing Au-device metrics (2.52 A/W, 388% EQE). Performance superiority stems from three factors: 1) buried-electrode-enhanced carrier collection; 2) efficient ZnO/PbPc exciton dissociation; and 3) optimized charge carrier transport pathways. This electrode-embedded/buried design resolves critical interfacial barriers while maximizing photon utilization, providing a viable strategy for high-performance NIR photodetectors in flexible optoelectronics.

title: ACAI 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)

title: Spectral Image Rendering of Fluorescent Objects Using a Conventional Renderer
authors: Shoji Tominaga, Giuseppe Claudio Guarnera, Ryo Ohtera
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We propose a practical spectral image rendering method for fluorescent objects using a renderer that does not natively support wavelength-shifting transport. For scenes composed of matte fluorescent and non-fluorescent surfaces, the illumination incident on a target fluorescent object is classified into three types: (1) direct illumination from a light source, (2) indirect illumination reflected from other reflective objects, and (3) luminescent illumination emitted from other fluorescent objects. We express the radiance observed on a fluorescent surface as a linear combination of five terms: reflection and fluorescence under direct illumination, reflection and fluorescence under indirect illumination, and reflection induced by luminescent illumination emitted by other fluorescent surfaces. Assuming isotropic emission and single-bounce fluorescence, the fluorescent shading terms reuse the diffuse-reflection shading produced by the renderer, while measured Donaldson matrices provide the wavelength conversion. Mitsuba is used as the underlying conventional rendering system. Experiments conducted with a physically built Cornell Box validate the proposed method through comparisons with direct measurements and an existing fluorescence capable renderer. Finally, we demonstrate an extension to non-planar fluorescent objects via sparse emitter discretization.

title: Distributed Data-driven Economic MPC for Frequency Regulation and Power Sharing of Microgrids
authors: Panxiao Yong, Yubin Jia, Changyin Sun
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven distributed economic model predictive control (EMPC) strategy for load dispatch and frequency regulation in microgrids. To address the nonlinearity introduced by inverter-interfaced distributed generators (DGs), a distributed Koopman approach is employed to achieve global linearization of the complex system dynamics. Based on this linear model, a distributed EMPC controller is designed to simultaneously optimize frequency stability and economic load dispatch. The controller is solved in real time via a synchronized iterative Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM), ensuring both computational efficiency and subsystem autonomy. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method in terms of dynamic performance and operational economy.

title: 2026 IEEE Space Hardware and Radio Conference (SHaRC)
authors: N/A

title: AI for All: Evaluating the Impact of Artificial Intelligence Tools on Students with Disabilities
authors: Preeti, Gurprem Kaur
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education has moved the sphere significantly forward; however, accessibility to students with disabilities is a relatively uncharted area. This research intends to evaluate awareness, usage, barriers, and perceived effectiveness of AI tools among the differently-abled students to support the inclusive digital learning. Structured survey was administered to students who were impaired in visual, hearing, mobility and cognitive domains. To quantify and qualify the data, the questionnaire contained binary, multiple-choice, Likert-scale and open-ended questions. Data collection was performed through Google Forms and analysis/visualization was performed through Python (pandas, seaborn, matplotlib, wordcloud, TextBlob). Findings indicate that 72.7 out of the respondents knew about AI tools, with the most mentions of ChatGPT, Google Assistant, Grammarly, and Microsoft Immersive Reader. Students who are visually and mobility challenged posted greater effectiveness than those with cognitive disabilities who reported mixed results. The problems that were shared in open-ended responses included poor features of accessibility, reliance on internet connectivity, and voice recognition problems. In spite of such barriers, sentiment analysis revealed that there was a general positive perception, with a mean sentiment score of +0.32. The results indicate the increasing significance of AI in inclusive education and points to the necessity of more accessible designs and more specific support systems. The research could contribute to the concept of the transformative capabilities of AI and offer clues to the elimination of disparities in access to educational technologies.

title: Safe Media Algorithm: A Graph-Based Deep Learning Algorithm for Early Detection of Social Media Fraud Schemes using GNN-AED
authors: Pranav Krishna S, Harichandana S, Athulya M, Sahodev Roy, Nivedita Suneesh, Deepthi L. R
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: As social media platforms continue to evolve, so do the methods and complexity of fraud schemes and anomalous users. Traditional anomaly or fraud detection techniques focus primarily on identifying threats and often lack recovery methods. In this study, we introduce an algorithm to enhance the integrity and security of social media by using Graph Neural Network-based Anomaly and Event Detection (GNN-AED). Our methodology involves detecting anomalous nodes using GNN-AED, followed by a centrality-based evaluation to assess node importance. Nodes deemed unimportant are pruned directly, while influential nodes undergo further neighborhood analysis, community detection, and structural recovery to maintain graph cohesion. We implemented this approach using various machine learning paradigms and mechanisms designed to preserve graph connectivity post node-removal. The supervised GNN-AED module achieved 95% accuracy, 95% precision, and a 98% AUC ROC, demonstrating strong performance and indicating its applicability and potential for deployment in dynamic social networks.

title: AI-Powered Document Summarization: Existing Technologies, Challenges, Proposed Solutions, and Future Prospects
authors: Sharvit Kashikar, Atharva Dethe, Priyanshu Deshmukh, Pratik K Agrawal
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: The fast-paced rise in the magnitude of digital text brings the urgent requirement for intelligent summarization systems that can adapt to diverse document types and domainspecific language. This paper presents a context-aware system for multi-model text summarization that internally uses state-of-theart Transformer models, namely, BART, PEGASUS, and FLANT5. In classifying documents and analyzing content complexity, it selects the most appropriate model to generate accurate and coherent summaries with respect to the type of document and complexity of contents. Preprocessing methods target specialized elements like code blocks, equations, citations, and technical terms that are detrimental to output quality. The web-based architecture is built on a stack comprising React, Node.js, and MongoDB allowing for scale and ease of access from a user standpoint. For long inputs, performance test results demonstrate an accuracy of 85-90% with a turnaround time of less than 10 seconds. This work presents the architecture and gives insight into the operational procedures, thereby outlining its usefulness in academic, technical, and media domains. It thus strongly affirms the usefulness of adaptive summarization in handling complex domain-rich digital content.

title: Overcoming Issues with Source Data for Early Fault Detection in Power Transformers: A Comparison with Combined Triangle Methods
authors: Abdelmoumene Hechifa, Abdelaziz Lakhel, Chouaib Labiod, Said Djaballah, Ali Bebboukha, Redha Meneceur
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: Early identification of power transformer disorders is essential to guarantee the safety and continuous functioning of electrical systems. The technique of Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) is highly useful in diagnosing problems in oil-filled transformers. In these transformers, the oil functions as both a cooling and insulating medium, effectively reflecting internal stresses. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of the various defect diagnostic algorithms belonging to either Duval or Gouda Triangle approaches set across different datasets. The samples that were examined were a total of 287, representing 117 samples from IEC TC 10 data, and 170 other samples that derived from Egyptian scientific laboratories. In the IEC TC 10 data set, the Gouda Triangle method (88.89% accuracy) outperformed the Duval Triangle method (85.47%). On the other hand, for Egyptian data, specificity was 76.47% and PPV could be obtained using the Gouda Triangle method and respectively were 65.88% and 94.44% with Duval Triangle method; while in either of these methods. Notably, the use of the Combined Triangle methods, which combine the advantages of both the Duval and Gouda Triangle approaches, led to remarkable improvements in performance, with accuracies of 93.16% for Data 1 and 88.24% for Data 2. This novel methodology provides a more resilient and flexible diagnostic instrument, yielding consistent and dependable outcomes across diverse data sources.

title: Integrated Real-Time Automated fiber placement Inspection System with High-Speed Point Cloud Alignment and Fusion
authors: Yipeng Tang, Haoge Jiang, Baoping Cheng, Ting Huang, Fengyi Zhang, Qiancheng Ye, Zhenyu Xu, Jinfei Gao, Qiran Pu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronic Engineering and Artificial Intelligence (MEAI)
abstract: Automated fiber placement (AFP) demands realtime dimensional verification and defect detection. We present an integrated inspection system that fuses $\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ H z}$ line-laser profiles with CNC pose data via a linear resampling algorithm using a center-symmetric sparse matrix, ensuring uniform-density point clouds despite sampling-rate mismatches. Fusion is accelerated through sparse tensor multiplication, reducing CPU time from 39.8 ms to 6.8 ms per batch. Experimental validation demonstrates point cloud stitching accuracy within 0.9 mm across large-scale component scanning.

title: Optimization of Security Mechanisms for Operator-Oriented Identity Authentication Systems
authors: Juan Yin, Mingyu Qi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: With the continuous growth of carrier service scale, identity authentication systems face increasing demands in data security, session management efficiency, and abnormal access detection. To address the limitations of existing systems in key transmission, user experience, and risk identification, this paper presents a security mechanism optimization scheme tailored for carrier authentication scenarios. First, a hybrid encryption approach combining RSA and AES is introduced, where asymmetric encryption protects the session key and symmetric encryption handles data encryption, enhancing the security of key transmission while reducing computational overhead. Second, a token renewal mechanism based on a sliding expiration strategy is designed, enabling the system to adjust session lifecycles according to actual user activity, thereby reducing frequent logins and improving resource utilization. Finally, an unsupervised machine learning–based anomaly detection model is applied to authentication traffic analysis. By learning normal behavior patterns, the model can identify potential anomalous requests and strengthen risk prevention. Experimental results show that the proposed solution achieves a balanced trade-off between security and performance, providing a feasible enhancement for carrier authentication systems.

title: Attack Path Optimization in Connected Vehicular Networks: A PPO-based Learning Approach
authors: Hongyang Nie, Sen Li, Cong Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: V2X connectivity patterns establish the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) paradigm. Nevertheless, the notable benefits of V2X connectivity inadvertently introduce security vulnerability challenges. This paper applies the Markov Decision Process (MDP). It describes the attacker’s selection problem, specifically how they exploit vulnerabilities to achieve topological attack objectives. Specifically, we define a topological network by analyzing the IoV’s cloud-channel-edge terminal architecture. Subsequently, attacks targeting the IoV are integrated with attack scenarios to construct a vulnerability network. The attack process is then established as a dual-network model. This model is based on the one-to-many relationship between topological nodes and vulnerability nodes. We utilize an approximate policy optimization (PPO) algorithm to solve the MDP problem. Its primary objective is to identify the optimal attack path within the IoV. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed PPO algorithm can identify potential attack paths. Furthermore, it can determine the optimal attack path. This contributes to a comprehensive, global analysis of vulnerability exploitation within IoV systems.

title: Social-HGRT : Socially-Aware Hypergraph Relational Transformer Networks for Multi-agent Trajectory Prediction
authors: Jiangen Li, Haifeng Zheng, Xinxin Feng
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Accurate trajectory prediction in irregular enclosed spaces is challenging due to high behavioral randomness from unconstrained paths and complex group dynamics. To address the fundamental trade-off between prediction accuracy, computational efficiency, and resource consumption, we propose the Socially-Aware Hypergraph Relational Transformer Network (Social-HGRT). This hybrid framework incorporates a weakly rule-based feature initializer and an adaptive topology generator into a hypergraph transformer to enhance social representation and model authentic group dependencies. Extensive evaluations on the ETH/UCY and Stanford Drone datasets show that Social-HGRT achieves state-of-the-art performance, delivering competitive ADE/FDE accuracy while significantly reducing model parameters by 35.33%, MAC operations by 74.4%, and inference latency by 25.1%.

title: Weighted Text-to-Image Retrieval Using CLIP, OCR, And Keyword Matching
authors: Abhinav Prakash, Abhishek S., Rhon S. George, Ritin George D.
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Traditional text-to-image retrieval systems have relied primarily on either caption-based or OCR-based searching which often fails to capture the semantic content of the images. In this paper, we propose a weighted image retrieval system that utilizes CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training), OCR (Optical Character Recognition), and user-defined keywords to retrieve images that are relevant to the user's search query. We present how to preprocess each image by extracting their corresponding CLIP embeddings and OCR-extracted text embeddings, which are then stored in a vector store; Meanwhile user-defined keywords and other additional information are extracted from their metadata and stored in a separate metadata store. For a given search query, we rank all of the indexed images effectively using a weighted similarity score approach and retrieve the top-k results, which are then further re-ranked. This approach comprises of initially calculating each image's CLIP, OCR, and user-defined keywords' similarity scores to the query and aggregating them using a weighted average, where the weights are a function of these similarity scores. This ensures more accurate and context-aware search results as compared to static weighting approaches. We also demonstrate our approach on a set of curated public domain images containing images with visual content, text content, and user-defined metadata keywords (eg, ‘Family’, Sophia’, ‘Birthday’, etc.) by showcasing its retrieval effectiveness in real-world scenarios using example text queries.

title: Agentic AI for Sales Intelligence
authors: Nidhi Sharma, Sathish Kumar Velayudum
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Today's Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems contain a goldmine of unstructured data—call transcripts, email conversations, and support notes—that traditional sales intelligence tools fail to leverage effectively. Unlike existing approaches, this paper presents a novel paradigm shift in three fundamental dimensions: Traditional BI tools require manual keyword searches and operate reactively; This Agentic AI framework introduces continuous autonomous reasoning via a "Reason-Act" loop that proactively mines data without human prompting. "Copilot" systems process each query in isolation with single-shot inference; this framework maintains long-term semantic memory using vector databases (FAISS) to connect patterns across customer histories spanning months. Existing ML/NLP approaches provide static classifications without context; this system performs self-initiated alert generation with calibrated confidence scoring (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>>0.85 correlation with business impact). This framework is evaluated on a simulated dataset of 441 sales communications using Python, GPT-4 as the reasoning engine, and FAISS for vector similarity search. Results demonstrate autonomous identification of high-value sales events—churn risks (F1=0.94), upsell opportunities (F1=0.85), and competitor mentions (F1=0.98). Critically, historical context retrieval improved alert quality by 5.3 points (55% relative improvement) compared to analyzing new data in isolation, validating the necessity of the agentic memory architecture. This work establishes a new paradigm where CRM systems transition from passive repositories to intelligent, proactive sales assistants capable of continuous learning and autonomous decision-making.

title: Enhancing E-Learning Content Security with AES 256-CBC Encryption and Storage on Interplanetary File System in Ethereum Network
authors: Rini Hardiyanti, Amil Ahmad Ilham, Ady Wahyudi Paundu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This research proposes the use of AES-256-CBC encryption, decentralized storage using Interplanetary File System (IPFS), and CID hash logging into the Ethereum. The system was tested using files of 5, 10, 15 and 100 MB, and shows that the encryption-decryption process has no significant impact on system performance. The security evaluation resulted 6 out of 7 test scenarios successfully prevented data theft, manipulation, and duplication after using attack simulations such as reply attacks, bit-flipping, Man-in-the-Middle (MITM), and Brute Force attacks. The total cost for logging CID hashes to Ethereum was $0.00913-0.01080$ ETH. Although throughput performance and execution time are volatile in both proposed and comparison system, the proposed system is superior in terms of system security and cost efficiency, making it feasible to use for e-learning content protection.

title: Image Classification Employing an Efficient Deep Learning Model with Improved Parameter Utilization
authors: Debi Prasad Panda, Pratik Kiran Rout, Manoj Kumar Sahoo, Rashmi Rani Patro, Parimal Kumar Giri, Ashish Rastogi
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Today's lightweight and portable neural network architectures sometimes have trouble striking a compromise between classification accuracy and computing efficiency, especially when using complicated datasets like CIFAR-100. In this work, we present a new architecture, P100SuperCNN, inspired by EfficientNet, which achieves remarkable parameter savings while preserving competitive accuracy. We integrate Squeeze-and-Excitation attention methods, progressive data augmentation techniques, and Mobile Inverted Bottleneck Convolution with Stochastic Depth (MBConvSD) blocks into our design. Through extensive testing, we have confirmed that advanced training methods can be outperformed by reduced optimization strategies. P100SuperCNN achieves an efficiency score of 18.32 accuracy points per million parameters, achieving 73.83% validation accuracy on CIFAR-100 with only 4.03 million parameters. This is 26% more efficient than EfficientNetB0 and 32% more efficient than MobileNetV3-Large, while using 25% less parameters than comparable mobile architectures. Our findings disprove the idea that sophisticated optimization strategies invariably produce superior outcomes, showing that refined, simple methods achieved 1.03 % higher accuracy while training 2.4 times faster. Because of its modular design and simple implementation, P100SuperCNN can be used in resourceconstrained systems for both practical implementation and research.

title: Deep Learning-Based Investigation of Glioma Detection and Grading Using Ensemble CNN
authors: Sariyev Shokhrukh, M. Rajunaiak, Munish Sabharwal, Ankita Awasthi, Rallabandi Venkata Santoshi Saraswati Swetha Nagini, Vanya Arun
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The World Health Organization (WHO) identified Gliomas as the most widespread brain tumour category. Toxicity levels combined with growth rates serve as the basis for dividing glioma malignancies into four distinct categories. The accurate grading of tumors determines which treatment method could be best suited including chemotherapy, surgery or radiation therapy. The use of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images to separate and grade tumours works well yet demands significant time and money and includes intricate management variations. The objective of this study is to meet the need for precise and reliable tumour classification by proposing a computational method for glioma grading that leverages an ensemble of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The proposed method introduces two ensemble models named Mean-Ensemble-CNNs & NN-Ensemble-CNNs that merge the pretrained CNN models ResNet-101, ResNeXt-50, Inception-v3 and DenseNet-161. Neural Network Ensembles combine deep features from multiple algorithms through a neural network to identify gliomas while Mean Ensembles calculate the average predictions from various algorithms to detect gliomas. The following sections provide a detailed analysis of Mean-Ensemble-CNNs. The system utilizes advanced data preparation and augmentation methods to fully exploit available informational resources. The technique reduces dependence on invasive biopsies while enabling flexible clinical treatment planning. Automatic segmentation and grading of tumours makes this process possible. The proposed NN-Ensemble CNNs achieve the top score with 96.21% accuracy which surpasses all individual models.

title: Estimating efficient Wi-Fi router using complex Fermatean picture fuzzy ARAS and COPRAS method
authors: S. Anita Shanthi, S. Poonkodi
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Wi-Fi routers are now much more than just simple internet-sharing gadgets. Powerful features that address speed, security and smart home connectivity are packed into today’s models. Router used to guarantee that data is going to the right place, including when individuals send emails to the appropriate recipient and internet provider. In this paper ARAS and COPRAS methods on Complex Fermatean Picture Fuzzy set is dealt with. Difference values, Shannon entropy measure, weight values for these sets are determined. Complex Fermatean Picture Fuzzy aggregated optimality function is constructed. Finally utility degree is determined. Complex Fermatean picture fuzzy COPRAS utilizes sum of weighted normalized decision matrix. This method has simple relative significance of alternatives predictions and quantitative utility normalization. Four types of Wi-Fi are investigated on the speed, height, width and length, using Complex Fermatean picture fuzzy ARAS and COPRAS methods. Both methods suggest that Netgear is the best Wi-Fi router.

title: Dual-Objective Deep CNN for Simultaneous Disease and Nutrient Deficiency Diagnosis in Rice: A Comparative Study of Transfer Learning Models
authors: Riki Ruli A. Siregar, Kudang Boro Seminar, Sri Wahjuni, Edi Santosa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: However, its productivity is increasingly at risk due to plant diseases and nutrient deficiencies, further intensified by climate change and soil degradation. This study introduces a dual-task deep learning framework for systematically classifying rice leaf diseases and nutrient deficiencies using advanced Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures. 1,650 images for disease categories (Brown Spot, Healthy, Leaf Blast, Leaf Blight, Leaf Smut) and 1,831 images for nutrient deficiencies (Healthy, Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium) were obtained from publicly available datasets. Five CNN models DenseNet201, MobileNetV2, EfficientNetB0, VGG19, and NASNetMobile, were trained and evaluated across multiple image resolutions ($128 \times 128$ and $224 \times 224$ pixels) and epochs (5, 10, 20, and 30). DenseNet201 achieved the highest performance, with 94.55% accuracy in disease detection and 95.08% in nutrient deficiency classification. Unlike previous approaches that addressed these tasks separately, the proposed model integrates both into a unified multitask CNN pipeline. This solution enhances diagnostic efficiency, offering practical benefits for early-stage plant health monitoring in resource-limited agricultural settings and supporting sustainable rice production.

title: YOLOv11 with RCM-SHSA Enhancement: Optimizing Small Target Weapon Detection in Complex Security Scenarios
authors: Chufeng Wan, Lin Wan, Huo Gao, Shu Dong, Junhua Xiao, Youyu Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: This study introduces a You Only Look Once version 11(YOLOv11)-based fusion model to tackle small-object weapon detection in complex security environments. By integrating a Spatial and Hierarchical Self-Attention (SHSA) module into the backbone, the model enhances global feature extraction, while a Refinement Convolutional Module (RCM) in the head improves boundary discrimination. Tested on a self-made weapon detection dataset, the model outperforms the baseline YOLOv11n and the YOLOv11+BAR model across key metrics: Precision, Recall, F1-score, mAP@0.5, and mAP50-95. It achieves an mAP@0.5 of 0.762 and mAP50-95 of 0.510, surpassing the baseline by 11.0 and 9.7 percentage points, respectively. Ablation studies confirm that SHSA improves object coverage, while RCM enhances precision and localization, with their combination creating a strong synergistic effect. Confusion matrix and precision-confidence curve analyses further demonstrate the model’s robustness under challenging backgrounds. This solution offers high accuracy and deployability for multi-target weapon detection in intelligent security systems, addressing critical needs in real-world applications.

title: Random Forest Assisted Floorplanning Optimization in VLSI Design
authors: Vikram G S, Arun Kumar K, Navaneethan S, Venkatasubramaniam R
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Floorplanning is an essential step in the Very Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) physical design flow that has serious implications in terms of area usage, wirelength, power consumption, and timing closure. Typical heuristic techniques such as simulated annealing and genetic algorithms yield satisfactory solutions but are marked by excessive computation cost. In this case, a machine learning framework based on the Random Forest (RF) algorithm is used to estimate the quality of candidate floorplans. The framework discerns features such as block sizes, aspect ratios, and netlist connectivity, and uses Random Forest regression to predict parameters such as delay, congestion, and timing slack. On benchmark circuits, experimental results demonstrate that the proposed technique reduces the search space by at most 40%, improves runtime efficiency by approximately 25%, and achieves a maximum of 12% wirelength savings compared with traditional heuristic methods. This research indicates the potential for integrating machine learning models into electronic design automation (EDA) systems for accelerating design closure.

title: Poster: Inferring Floorplans from Walking Trajectories via Contrastive Diffusion Guidance
authors: Sattwik Basu, Chaitanya Amballa, Zhongweiyang Xu, Jorge Vančo Sampedro, Srihari Nelakuditi, Romit Roy Choudhury

title: Feedback control of a boost converter using a NARX-NN-based Digital Twin Instance: Multi-domain analysis and comparison
authors: Karol Najdek, Radoslaw Nalepa
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven feedback control framework for regulating the output voltage of a boost converter using a Nonlinear AutoRegressive model with eXogenous inputs Neural Network (NARX-NN). The NARX-NN operates as a Digital Twin Instance (DTI) that estimates the output capacitor voltage in real-time, reducing reliance on direct voltage sensing under noisy or faulty measurement conditions. Robustness is validated in closed-loop operation under sensing degradations. The DTI is embedded within the closed-loop structure, where its output serves as a surrogate feedback signal. A systematic, information-theoretic design procedure integrates Mutual Information (MI) analysis for optimal data-sampling frequency selection, correlation and multicollinearity analysis for informative input identification, and False Nearest Neighbours (FNN) for signal-specific delay embedding. These steps ensure that the trained NARX-NN represents the converter’s nonlinear, time-varying dynamics. The case-study circuit with input and output filters exhibits nonlinear and non-minimum-phase behaviour, making it a benchmark for power-electronic control. Frequency Response Analysis (FRA) characterises dynamics across multiple load conditions. Closed-loop stability and robustness are evaluated using the D-decomposition providing intuitive gain-selection criteria and stability boundaries in (KP,KI) space. Three feedback strategies are compared: (i) conventional measurement-based; (ii) DTI-based; and (iii) DTI-based and look-up-table (LUT) compensator. Simulation results confirm that the DTI-enhanced controller maintains accurate voltage regulation under dynamic conditions, achieving steady-state deviations below ±3% and robust transient performance while keeping computational effort within real-time constraints (sub-GFLOPS). The proposed framework establishes a unified foundation for integrating neural estimators into power-electronic control and advancing adaptive Digital Twin (DT) architectures for next-generation energy-conversion systems.

title: Human-Exoskeleton Kinematic Calibration to Improve Hand Tracking for Dexterous Teleoperation
authors: Haiyun Zhang, Stefano Dalla Gasperina, Saad N. Yousaf, Job D. Ramirez, Toshimitsu Tsuboi, Tetsuya Narita, Ashish D. Deshpande
abstract: Hand exoskeletons are critical tools for dexterous teleoperation and immersive manipulation interfaces, but achieving accurate hand tracking remains a challenge due to user-specific anatomical variability and donning inconsistencies. These issues lead to kinematic misalignments that degrade tracking performance and limit applicability in precision tasks. We propose a subject-specific calibration framework for exoskeleton-based hand tracking that estimates virtual link parameters through residual-weighted optimization. A data-driven approach is introduced to empirically tune cost function weights using motion capture ground truth, enabling accurate and consistent calibration across users. Implemented on the MAESTRO hand exoskeleton with seven healthy participants, the method achieved substantial reductions in joint and fingertip tracking errors across diverse hand geometries. Qualitative visualizations using a Unity-based virtual hand further demonstrate improved motion fidelity. While demonstrated on the MAESTRO exoskeleton, the framework may be extended in principle to other hand exoskeleton systems that provide comparable kinematic constraints and sensing capabilities.

title: Physics-Data Synergy in Structural Health Monitoring: A Multi-Scale Graph Contrastive Framework With Temperature-Adaptive Fusion
authors: Shuxin Zhang, Lei Qiu, Ziyang Zeng
abstract: Operational Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) currently faces a tripartite dilemma: the scarcity of labeled damage data from real-world infrastructure, the masking effects of environmental variability, and the prohibitive computational costs associated with high-fidelity physics simulations. To resolve these conflicts, this article presents a physics-data dual-driven framework that harmonizes finite element analysis (FEA) with self-supervised contrastive learning. The architecture employs a novel dual-matrix strategy: a static, physics-based matrix encoding mechanical constraints derived from modal analysis, and a dynamic monitoring matrix learned via hierarchical InfoNCE objectives across node, subgraph, and global levels. To distinguish genuine structural degradation from benign environmental shifts, we introduce an attention-based temperature-adaptive fusion mechanism. This module dynamically modulates the weighting of physics-driven versus data-driven features based on observed thermal gradient patterns. Furthermore, by leveraging parametric damage simulations as negative training samples, the framework achieves open-set classification capabilities, enabling the detection of unforeseen failure modes. Extensive validation on three benchmark datasets (IASC-ASCE, Z24 Bridge, and Guangzhou Tower) demonstrates that this approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods by margins of 4.4-6.1%, achieving 94.2% accuracy on the IASC-ASCE benchmark with only 20% labeled data. Notably, the model exhibits exceptional label efficiency, retaining 89.7% accuracy with merely 5% labeled samples—effectively matching supervised baselines that require sixfold more annotation. This work establishes a robust methodology for unifying structural mechanics with deep representation learning, ensuring reliability under limited supervision and varying thermal conditions.

title: EIT to CT Cross-Modality Translation Using Diffusion Transformer
authors: Ibrar Amin, Shuaikai Shi, Ruiyuan Kang, Hasan Al Marzouqi, Theodora Kerenidi, Mahmoud El-Kaissi, Panos Liatsis
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Computed Tomography (CT) plays a crucial role in medical imaging due to its superior spatial resolution and diagnostic capability, particularly in pulmonary applications. However, the associated radiation risk, high equipment cost, and limitations in accessibility present significant challenges. In contrast, Electrical Impedance Tomography (EIT) offers a radiation-free, cost-effective, and portable imaging alternative, making it attractive in bedside and resource-limited settings. In this work, we demonstrate the feasibility of generating high-resolution synthetic CT (sCT) images from raw EIT voltages, enhancing EIT's clinical application by effectively unifying the benefits of both modalities. We propose an EIT-to-CT translation approach that leverages a diffusion transformer conditioned on the voltage measurements to guide sCT image generation through the reverse diffusion process. The model was trained on a synthetic dataset and evaluated on both the simulated and experimental data, resulting in anatomically coherent and visually realistic sCT images. Quantitative assessment demonstrates the method's effectiveness on both pixel-level and distribution-based metrics. It achieved a Correlation Coefficient of 0.9282, Peak Signal-to-noise ratio of 24.64 dB, Feature Similarity Index of 0.8321, and deep Image Structure and Texture Similarity of 0.1289, indicating a high level of structural consistency. Similarly, it attained strong alignment with the targeted CT distribution with a Fréchet Inception Distance of 33.36 and Kernel Inception Distance of 0.0110. The clinical relevance of the sCT images was further validated through a downstream lung cancer classification task, where models trained on real CT showed comparable performance on sCT, and a pilot reader study, where clinical experts rated over 90% of the generated images as anatomically correct and realistic. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/AminIbrar/eit2ct-translation</uri>.

title: A Multi-Step Prediction Method Based on Small Sample Data Augmentation to Assess Wheat Flour Safety Risk
authors: Wanbao Sheng, Huawei Jiang, Wenqiang Pi, Zhen Yang, Like Zhao
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Food safety significantly impacts the human health, establishing an effective prediction method to assess food safety risk is crucial for food safety control. At present, in the situation of insufficient detection data, it is necessary to employ data augmentation methods to generate large-scale detection data and accurately capture the long-term variation patterns through safety risk prediction models. However, existing data augmentation methods and safety prediction models face challenges such as gradient vanishing and difficulty in capturing long-term dependencies. Therefore, this paper proposes a Small sample Data Augmentation Multi-step Prediction Method (SDAMPM) to assess wheat flour safety risks. Firstly, we improved time-series generative adversarial networks based on external temporal convolution and Wasserstein distance to expand wheat flour hazard factor detection data. Secondly, we employed expanded data to establish dietary exposure evaluation system for wheat flour, serving as the desired output for multi-step prediction models. Finally, we constructed a stable Informer (Stainformer) multi-step prediction model by designing symmetric ProbSparse self-attention and distilling layer based on dilated causal convolution. Experiments on the wheat flour dietary exposure evaluation system demonstrate that compared to other methods, the expanded data is similar to the distribution of the original data. This approach effectively predicts long-term safety risks associated with wheat flour consumption and can provide assistance and technical support in decision-making for relevant departments, thereby reducing the occurrence of food safety incidents.
