title: A Hybrid EOG System featuring Flexible a-IGZO TFTs and a 180 nm CMOS IC
authors: Mohammad Zulqarnain, Stefano Stanzione, Elisabetta Peri, Steve Smout, Myriam Willegems, Pieter Harpe, Kris Myny, Eugenio Cantatore
abstract: Flexible wearable sensors are becoming increasingly popular due to their capability to monitor physiological signals while ensuring high user comfort. Wearable sensors built using only flexible electronics result in inferior system performance due to the inherent limitations of flexible technologies. Flexible hybrid electronics (FHE) combines the strengths of flexible technologies (low cost, larger area coverage, user comfort) with the high performance and energy efficiency of silicon ICs. Here, an FHE system is described that addresses the challenge of system functions partitioning between flexible and silicon (Si) subsystems. The presented design approach is applied in this work to a multichannel electro-oculography (EOG) system, but can be expanded to various FHE application domains. The proof of concept fully-integrated 8-channel FHE EOG system exploits a front-end in a-IGZO TFT technology, interfaced with a custom Si IC that supports the front-end and provides conversion of the acquired signals to the digital domain. In this way the low cost, flexible form factor and the large area character of the flexible thin-film transistor (TFT) technology are leveraged, while exploiting at the same time the accuracy, computational capability and energy efficiency of the Si IC. We propose a hybrid high-pass filter (hHPF) as interface between the electrodes and the front-end, whereby TFTs and capacitors on foil are being controlled by the Si IC. The hybrid approach exploits the capabilities of flexible electronics to bring functionality close to the electrodes and its potential to cope with a high number of sensor channels, with the better precision of CMOS, enabling a fully integrated filter without discrete components. The TFT front-end converts the input signal voltages to a pulse-width modulated (PWM) representation, which is then quantized in the Si IC, to provide a digital output. The Si IC enables conversion to the digital domain with much higher efficiency compared to state-of-the-art TFT solutions. The digital output is further used in an algorithm for feature extraction and classification to detect the desired signals. The experimental electrical characterization of the system demonstrates an input-referred noise of 6.68 μVrms in a 1-30 Hz band, with a chopping frequency of 1 kHz. The input impedance offered by the integrated hHPF is 6.88 GΩ at 0.089 Hz (with multiplexing disabled). The total power consumption per channel is 84 μW. In-vivo experiments have also been performed against a gold standard (a commercial bio-potential amplifier), to confirm a faithful representation of the eye activities. The signals acquired during in-vivo experiments are successfully classified using a support vector machine (SVM) classifier, with an average accuracy of 87.6 ± 2.9%.

title: Integrated Visual Sensing and Efficient Deep Learning for UAV-Based Track Foreign Object Detection
authors: Haifeng Song, Yu Mei, Shaoqing Liu, Zixuan Zhang, Hairong Dong
abstract: Deploying deep learning models on UAV-based edge platforms for railway track foreign object detection is often limited by large model size and heavy computation. To address these issues, This paper presents an efficient foreign object detection model, termed RVGC-YOLO. The model introduces three key improvements: (a) designing a new C2f-RVG module to enhance multi-scale feature fusion; (b) replacing standard convolutions with GSConv to reduce parameters while maintaining feature extraction capability; and (c) embedding a coordinate attention mechanism to strengthen focus on critical regions. Furthermore, for efficient deployment on edge devices, this paper proposes an efficient model pruning and distillation framework, based on which a lightweight version of RVGC-YOLO, named RVGC-YOLO-Tiny, is introduced. Experiments on the UAV-RSOD dataset demonstrate that RVGC-YOLO achieves 89.60% mAP@0.5 and 63.72% mAP@0.5:0.95. Building upon this performance, the optimized RVGC-YOLO-Tiny significantly reduces parameters and computation by 61.86% and 55.06%, respectively, while maintaining a detection performance nearly consistent with the baseline. These results demonstrate that the proposed model optimization framework provides a practical solution for real-time railway safety monitoring.

title: An Attitude Control Method for UAV Based on Time-Varying Switching Threshold Event-Triggered Mechanism and Linear Extended State Observer
authors: Xiangze Lin, Weili Fu, Junwen Tu, Ju H. Park
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: To address the dual challenges of tracking accuracy and resource efficiency for quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) attitude control, an event-triggered based control strategy combining backstepping sliding mode control (BSMC) with a linear extended state observer (LESO) is proposed. With a quadrotor UAV dynamic model established via Euler-Newton equations, a BSMC which combines sliding mode control and backstepping is proposed to guarantee stability of UAV and a LESO is used for real-time compensation of total disturbances to improve tracking accuracy. Moreover, a time-varying threshold event-triggered (TVST) mechanism is also deliberately developed to reduce controller update frequency while maintaining the system performance. The proposed methed is an emulation one which significantly reduces the communication and computation burden and avoid the unnecessary wear and tear of the actuator. Simulations and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed control strategy achieves an effective balance between tracking accuracy and resource conservation.

title: An Augmented Reality System for Tool Tracking and Markerless Registration in Head and Neck Cancer using Depth Camera
authors: Yaning Liu, Xujing Chen, Lingxiao Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Augmented reality (AR) can enhance hand–eye coordination and depth perception during cranial procedures, providing physicians with improved intraoperative visualization. We present an AR navigation system that (i) tracks surgical tools equipped with retro-reflective markers using a time-of-flight (ToF) camera and (ii) performs markerless registration of 3D medical images to a 3D-printed head phantom for visualization on an optical see-through head-mounted display (HoloLens2). We compute the spatial relationship between the Azure Kinect ToF sensor and the HoloLens2 automatically using a 3D-printed multimodal marker, enabling device-to-device coordinate transformation without manual placement of patient fiducials. We evaluate tool tracking accuracy and registration performance on a 3D-printed phantom: the ToF-based tool tracking achieves sub-millimeter localization within the system workspace, and the markerless registration yields a target registration error (TRE) of 2.58 mm (mean, N=10). These results suggest that combining high-fidelity external depth sensing with OST visualization can reduce reliance on patient-attached fiducials while delivering clinically relevant registration and tool-tracking performance.

title: AI-Driven Multimodal Mind Mapping: A Framework for Enhanced Knowledge Modeling and Human-Computer Interaction
authors: Sarthak Karandikar, Kabeer Ahmed Merchant, Minakshi Atre, Abhijeet Suryawanshi
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Mind maps are powerful tools for modeling complex information, aiding tasks like idea generation and knowledge classification. Manual creation is a bottleneck, and early automated methods failed to capture semantic nuances or integrate diverse data modalities. This paper presents a unified multimodal framework to address these challenges using modern AI, contributing to knowledge modeling, cognitive systems, and human-computer interaction (HCI). We leverage fine-tuned Large Language Models (LLMs)—advanced neural network architectures—for high-fidelity text-to-map conversion. The framework integrates Vision Transformers (ViTs) to process visual data from images, videos, and audio streams for comprehensive knowledge extraction. A core contribution is "mind map prompting," a novel HCI paradigm where pre-structured mind maps guide LLM reasoning. Human evaluations show this method improves response completeness and relevance by over 17% and 21%, respectively. This study demonstrates a robust system for generating context-aware mind maps, advancing intelligent cognitive tools.

title: Fuzzy Cognitive Study on Criteria Decision of Effective Educational Tools in Engaging Generation Beta Learners
authors: Nivetha Martin, Ramila Gandhi, P. Pandiammal, Priya R, P. Sathya, A. Theeba
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The existing educational framework is witnessing rapid digitalization in the process of teaching and learning. The evolution of Generation Beta (2025-2039) sets a new beginning of tech savvy learners. It is essential to proactively design effective tools of education for engaging Gen Beta learners. This study explores the critical criteria to be considered in developing educational tools using the tool of Fuzzy Cognitive Map (FCM). The key criteria of Interactivity, Personalization, Gamification, Quality, Flexibility, Accessibility, Feedback Mechanisms, and Technological Compatibility are considered in FCM modelling. The objective of this work is to determine the interdependencies among these criteria using expert driven fuzzy logic. Sensitivity analysis using FCM simulation is performed to identify the dynamic and more influential criteria. The adaptability of the model is enhanced with weight updation using Hebbian learning algorithm. The results demonstrate that the criteria Interactivity and Personalization are dominant factors which perform a pivotal role in building educational tools for the upcoming learner generation. The ranking correlation values of 1 and 0.976 exhibit the alignment of FCM results with AHP & DEMATEL. The comparisons of FCM results with MCDM methods exhibit the efficacy of the proposed approach.

title: DSIG within a wind turbine system-based high-order sliding mode control
authors: Khaled Benzaoui, Ahmed Bendib, Ismail Ghadbane, Abdelmoumin Ouali, Abderrahmen Bouguerra, Samir Zeghlache
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: Various methods have been proposed to harness wind energy and convert it into electricity. This research specifically examines the control design and dynamic modeling of dual stator induction generator (DSIG) -based wind energy conversion system. To optimize power delivery to the grid and ensure peak efficiency, the generator-side controls and output parameters of the conversion system must be finely tuned. While PI controllers are commonly used for machine control, determining their gains presents a challenge in this context, as the rectifier supplying the DSIG is regulated with a simultaneous 30-degree phase angle shift. To address this issue, a nonlinear high-order sliding mode control (HOSMC) strategy is proposed. A comparative study evaluates the performance of both control methods through extensive simulation tests. Under steady-state conditions, both controllers exhibit similar tracking performance; however, the HOSMC approach demonstrates good transient response with lower overshoot and faster settling. The designed controller is rigorously analyzed and validated using high-fidelity simulations in the Plecs/RT BOX environment.

title: Planar Meta-Lens-Enabled High-Gain Circularly Polarized Fixed-Beam Antenna for Wideband IoT Applications at Millimeter-Waves
authors: Ubaid Ullah, Slawomir Koziel, Anna Pietrenko-Dabrowska
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: A planar metamaterial lens-based single-element circularly polarized (CP) antenna is proposed for wideband millimeter-wave applications. The antenna employs a modified patch excited through a coaxial probe and integrates two displaced layers of a novel meta-lens structure. This configuration enables the simultaneous excitation of orthogonal components with equal magnitudes, ensuring stable circular polarization. The meta-lens, composed of subwavelength disconnected cross-shaped unit cells, enhances the antenna gain by effectively focusing the outgoing CP wave. The design achieves a wide operating bandwidth from 24 GHz to 31 GHz, an axial ratio below 3 dB across the band, and a realized gain improvement of more than 4 dB compared to a conventional patch antenna without a lens. Owing to its simple geometry, lightweight structure, and ease of integration, the proposed antenna is highly suitable for emerging Internet of Things (IoT) applications at millimeter-wave frequencies, including high-data-rate device-to-device communication, smart sensing platforms, short-range radar systems, and line-of-sight wireless links in 5G and beyond networks. The combination of wide band performance, high gain, and polarization purity positions this design as a promising candidate for future IoT -driven millimeter-wave communication systems.

title: RISC-V Based Modular ALU Framework: Simulation of Complete ISA and FPGA-Based ALU Operation Implementation
authors: Venu Gopala Krishna Davala, Manikanta Reddy Medagam, Shyamosree Goswami, Anup Dandapat
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: In this paper, we present the process of designing, simulating, and implementing a 32-bit single-cycle RISC-V processor with ALU custom operation logic using Zynq-7000 XC7Z020CLG484-1 FPGA. We designed the processor to support a typical instruction set with R-type, I-type, S-type, and B-type operation instructions and to use custom ALU operations: NAND, NOR, XOR, AND, OR, addition, subtraction, and multiplication. The model is provided in Verilog HDL, and the design was developed using Xilinx Vivado 2019.2. The ALU output and the debug state of the Program Counter (PC) output were connected to the ZC702 board LEDs, which enabled viewing processor activity in real-time. The project was validated using functional simulation with GTKWave, along with hardware debugging with the FPGA, ensuring correctness. This work provided an excellent example of extending RISC-V modularity and an end-to-end project of a single-cycle processor and a foundation for custom processor designs and educational projects.

title: The Calibration Gap: Model-Specific Confidence Thresholds for Reliable Customer Service LLMs
authors: Nitin Kumar, Meetu Malhotra, Rajeev Kumar
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Every automated reply in customer service is a bet on a model's self-belief. When that confidence is wrong, brands either frustrate customers with bad answers or swamp agents with avoidable escalations. Deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) for customer service therefore hinges on calibrated confidence-i.e., the alignment between a model's stated confidence and the likelihood it is correct-because that signal governs automate-vs-escalate decisions. We benchmark three LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, LLaMA-70B) on 2500 realistic hospitality cases spanning billing disputes, room service requests, experience complaints, and policy inquiries. Using an LLM-as-Judge framework that scores factual accuracy, process correctness, and completeness, we quantify calibration with reliability curves and confidence-accuracy gaps, and we sweep decision thresholds under a utility objective (accuracy minus escalation cost). Results show clear, category-dependent calibration differences: Claude-3.5-Sonnet is best aligned with the reliability diagonal overall; LLaMA-70B is most overconfident-especially for subjective experience complaints (gap 0.183)-and GPT-4o sits between these extremes. Threshold analysis indicates that optimal confidence cutoffs are model-specific and lower than common industry defaults: 0.55 for Claude-3.5-Sonnet, 0.60 for GPT4o, and 0.55-0.65 for LLaMA-70B (sensitive to escalation costs). These findings argue that confidence-based automation must consider calibration quality alongside accuracy, with model- and category-specific thresholds (stricter for subjective complaints) to balance automation gains against service reliability.

title: Mitigating MRI Domain Shift in Sex Classification: A Deep Learning Approach with Combat Harmonization
authors: Peyman Sharifian, Mohammad Saber Azimi, Masoud Noroozi, Ali Reza Karimian, Hossein Arabi
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Deep learning models for medical image analysis often suffer from performance degradation when applied to data from different scanners or protocols, a phenomenon known as domain shift. This study investigates this challenge in the context of sex classification from 3D T1-weighted brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans using the IXI and OASIS3 datasets. While models achieved high within-domain accuracy (around 0.95) when trained and tested on a single dataset (IXI or OASIS3), we demonstrate a significant performance drop to chance level (about 0.50) when models trained on one dataset are tested on the other, highlighting the presence of a strong domain shift. To address this, we employed the ComBat harmonization technique to align the feature distributions of the two datasets. We evaluated three state-of-the-art 3D deep learning architectures (3D ResNet18, 3D DenseNet, and 3D EfficientNet) across multiple training strategies. Our results show that ComBat harmonization effectively reduces the domain shift, leading to a substantial improvement in cross-domain classification performance. For instance, the cross-domain balanced accuracy of our best model (ResNet18 3D with Attention) improved from approximately 0.50 (chance level) to 0.61 after harmonization. t-SNE visualization of extracted features provides clear qualitative evidence of the reduced domain discrepancy postharmonization. Cross-domain balanced accuracy improved from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.50$</tex> to 0.61 after ComBat, a modest yet meaningful gain that moves the model from chance-level failure toward more reliable generalization while remaining below clinical utility. This work underscores the critical importance of domain adaptation techniques for building robust and generalizable neuroimaging AI models.

title: Enhancing Population Diversity and Optimization Efficiency in Cat Swarm Optimization Using a Fuzzy Controller
authors: Seyedeh Maryam Rezaei, Reza Boostani
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Optimization plays a critical role in artificial intelligence and various computational tasks. Metaheuristic algorithm such as GA, PSO, and CSO are widely used, yet CSO suffers from low exploration due to rapid convergence. In this study, we propose a fuzzy-based enhancement to CSO to increase population diversity and prevent premature convergence. A fuzzy controller dynamically adjusts agent velocities based on the execution stage and fitness relative to the global best. The proposed method was evaluated on ten complex benchmark functions, each executed 30 times to ensure statistical validity and compared to the standard CSO, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA). Experimental results show that the fuzzy-based CSO significantly improves convergence accuracy and avoids local optima, achieving an average improvement of 12.4% in solution quality over the standard CSO and outperforming PSO and GA in 80% of the test cases. The proposed scheme can be applied in optimization of biomedical data such as learning of weights in deep learning structures and biomedical data analysis.

title: A 22 nm FD-SOI CMOS K-Band Sliding-IF Transmitter with Stacked Power Amplifier
authors: Zhize Ma, Yufei Liu, Sutton Hathorn, Saeed Mohammadi
publish: 2026 IEEE Topical Conference on RF/Microwave Power Amplifiers for Radio and Wireless Applications (PAWR)
abstract: A two-step sliding-IF K-band transmitter (TX) is implemented in GlobalFoundries' 22 nm FD-SOI CMOS technology. The sliding-IF architecture reduces LO frequency requirements and mitigates LO-induced distortion, while two passive double-balanced mixers and an IF Cascode amplifier provide sufficient conversion gain with improved linearity. A six-stacked transistor differential power amplifier, together with a driver amplifier and on-chip baluns, delivers large output swing and robust matching. A divide-by-2 frequency divider enables operation with a single external LO. The TX achieves RF transmission from 18.5-24 GHz with baseband input from 0.5-1.5 GHz and LO range of 12–15 GHz. At 22 GHz, it demonstrates 18.3 dBm output power and 16.3 dB conversion gain with a total power consumption of 0.92 W and area of 1.73 mm2. Owing to the sliding-IF approach and the stacked PA, the design achieves competitive linearity and enhanced output performance, while the use of back-gate biasing in 22FDX further improves gain and power efficiency. These results establish the design's suitability for 5G/6G K-band front ends.

title: Semi-Supervised Binary Particle Swarm Optimization Clustering Algorithm for Open Set Recognition in ECG-Based Authentication
authors: Mamata Pandey, Anup Kumar Keshri
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: The basic machine learning models use the closed-set approach and are limited to recognizing only the categories on which they have been trained. When a new, unknown category emerges, these models incorrectly classify it into one of the known categories. This limits the utility of this traditional closed-set approach for authentication. This study introduces an open-set recognition system using a novel variant of the Semi-supervised Binary Particle Swarm Optimization Clustering (SBPSOC) Algorithm for ECG-based authentication, which identifies known categories and rejects unknown categories. This straightforward and effective method has been used for ECG-based authentication using statistical features. It contributes to the development of the next generation of biometric systems that utilize ECG. Additionally, the proposed method aids in open-set recognition for statistical data.

title: ESG Rating Model Based on GA-BP Neural Network
authors: XiaoYan Xu, Gui Lan Ma, Su Ying Lan, Li Ling Wen
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: As global efforts toward sustainable development deepen, Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) ratings have become a key benchmark for assessing corporate sustainability performance. However, traditional rating paradigms face limitations in terms of rating consistency and predictive validity, either due to the subjective biases in expert scoring or the difficulty faced by linear weighting methods such as AHP and entropy weight method in characterizing the complex nonlinear interactions between indicators. In view of this, the proposed model constructs a hybrid intelligent model integrating Genetic Algorithm (GA) and BP Neural Network, aiming to enhance the objectivity and accuracy of ESG ratings. The research samples are obtained from the CSMAR and Huazheng ESG databases, covering the data of A-share non-financial listed companies from 2020 to 2024. The empirical results show that compared with the standard BP neural network, the GA-BP model exhibits better robustness and generalization performance in cross-year and cross-industry scenarios, significantly reducing prediction errors and improving rating consistency. It provides a transferable intelligent solution for ESG evaluation of non-listed companies and investment decision-making.

title: Reliability-Aware Deep Reinforcement Learning Scheduling for Microservices in Maritime Distributed Multi-Cloud Environment
authors: Haotong Qiu, Chen Ma, Jingqi Wu, Ning Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: The maritime distributed computing environment exhibits inherent characteristics such as high network latency, resource constraints, and significant heterogeneity in node reliability. In this context, individual vessels can be abstracted as homogeneous distributed cloud units, collectively forming a dynamic ship-based multi-cloud computing platform. To ensure the efficient execution of data-intensive maritime missions, this paper investigates the microservice scheduling problem within this platform, focusing on the critical challenge of co-optimizing task completion makespan and reliability assurance. Traditional heuristic scheduling algorithms struggle to achieve a dynamic balance among these multi-dimensional objectives in such a dynamic environment, often leading to suboptimal resource efficiency or reliability violations. To address this, we propose a reliability-constrained cooperative microservice scheduling framework based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL). First, a formal model of the maritime multi-cloud system is established, explicitly incorporating variable communication delays and node failure rates. The scheduling problem is then formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), and a policy-based DRL agent is designed to learn an end-to-end dynamic scheduling policy that jointly considers execution efficiency and reliability. This agent is trained via a composite reward function, which guides it to minimize the overall scheduling makespan while strictly adhering to predefined task reliability thresholds. Extensive simulations under various scenarios demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms several classical heuristic and meta-heuristic baseline algorithms across different load conditions. Particularly under high-load scenarios, it effectively reduces the maximum task completion time while guaranteeing that task reliability remains above the specified threshold.

title: Transformer Beyond Semantics: Next-Generation Transformer Integrating Emotional Representations
authors: Qingshi Wang, Yin Li
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Current Transformer architectures primarily rely on semantic pattern matching but lack a systematic mechanism for modeling self-referential emotional representations. Drawing from both Eastern and Western psychological theories, this study examines their correspondence with the information-processing structure of Transformers and identifies a crucial gap in affective computing—the absence of self-emotional representation. To address this, we propose the Reactive Emotion Flow Transformer (REFT), which extends the Transformer with a dynamic emotional representation module. Through cross-attention and a dual-level gated weighting mechanism, REFT achieves an adaptive fusion of semantic and emotional information. A staged training strategy further enhances its stability and performance. Based on REFT, we implement the Affect Semantic Model (AS Model), which jointly processes emotional reactions and semantic cues to perform affective reasoning. Experimental results show that our model surpasses existing architectures—despite their far larger parameter scales—in both emotion perception and simulation. While built upon the Transformer framework, which has been succeeded by newer architectures, REFT serves as a meaningful paradigm for exploring the integration of cognitive and emotional processes within AI. This research not only provides a novel interpretive lens for understanding Transformers through the synthesis of Eastern and Western psychological concepts but also establishes theoretical and practical foundations for next-generation AI capable of emotional reasoning, adaptive human–AI interaction, and safety-aligned decision-making.

title: Graph Attention Networks for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition: Focus on Channel-Level Attention
authors: Akbar Asgharzadeh-Bonab, Hamid Bigdeli, Mohammad Javad Heidari
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: We investigate the specific contribution of channel-level attention to EEG-based emotion recognition by introducing Channel-GAT, a compact two-layer graph attention network that operates on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-20$</tex> sensor graph. Each 1-s window is represented as a 32-node graph with differential-entropy band features; edges capture spatial proximity and, when reliable, functional connectivity. On the DEAP dataset, Channel-GAT consistently outperforms nonattention graph models and common grid-based baselines while remaining lightweight, reaching around 93% accuracy for valence and 92 % for arousal in subject-dependent evaluation and competitive performance under leave-one-subject-out testing. Ablations show that attention yields a clear gain over a plain GCN, and that using an explicit graph structure substantially improves over MLPs without topology. Attention maps emphasize frontal regions for valence and parietal-occipital regions for high arousal, frequently highlighting F3/F4, T7/T8, and Pz/Oz-patterns that align with affective neurophysiology and support principled channel selection for low-density wearables. The resulting pipeline is reproducible, trains within minutes per fold on a single GPU, and offers an interpretable, deploymentfriendly pathway to real-time affect monitoring.

title: Federated Learning-Based Network Intrusion Detection System with Differential Privacy: A Multi-Model Approach
authors: Sisira R Nair, Piyoosh Purushothaman Nair
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Traditional centralized intrusion detection models in IoT systems have several limitations, such as privacy violations, communication overhead, and single points of failure. To overcome such limitations, Federated Learning-based Intrusion Detection Systems (FL-IDS) are designed for distributed IoT environments. The FL-IDS framework trains machine learning models locally on each client side and ensures data remain confidential at each local node itself. In our work, we propose three security-enhanced decentralized FL-IDS frameworks by integrating model-specific differential privacy (DP) schemes to protect the model parameters from inference attacks. Here, the method of integration of differential privacy is tailored for each machine learning model used for FL-IDS. In the Random Forest setup, Laplace DP mechanism is applied directly to model hyperparameters with a decaying privacy budget. For Artificial Neural Network (ANN), gradient clipping and gaussian noise are introduced, both during local model training and during server-side aggregation. The Autoencoder-based model applies client-side differential privacy through adaptive gradient clipping and gaussian noise injection. The model updates are then aggregated using the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm, which allows the central server to build a good IDS model without the need to access sensitive client data. Evaluations on the dataset CIC-IDS2017 show high detection accuracy (99.6% for Random Forest, 89% for ANN, and 80% for Autoencoder) while maintaining privacy. This modular FL-IDS with a differential privacy framework ensures secure, scalable and privacy preserving intrusion detection suitable for distributed environments.

title: Enhancing Visual Perception in Darkness: A Lightweight Attention-Based Model for Nighttime Surveillance
authors: Abhishek Ojha, Nitin Tyagi, Pushpa Singh
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Low-light image enhancement (LLIE) remains a challenging task due to various reasons like amplification of noise, colour distortion, uneven illumination, and loss of detail like texture and edges under poor illumination conditions. Recent works either majorly depends on computationally expensive transformer-based models or handcrafted Retinex-inspired frameworks. In this paper, we propose IA-DILAT-CBAM, it is a lightweight and efficient convolutional neural network model for real-time low-light image enhancement. By combining Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) blocks, Dilated Convolutions, and Illumination-Adaptive features, our model presents a novel hybrid attention strategy This model maintains colour and spatial consistency while allowing adaptive enhancement and multi-scale feature extraction. The use of residual learning improves convergence and enhances detail reconstruction without paired supervision. IA-DILAT-CBAM outperforms a number of current state-of- the-art techniques on the LOLv2 dataset, achieving notable improvements in both PSNR and SSIM in contrast to conventional Retinex-based or event-driven approaches. Additionally, our model shows good generalization on difficult real-world dark scenes with low computational complexity, which makes it appropriate for low-power and embedded vision systems.

title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning for Secure Healthcare Data Processing
authors: Ateeq Ur Rehman, Anoud Bani Hani, Sana Maroof, Sara Shar Faiz Alshehri
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Software Defined Systems (SDS)
abstract: Machine learning is growing in significance in the medical sector because it has the capability to gather useful data from high-dimensional information such as medical images. By Federated Learning, it has been made easier to train machine learning models privately without centralizing the personal data of patients. FL is still weak on privacy and security constraints. This paper proposes a federated learning system to classify brain tumors in MRI images while maintaining model confidentiality, thus keeping sensitive healthcare data secure. Our approach combines data governance and differential privacy principles, utilizing Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) at the local level and Gaussian noise for global differential privacy. Results on a brain tumor MRI dataset demonstrate improved accuracy and precision while preserving privacy.

title: Travel Navigator : A Generative AI Driven Personalized Travel Planning Website
authors: Fathima Nasrin P. K, Hima Jyothish, Sameera T U, Seethal Anil, Chinchu Jacob
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: The Travel Navigator project is one of the AI-powered solutions to help users reduce the hassle of travel planning by providing recommendations on travel destinations, accommodations, routes, and activities according to the user's preferences. Backed by state-of-the-art techniques in AI, such as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), travel navigator supports text and voice inputs intuitively, therefore making it very interactive and engaging for the user. Once registered, users will be required to take a short test to indicate the nature of their trip, such as duration and type of travel, whether solo, couple or family. Its recommendation engine not only provides personalized insights into travel but also predicts future interests, so it refreshes recommendations every time users interact. In addition, it also provide a PDF converting feature where the whole itinerary can be downloaded. This way, the Travel Navigator blends generative AI with RAG for answering real-time relevant contextually accurate information drawn from a huge pool of travel data, thus providing an adaptive and highly comprehensive travel planning experience. The system also provides a Chatbot interface where the users can have live interaction with the model.

title: Hierarchical BiLSTM Encoding for Vocal-Driven Cover Song Detection
authors: Randeep Palit, Satya Ranjan Dash, Jnyana Ranjan Mohanty, Anshuman Mahabhoi, Ayush Anmol
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Cover song detection remains a challenging task due to significant variations in key, tempo, timbre, and arrangement across different versions of the same song. In this paper, we propose a deep learning neural net that leverages isolated vocal stems and extracts multiple complementary low-level beat synchronized audio features— MFCC, Chroma and CQT. These features are processed through a multi-branch BiLSTM-Attention architecture designed to generate musically discriminative embeddings. We use triplet focal loss in training process.The unique combination of BiLSTM and hierarchical sparse attention mechanism improves the embedding separability. Experiments on the SHS100K dataset demonstrate good retrieval performance, achieving a MAP @ 10 of 0.713 and an MRI of 23.7.

title: Experimental and Modeling Study of a Solar-Powered Greenhouse Dryer: Energy Efficiency and Thermal Performance under Semi-Arid Climate
authors: Abdelouahab Benseddik, Abdelghani Boubekri, Hocin Bensaha, Djamel Daoud, Adiba Benahmed Djilali, Ismail Ghadbane
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference of Advanced Technology in Electronic and Electrical Engineering (ICATEEE)
abstract: In this study, a solar-powered greenhouse dryer was designed, constructed, and evaluated for drying zucchini slices under semi-arid climate conditions. The system's thermal performance was assessed through temperature distribution, drying kinetics, and drying rate, and compared with the conventional open sun-drying method. Results show that the greenhouse dryer significantly reduced the total drying duration to 2 days, compared to more than 3 days for open sun drying, and provided more stable thermal conditions with peak absorber, air, and product temperatures of 65 °C, 57.5 °C, and 50 °C, respectively. Energy performance indicators demonstrated improved solar utilization efficiency and enhanced moisture removal under controlled greenhouse conditions. Experimental drying kinetics data were fitted to seven mathematical models, with the Midilli-Kucuk model showing the best agreement (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9989 and 0.9996, χ = 1.93×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup> and 5.71×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>, RMSE = 0.0139 and 0.0076). These results highlight the potential of greenhouse solar dryers as energy-efficient solutions for agro-food processing in arid regions, contributing to sustainable renewable energy applications in agricultural engineering.

title: Breast Cancer Segmentation and Classification Using Attention Unet and EfficientDFE-Net
authors: Anitha V, Inisha Sallove R, Karishma P, Madhumitha S, Sivasankari G
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the most prevalent and life-threatening diseases among women worldwide, making early detection and precise segmentation crucial for effective diagnosis and treatment. Traditional hand- crafted manual segmentation is usually time-consuming, inter-observer dependent and skill-based. Keeping pace with advancements in deep learning, professional neural networks have shown remarkable potential in the processing of medical images. This work proposes a novel framework for breast cancer segmentation and classification that utilizes Attention U-Net for segmentation and EfficientDFE-Net for classification. Attention U-Net is a new composite model integrating the power of U-Net and attention blocks to enhance feature extraction and precision in segmentation. For classification, EfficientDFE-Net, aspecifically optimized deep learning architecture integrated with Deep Feature Enhancement(DFE) blocks, is employed for classification to enable maximum performance in distinguishing malignant, benign, and normal cases. Our experimental findings validate the efficacy of the given framework, obtaining 99.5% segmentation accuracy and 99.94% classification accuracy, thus indicating its promising applications to clinical practices.

title: Assessment of Feature Importance with Integrated Gradients for LSTM Streamflow Forecasting Across Flow Regimes: UBA River Basin Case Study
authors: Almas Alzhanov, Kamilla Rakhymbek, Yerzhan Baiburin, Aliya Nugumanova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Deep learning models such as Long Short-Term Memory networks have shown strong potential for streamflow forecasting. However, their limited interpretability poses challenges for operational use. This study examines how predictor importance varies across flow regimes and whether this can inform the development of more efficient, transparent models. Using the Uba River Basin as a case study, we apply a stratified modeling approach with Integrated Gradients to analyze feature relevance during high, normal, and low flows. Models trained on reduced feature sets derived from the analysis performed competitively with the baseline model using all features. The model with universal and high-flow-specific predictors achieved higher accuracy (NSE 0.787 vs. 0.745), while the normal-flow model reduced high-flow bias. Meanwhile, the baseline remained the most balanced overall, achieving the highest KGE score (0.829). These findings suggest that regimeaware feature selection can improve interpretability and efficiency without substantially compromising performance and contribute to the broader operational adoption of deep learning in hydrological forecasting.

title: MediBox - Smart Autonomous Medicine Reminder & Dispenser for Efficient Healthcare
authors: Shubham Singh, Ifra, Asha Rani Mishra, Sagufta, Shubham
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: Adherence to medication is a severe problem among the aging, physically challenged as well as Alzheimer patients who are not able to take medications on their own. The current pill boxes, reminders apps, and static dispensers do not have secure authentication, mobility, and real time availability which results in missed or wrong doses sometimes. The work describes an independent medicine dispensing robot that integrates LiDAR-based SLAM and ROS framework to navigate safely around the location, facial recognition with the help of the TensorFlow Lite to verify patients, and monitor dispensed medications in real-time. A web app based on React Native allows scheduling, alerts and remote monitoring through the cloud. The proposed model can enhance medication adherence, patient safety, and caregiver independence in home healthcare, as it will combine autonomous navigation, identity verification, and intelligent dispensing into a single system.

title: Neural Encoding of Outcome Magnitude: Evidence from fMRI
authors: Amin Mohammad Mohammadi, Shaghayegh Mahmoudi, Narjes Amin, Farid Hosseinzadeh, Elias Ebrahimzadeh, Hamid Soltanian-Zadeh
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Reward and punishment outcomes play a central role in adaptive decision-making, yet while the effects of outcome valence have been extensively studied, the neural encoding of outcome magnitude remains less well understood. Previous neuroimaging research has typically examined reward magnitude in isolation, often with only two outcome levels, limiting the precision of magnitude-related findings. To address this gap, we conducted a functional MRI (fMRI) study using a modified monetary gambling task designed to parametrically vary both gain and loss magnitudes. Twenty-four healthy participants performed 100 randomized trials while undergoing 3 Tesla fMRI scanning. Outcome magnitudes were modeled as parametric modulators within the general linear model framework, allowing us to identify brain regions whose blood-oxygen-level-dependent (BOLD) responses scaled with feedback size. Group-level analyses revealed significant magnitude-related activations in a distributed network including the dorsal anterior cingulate cortex (dACC), Rolandic Operculum extending into the insula, bilateral inferior frontal gyrus (IFG), precuneus, and right cerebellum. Notably, the dACC showed robust bilateral engagement, consistent with its role in evaluating outcome salience and encoding motivationally relevant information. These findings demonstrate that outcome magnitude, across both rewards and punishments, recruits neural circuits beyond classical reward areas, engaging regions involved in cognitive control, interoceptive processing, and adaptive behavioral regulation. Our results advance the understanding of how the human brain encodes the quantitative features of feedback and highlight the importance of considering magnitude alongside valence in models of decision-making and reinforcement learning.

title: Embedded-Based Target Recognition and Tracking Vehicle
authors: Yuhang Yang, Shuduo Zhao, Yunsheng Chen, Jinlong Tang, Jinwen Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning, Computational Intelligence and Pattern Recognition (MLCIPR)
abstract: Traditional embedded smart vehicles struggle to balance high-quality visual processing with highly reliable real-time control. This paper designs and implements an embedded target-tracking vehicle based on Raspberry Pi 5 and STM32F407. The system adopts a master-slave architecture. The Raspberry Pi 5, equipped with a Hailo-8 AI expansion board, runs YOLO object detection for real-time target detection. The STM32F407 serves as the execution layer, responsible for motor control and real-time obstacle avoidance decisions based on five infrared sensors, featuring autonomous obstacle avoidance with interrupt priority. Test results demonstrate the vehicle’s stable performance in complex environments for target recognition and dynamic tracking, reliably triggering obstacle avoidance when obstacles appear. The proposed hardware and software design provides a reference implementation path for research and application in embedded intelligent mobile platforms.

title: Model Identification of the IGBT Current and Its State Observer for Control Purpose
authors: Feriyonika, Arief Syaichu Rohman, Mervin Tangguar Hutabarat, Tri Desmana Rachmildha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: The design of power electronic systems involving Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBT), such as inverters and converters, will be more optimal if designed based on its appropriate behavioral model. However, IGBT modeling using analytical methods is difficult to realize because not all model parameters are available in the datasheet. Another problem is that IGBT naturally has internal dynamics affecting the modeling parameters, so its behavior automatically changes. Both of these problems are proposed to be this research contribution. The first is to obtain the IGBT model using the identification system technique. The second is to add a state observer so that all model states can be updated based on the IGBT dynamics. This study’s data is the gate-emitter voltage ($\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{Ge}}$) with the variation of duty cycle and collector current (IC). To reduce raw data noise, a first-order low-pass filter is employed. After completing all identification process, the derived model is then verified for training and testing data showing significant differences. Thus, the state observer is introduced. The design of the state observer begins by first analyzing the observability requirement and then determining the pole of the state observer (in this study, the gain is $5,10,50$, 100, 159 times the pole of the IGBT model). The test results show that the coefficient of determination $\left(R^{2}\right)$ is $0.945,0.9797$, $0.9969,0.9972$, and 0.9974 for each gain. Considering the gain of 159 causes the resulted observer matrix is close to the safe matrix operation limit, both gain of 50 and 100 are recommended as the state observer used. The proposed method of this study can be used to obtain both the IGBT model and the updated state, which are essential for the realization of statebased control algorithms such as state feedback, LQR, LQG, MPC, and SMC.

title: Detection of islanding condition in ongrid solar power plant with Wavelet Neural Network method based on Multi-Layer Preceptron (MLP)
authors: Ahmad Caputra Wisesa, Syamsir Abduh, Marwan Rosyadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: Using renewable energy to meet the world’s energy needs is becoming a trend. The application of distributed energy alters the structure of the distribution system, making the electricity system more complex. In order to maintain power quality and distribution reliability, a system that can detect these conditions is necessary. Islanding can cause interference and reduce energy quality. The use of many nonlinear loads in renewable energy plants can increase harmonic distortion, frequency fluctuations, and voltage fluctuations. Islanding detection is necessary to maintain energy quality and secure the electrical system. This research applies an islanding detection method that utilizes wavelet transform as part of a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)-based neural network process. The simulation achieved an accuracy rate of 98.11% with a detection time of 0.006254 seconds per sample. The average mean square error (MSE) value is 0.0200 after 21 retraining iterations. External validation yielded an MSE value of 0.02023 and an accuracy rate of $97.57 \%$.

title: Dual Attention Network for Multimodal Physiological Signal-Based Fatigue Detection
authors: Santosh Kumar, Sanchita Paul
publish: 2025 OITS International Conference on Information Technology (OCIT)
abstract: Maintaining focused attention and mental wellbeing is essential for safety in industrial environments, particularly for drivers operating under high-pressure and fastmoving conditions. This study proposes a Dual Attention Network (DAN) integrates temporal attention to capture critical time-dependent patterns and modality attention to dynamically weigh the contribution of EEG, ECG, GSR, and body temperature while also considering the driving stressor factors such as noise from traffic, overall temperature and humidity. This dual mechanism enhances interpretability and improves fatigue classification accuracy. For robust evaluation, the study uses two different datasets including the publicly available Fatigue Set dataset and locally collected multimodal dataset using wearable device for Indian Industrial drivers. This integration improved the diversity of subjects and driving conditions, enabling the DAN model to achieve higher generalizability. Demographic analysis revealed significant differences in fatigue across socio-economic status, marital status, geographical location, and age. Sleep duration showed a strong protective effect, inversely correlated with fatigue. Ground-truth labels were obtained using the Multidimensional Fatigue Inventory (MFI-20), and features including heart rate variability, EEG band powers, skin conductance, and thermal statistics were extracted. Multiple classifiers were trained after preprocessing and balancing (SMOTE). Among traditional approaches, Support Vector Machine (SVM) achieved the highest accuracy (90.28%), with Logistic Regression, and Gradient Boosting, RNN-LSTM hybrid model performing competitively. A Dual Attention Network (DAN) model, incorporating temporal and modality attention, further advanced performance, reaching 98.40 % accuracy. This research provides technical support for the development of affordable and dependable wearable motion monitoring devices.

title: Efficient Deep Learning for Biometrics: Overview, Challenges and Trends in Ear of Frugal AI
authors: Karim Haroun, Aya Manel Zitouni, Aicha Zenakhri, Meriem Amel Guessoum, Larbi Boubchir
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Software Defined Systems (SDS)
abstract: Recent advances in deep learning, whether on discriminative or generative tasks have been beneficial for various applications, among which security and defense. However, their increasing computational demands during training and deployment translates directly into high energy consumption. As a consequence, this induces a heavy carbon footprint which hinders their widespread use and scalability, but also a limitation when deployed on resource-constrained edge devices for real-time use. In this paper, we briefly survey efficient deep learning methods for biometric applications. Specifically, we tackle the challenges one might incur when training and deploying deep learning approaches, and provide a taxonomy of the various efficient deep learning families. Additionally, we discuss complementary metrics for evaluating the efficiency of these models such as memory, computation, latency, throughput, and advocate for universal and reproducible metrics for better comparison. Last, we give future research directions to consider. Index Terms-Frugal AI, Efficient Deep learning, Biometrics.

title: Face Recognition under Extreme Occlusion and Low-Resolution: A Comparative Study of Deep and Non-Deep Methods
authors: Lili Song, Zhenxue Chen, Chengyun Liu, Jiyang Chen
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Under complex environmental conditions, the performance of various face recognition methods exhibits significant variability. However, systematic comparisons between traditional methods and deep learning approaches under a unified standard are still limited. This study aims to conduct a comprehensive performance evaluation of four representative face recognition methods — Eigenfaces, Fisherfaces, Vgg16, and ResNet50 — on the PubFig dataset through a standardized experimental protocol, focusing on their recognition robustness and computational efficiency under complex conditions. The PubFig dataset was utilized, and a rigorous evaluation process based on five-fold cross-validation was implemented. Performance metrics, including accuracy, F1 score, and ROC curves, were measured under precisely controlled variations in illumination intensity. Experimental results indicate that convolutional neural network (CNN)-based deep learning methods significantly outperform traditional approaches in terms of recognition accuracy, with ResNet50 achieving the highest performance at 94.8% accuracy and demonstrating commendable stability. The findings suggest that method selection should align with specific application requirements: ResNet50 is recommended for high-precision security applications, while Fisherfaces is more suitable for mobile applications with limited computational resources. This study serves as an important reference for future research and practical applications.

title: Hybrid Robust Watermarking System Leveraging Polar Harmonic Transform and Convolutional Code
authors: Fabian Rashed Majduddin, Gelar Budiman, Linda Meylani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies in Energy and Informatic (ICATEI)
abstract: This paper presents a robust digital image watermarking framework with the explicit goal of surpassing the robustness of prior studies against geometric distortions and signal processing attacks. The framework integrates three key components:Polar Harmonic Transform, Spread Transform Dither Modulation, and Convolutional Coding. PHT provides geometric invariance, establishing a stable transform domain resistant to rotation and scaling. Within this domain, STDM enables efficient and imperceptible watermark embedding, ensuring resilience against noise and compression. To further enhance reliability, Convolutional Coding is employed as a forward error-correction scheme, where the coding rate introduces structured redundancy to the watermark message prior to embedding. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a Bit Error Rate (BER) of zero under severe conditions, including JPEG compression and geometric transformations. Moreover, the system maintains high perceptual quality, with Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) values exceeding those of state-of-the-art techniques, thereby confirming its effectiveness in advancing watermarking robustness for real-world applications.

title: Real-Time Collaborative Programming: An Approach to Improving Productivity and Code Reliability
authors: Sandhya Umrao, Upendra Dwivedi, Sanjay Kumar, Bhawna Singh
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The Internet is growing rapidly, and many desktop apps are already being developed for online use. With the Internet, many apps are easily accessible anytime, anywhere. The Cloud Editor is one of the tools developers need to design their applications. The Joint Real-Time Cloud Editor also allows multiple users to edit the same document that replaces traditional document laying and document techniques. However, developing joint web applications is a time-consuming and complex effort, as document synchronization and implementation of competitive solution services is required. The purpose of this application is to use web socket technology to create and develop real-time code editor applications that will make it easier for users to collaborate with and collaborate when working on a project. Users of this program can collaborate in real time on projects. Cloud Editor is a web application that provides a workspace for creating, running and interacting with other users in real time above the terminal. The proposed system discuss a cloud-based real-time collaborative code editor designed to support synchronous multi-user programming. Unlike conventional desktop IDEs, the solution adopts a distributed, event-driven architecture using WebRTC, Socket.io, Node.js, and React.js. The primary contribution of this work lies in the complete design and technical implementation of a real-time collaborative programming environment using WebRTC, Socket.io, Node.js, and React.js. The proposed architecture and synchronization strategy provide a foundation for future empirical studies and performance analysis.

title: Robust Jailbreak Detection in Large Language Models Using Behavioral Fingerprinting
authors: Chaitanya Tumma, R Archana Reddy, Rajat Yadav, Abid Salati, Surendarkumar S, Karthikayen A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Modeling, Simulation &amp; Intelligent Computing (MoSICom)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in real-world settings, and with their growing popularity, there have also been growing jailbreak attack problems, wherein users deliberately circumvent security controls to receive harmful or illegal responses. Traditional defense techniques, including static prompt classifiers and structural fingerprinting, are not likely to be effective under adaptive adversarial attacks, particularly in multi-turn dialogue. Here, a behavioral fingerprinting technique is proposed that detects sequential user-LLM interaction patterns for distinguishing between adversarial and normal behavior. Temporal and semantic drift modeling across sessions augments the detection of adversarial purpose evaded by one-prompt inspection techniques. Experimental results show that the proposed framework attains an F1-score of 95.4%, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines like MergePrint, AAD-LLM, and static classifiers. These findings affirm that behavior fingerprinting offers an interpretable and scalable solution for enhancing jailbreak resistance in LLMs. The research encourages adaptive defense strategies that are effective at protecting LLM deployments in adversarial and dynamic environments.

title: An Edge-Cloud Enabled Machine Learning Framework for Anomaly Detection and Predictive Analysis in Smart City Power Grids
authors: Tamara Zhukabayeva, Zulfiqar Ahmad, Dina Satybaldina, Yerik Mardenov
publish: 2025 12th International Conference on Internet of Things: Systems, Management and Security (IOTSMS)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive machine learning framework for anomaly detection and predictive modeling in smart grid environments, tailored to support the stability and efficiency of energy systems within smart cities. The proposed framework initially applies the unsupervised learning methods to find anomalous or unstable states of the grid with Isolation Forest and Local Outlier Factor (LOF). The performance of Isolation Forest in terms of anomaly detection with the help of Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) is higher than LOF. Anomalies are eliminated, and subsequently predictive modeling is done based on three supervised algorithms: logistic regression (LR), support vector machine (SVM), and random forest (RF). It is shown in experimental analysis that SVM and RF perform better than LR with classification accuracies of 98 percent and 95 percent, respectively. The models are also tested on the performance measures, which include precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrices, Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC), and Precision-Recall (PR) curves. The framework is able to identify anomalies and forecast grid behavior, which are important to the intelligent, fault-tolerant, and data-driven energy management systems that are required in a smart city infrastructure.

title: Research on AIS Navigational Aid Data Authentication Mechanism Based on Quantum Key Distribution
authors: Qiang Guo, Kehuai Ji
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: This paper aims to design a highly secure, low-latency quantum key distribution (QKD)-based AIS navigational aid data authentication mechanism. The core method integrates QKD with a lightweight authentication protocol, constructs a system model adapted to maritime scenarios, proposes a resource-constrained QKD key negotiation optimization strategy, and develops a lightweight quantum key authentication framework. Simulation experiments verify that the mechanism meets five security objectives, including data integrity and identity authentication, with an authentication latency as low as 8.2ms, CPU utilization of 8.2%, single authentication energy consumption of 3.1mJ, and key utilization of 92.3%. It can effectively resist various attacks such as quantum computing and forgery. The two major innovations are the QKD key negotiation optimization strategy adapted to the AIS "work-sleep" mode and the lightweight quantum key authentication framework, which fully adapts to the resource constraints of navigational aid nodes.

title: Semg-Based Knee Torque Estimation
authors: Mohammad-Reza Sayyed Noorani, Mariya A. Vaziry, Seyed Alireza MirTajeddini
publish: 2025 32nd National and 10th International Iranian Conference on Biomedical Engineering (ICBME)
abstract: Active rehabilitation devices use sensors to monitor movement and assess a patient's physiological capacity. Surface electromyography (sEMG) provides a non-invasive method for capturing muscle activity, while musculoskeletal models allow estimation of joint torque without bulky or expensive equipment. In this study, we developed a system that simultaneously records 2-channel sEMG and knee joint angles, integrated with a seated-leg musculoskeletal model in OpenSim via MATLAB. Experimental data were used to estimate muscle forces and compute net knee torque, yielding a peak torque of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 25 \mathrm{N} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex>, angular impulse of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 50 \mathrm{N} \cdot \mathrm{m} \cdot \mathrm{s}$</tex>, and average torque of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 12.5 \mathrm{N} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex>-values consistent with physiological expectations. This integrated approach offers a practical and cost-effective tool for real-time knee torque estimation, enabling improved assessment and monitoring in rehabilitation and biomechanical applications.

title: Hybrid Cloud-Edge Architecture for Enhancing IoT Device Autonomy
authors: Kiran Malik, Suman Chahar, Kuldeep Singh Kaswan, Jagjit Singh Dhatterwal
publish: 2025 Modern Electronics Devices and Intelligent Communication Systems (MEDCOM)
abstract: The scaling up of IoT requires smart architectures for near real-time processing of large amount of data with minimal latency and increased degree of decentralization. As compared to the existing models based solely on the cloud concept with vast computational capabilities, they make use of complex network structures that lead to increased interference and inter-data packet latency that is not ideal for time-sensitive IoT usage. The Hybrid Cloud-Edge architecture studied in this paper will increase the IoT device's degree of self-organizing relative to computational loads, by deploying sophisticated tasks between the cloud and edge levels. In this model IoT devices and edge node process data that offers real time decision making and cloud is used for storing large amount of data and complex analytics. Hence, this approach minimize latency, energy consumption, and enhance data privacy as it reduces instances of data transmission to the cloud. The hybrid model performances simulation experiments and shows that compared to cloud only frameworks the latency is reduced by 40% and energy efficiency is improved by 30%. Furthermore, the application of federated learning at the edge increases independence in IoT devices through increased control by a factor of 50%. The study therefore shows that Hybrid Cloud-Edge architecture is indeed feasible for applications that need timely, efficient, and self-sufficient IoT operations which is particularly pertinent in the case of smart city, industrial IoT and environmental IoT applications.

title: Research on Methods of Image Generation for Safety Education Based on Stable Diffusion and LiblibAI
authors: Xihao Niu, Zhengdao Lin, Hanzhe Zhang
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: In recent years, AI-generated imagery has shown significant promise in education, especially in domains requiring high-quality, scenario-specific visuals. This paper proposes a systematic workflow to generate safety education illustrations using Stable Diffusion and the LiblibAI platform. The approach encompasses data collection and preprocessing, prompt extraction and optimization, model selection and tuning, and scenario-driven image synthesis. The key innovation lies in leveraging "image-to-image" generation based on real equipment photographs, enriched by carefully designed prompt structures and parameter configurations. The resulting visual resources exhibit strong realism, relevance, and pedagogical applicability. This work demonstrates the potential of AI drawing technology in enriching safety education materials and paves the way for its broader application in professional education contexts.

title: Deep Audio Perception: Classifying Emotional Tone from Button Press Sounds Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: A. Divya, T. Sangeetha, S. Ponmaniraj, K. Anbazhagan, B. Bharathikannan, Karishma Sj
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: Typically, people recognized emotions using speech or faces which can be concerning for privacy, language and accessibility. In this study, I suggest Deep Audio Perception which makes use of CNNs to classify whether button press recordings are positive, neutral or negative. By processing the content of the Button Tone SZ dataset with raw 1-second audio samples, new 128×128 mel-spectrogram images are produced. Majorly, the preprocessing consists of decreasing the gating noise level, standardizing recording duration and performing augmentations such as time stretch, change in pitch, noise mixing and dynamic compression. Proposed features of the CNN include three sets of convolutions (with filter sizes of 32, 64 and 128) with batch normalization, ReLU activations, max-pooling, dropout layers and then dense layers ending with a softmax classifier. The model is built using categorical cross-entropy loss and adaptive optimization while also using early stopping. Overall, it is able to identify emotions accurately, with equal F1-scores for all the emotions. Conducting an ablation study, we noticed that mel-spectrograms with augmentation are superior to waveforms and MFCCs and also performed much better in comparison with SVM, Random Forest and k-NN. It is the first time that emotion recognition has been done using sounds from mechanical key presses, providing an approach that is safe for privacy, language-free and works great in mental health development, affective computing and real-time embedded frameworks.

title: eSPEED: Adaptive Real-Time Routing with Probabilistic Delay Guarantees in WSNs
authors: Heqing Huang, Yong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Communication Technology and Information Technology (ICCTIT)
abstract: Real-time routing in wireless sensor networks (WSNs) faces significant challenges due to the dynamic and uncertain nature of path delay. Existing studies mainly focus on minimizing average delay but fail to provide probabilistic delay guarantees, leading to performance degradation in highly dynamic link environments. This paper proposes an adaptive delay boundary estimation algorithm that efficiently estimates the mean and variance of path delay on dual time scales by decoupling queue dynamics from packet transmission time, thereby computing its probabilistic bound. Based on this, we design an enhanced real-time routing protocol eSPEED, which innovatively introduces forwarding reliability as a routing metric. Under the constraint of a delay threshold, eSPEED jointly optimizes link quality and transmission delay to ensure both timeliness and reliability of end-to-end delivery. Experimental results on the NetEye testbed show that compared to the classic SPEED protocol, eSPEED reduces the deadline miss rate by about 10%, increases network throughput by up to 23.8% under moderate load, and maintains high packet delivery success even under poor channel conditions.

title: AI-Assisted Adaptive Encryption for Secure Data Protection Using Honey Encryption and Post-Quantum Methods
authors: Yuvarani S, Subhashini R, Monika T, Dhatchayani K, Haarippriyah I, Selvabarani K
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference for Women in Computing (InCoWoCo)
abstract: With the emergence of quantum computing, many traditional encryption approaches are becoming increasingly vulnerable, raising the need for more resilient data protection techniques. This paper introduces an AI-Assisted Adaptive Encryption model that combines Honey Encryption with Post Quantum Cryptographic (PQC) schemas to enhance both security and efficiency. The proposed system employs artificial intelligence to monitor evolving risks and adjust cryptographic parameters dynamically, allowing it to maintain a balance between strong protection and system performance. Honey Encryption strengthens the defense mechanism by generating realistic but incorrect ciphertexts when subjected to brute-force attempts, thereby misleading attackers. Meanwhile PQC algorithms, particularly those based on lattice problems, ensure that the encryption remains resistant against quantum-level threats. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the framework outperforms conventional methods in terms of throughput, latency, and resistance to cryptanalysis. The results highlight that this hybrid approach not only secures sensitive information but also adapts effectively to diverse communication scenarios. By merging AI adaptability, deceptive security, and quantum resistance, the model represents a forward-looking solution for safeguarding digital systems in the post-quantum computing era.

title: 3D Low-Light Enhancement using Zero-Reference Learning
authors: Mpilo Mbulelo Tatana, Mohohlo Samuel Tsoeu, Rito Clifford Maswanganyi
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence (ACAI)
abstract: Low-light enhancement is an emerging subfield of computer vision. Low-light enhancement aims to tackle the issue of visual data captured in low-light conditions. These conditions make it hard to obtain any helpful information from the captured frames. Low-light enhancement improves these frames, such that data captured under low-light conditions appear as though they were captured under normal light conditions. This allows for valuable information to be obtained from these frames, which can then be used for further computer vision tasks such as detection and classification. This is particularly useful in nighttime surveillance tasks. Low-light enhancement has also been used for subaqueous applications, along with applications in the medical field. The majority of the proposed low-light enhancement models employ supervised learning, which presents a significant challenge in acquiring paired low-light and normal light data. This leads researchers to use synthetic data, which results in models that are unable to generalize to the real world. Zero-reference low-light enhancers tackle this issue by employing zero-reference loss functions instead of paired data during training. This allows the model to learn low-light enhancement using real low-light data, enabling it to generalize better to the real world while also being trainable on constrained data. This paper proposes Zero-3DCE, a zero-reference low-light image and video data enhancement. The model utilizes 3D separable convolutions to capture spatial and temporal information while maintaining model efficiency. A spatial attention network is employed, allowing the model to focus on key features. For zero-reference training, Zero-3DCE achieves this with zero-reference loss functions, designed using image priors, which guide the enhancement of frames. The results obtained show that Zero-3DCE achieves better enhancement when compared with other enhancement models on both 2D and 3D data. Zero-3DCE was also shown to make other computer vision tasks more robust to varying light conditions.

title: Attention-Based Multi-Sensor IMU Framework for Parkinson's Disease Detection Using Hybrid Deep Learning Models
authors: S Lakshmi Sankeerthi, Srinidhi Srinivasan, M Nohitha, N Chandana, Abhishek S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Smart and Intelligent Systems (SISCON)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a disorder that gradually degenerates the brain's neurons and, therefore, it affects mainly motor functions as well as the patient's daily life. Precise and early diagnosis is vital for treating the disease effectively, but still, the clinical diagnosis of PD is mostly done based on subjective decisions. This research work puts forward a hybrid deep learning framework that consists of CNN and LSTM models for the detection of PD using multi-sensor IMU data. Along with the aforementioned features, other features like Statistical, wavelet, spectral, and fractal features were also extracted to represent the complex motion characteristics; analyzed two datasets-the IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) activity dataset and the ALAMEDA tremor dataset. The application of traditional machine learning models on the pre-processed ALAMEDA data reached up to 97% accuracy while the CNN-LSTM hybrid model got 80% accuracy on the IMU-based recordings which were in raw format. The findings illustrate the possibility of hybrid wearable IMU-based models for objective, real-time PD monitoring and early-stage diagnosis.
